activity_name_activity_session: "Companion Teacher Guidance Skills Lecture"
activity_content:
"Companion Teacher Guidance Skills Lecture"
※This series of lectures is conducted entirely in Chinese.

How do you help students with their emotions when all you want to is to assist them, but often feel helpless? How does chatting turn into arguments without even realizing it? Why are the people we care about most the ones who seem to hurt us the most easily? Invite your teaching colleagues to explore the world of psychology and learn how to effectively resolve misunderstandings and find opportunities for cooperation. Let’s take the path towards conflict management together!

Speaker Introduction: Zhou Ziheng, Consulting Psychologist
Taiwan Guidance and Counseling Association Certified Professional Supervisors (Certification No. 114016)
Breathe Heart & Mind Clinic Counseling Psychologist
National Taipei Technology University Counseling Center Psychologist
Cancer Hope Foundation Counseling Psychologist
Flower Psychological Counseling Center Counseling Psychologist
Triple Community University's "Heartbeat Together" partnership with psychologists
Proposal for A New Life Along with Emotions

Topic: Why Are There Always Gaps in Communication? A Psychological Guide to Teacher-Student Communication and Conflict Resolution
Target: Approximately 80-100 school faculty members
Time: June 8th (Monday), noon 12:20 – 2:00 PM (total of 100 minutes)
Location: Administration Building – First Conference Hall, Central Campus

activity_name_activity_session: NTUHC-115/5/26, 27 Basic Life Support (BLS) Training
activity_content:
Office of Student Affairs Health Center BLS training in May
One, this center was established to enhance the emergency handling capabilities of faculty and staff at three affiliated universities of National Taiwan University for dealing with unexpected accidents. Specialized in-house training on "Basic Life Support" (BLS) has been organized, featuring physicians from the Emergency Medicine Department of Taichung Area Hospital, senior rescue technicians, physical therapists from Taichung Area Hospital, as well as colleagues from this center. The training aims to organize practical exercises to cultivate students' ability to handle crises in real-life situations, thereby ensuring campus and club activity safety.
Two, Date: May 26 and 27th. (May 26 from 17:15-21:50, May 27 from 17:45-21:15).
Three: Target audience: Employees and students of three affiliated universities under National Taiwan University system, with 60 spots filled.
Four、 Location: 2F Wellness Classroom in Health Center.
Five: Content: Trauma emergency, casualty搬运techniques internship (carried out in groups), Hemostasis bandaging techniques internship.
CPR training, CPR technical practice (in groups), and practical exam, written
Test.
To obtain a certificate for Basic Life Support, one must attend the course in full, pass the written test, and complete the technical assessment. In addition, public servants can earn an 8-hour lifetime learning credit.
Seven, Certificate Printing Fee Explanation:
The "certificate" for this course is optional and if you need one, you must bear the cost of NT$ per certificate.
100 yuan (production cost of Taiwan Sports Safety and First Aid Skills Promotion Association), and upon class commencement on that day.
Inform the staff and pay for fees when checking in.
Contact Information and Enrollment Eligibility:
Please make sure to provide your valid Taiwan mobile phone number when registering so that you can receive course notifications and contact information. If not provided,
Taiwan mobile phone numbers will be considered as abandoning the right to attend classes (for special circumstances, please contact承办02-)
panhx@ntu.edu.tw)
Nine, welcome on-site registration: [Registration for the course will be open to participants who arrive on site after the class has already started and is over 15 minutes. The quota of 60 people will be filled up.]
Tenth, no meals will be provided for this course. Please bring your own.
Rewards for Activities:
Students who participate in this activity will receive one lottery ticket and can participate in a prize draw at the end of the semester. The highest prize value is NT$ 4,000.
Activity-related information can be found on this center's official Facebook/Instagram platforms:
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=61585288985745
IG: https://www.instagram.com/ntu.healthcenter/

activity_name_activity_session: NTUHC: Know All About Cancer Screening
activity_content:
Healthy Sweep - Announcement for Cancer Screening Activities
Content of the activities
Protecting Health: Understanding Cancer Screening (Both Physical and Online)
Time: May 18, 2011 (Monday) 12:20–13:20
Location: Health Center Second Floor Wellness Classroom
Speaker: Family Medicine Department Physician of National Taiwan University Hospital附属Taiwan University Medical College Hospital
Cancer Screening Mobilization Practice Guidance Activity
Time: May 21, 2015 (Thursday), May 22, 2015 (Friday) 13:30-16:30
Location: First Floor Health Center
Note: This activity does not require prior registration. Qualified individuals can directly bring their Health Insurance Card to the site for screening.
Second, target audience - faculty and staff members of this school
Health Literacy Lecture预计 100 people
Cancer screening action practice activity is expected to involve 50 people.
Anyone who fully participates and completes check-in/check-out as well as satisfaction surveys can log their lifetime learning hours.
Three, activity explanation
Protecting Health: Understanding Cancer Screening
Explain through medical expertise the medical basis of cancer screening, government subsidy policies, and correct screening concepts to enhance health literacy.
Cancer Screening Action Practice Guiding Activity (On-site Actual Screening Projects)
Cervical smear test: every 3 years for females aged 25-29; annually for females over 30.
Human Papillomavirus (HPV) testing: Women aged 35, 45, and 65 can get a free test once a year.
Fecal occult blood test: every 2 years for those aged 45-75; and annually for those aged 40-45 with a family history.
Helicobacter pylori screening: once in a lifetime for individuals aged 45 to under 75 (birth years 40-70).
Breast X-ray (requires预约 at the main hospital for screening): every 2 years for females aged 45-75.
Low-dose computed tomography (LDCT) of the lungs (appointment required at Total Hospital for screening)
Family history of lung cancer: 45–75-year-old males or 40–75-year-old females with parents, children, or siblings who have been diagnosed with lung cancer.
Heavy smokers: aged 50–74, with a smoking history of over 20 packs – and who have quit smoking for less than 15 years.
Dining Instructions
There is no provision of boxed lunches for this activity; faculty members can collect points by attending lectures, while students can participate in a lottery.

Detailed activity content can be found on Facebook and Instagram. 🔍Taiwan University Health Care Center

Not applicable
IG:
https://www.instagram.com/ntu.healthcenter/

session_name: Cancer Screening (May 21st Session)
session_content:
Time: May 21st (Thursday), May 22nd (Friday) 13:30-16:30.
Location: First Floor of the Wellness Center.
Screening items include: Cervical smear test for women aged 25-29 years old, once every three years; for women over 30 years old, once a year. Human papillomavirus (HPV) testing: Women aged 35, 45, and 65 can have one free HPV test annually. Stool occult blood test: For individuals aged 45-75 with family history of colorectal cancer, every two years; for those aged 40-45 with a family history, once a year. Helicobacter pylori screening: Once in their lifetime for adults aged 45 to under 75 (born between the ages of 40 and 70), regardless of gender. Breast X-ray imaging (scheduled appointments at General Hospital): For women aged 45-75, once every two years. Low-dose computed tomography (LDCT) for lungs (scheduled appointments at General Hospital): For men or women aged 45 to 75 with a family history of lung cancer; or for severe smokers aged 50 to 74 who have smoked for over 20 packs per year but quit smoking less than 15 years ago.
Persons who meet the criteria for screening should bring their Health Insurance Card.
organizer_unit: 學生事務處保健中心

Heavy smokers: aged 50–74, with a smoking history of over 20 packs – and who have quit smoking for less than 15 years.

There is no provision of boxed lunches for this activity; faculty members can collect points by attending lectures, while students can participate in a lottery.

session_name: Cancer Screening (May 22nd Session)
session_content:
Time: May 21st (Thursday), May 22nd (Friday) 13:30-16:30.
Location: First Floor of the Wellness Center.
Screening items include: Cervical smear test for women aged 25-29 years old, once every three years; for women over 30 years old, once a year. Human papillomavirus (HPV) testing: Women aged 35, 45, and 65 can have one free HPV test annually. Stool occult blood test: For individuals aged 45-75 with family history of colorectal cancer, every two years; for those aged 40-45 with a family history, once a year. Helicobacter pylori screening: Once in their lifetime for adults aged 45 to under 75 (born between the ages of 40 and 70), regardless of gender. Breast X-ray imaging (scheduled appointments at General Hospital): For women aged 45-75, once every two years. Low-dose computed tomography (LDCT) for lungs (scheduled appointments at General Hospital): For men or women aged 45 to 75 with a family history of lung cancer; or for severe smokers aged 50 to 74 who have smoked for over 20 packs per year but quit smoking less than 15 years ago.
Persons who meet the criteria for screening should bring their Health Insurance Card.

session_name: This text is a title or introduction for an academic paper, likely in the field of law. Here's the translation to English:\n\nOn the journey towards legislative progress – tracking the creation of stalking prevention laws and offenses against sexual privacy and false images [Professor Xiangting Wang, Special Appointment Professor of Law]
session_content:
Speech Overview
From campus sexual equity practices to legislative arenas, this topic reviews how the Sexual Equity Committee has accumulated institutional experience in pushing for the creation of the stalking harassment prevention law and the "offense against privacy and false images." Through an analysis of the legislative process, it leads university students and faculty members to understand legal responses to gender-based violence in the digital age, while considering how gender equity rights can be transformed into practical legal norms.

Speaker Introduction
Professor Wang Huanju graduated from the Law Department of National Taiwan University and obtained a Master’s and Doctoral degree in law from the University of Heidelberg, Germany. Since 2003, he has been teaching at the Faculty of Law of National Taiwan University, with research interests covering criminal law, medical law, criminology, criminal policy, and legal protection for children and women. He has long focused on issues such as penal systems, medical legislation, and bioethics. He is the author of several influential academic monographs. Apart from his academic research, he actively participates in public legal and policy discussions, having had a profound impact on Taiwan’s criminal legislation and legal practices. He currently serves as the President of the Faculty of Law at National Taiwan University and Deputy Provost for Legal Affairs.

session_name: Light, consciousness, semiconductors – sharing of interdisciplinary research experience [Professor Zhu Shiwu from Physics Department]
session_content:
Speech Overview
Key breakthroughs in 20th-century physics were relativity and quantum mechanics, which originated from studies of the speed of light and black-body radiation. In the 21st century, optical research remains at the core. We are dedicated to developing innovative optical microscopy technologies that have broad applications in neuroscience and semiconductor research. On one hand, we use live imaging to dissect neuronal interactions to explore mechanisms of consciousness. On the other hand, we push beyond diffraction limits to achieve nanoscale resolution in semiconductor detection.

Speaker Introduction
Professor Zhu Shiwai is a full professor at the Department of Physics, College of Science, and also serves as the Deputy Director for Student Affairs at the Office of Student Affairs. He has held positions such as Associate Director for Leading Programs, Associate Director for Teaching Development Center, and Program Director for Bachelor’s Degree in Innovation Field, contributing significantly to the school's teaching, innovation, and design fields. Zhu’s main research interests involve exploring interactions between biological tissues and light by combining neuroscience and laser optics technology, conducting interdisciplinary studies on brain nerves. His research has yielded fruitful results, earning him awards such as the Outstanding Young Scholars Award from the Foundation for Distinguished Scholars and the Excellent Young Scholars Program Award from NSFC. Zhu is also highly popular among students for his teaching; he has offered courses like “Fundamentals of Optics” and “General Physics” on Coursera, with over 20,000 people having enrolled in these courses.

session_name: As Grandpa says, it’s still what’s written in books – re-examining modern history through local and民间social perspectives [History Department Professor Louis Loo]
session_content:
Speech Overview
History exists not only in official records but is also vividly transmitted through the memories and daily lives of one’s ancestors. This lecture pairs “Uncle’s Words” with “recorded history,” guiding readers to penetrate the surface layers of literature, re-examining the marginalized perspectives of modern history from an everyday angle. By piecing together fragments of oral histories and memories from local societies, a more comprehensive and warm human history landscape is revealed, showing how history vibrantly pulses in the crevices of daily life.

Speaker Introduction
Professor Lu Shiejie graduated from the Department of History at Brown University in the United States, served as an adjunct professor at the University of Connecticut, and is now a full-time professor at our school’s Department of History. His research interests cover modern Chinese social history, religion and local politics, and historical anthropology.
Professor Luo specializes in combining archival research with fieldwork to examine historical issues from the perspective of local society, focusing on the actions, motivations, and life experiences of ordinary people during major historical transformations. His research aims to reconstruct everyday politics and religious practices within local societies, engaging academically with existing historical images centered around nation-states, elite officials, and modernization discourses, and proposing a more localized interpretation of history.
instructor: 歷史系 羅士傑教授

session_name: Ocean Connect - A Ten-Thousand-Mile Journey from Marine Science to Marine Conservation [Professor Ke Qianyin of the Fisheries Research Institute]
session_content:
Speech Overview
The ocean harbors unique biological diversity, provides precious food resources, and is a significant sink for human activities’ carbon emissions. However, the ocean faces an increasing array of challenges, including habitat loss, pollution, environmental changes, and overfishing. Each challenge requires scientists to scientifically understand and grasp the changes in the ocean; once scientific data are available, how can we collectively brainstorm to turn these into actionable policies and management solutions that allow the ocean to address these challenges? This era demands innovation and collaboration, and let us together think of new possibilities by exploring more from sea turtles, marine debris, overfishing, and the development of marine protected areas both domestically and internationally.

Speaker Introduction
Professor Ke Jaying is a PhD from the Institute of Ecology and Evolutionary Biology at our university, currently serving as a professor in the Fisheries Science Research Institute, an adjunct professor in the Life Sciences Department, and director of the Master’s Program in Biodiversity. Her research expertise spans global change biology, marine ecosystem conservation studies, interdisciplinary science, big data and long-term dataset analysis, and spatial analysis and Geographic Information Systems (GIS), among others. She has received a grant from the Ministry of Science and Technology (previously known as the National Science Council) for young scholars in 2018. Professor Ke collaborates across disciplines with her research team to publish multiple papers in internationally renowned journals, and she is dedicated to exploring more conservation management strategies to contribute to marine education and biodiversity conservation development.
instructor: 漁業科學研究所 柯佳吟教授

activity_name_activity_session: "The Welcome to North Car Large Passenger Lounge Documentary Screening and Post-Movie Discussion"
activity_content:
Welcome to Taipei Main Station Hall

At Taipei’s busiest transportation hub, the Datun Railway Station, the lobby is always crowded on Sundays with a group of friends from different countries. The documentary "Welcome to Big Living Room at Datun Railway Station" filmed four Indonesian female caregivers from different generations working in Taiwan for nearly ten years. During weekdays, they care for elderly or children in their employers' homes; during holidays, they meet up and visit the lobby of Taipei Railway Station with friends. The lobby of Taipei Railway Station is their living room where they shed the identity of caregivers and fully express themselves, leading to many thoughts on cross-border female migrant workers.

"Exploring Learning" believes that true understanding often comes from stepping out of one’s own perspective and entering the lives of others. Through images, we are able to get closer to stories that are usually not seen; through dialogue, we also have an opportunity to re-examine how we understand each other in the same city and see the circumstances of different lives. This session of "Exploring Unrestricted | Explorer Talk" not only screens documentaries but also invites directors and multiple guests, including former explorers who participated in Exploring Learning, for post-screening discussions. Starting from images, social observations, and personal experiences, we explore together how to understand migrant workers, public spaces in the city, and societal understanding.

Looking forward to opening a new perspective of exploration through a movie and a dialogue this night.

activity_content:
Quit Smoking in 2026! Let's Win Together!!

Is anyone nearby smoking?
Actually, nearly 70% of smokers have曾经 considered quitting smoking but often fail due to various reasons.

Why is quitting smoking so difficult? Are new tobacco products really safer?
Through the series of lectures, help you understand the truth about smoking and addiction once and for all, so that quitting smoking is no longer just "thinking about it."

Whether you're someone who wants to quit smoking or someone who cares about loved ones around you, welcome to join us!
To attend the lecture is not just about quitting smoking; it also enhances health knowledge and offers the chance to win great prizes 🎁.

Take the first step towards change and create a smoke-free, healthy campus environment!

activity_name_activity_session: NTUHC: The Nicotine Addiction Scam – Smoking Isn’t as Simple as You Think
activity_content:
Quit Smoking in 2026! Let's Win Together!!

Is anyone nearby smoking?
Actually, nearly 70% of smokers have曾经 considered quitting smoking but often fail due to various reasons.

Why is quitting smoking so difficult? Are new tobacco products really safer?
Through the series of lectures, help you understand the truth about smoking and addiction once and for all, so that quitting smoking is no longer just "thinking about it."

Whether you're someone who wants to quit smoking or someone who cares about loved ones around you, welcome to join us!
To attend the lecture is not just about quitting smoking; it also enhances health knowledge and offers the chance to win great prizes 🎁.

Take the first step towards change and create a smoke-free, healthy campus environment!

Campus Static Exhibition

activity_name_activity_session: NTUHC-115/4/28, 29 Basic Life Support (BLS) Training
activity_content:
Office of Student Affairs Health Center BLS training in April
One, this center was established to enhance the emergency handling capabilities of faculty and staff at three affiliated universities of National Taiwan University for dealing with unexpected accidents. Specialized in-house training on "Basic Life Support" (BLS) has been organized, featuring physicians from the Emergency Medicine Department of Taichung Area Hospital, senior rescue technicians, physical therapists from Taichung Area Hospital, as well as colleagues from this center. The training aims to organize practical exercises to cultivate students' ability to handle crises in real-life situations, thereby ensuring campus and club activity safety.
Two, Date: April 28 and 29th. (April 28 from 17:15-21:50, April 29 from 17:45-21:15).
Three: Target audience: Employees and students of three affiliated universities under National Taiwan University system, with 60 spots filled.
Four、 Location: 2F Wellness Classroom in Health Center.
Five: Content: Trauma emergency, casualty搬运techniques internship (carried out in groups), Hemostasis bandaging techniques internship.
CPR training, CPR technical practice (in groups), and practical exam, written
Test.
To obtain a certificate for Basic Life Support, one must attend the course in full, pass the written test, and complete the technical assessment. In addition, public servants can earn an 8-hour lifetime learning credit.
Seven, Certificate Printing Fee Explanation:
The "certificate" for this course is optional and if you need one, you must bear the cost of NT$ per certificate.
100 yuan (production cost of Taiwan Sports Safety and First Aid Skills Promotion Association), and upon class commencement on that day.
Inform the staff and pay for fees when checking in.

panhx@ntu.edu.tw)

activity_name_activity_session: Lecture: A Look at Academic Tools in the Age of AI: From Literature Review to Analytical Writing
activity_content:
Activity information
Topic: A Brief Look at Academic Tools in the Age of AI: From Literature Reviews to Analytical Writing
Speaker: Liu Zhenghen MD (Assistant Professor of Teaching at Taipei Medical University Hospital)
Date: April 28, 2026 (Tuesday)
Time: 11:20-12:20
Location: Social Science Academy Library 2nd Floor Seminar Room
activity_name_event_page: Lecture: A Look at Academic Tools in the Age of AI: From Literature Review to Analytical Writing
session_name: Reflections on Academic Tools in the Age of AI: From Literature Reviews to Analytical Writing
session_content: Are you often lost in the vast sea of literature? Faced with a multitude of theoretical schools and countless journal papers, do you feel that taking notes is always slower than reading speed? In this era when AI is sweeping through academia, how can we turn being passive into proactive and make AI your strongest assistant for writing papers?\nThis session invites Dr. Chih-Heng Liu from the Teaching Department of Taipei Medical University to share practical applications of digital tools driven by large language models (LLMs) in a cross-disciplinary manner. The lecture will introduce several powerful academic tools and explore how these can be integrated into research processes in the social sciences.
location: 社科院圖書館 2 樓簡報室
organizer_unit: 圖書館社會科學資源服務組

activity_name_activity_session: 114-2 FLCT English Learning Lecture
activity_content:
TELL Talks is a series of English language learning lectures launched by the Foreign Language Teaching and Resource Center at National Taiwan University, aimed at providing students with enriching and practical knowledge while helping them improve their English skills. We invite professionals from various fields as speakers, whose lecture topics combine aspects such as language learning, cultural exchange, and new life insights, in order to offer inspiring and practical sharing. Whether you are just starting your English studies or wish to express yourself more confidently in academic, professional, or international exchanges, TELL Talks will provide you with diverse and practical learning resources!

Limited spots available, please confirm your attendance before registering.
📸 This activity will use photos or videos taken as the main promotional records.
🎁 This activity can accumulate bilingual education center's "English Learning Passport" points. Please scan the QR code on-site to complete the registration.
activity_name_event_page: 114-2 FLCT English Learning Lecture
session_name: AI-Powered English Learning in Higher Education: The Case of Cool English
session_content:
This presentation examines the design rationale, instructional framework, and implementation model of the Cool English College Section. Developed to support university students' autonomous learning as well as teachers’ classroom preparation and instruction, the College Section provides a wide range of resources aligned with university coursework, English proficiency test preparation, workplace communication, and the development of the four core language skills: listening, speaking, reading, and writing. The platform also incorporates AI-powered tools, including speech recognition and AI-based dialogue systems, to create interactive, personalized, and multimodal learning experiences.

activity_name_activity_session: 诚挚邀请老师和助教参加 4.24 (Fri) & 5.22 (Fri) 的 Gradescope 作业批改工具培训
activity_content:
Gradescope is a cloud assessment platform that integrates the latest machine learning (ML) and artificial intelligence (AI) features, aimed at helping teachers simplify grading work by saving them a significant amount of time and effort compared to manually grading. It also ensures objective consistency in scoring, preventing student grades from being affected by personal biases or human calculation errors.

We evaluate that using Gradescope is helpful in reducing the workload of teachers and teaching assistants when grading assignments for large-class lectures at NTU COOL. NTU COOL plans to host two educational training sessions on Gradescope in April and May, inviting all course instructors and teaching assistants who are teaching over 100-student large classes during the Spring 2011 semester to participate.
The activities will be conducted in person and offer live simultaneous streaming online, so you can choose to participate in one session either online or in person.
Please bring your computer with you on-site for effective participation in the activities on that day.

April 24 (Friday) 10:30 - 12:00 @ National Taiwan University Bocai Teaching Hall Room 406 Future Classroom or
May 22 (Fri) 10:30 - 12:00 @ National Taiwan University Bocai Teaching Building Room 406 Future Classroom

The course content for that day includes an introduction by iGroup Taiwan of the various features and methods of using Gradescope.

If teachers are interested in participating in this Gradescope training, please fill out the following form before April 17 to select the date and format (online or in-person) you wish to attend.

If you have any questions, feel free to contact us at any time, thank you!

activity_name_activity_session: "Stress doesn't accumulate, sleep is more restful" - A lecture on modern people's daily养生methods
activity_content:
"Stress doesn't accumulate, sleep is more restful" - A lecture on modern people's daily养生methods

Are you also troubled by such concerns?
Busy during the day and having difficulty sleeping at night?
After work, you can't put down your phone or computer, even though you should be resting, you get more alert as it gets later.
Feeling very tired but unable to sleep well and feeling even more exhausted upon waking?

The "insomnia disturbance" that modern people often face can actually be improved by starting with daily care. This lecture specially invites Teacher Chen Mili from National Nursing and Health University to bring practical and easy-to-apply "acupressure techniques for insomnia," helping you relax naturally in your daily life and improve sleep quality.

Speaker Introduction:
Teacher Chen Miliyi from National Taipei University of Nursing and Health Sciences
Specializes in health and well-being, stress management, and holistic living, with rich teaching and practical experience,擅长用简单易懂的方式带领大家将健康落实在生活中。

Activity information:
Time: April 24, 2026 (Friday) 12:00–13:30
Location: Administration Building First Conference Room, Central Campus
Note: Login for 1 hour of learning time reserved for simple vegetarian dishes.

National Taiwan University Fu Chi Faculty Staff Association Invites You

activity_name_activity_session: Broaden your视野, Challenge the future – Bachelor's Application说明 meeting of the 115 Institute
activity_content:
Broaden your视野, Challenge the future – Bachelor's Application说明 meeting of the 115 Institute

Would you like to break the boundaries of a single discipline during your university career and explore more possibilities?

National Taiwan University’s “Interdisciplinary Bachelor’s” (College Interdisciplinary Bachelor's Programs) offers an excellent opportunity for cross-disciplinary learning.
In the 115th academic year, the College of Liberal Arts, the College of Science, the School of Social Sciences, the College of Medicine, the College of Engineering, the College of Biological Resources and Agriculture, and the College of Life Sciences have established an undergraduate program. "College Bachelor's" will be your starting point to step out of your comfort zone and enhance your future competitiveness.

The orientation will explain each college’s eligibility requirements and program features for bachelor’s degrees. We sincerely invite undergraduate students from our school to attend the orientation, where light refreshments are also available to help you gain a deeper understanding of the relevant information.

Location: Boya Teaching Building Room 102
To: Undergraduate Students of This University

Introduction of College Bachelor's Degree Programs

activity_name_activity_session: Total number of training courses for asset盘点, risk assessment and handling education and training in terms of hours
activity_content: Training and education requirements for information security compliance have been categorized by personnel type into three groups: dedicated information security staff, IT staff other than dedicated information security staff, general users and supervisors. General users and supervisors refer to individuals listed in the organizational staffing table or those who can access or use the agency's IT systems or services. Therefore, apart from dedicated information security staff, each of the remaining categories should receive at least 3 hours of information security awareness training annually.\nThe training session is scheduled for April 23, 20115 (Thursday) from 09:00 to 12:00. It will be conducted both in a physical classroom and online (choose one). Please have the security contacts and network administrators from Level I and II units participate. Also, anyone interested in the course is welcome to register for the online session.

activity_name_activity_session: 2026 Career Training Workshop: The Complete Guide to Job Hunting: From Legendary Resumes to Mock Interviews, All in One!
activity_content:
On your job hunting journey, what you lack is not your capability, but how you present your capabilities!

Two Career Training Workshops in 2026 will dispel the troubles of being jobless and illuminate a bright path to employment.

First Station: "Resume Writing and Interview Techniques": Unlock the Perspective of Interviewers, Teach You to Identify Key Words in Various Industries, and More Importantly, Teach You How to Use AI for Superstar Refinement to Stand Out Among Thousands!

Second Station: Mock Interview Practice - Practice Makes Perfect! We group you based on your target job (marketing, research and development, engineering, etc.) with expert consultants who provide real-time feedback on-site to help you overcome interview anxiety. Keep your nerves in the practice room and bring your confidence to the interview hall.

Limited spots! Hurry up! Let’s accompany you from “job interview anxiety” to “confident acceptance!”
activity_name_event_page: 2026 Career Training Workshop: The Complete Guide to Job Hunting: From Legendary Resumes to Mock Interviews, All in One!
session_name: "2026 Career Training Workshop: AI Assistance x HR Perspective, Build Your Perfect Resume!"
session_content:
"Essential Job Hunting Course": AI Assistance x HR Perspective: Crafting the Ultimate Career Entry Ticket! 🚀
"Sending out dozens of resumes, all went nowhere?"
"Wanting to write a resume in English but only know how to write 'I am a hardworking student...'?"
"Why do interview notifications never come despite being very优秀?"

Stop blindly submitting applications! Job hunting also requires "add-ons" and a strategy.
This lecture teaches you not just how to write in the correct format, but also how to write with the heart of HR in mind, and even shows you how to turn ChatGPT into your personal career mentor!

This lecture will leave you with these four求职 secrets:
Basic Skills for an English-Cantonese Resume (Avoiding Format Disasters!)

AI Styling Secret: ChatGPT Spells Success 🤖

HR Brain Theater: Keyword Packaging Techniques and Highlights Packaging

The Final Push Psychological Warfare Tactics Before Interviews 🧠

What should you prepare 24 hours before an interview?

How to do corporate research so that interviewers feel: "Oh my god, you really understand us!"

Event Information (Quick Notes!)
Date: April 21, 2015 (Tue) 6:30 PM – 8:30 PM

Location: Boya Teaching Building Room 202

To: NTU, NTUST, NTNUST students and alumni

session_name: Career Training Workshop 2026: Decoding Interview Simulation from "Passing the Written Exam" to "Getting Hired"
session_content:
Always blanking out during interviews? Here’s a saving star for mock interviews!

"Please introduce yourself..."
Thinking this sentence makes your hands start sweating and your tongue feel tied? 😰
I have prepared for a long time, but don't know how to showcase my advantages?
Don't let your efforts stop at the interview门槛!

This is not a lecture, but rather a “live exercise!” We bring the interview scenario into the campus and let you directly face-off with professional consultants in front of them to receive firsthand precise feedback.

Highlights of the Activity
Precise Grouping: Conduct group exercises based on your target job (marketing, research and development, engineering, finance, etc.).

Professional instructors: Led by industry-savvy consultants, on-site analysis of must-pass questions.

Real-time Feedback: Better to be blind! Point out your own weaknesses for an instant upgrade.

Event Information
Time: April 27, 2015 (Mon) 6:30 PM – 9:00 PM

Location: Administrative Building First Conference Room

Target: National Taiwan University Students and Alumni (limited to 80 participants, miss this session and you'll have to wait a year!)

Registration Note
Fill-in-the-blank note: Please write down the position you are applying for (e.g., IC Design Engineer, Financial Staff) so we can group you precisely!

Punctuality is a virtue: For simulated interview scenarios, being late will not allow you entry.

session_name: "Finding Your Career North Star" - CPAS Group Explanation (Free CPAS Test Available)
session_content:
"Finding Your Career North Star" - CPAS Group Explanation (Free CPAS Test Available)

Speaker: Lin Yi-Jie / Career Consultant
Activity date: April 21, 2026; Time: 12:20-14:10
Venue: CAS Building Room 102

(Free CPA Exam Available)(This is a link to the CPAS operation manual: https://reurl.cc/qpNp7N)
Content of the round:
Career navigation is not just about looking at data but also requires "temperature" and "strategy." This session was led by Lin Yi-chieh, a career consultant with a psychological background, who used the CPAS assessment tool to help young people extract workplace competitiveness from their personality traits, positioning themselves for their own North Star in the vast sea of careers.

After you register, we will send a notification about the test to the email address you provided on April 10th. (Please complete the test according to the user manual.)
Please note that registration students need to complete this CPAS test by April 12th.
The results of the CPAS test will be brought with the paper on the day of class.
Thank you for your assistance!


Speaker: Lin Yi-chieh
Speaker Bio:
Current Position: Career Counselor at Taipei Youth Career Development Center (TYS)
Core Qualifications
Professional background: Skilled in applying psychological counseling techniques to accurately identify job seekers' needs and soothe anxiety.
Matchmaker Achievements: Formerly employed at Taipei City Employment Service Office, established a record of successfully matching 137 job seekers in a single year and was hailed as a “job matcher expert.”

Current Position
Taipei Youth Career Development Center (TYS) Career Counselor
Core Qualifications
Professional background: Skilled in applying psychological counseling techniques to accurately identify job seekers' needs and soothe anxiety.
Matchmaker Achievements: Formerly employed at Taipei City Employment Service Office, established a record of successfully matching 137 job seekers in a single year and was hailed as a “job matcher expert.”
Professional certification:持有一級就業服務證照,精通勞工法規、就業服務程序以及職場所需技能。
Cross-cultural service experience: With years of frontline practical experience, the services cover young people, middle-aged and elderly individuals, persons with disabilities, and re-employed women. Skilled at assisting cases in career downturns to return to work.
Specialized Modules
Career counseling and positioning: Combining psychology with market trends to assist job seekers in clarifying their self-advantages.
Job hunting psychological building and emotion management: Systematic support and counseling for unemployment anxiety and interview setbacks.

Style: Balanced between rationality and emotionality, with a warm yet resilient tone.
Belief: "When you have the ability to be someone else's mentor, all it takes is a hand to pull them up and their life will be different."

session_content:
Registration for two sessions of "CPAS Personal Counseling Explanation" (priority admission for colleagues from the Social Science Academy in this session) has started!

4/24 (Fri) 13:00–16:30
April 28 (Tuesday) 13:00–16:30, total of 2 sessions
Please arrive 5-8 minutes early, thank you!

Location:
Social Science Academy building, discussion rooms 736 and 737 on the west side, Room 611 (please check in at room 736 on the 7th floor first)

Activity content:
Bring your resume, interview materials, CPA report, or any information you want to discuss with Taipei City Youth Bureau and Taipei City Youth Career Development Center’s professional mentors for one-on-one guidance.

Common mentoring topics:

Resume Optimization: No response when submitting resume? How to turn internship experience into a highlight?

Registration method:
Maximum of 9 people per session, please register first through the Google form: https://reurl.cc/O6XkGR (Reservation slot)

Successful registration will be notified by the Taipei City Youth Bureau via email. Please keep an eye on the email address you provided (registration for this event is open until April 9, notification of registration results and a list of substitutes will be unified on April 13, and the substitute list will be notified on April 17).

Cancellation: Please notify us at least 3 days in advance if you are unable to attend a reserved appointment for any reason, so we can arrange for another student who needs it.

session_name: "Being in No Place Without Self-Enjoyment: The Cross-Disciplinary Influence of Social Workers"
session_content:
Topic: Finding Fulfillment Without Specialization: Social Workers' Cross-Disciplinary Influence

Speaker: Ling Bing General Secretary Richemont Education Foundation Board of Directors
Host: Professor Chen Yumin, Department of Social Work, Taiwan University
Time: April 29, 2026 (Wed) 12:30-14:00
Location: Social Science Institute, Fifth Floor 502 Classroom, National Taiwan University Main Campus

When cross-domain collaboration becomes routine, responses to social issues no longer rely on a single professional perspective but rather the intersection and cooperation of multiple viewpoints.
How do social workers identify their position in different settings and leverage their professional advantages in understanding individuals and structures to promote cross-disciplinary dialogue and integration of actions?

session_content:
"Words from Old Senior"

Speaker: Li Dawei, Honorary Chairman
Speaker Experience:
Honorary Chairman of Major Digital Philanthropy Foundations
Ministry of Foreign Affairs Secretary-General, President's Office Secretary-General, National Security Council Secretary-General, Consul General in the United States and Canada, Deputy Minister for Public Diplomacy
Chairman of the Taiwan Friendship Foundation (Taiwan Exchange Fund) Ltd., a non-profit organization

Host: Dr. Kang-Hui Liu from National Taiwan University Department of Political Science

Activity content:
Presenter Li Dawei has extensive background in diplomacy, governance, and public service. He曾 held positions such as Director of the News Bureau, Ambassador-at-Large, Minister of Foreign Affairs, Secretary-General of the National Security Council, Secretary-General of the Presidential Office, Chairman of Strait Exchange Foundation, and Honorary Chairman of Digital Philanthropy Fund. Through this event "Exchanging Experiences & Reflecting on Life," in a relaxed dialogue style, he shared his life transitions, career choices, and values from the perspective of seniors, offering some inspiration to young participants.

Welcome students, coming to attend ~ (this session accepts on-site registration, with seats available).

activity_name_activity_session: ⭐️【The 8th Social Science Academy - Student Career and Life Lecture Series】~ Welcome students from all departments to register and participate ~⭐️
activity_content:
"Double Life Week" series of academic and social science students career guidance activities and Gu Zhenfu Library's Career Exploration Book Exhibition
This will be officially unveiled on April 20, 2026! This will be a great opportunity,
Encourage students to explore future careers and personal development, welcome all students from different colleges and departments to participate and register~

Series Lecture: CAS Student Career and Life [Dual Career Week] 2026,活动时间是 April 20 ~ May 7.
Multiple activities will be held this year, welcome students to踊跃报名!

Book Exhibition: Future Generationing: 100 Possible Modes of Progress (Future Unfolding: 100 Possibilities in Progress)
Social Science Press –辜振甫 Library specially curated Future Generation in progress: Selection of 100 possible futures, featuring books on entrepreneurial and AI work skills from both Chinese and Western perspectives. This book fair will run from April 20 to June 15.
Welcome students to visit and embrace the future, transforming challenges into limitless possibilities!

Books and successful entrepreneur profiles on the "Entrepreneurship" theme, along with AI work skills, are available. For more details, see the Social Science & Arts activity webpage. Welcome interested students to participate!

session_name: "From Securities Analyst to Head of Family Office"
session_content:
"From Securities Analyst to Head of Family Office"

Speaker: Huang Xuhui, General Manager of Blue Dragon Asset Management Ltd.
Host: Professor Su Yihao from National Taiwan University's Department of Political Science

Experience:
Served as the Chief Operating Officer and Investment Head of multiple joint family offices in Hong Kong, served as the Chief Strategist for Haitong International (Hong Kong), and held a Deputy Manager position at Group Yi Securities (Hong Kong) Research Department. Has experience in assisting wealthy individuals in establishing family offices, managing hedge fund investments, multi-asset portfolio management, and overall economic and industry analysis.

Expertise: Macro Economics, Geopolitics, Asset Allocation, Multi-Asset Portfolio Management, Family Wealth Governance, Client实战应对.

activity_name_activity_session: Public Lecture: Discussing Students' Mental Health from the Perspective of Neurodiversity
activity_content: From a "neurodiversity" perspective, this lecture will introduce the importance of this concept in contemporary mental health and education fields, and explore how to incorporate a neurodiversity lens into campus mental health promotion strategies. The speech will also review the latest clinical research and practical experience, summarize common mental health challenges faced by neurodiverse individuals (especially those with autism spectrum), analyze the formation of these challenges from multiple levels including development, biological, psychological, and social perspectives, and further discuss possible support and intervention approaches.

activity_name_activity_session: Learning Planning Office: Discovering the Light: Skills and Art of Learning Planning
activity_content:
In recent years, various colleges and universities have actively established a "learning planning system" centered on students as an important mechanism to support cross-disciplinary learning and autonomous learning. To further deepen the practical understanding and capacity building for guiding the implementation of the learning planning system, the Ministry of Education is organizing a hands-on workshop called “Uncovering That Light: The Skills and Arts of Learning Planning” in collaboration with National Taiwan University and National Chengchi University. This workshop invites educators and administrative staff involved in student guidance and learning planning to participate and exchange ideas.

This training will share practical experience from NTU in promoting learning planning systems, including core competencies and guiding strategies for learning plans, as well as the application of "Lighting the Guiding Cards." It will also use practical exercises to help participants understand the concepts and methods of learning planning, enhance their professional guidance abilities, and support students in exploring directions, developing potential, and planning their learning paths more effectively.

Activity Theme
Uncovering That Light: The Skills and Art of Learning Planning

Content of Activities
Core skills for learning plans, guiding techniques, and practical exercises

Speaker
National Taiwan University Office of Learning Planning Affairs

Number of events
Location - National Taiwan University Tsung Chih-chiang Hall Leather Room (ME109)
Time: April 16, Thursday (10:00–12:00) (Registration starts at 9:30)

Recommended Participants
Teachers and administrative colleagues engaged in student guidance work, focusing on students' individual development and learning support mechanisms.

Registration Information
Registration period: Now until March 8 (Wednesday) at noon
Registration link: https://forms.gle/5QRMMBjYvrTog5Hv9
Admission Notice | Notification of admitted list via email is sent by April 10th (Friday), no notification for those not admitted.

We sincerely invite all faculty members and administrative colleagues to participate in the exchange, jointly exploring professional practices for learning planning, helping students find their own direction and light within the diverse paths of learning.

activity_name_activity_session: Craft & Chat Creative Handmade Chat
activity_content:
Welcome to NTU's Craft & Chat English Creative Hand-Work Café!
Would you like an activity to practice English while doing something hands-on and easily? This is for you!
We have launched the Craft & Chat series with a space that is both relaxed and creative, where you can learn through play and conversation. Don't worry about having perfect English; just come with a curious mindset!
There is a different crafting theme every week, allowing you to learn new things, meet new friends, and have the opportunity to encounter like-minded partners ✨

session_content: You might think coloring books and sketchbooks are just toys for kids, but do you know they were very popular among adults in the US as well? Join Fulbright EMI-ETF to explore the "adult coloring craze" of mid-2010s and experience firsthand whether this hobby is still fun!

activity_name_activity_session: AI Assistance: Advancing Essay Writing, Academic Communication, and Critical Thinking
activity_content:
AI is rapidly changing the way academic learning and research are conducted. This center has specially planned a series of three hands-on workshops, where participants will guide学员 on how to use AI tools to enhance their ability to write academic summaries, improve reading efficiency for journals, and assist with data analysis and visualization.

Academic abstract: Structure in Chinese and English, and practical applications of AI

Research equipment upgrades: Utility of journal reading assistance tools

From Data to Discovery: A Hands-On Introduction to Julius AI
activity_name_event_page: AI Assistance: Advancing Essay Writing, Academic Communication, and Critical Thinking
session_name: Abstract: This is written in both Chinese and English structures with practical applications of AI.
session_content: The quality of the abstract will directly impact the effectiveness of communication for a study. This workshop will introduce the structure and writing focus of Chinese and English abstracts, and explain how to use AI assistance tools ethically to revise them while balancing efficiency and quality.
instructor: 陳巧玲老師,蘇虹菱老師

session_name: Data to Discovery: A Hands-On Introduction to Julius AI
session_content: In this hands-on workshop, graduate students and researchers will learn Julius AI, an AI-powered platform for exploring datasets using simple English prompts and no coding. We’ll practice cleaning data, generating quick summaries and visualizations, and writing prompts that produce clear, insight-driven charts. The session concludes with a fun team challenge to investigate a research-style dataset and share mini “discoveries” through data storytelling.

activity_name_activity_session: College of Music No.282: Original and Cross-Disciplinary Sounds
activity_content:
Target: 282. College Music Festival "Original and Cross-disciplinary Sounds"
Performance time: April 10, 2026 (Fri), 12:30-13:20
Venue: National Taiwan University College of Literature Main Hall
Organizers
National Taiwan University College of Literature
Organizing Unit: National Taiwan University Musicology Institute
Introduction of Performance Group
Sound动乐团由谢韵雅（Mia）与Scott Prairie创立，在纽约相遇，横跨太平洋来到台湾扎根创作，并将音乐唱响到世界舞台。开放多元的文化包容态度塑造了Sound动的跨界精神和原创风格，并将台湾在地元素融入创作中。乐曲结合传统民族乐器，搭配人声，创新活泼编曲，其真诚、自由的特点突破形式与范畴的限制，超越时空与语言的界限，国际乐评赞誉为“世界音乐舞台上最具原创性的团体之一”。

Sound of Taiwan is one of the few in Taiwan that has been consistently耕耘on the international stage for over 20 years, and has achieved impressive results! Invited to perform at over a hundred concerts in more than twenty countries around the world, including the Melbourne Arts Festival in Australia, the largest Cervantino Arts Festival in Latin America, and the Kennedy Center in the United States. International media giants such as Discovery, BBC, and NPR have all conducted interviews with him. He has also collaborated with various fields of visual arts, films, and fashion. The album was nominated three times for the Golden Melody Awards and won the "One World" world music album award from Europe.

Fusing traditional influences with a global sensibility, award-winning ensemble A Moving Sound from Taiwan built a worldwide following. Featuring the distinctive timbres of traditional instruments and the transcendent vocals of Mia Hsieh, A Moving Sound has devised a unique and compelling style all their own – a Taiwanese whirlwind that veers between the meditative and the exuberant with irrepressible spirit.

A Moving Sound has been featured on BBC, National Public Radio in the US, and on The Discovery Channel.

Sound of Taiwan is one of the few in Taiwan that has been consistently耕耘on the international stage for over 20 years, and has achieved impressive results! Invited to perform at over a hundred concerts in more than twenty countries around the world, including the Melbourne Arts Festival in Australia, the largest Cervantino Arts Festival in Latin America, and the Kennedy Center in the United States. International media giants such as Discovery, BBC, and NPR have all conducted interviews with him. He has also collaborated with various fields of visual arts, films, and fashion. The album was nominated three times for the Golden Melody Awards and won the "One World" world music album award from Europe.

Fusing traditional influences with a global sensibility, award-winning ensemble A Moving Sound from Taiwan built a worldwide following. Featuring the distinctive timbres of traditional instruments and the transcendent vocals of Mia Hsieh, A Moving Sound has devised a unique and compelling style all their own – a Taiwanese whirlwind that veers between the meditative and the exuberant with irrepressible spirit.

A Moving Sound has been featured on BBC, National Public Radio in the U.S., and on The Discovery Channel. They have performed in twenty countries including The Kennedy Center, the Melbourne International Arts Festival, etc. A Moving Sound's music is distributed internationally through the biggest world music record label ARC Music.



Content introduction

Taiwan is a blend of diverse ethnic cultures, residing at the core of Asia where tradition meets modernity. This nourishment has inspired Crosswave’s cross-genre compositions. The music for this program draws inspiration from the cultural hues of Pan-Asia, extending from Taiwan to India, Tibet, Central Asia, and the Middle East. It incorporates traditional Taiwanese instruments (erhu, yangqin) with modern bass guitar arrangements, Mia's emotive vocals, and her original language creation. The tracks include "Dingfengbo," inspired by guzheng and Nanju music styles; "Xinpo," featuring Tibetan chanting paired with human voices; and "Xiandian," blending Indian music and festive energy.



Performer

Shen Yunya Mia | Lead Singer, Hand Drum

Scott Prairie | Sanxian, Bass

Chen Zhir翎 | Chin plucked string (Chinese plucked lute), Liuqin

Gao Yujingin | Erhu

activity_name_activity_session: Career Center x Central News Agency ✍️ Lecture: Attending a Correspondent Training in India and Turkey
activity_content:
Career Center [Taiwan Guide Program] X Central News Agency [I Am a Foreign Correspondent]
India Turkey Live with my Correspondent Training

Dreaming of becoming a news reporter? Want to be a correspondent for Central News Agency?
A golden opportunity! No need to endure decades of struggle, you can be stationed overseas too! The CTS [I am a Foreign Correspondent] plan offers you the chance to experience different customs and cultures, explore multiple career possibilities, and document the world through your perspective while sharing stories with the global community—your next correspondent might just be you!
The Career Center invited Hsiao Hong-Ju, deputy director and chief editor of Central News Agency's International and Taiwan Strait News Division, to share his experience on the front lines of journalism and his career as an overseas correspondent.
The event will also explain the "Overseas Correspondent Internship" program. We welcome participation from both students interested in journalism careers and those participating in the overseas correspondent internship program.

Activity time: April 9 (Thu) 14:30-16:30
Location: Administrative Building First Conference Room

To apply for the Internship Program of 'I Am an Overseas Correspondent', please apply at the Career Center’s website [Cross-Disciplinary Career Training - Taiwan Guide Project], and you need to participate in the Central News Agency's [News Training Camp]. For more details, see: https://ntucace.ntu.edu.tw/institution/profile.detail/sn/21
★ Opportunity to intern at Central News Agency during summer program and get exposure to various news topics, plus opportunity to be selected for one-month internship overseas!

activity_name_activity_session: Good Books Online Reading Lecture Series
activity_content:
"Life is a book; in which lies the infinite of life."
The 2025 OpenBook Good Book Award Exhibition will once again showcase "Annual Chinese Literature," "Annual Translation Books," and "Annual Lifestyle Books," and invites everyone to participate in online book readings.

Good Book Recommendation. First Online Lecture ~
"Introduction to 'Multipolar Republic: Hope and Risks of Artificial Reproduction in Taiwan'"
Speaker: Professor Wu Jiaglin
Time: April 1, 2026 (Wed) 2:20 PM - 3:20 PM
Why is the multiple birth rate in Taiwan the highest worldwide? What are the risks and hopes brought by artificial reproductive technology? Professor Wu Jiaglin shares her扎实 sociological research and years of deep interviews with simple language and a logical structure, sharing her journey and findings with you. Welcome to participate enthusiastically.

Speaker Bio:
Professor from the Department of Sociology at Taiwan University, whose research areas include medical sociology, gender studies, and science and technology studies. She has served as an editor-in-chief for journals such as "East Asian Science, Technology and Society," "The Feminist Journal," and "Taiwan Sociology." She also participated in founding the “Alliance for Reproductive and Midwifery Reform.” Recently, Wu Jialin is involved in a transnational project on reconstructing professional standards of reproductive and midwifery in East Asia. Her efforts are focused on establishing more supportive social systems for pregnant and birthing families.

Sure, please provide the text you would like translated.

Book Recommendation. Online Lecture Second Session
"Looking Up: A Bird’s Universe Connecting Culture and Nature, Time and Space Through Flying from Taiwan to the World" Introduction
Speaker: Dr. Dilin Lin Assistant Professor
Time: 2026-04-29 14:20-15:20
Have you ever wondered what the world of bird watching is like? Teacher Lin Daili combines his rich knowledge of birds with personal experiences from work, travel, and studying to bring us into this captivating international world of birds!

Speaker Bio:
PhD from the School of Biological Sciences, University of Queensland, Australia. Deputy Researcher at the Biodiversity Institute and Centre of Excellence for Environmental Genomics, Department of Agriculture. Translator and reviewer of科普books. Mainly researches habitat of birds, forests, and wild animals. Always carries a book with him, stares at maps for long periods to count how many bird species he has seen; also an overly meticulous reader who believes that being meticulous is a virtue in scientific writing. Has written works such as "Oh! That’s Interesting: An Engaging Guide to Ornithology" and "Are Viruses Willful? Understanding the Big Picture of Infectious Diseases."

This activity will be held in a Google Meet online lecture format, and the online lecture links will be sent out陆续a few days before the event.

activity_name_activity_session: Writing Together, Stronger Than Ever!
activity_content:
Writing Together, Stronger Than Ever!

100% satisfaction, extra好评~

The writer you are, do you suffer from writer's block? Are you often feeling stuck, with the paper always seeming to be unfinished? Actually, you're not alone. Writing doesn't have to be a solo battle; let's Write Together and work together to overcome this obstacle and win this tough fight!

Top-tier foreign universities have recently been catching the "Writing Together, Mutual Assistance" trend. The Writing Teaching Center at National Taiwan University hopes to bring this warm wind into campus and create a positive writing experience tailored for graduate students. activity_name_activity_session: Psychology Lecture: "Spring • Interpersonal Soft Power" 114-2 Counseling Center Mental Health Promotion Lectures
activity_content:
Taiwan University Student Affairs Office Counseling Center for Students主办"The Season of Psychology: Spring & Interpersonal Soft Power" lecture

As spring takes hold, everything in the warmth of sunlight and the softness of rain quietly unfolds. Just as seasons change, there are opportunities for re-understanding and connecting relationships. This semester’s psychology lecture series focuses on “Interpersonal Relationships” and “Family Interaction,” inviting professional psychologists to lead us through their rich practical experience and psychological perspectives, helping us to notice blind spots in social interactions, learn to establish healthy and comfortable interpersonal boundaries, and explore ways of interacting with parents from the concept of upward parenting. From understanding others to caring for oneself, the lectures will accompany us as we cultivate a gentle yet stable force of relationships during the spring’s budding rhythm, injecting more possibilities for connection into our lives.

Session 1: Social Blind Spots? Understanding the Need for Interpersonal Relationships
Time: March 24, 2026, Tuesday, 18:30-20:30 (there is no intermission for this session)
Location: Yuying Teaching Building Room 202
Introduction: Escape the one-sided anxiety brought by digital habits, practice reading between the lines without crossing boundaries, and accurately understand key points while effectively communicating. Upgrade your social soft power through public speaking to become a confident influencer!
Speaker: Qiao Rujun Consulting Psychologist
Graduate of the Counseling Psychology Master's program at Texas Tarleton University, currently a counseling psychologist at Heart Return Counseling. Formerly a lecturer and counselor at New Taipei City Lifeline Employee Assistance Program; adjunct counselor at National Taipei Education University, Ming Chuan University; visiting lecturer and part-time counselor at Tamkang University; supervisor and part-time counselor at National Taipei Business University, and兼任lecturer teaching "Interpersonal Relationships and Communication Skills."

Session 2: Parenting Upward and Family Interactions: How to Talk Well with Parents After Growing Up?
Time: Friday, May 8, 2026, 6:30 PM - 8:30 PM (there is no intermission for this session)
Location: Yuying Teaching Building Room 202
Introduction: From a psychological and neuroscience perspective, understanding parents' emotions and behaviors while learning to respect and care for one's own feelings, and re-establishing the possibility of good communication and相处 with parents.
Speaker: Hé Xiaoting, Clinical Psychologist
Doctor of the Institute of Health Care Science at Chengda Medical College, currently serving as a clinical psychologist. He provides services in multiple units such as Heart’s Forest Psychological Treatment Center, Chengda Hospital Occupational Injury Rehabilitation Program, and the Tainan City Mental Health Center. He has authored "Upward Parenting: Communicate Without Hurting Parents’ Feelings with Psychology and Neuroscience to Live Happy for Life" and written numerous articles on mental health.

Series Lecture注意事项:
Due to the inconvenience of personnel entering and exiting seats for scheduling reasons, no中场 break time was arranged, please forgive us.
To make the lecture content more relevant to the audience’s needs, invite the audience to submit their questions or expectations via an anonymous Google form before the scheduled session. We will compile the feedback from the form and provide this information to the speaker for reference. Form link: https://forms.gle/giKRhR4UkfkwP4qp7
The Q&A is scheduled to take place at 8:15 PM and the event will end precisely at 8:30 PM.
activity_name_event_page: Psychology Lecture: "Spring • Interpersonal Soft Power" 114-2 Counseling Center Mental Health Promotion Lectures
session_name: Social blind spots? Understanding the need for interpersonal relationships
session_content: Social skills are not innate but a learned ability. To escape the one-sided anxiety brought by digital habits, practice reading people’s cues without crossing boundaries, accurately grasp the key points and express yourself well. Master two-way communication and social boundaries to cultivate long-term relationships with comfortable distances. From understanding atmosphere to precise responses, upgrade your social soft skills to become an effortlessly confident influencer!
instructor: 喬如筠 諮商心理師
organizer_unit: 學生事務處學生心理輔導中心

activity_name_activity_session: CBE x Fulbright Academic and Career English Club (Academic and Career English Table) Workshop
activity_content:
CBE x Fulbright Academic and Career English Club (Academic and Career English Table) Workshop

Avoiding EMI: Opening up without stalling, strengthening academic communication and expression skills
Enhance my ability to deliver presentations and speeches, and seize each speaking opportunity to clearly express my ideas.
Here's the translation:

✅ Facing key stages of studying and job hunting, wanting to prepare more strategically and add points?
Want to warm up and practice workplace communication and career expression ahead of time to make yourself more competitive?

Welcome to the "🎖️ACE Club" jointly organized by National Taiwan University Bilingual Education Center and the Fulbright Academic Exchange Program!

Academic discussion in EMI classes → Report and speech techniques → Resume and interview实战 skills → Career communication and personal branding
Double down on AI to help you practice and navigate the boundaries of ethics, allowing you to become more confident in both academic and career fields. 🌍🎤

Please provide the text you would like translated.

Date: (each session can be independently registered for ★)

Which would you prefer: AI or be used by AI in Session One (March 17)? Academic expression is up to you.
Session Two (March 24) EMI Course Survival Guide: From English Reading to Academic Expression
Session 3 (3/31) Presentation No More Legs Shaking Part 1 | Standing Up Without Social Death: Building Your Academic Expression Confidence
Session 4 (4/7) Presentation No Longer Nervous – Part II: Start Speaking Without Embarrassment: Academics Can Also Be Told Like Everyday Conversation
Game Five (April 21) is not about selling your history; it’s about pitching your story into something the employer wants to hear.
Session 6 (April 28) interview is not a chat; it’s key dialogue that brings you the offer.
Match 7 (May 5): Exploring Self: Building Confidence and Planning Your Career Map
Match 8 (May 12): Your Stage Is in Your Hands: Your Brand and Your Story 2.0

Time: Tuesday 18:30-20:00

Teacher: Fulbright Academic Exchange Program Lecture Team

Course information:
[Latest Information] https://cbe.ntu.edu.tw/events/281
Course Content: https://cbe.ntu.edu.tw/files/71a1000605b8e53c

Registration Offer:
This series is free courses.
Course provides delicious meal boxes
Register and attend to receive CBE registration礼 (one per person)🎁
Register for sessions 1-4 or 5-8 and attend all sessions to receive a精美全勤禮🎁 and full attendance certificate🎖️.
Trained participants with excellent ratings from instructors can receive a training certificate afterward.

Location: To be announced later

Organizers: National Taiwan University Bilingual Education Center and the Fulbright Scholar Exchange Program Fund

Avoiding EMI: Opening up without stalling, strengthening academic communication and expression skills
Enhance my ability to deliver presentations and speeches, and seize each speaking opportunity to clearly express my ideas.
Here's the translation:

✅ Facing key stages of studying and job hunting, wanting to prepare more strategically and add points?
Want to warm up and practice workplace communication and career expression ahead of time to make yourself more competitive?

Welcome to the "🎖️ACE Club" jointly organized by National Taiwan University Bilingual Education Center and the Fulbright Academic Exchange Program!

Academic discussion in EMI classes → Report and speech techniques → Resume and interview实战 skills → Career communication and personal branding
Double down on AI to help you practice and navigate the boundaries of ethics, allowing you to become more confident in both academic and career fields. 🌍🎤

activity_name_activity_session: Real-Life Scenarios in English Speaking: Workshop and Practicum
activity_content:
Real-Life Scenarios in English Speaking: Workshop and Practicum Tuesdays 1:20-3:10
Wanting to speak English but always feeling like you need to translate in your head first?
Want to clearly express your experiences, opinions, and views in daily or job-seeking situations?

The Institute of Social Sciences Library invites Professor Zhuang Shiqian from the Foreign Languages Department.
Take you into情境式口說訓練, a relaxed yet super practical scenario-based speaking training.
Rich in themes and content, allowing you not to be afraid to speak,
Take a more confident attitude and use a wider range of sentence structures to handle various communication scenarios!

Limited spots available! Register now while slots last!
Come register and enrich your English conversation skills!

Class instructor:
Shih-Chuan Chuang老师
National Taiwan University Bachelor of Foreign Languages, Master of Interpretation
Tamk University Foreign Languages Department Adjunct Lecturer
Tzu Chi Center for Foreign Language Education and the Bilingual Education Center of National Taiwan University provide training faculty

Date Theme

New Semester, New Ambitions?
Do you always feel super ambitious at the beginning of the semester but find yourself overwhelmed by the workload at the end? Let's talk about ways to devise realistic plans and make the efforts count!

Are Brick Phones Making a Comeback?
Do you feel anxious when seeing incoming emails and messages? Do you feel the need to detox and escape from the digital world? Let's discuss why so many people have decided to quit using smartphones.

How to Ace a Job Talk or Interview on April 21
We will start with discussing how to make a positive impression in self-introductions, followed by questions that are commonly asked during a job talk or interview.

Resume and Cover Letters: Tips for Success
In this workshop, we will examine some effective and ineffective examples of resumes and cover letters. What do HR managers value when processing such documents? Let's dive in and try to get ahead!

Class Information
Location: SOA 2F Presentation Room of Dr. Tseng Jun-pu Memorial Library, National Taiwan University Social Science Institute
Time: March 17, March 24, April 21, April 28; 1:20 PM – 3:10 PM.

Online registration link: https://my.ntu.edu.tw/actregister/sessionList.aspx?actID=20261911_01
Organizers: Gu Zhenfu Professor Memorial Library of Sociology and Social Science College, NTU
Organizing Unit: National Taiwan University Bilingual Education Center
Contact for Activities: (02) 3366-8300转55613, ntukoolib@ntu.edu.tw

activity_name_activity_session: 2026 Taiwan Guide Plan - Enterprise Seminar
activity_content:
The 2026 "Interdisciplinary Career Training - Taiwan Pathfinder Program" is now open for applications!
The Student Career Center in the Office of Academic Affairs invites partnerships with various enterprises to offer internship positions across disciplines, with enterprise mentors leading the way so that students can develop key workplace skills and explore their future career paths.
Starting in March, all cooperating enterprises will come to explain the training plan to the students. Whether you have already decided on your career path or are still迷茫, welcome to listen to what the enterprises have to say and take that first step into cross-disciplinary career exploration bravely!

Event schedules will be announced in batches.

session_name: Introduction Workshop on Design Thinking
session_content: This workshop allows participants to experience design thinking and provides methods for narrative design and practice, turning narratives into concrete design practices for further development of individual topic ideas. Led by trainers from Alpha Team’s Design Thinking talent training program, the two-hour immersive experience guides students through the five steps of design thinking: Empathize, Define, Ideate, Prototype, and Test. Participants will explore the potential of design thinking in practical operations to lay a foundation for subsequent project development and iteration in this course.\n\nSee detailed information on this department's webpage for latest news and academic activities.

session_name: Content creators of this era
session_content: AI can almost costlessly generate text and produce images; in an algorithmic age, free content has no readers either. How do content creators struggle to survive? From media to content creators themselves, professional specialization has transformed into learning multi-media storytelling methods. They master the narrative forms of magazines, websites, podcasts, long videos, short videos, as well as physical workshops. By understanding and developing these formats’ narratives and production processes differently, they create content structures that suit various types of readers/listeners/users/consumers.\n\nSee detailed information on this department's webpage for latest news and academic activities.

activity_name_event_page: English translation: Forum of Foreign Language Literature Students
session_name: Co-creating with AI: An English Major's Journey into AI Engineering
session_content:
What kind of transformation would a graduate with an English/Language background undergo when entering the world of artificial intelligence? This talk will share a story of cross-domain transition – from text and language to programming and data; from grammatical analysis to commercial analytics integrated with AI systems. Along the way, we will also delve into the concept of "AI agents": these digital collaborators can automate workflows, generate insights, and gradually reshape how businesses operate.

However, technology alone is not sufficient to constitute a complete solution. Humanistic素养 – such as communication skills, empathy, and critical thinking – are key elements that make it possible for artificial intelligence to be truly以人为本. This lecture will also focus on the core competencies that language-related science students can further develop, including data literacy (data literacy), literate programming, and cross-disciplinary teamwork capabilities, so they can leverage their strengths in this new era and continue to grow.

The most important message is: Language background is not a limitation but rather an important starting point that allows us to co-create the future with artificial intelligence.

See detailed information on this department's webpage for latest news and academic activities.

session_name: What are the sign language interpreters doing in the hospital?
session_content:
When crossing a river, you can choose to walk across an already built bridge or jump from stone to stone. The landing choices after crossing the river are more varied. Starting from the English department, your route and career options are wide-ranging. In this speech, I will talk about my winding path and non-permanent career options. I'll also share what you can do with a background in linguistics to become a language therapist under the current system. Perhaps through my experience, you can imagine what it's like to land at a hospital with an English background.

session_name: The Narrative Value Cycle: A Business Practice Crossing Marketing and Product
session_content: Narrative is not the exclusive domain of creators. In a commercial setting, it is a set of learnable and repeatable thinking frameworks – from a line of copy on an app interface to a brand proposal that persuades partners – all built upon the same core logic: clarifying the audience, delivering messages precisely, and inspiring action. This lecture draws on the speaker’s practical experience in software startups to explore how narrative practice forms a complete value cycle between marketing communication and user experience design – where brands establish commitments through narratives, products fulfill those commitments through experiences, and users build long-term trust within this process. The lecture will then guide students beyond the product framework to consider the broader workplace and communicative contexts in which this narrative logic can be extended.\n\nSee detailed information on this department's webpage for latest news and academic activities.
