Yushan Bank Recruitment Seminar!
This lecture invited three outstanding alumni—Deputy Li Shouyu, Senior Specialist Zheng Shipu, and Deputy Engineer Li Yue庭—to share their career experiences!

Through experiences shared by Tsinghua University seniors, gaining a deeper understanding of the feasibility of a career at Qishe Bank!
Invitation is extended for eager报名 participation!

Character Strengths of Reserve Cadre Cadre Plan Characteristics

Reserve Officer (MA):
Financial holding company perspective training
various part-time positions
Foreign training opportunity

TMA Technology Reserve Staff
Information Technology and Management Capability Dual Development

Treasury and Financial Reserve Officers (TA):
Establishing an international perspective
Build Treasury professional team

Presenter Li Shaoyu introduced:
Current position: Deputy of Consumer Finance Section, Personal Credit Department (2021MA)
Education: NTU Business Research Institute R08
Motto: The paths we walk today will be lit by the light of tomorrow.
Introduction: After graduating from the Business Research Institute of National Taiwan University, I participated in the 2021 Wusam Bank MA program. Currently, I work as a Product Manager for small business loans at Wusam. My responsibilities include setting conditions for small business loan products (such as interest rates and额度), streamlining the credit approval process, revising key points and explanations related to these products, among other duties. I have also been involved in several important projects at Wusam, such as working with international consultants like McKinsey.

Presenter Zheng Shiping introduces:
Current Position: Senior Specialist in the Sustained Finance Department of the Corporate Finance Business Unit (2024MA)
Education: National Chiao Tung University, IE Program R10
Motto: Big things without fear of challenges, small things with full responsibility and heart.
Introduction: Graduated from National Taiwan University's School of Business and Economics, currently a reserve officer at Qishe Bank, where I serve as the Product Manager for sustainable finance products in the legal and financial department. I also provide customized sustainable consulting services to enterprises.
The most appealing aspect of daily duties is being able to assist enterprises in aligning with sustainable standards and trends while tailoring sustainable transformation and green finance solutions according to their needs amidst rapidly changing political and economic landscapes.
In their spare time, they are also enthusiastic about participating in activities such as badminton clubs and choir groups, which nourish both work and leisure interests, being my philosophy for maintaining enthusiasm and creativity in life.

Presenter Li Yue庭 introduced:
Current position: Senior Engineer in Intelligent Finance Department
Experience: Research Assistant at National Taiwan University's Institute of Computer Science
Education: National Taiwan University, Computer Science and Engineering Program R06
Motto: Play to Your Strengths, Explore Limits
Introduction: After graduating in 2020, I joined Qishe Bank and was mainly responsible for the foundational development and operation of intelligent service systems. During my tenure, I participated in projects related to voice dialing and intelligent customer service. Later, I became a DevOps engineer, focusing on the establishment and improvement of CI/CD processes across the department. After two years, I moved to a new team where I served as a project lead, developing mechanisms for MLOps model management, guiding newcomers through development processes, and sharing operational experience. Simultaneously, I supported Qishe's cloud transformation by evaluating and introducing emerging cloud tools, helping Qishe quickly deploy AI services into production. My academic background is in machine learning, with my thesis focusing on speech recognition. After graduation, being able to join Qishe in the field of intelligent finance, applying what I learned from AI to financial scenarios, and assisting various development teams in deploying their services smoothly was very rewarding for me.

activity_name_activity_session: English Speaking Reinforcement Workshop
activity_content:
Feeling that the time for the school's oral English speaking activities is always too short and you can't express yourself freely? Do you hope for a space with more interactive and friendly atmosphere to practice speaking?

[Intensive English Speaking Workshop Series] are conducted in small groups, led by Professor Zhuang Shiquan from the Foreign Languages Department of National Taiwan University. Each session lasts for two classes and guides deep discussions on various topics to help students improve their speaking skills in academic settings and enhance their critical thinking abilities. Whether you want to refine your English expression or strengthen your communication skills, this workshop is expected to bring plenty of benefits to you. We welcome interested students to register and participate!

Only a limited number of people are allowed per session; please seize the opportunity to register; if there is still space available before the event, other categories will be open for registration.
Those who have registered but cannot attend are requested to cancel their registration as soon as possible; failure to do so will result in the organizing unit retaining the right to exclude them from other sessions.
Anyone who completes the online registration for this activity agrees to allow the organizing unit to record or photograph the event and use the video or photos on the official website, electronic newsletters, social media platforms, video-sharing platforms, or any form of media. Those who do not agree should not participate in this activity.
This activity applies to the Double Language Center's "Stamp Activity for the English Learning Passport." Please scan the QR code at the end of each session to complete stamp collection. No make-up distribution will be provided after the activity ends.

session_name: Vision 2026 Taipei University Campus Recruitment - Company Information Session - 3/10 - Second Round - Trend Micro
session_content:
Vision 2026 Taipei University Campus Recruitment - Company Information Session - 3/10 - Second Round - Trend Micro

The first 50 registrants will receive a stainless steel ice pack set with drawstring pouches, and there will also be a prize draw at the end of the event!

Trend Micro is a global leader in AI cybersecurity, dedicated to developing security solutions specifically designed for AI, strengthening internal technical capabilities, and building an external security ecosystem. This helps enterprises and consumers address information and fraud threats. Trend Micro’s team spans various professional fields including threat prediction, AI software development, big data analysis, cloud architecture, front-end design, user experience, among others. We foster a culture of innovation and openness paired with a flat network-type organization to promote cross-departmental collaboration and breakthroughs. We have also received recognition for the Taiwan International Brand Award and Best IT Employer award!
Fresh hires wanted! Software Engineer: https://www.104.com.tw/job/8j3ec?jobsource=company_job

Trend Micro Summer Internship Program for Young Talent: Includes Software Development and Testing, AI/ML, User Experience, with 20 interns in total. Positions are expected to open for applications in March. Please follow Trend Micro Careers Taiwan for the latest updates!

Cross-cultural, Multicultural, and Growth – Experience New Career Possibilities at SHL!
Have you ever wondered what it feels like to work with colleagues from 50 different nationalities in Taiwan? Every day, there are exchanges of expertise, culture, and ideas that make the workplace not just a place for work but an international collaboration adventure.
Dean will share personally about the daily life of foreign engineering staff, cross-border team collaboration, and the real challenges faced by newcomers.

Sign up now, spots are limited, don't miss out!

Corporate Image Team: Delving into the core of Foxconn and exploring its global outlook in full depth.
Innovation Group: Exploring前沿technologies in electric vehicles and mastering future technologies and trends.
Financial Finance Group: Understand Foxconn's financial and financial system, and observe trends in the international market.

activity_name_activity_session: Library HELP Lecture "Learning with Ease and Confidence" (Spring Semester of 114th Academic Year)
activity_content:
"The HELP Classroom teaches you how to complete reports and assignments efficiently, quickly, and accurately for improved academic performance!"

Fast: Master database query tricks, no more searching through haystacks.
狠：Utilize book management software to quickly cite references.
✅ Accurate: Master core databases and disciplinary subject literature resources, perform precise searches.

Help you navigate through your studies and learn with ease and grace.
Here are this semester's topic sessions. Welcome to踊跃报名!

Follow along and learn how to use bibliography management software - EndNote
Good Morning Japan - Application of Japanese Newspaper Database
Exploring Historical Treasures - Gale Primary Sources
Ending Zotero Reference Library Hell - A Quick Start Guide
Turnitin Originality Checker Explained
Using AI ethically, academic research stays within the lines.

Participants in the full-time physical courses can receive a dedicated exclusive L clip from the library and have a chance to win beautiful prizes. Welcome to participate enthusiastically.
⚫ Faculty and staff of this school receive bonus points: Attend any Master Lecture or HELP Lecture to earn 1 point. Collect a total of 3 points to exchange for a prize ticket at the service desk on the 1st floor of the library. On June 3rd (Wednesday), 8 winners will be drawn, each receiving a $100 supermarket gift voucher, and they will be notified via email about their prize claim information.
Here's the translated text:

● Welcome for faculty and staff members as well as students from outside the school to register online. Notification will be sent via email prior to the class.
The library reserves the right to make changes to series courses, which will be announced on our website if any corrections or cancellations are made.
activity_name_event_page: Library HELP Lecture "Learning with Ease and Confidence" (Spring Semester of 114th Academic Year)
session_name: Follow along and learn how to use bibliography management software - EndNote
session_content: EndNote is a comprehensive bibliographic management software that assists users in collecting and organizing bibliographic data, seamlessly integrates with Word writing, easily handles citation and reference formatting, and supports cloud synchronization for convenient cross-device management of personal bibliographic databases.\nThis course will introduce the core features of EndNote and lead you through practical exercises to become familiar with its operation and establish an efficient academic writing workflow.\nThis course is applicable for users of Windows and macOS operating systems. Please download and complete the installation from the "EndNote" webpage at our library (https://www.lib.ntu.edu.tw/endnote) prior to the class. For any related issues, feel free to contact us.

activity_name_event_page: Library HELP Lecture "Learning with Ease and Confidence" (Spring Semester of 114th Academic Year)
session_name: Good Morning Japan - Application of Japanese Newspaper Database
session_content: The Asahi Shimbun database contains the latest reports from the newspaper since its founding in 1879, and it covers full-text searches of original articles from the 1879-1989 abridged editions as well as the valuable reference book "Hihōchū" containing modern language terms. In contrast, The Yomiuri Shimbun provides full-text search access to the original manuscripts of the newspaper since its founding in 1874.\nThis session will introduce how to use these two databases to search for newswire, magazines, and historical newspapers about Japan. We welcome teachers and students from related fields such as history, Japanese language, sociology, and literature to register and participate.

activity_name_event_page: Library HELP Lecture "Learning with Ease and Confidence" (Spring Semester of 114th Academic Year)
session_name: Exploring Historical Treasures - Gale Primary Sources
session_content: Gale Primary Sources gathers historical and archival resources spanning eight centuries, covering important areas of the humanities and social sciences. This enables researchers to access primary sources from different eras and regions, starting with original texts, exploring historical connections and academic issues, and considering multiple intersections between Taiwan and the world.\nThis course will introduce the content structure and research applications of Gale Primary Sources, guiding you through the collections of multiple international authoritative repositories. It will demonstrate the search tools and digital research features of the platform using precious historical materials spanning regions and centuries to help researchers establish research threads within vast primary data sets, uncovering academic connections across borders and eras. It will also effectively transform these sources into concrete research outcomes.

session_name: Ending Zotero Reference Library Hell - A Quick Start Guide
session_content: Zotero is a suite of open-source bibliography management tools that emphasizes a "quick gathering, real-time organization" workflow. It allows for一键acquisition of web bibliographic information, full-text PDFs, and annotation content. It is suitable for users who need to gather大量amounts of online data and PDF literature.\nThis course will guide you from installation to actually operating the core features of Zotero, including web-crawling for bibliographies, managing PDFs and automatic categorization, and demonstrating how to insert citations into Word and generate reference lists. The course will also introduce cloud synchronization, shared library functionality with multiple users, and mobile app applications, helping you establish an efficient, painless, and long-term academic writing workflow when dealing with tedious bibliography management and citation formats.

activity_name_activity_session: NTUHC-2026 Muscle Gain and Fat Reduction Class
activity_content:
Purpose of the activity

To promote the establishment of correct eating and exercise habits among faculty, staff, and students at our school to enhance health literacy, and assist participants in improving their body composition and physical condition through a combination of nutrition lectures and fitness courses, we are organizing this "Muscle Gain Fat Loss Health Promotion Activity."

Second, activity time

February 23 to May 30, 115 years.

Location of Activities

Rehabilitation Center on the second floor of the Health Center and the Second Activity Center.

Object of activities

Expected 20 faculty members and 80 students from this school.

Applicants with a Body Mass Index (BMI) less than 19 or greater than 24 will be given priority; if the quota is full, applicants who have not participated in this activity before will be given priority. The list of admitted candidates will be notified via email separately.

Those with a BMI outside of the above range can still participate in lectures and exercise courses and enter the prize draw, but cannot compete in the "Muscle Gain Fat Loss Challenge."

Five、Activity Content

This activity includes the following three components:

Nutrition Lecture
Physical Education Courses
Muscle Gain and Fat Loss Challenge

Participants can choose courses according to their own needs; participants who meet the criteria can participate in the competition evaluation.

Six - Eligibility for Challenge Participants

Participants in the "Muscle Gain Fat Loss Challenge" meet the following conditions:
BMI less than 19 or greater than 24
Register for at least 3 nutrition courses.
Register for at least one fitness class.
Complete the pre-employment health position within the stipulated period.

Eligible individuals must complete a health baseline assessment at the Health Center (Office on the 2nd floor) before a specified time (before March 13th) to serve as the basis for subsequent evaluations.

Provisions for Pre- and Post-Test Correlation can be found:
Facebook/Sharing/1Mac7VWFiG?mibextid=wwXIfr

session_name: Beyond Weight: The Role of Body Composition and Metabolic Markers in Weight Loss (Practical)
session_content:
One: Activity Name: The Truth Beyond Weight: The Role of Body Composition and Metabolic Markers in Weight Loss
Second session: March 19, 2026 (Thursday) from 12:20 to 14:00.
Speaker: Dr. Yang Ruihan from Taipei Medical University Hospital.
Four、Activity Location: Second Floor Health Classroom of Wellness Center.
Five, target audience: School faculty and students.
Six, Meal Instructions: Lunch is not provided for those attending physical lectures; please prepare your own.
Note: Participants who fully attended and checked in/out as well as completed satisfaction surveys will be eligible to log their public servants' lifelong learning hours.
Eight, to enhance muscle building and fat reduction efficacy, the activity offers a personalized health pre-and-post assessment tracking plan for registration. This assists in monitoring body position changes and achieving more ideal weight loss results. Participants who register are required to complete a health posture pre-assessment at the Wellness Center from February 23rd to March 13th. Participation in this program will earn you a精美registration gift, and there is an opportunity to win various prizes. For more details about the activity, please refer to Facebook and Instagram.

activity_name_event_page: NTUHC-2026 Muscle Gain and Fat Reduction Class
session_name: Muscle Building and Fat Reduction Course - Pre-test
session_content:
To enhance the actual effectiveness of muscle building and fat reduction, this activity provides exclusive personalized pre- and post-testing tracking. Through health position testing and data comparison, it assists you in clearly understanding your body’s changes as you move towards more ideal and healthy weight loss goals.

All participants who register are required to complete the health baseline assessment at the Health Center's 2nd Floor Room 210 between February 23 and March 13. After the activity, there will be follow-up tests arranged to track progress through scientific data, making the results easily visible.


Weight loss course challenge | Open to students only

Qualification for the challenge:
BMI is less than 19 or greater than or equal to 24.
Be willing to participate in at least one sports course and three body composition/nutrition courses (online courses provided).

Award:
Bonus Attendance (8 Classes): Water壶 1
Weekly Step Count Prize: $200 Voucher for the Whole Family (2 winners per week)
Top 5 for Waist Fat Reduction King (ranked by percentage decrease in body fat after the event): $500 Gift Voucher

According to the Ministry of Education's funding incentive regulations, teachers and staff members are not allowed to participate in the above competitions or award evaluations; however, anyone who registers for this activity can receive an attendance prize.

activity_name_activity_session: User Education and Training of Animal Resource Center
activity_content: By introducing the Animal Resource Center, establish the correct methods and注意事项 for using the Animal Resource Center among relevant personnel.\nThe course is for those who apply to use the animal resource center both inside and outside the school, including principal investigators, students, or assistants. All personnel intending to raise experimental animals in the Animal Resource Center are encouraged to register and participate in this training course before using it.
activity_name_event_page: User Education and Training of Animal Resource Center
session_name: Center for Animal Resource Education and Training 113/8/8 (Thursday)
session_content: By introducing the Animal Resource Center, establish methods and precautions for correct use of the center by relevant personnel. The course is aimed at those who apply to use the Animal Resource Center both inside and outside the school (including principal investigators, students or assistants). All users intending to raise experimental animals in the animal resource center must enroll in this center's training courses before using it. Please encourage all interested parties to actively register and participate.\nTyphoons will be postponed for one week.
location: 實驗動物資源中心2樓會議室
organizer_unit: 實驗動物資源中心
note: 可現場報名

activity_name_activity_session: User Education and Training of Animal Resource Center
activity_content: By introducing the Animal Resource Center, establish the correct methods and注意事项 for using the Animal Resource Center among relevant personnel.\nThe course is for those who apply to use the animal resource center both inside and outside the school, including principal investigators, students, or assistants. All personnel intending to raise experimental animals in the Animal Resource Center are encouraged to register and participate in this training course before using it.
activity_name_event_page: User Education and Training of Animal Resource Center
session_name: ABS Biosafety Level 2 User Training Center for Animal Resource Center August 8, 2011 (Thursday)
session_content:
By introducing the Animal Resource Center, establish correct usage of the infectious animal housing area (ABSL-2 zone) within the center and its precautions. The ABSL-2 zone uses Class II TYPE A2 biological safety cabinets for containment, maintaining negative pressure conditions for housing machinery IVCs. Personnel entry and experimental operations must adhere to the guidelines set by the Animal Resource Center. The course is targeted at both internal and external users who apply to use the Animal Resource Center (including principal investigators, students, and laboratory personnel). Users must participate in training courses provided by the center before using its ABSL-2 facilities. All personnel intending to house experimental animals within the Animal Resource Center are requested to register for participation.

Course注意事项:
ABSL-2 Personnel Guidelines and Instrument Operation Manual, Exam (Written Test).
ABSL-2 Field Operation Manual and注意事项.

Location: 2nd Floor Conference Room of Taiwan University Animal Resource Center and ABSL-2 zone animal experimental area.
(Taipei City Da-An District Keling Road Third Section 155 Lane 118)
Conditions: One must first complete training on user access to the Animal Resource Center before participating in the ABSL-2 education training course.
This course requires entering ABSL-2 biosafety level laboratory. Please assess your health condition yourself.
If the personnel from the asset center assesses a participant as unfit to enter the ABSL-2 area on-site, the center will refuse the participant entry into the ABSL-2 operational zone.
location: 實驗動物資源中心2樓會議室
organizer_unit: 實驗動物資源中心

activity_name_event_page: User Education and Training of Animal Resource Center
session_name: ABS Biosafety Level 2 User Training Center for Animal Resource Center 114/4/30 (Wed)
session_content:
By introducing the Animal Resource Center, establish correct usage of the infectious animal housing area (ABSL-2 zone) within the center and its precautions. The ABSL-2 zone uses Class II TYPE A2 biological safety cabinets for containment, maintaining negative pressure conditions for housing machinery IVCs. Personnel entry and experimental operations must adhere to the guidelines set by the Animal Resource Center. The course is targeted at both internal and external users who apply to use the Animal Resource Center (including principal investigators, students, and laboratory personnel). Users must participate in training courses provided by the center before using its ABSL-2 facilities. All personnel intending to house experimental animals within the Animal Resource Center are requested to register for participation.

Course注意事项:
ABSL-2 Personnel Guidelines and Instrument Operation Manual, Exam (Written Test).
ABSL-2 Field Operation Manual and注意事项.

Location: 2nd Floor Conference Room of Taiwan University Animal Resource Center and ABSL-2 zone animal experimental area.
(Taipei City Da-An District Keling Road Third Section 155 Lane 118)
Conditions: One must first complete training on user access to the Animal Resource Center before participating in the ABSL-2 education training course.
This course requires entering ABSL-2 biosafety level laboratory. Please assess your health condition yourself.
If the personnel from the asset center assesses a participant as unfit to enter the ABSL-2 area on-site, the center will refuse the participant entry into the ABSL-2 operational zone.

activity_name_activity_session: Department of Geographic Information Studies time (limited to students from this department)
activity_content:
When is the department head's time?
It's the time when you can openly talk about anything with your department head.
You can share any questions or suggestions about our department that you normally wouldn’t have a chance to say during regular time now!

Who can attend the time of the Department Head?
Neither the undergraduate program nor the graduate school~
As long as you're a member of the GIS department, you can all sign up!

What does the department head need to prepare?
Just bring a happy mood and your stomach. ^^

To pre-order the quantity of boxed meals, participants who wish to join are requested to register in advance.
Please bring your own eco-friendly cups and utensils today, thanks!

session_content:
The College of Literature promotes faculty-student exchanges between programs within the college to enhance interactions.
A forum for exchange between faculty and students of the College of Literature and the Library and Information Science department was held this semester.
The session will involve exchanges between members of the College of Literature, department heads, and faculty and students from our own department.
Invitation is extended to all members of this department to enthusiastically participate!

activity_name_activity_session: Department of Geographic Information Studies time (limited to students from this department)
activity_content:
When is the department head's time?
It's the time when you can openly talk about anything with your department head.
You can share any questions or suggestions about our department that you normally wouldn’t have a chance to say during regular time now!

Who can attend the time of the Department Head?
Neither the undergraduate program nor the graduate school~
As long as you're a member of the GIS department, you can all sign up!

What does the department head need to prepare?
Just bring a happy mood and your stomach. ^^

To pre-order the quantity of boxed meals, participants who wish to join are requested to register in advance.
Please bring your own eco-friendly cups and utensils today, thanks!

session_name: College of Literature and Library Science Faculty/Students Exchange Seminar - 110.1 (Limited to faculty and students from this department)
session_content:
The College of Literature promotes faculty-student exchanges between programs within the college to enhance interactions.
A forum for exchange between faculty and students of the College of Literature and the Library and Information Science department was held this semester.
The session will involve exchanges between members of the College of Literature, department heads, and faculty and students from our own department.
Invitation is extended to all members of this department to enthusiastically participate!

activity_name_activity_session: CARDO 114-2 Vocational Workshop
activity_content: Business partners collaborate to lead students through different workshop formats, including career workshops, entrepreneurship workshops, portable skills workshops, and counseling workshops, to help students build skill sets.
activity_name_event_page: CARDO 114-2 Vocational Workshop
session_name: ATCC Heart Method Sharing
session_content:
"Cardo X ATCC Case Workshop - Lessons from the ATCC Case and National Champion Senior Students"

Registration link: https://reurl.cc/yO189O

The competition for internships, graduate school applications, and job hunting is getting fiercer!
According to reliable information from senior students, having experience in large competitions does indeed count as a plus!
Watching peers who all have rich competition resumes or awards
But aren't you still staying in place, afraid of stepping out of your comfort zone?
Eager to compete in business competitions but unsure how to prepare?
Want to participate in the ATCC Business Case Competition but struggling to find teammates or understand the competition format?
ATCC National Taiwan University Workshop officially kicks off! 🔥
Limited-time and limited quantity resources open for all.
🔥 Explanation of registration template, open access to outstanding portfolios, highlight themes and key points 🔥

What is ATCC?
The largest commercial case competition in the whole Taiwan, jointly launched by well-known enterprises to present real-world business issues, allowing contestants to view problems from an industry perspective and solve corporate challenges. Over 22 years, ATCC has collaborated with more than 128 companies, government units, and NPOs, accumulating over 47,000 student participants. From strategy formulation to presentation pitches, it comprehensively cultivates your business thinking and team combat capabilities!

The 23rd ATCC invites industry-leading brands, startups, NGOs, and public sector entities as partners for issue collaboration. It kicks off with the ATCC Bootcamp on March 28, followed by the Opening Forum/Practical Workshops on April 11, Issue Workshop on April 18, and the ATCC Camp on April 25. This officially marks a series of intense competition rounds from the preliminary to the semi-finals. The national finals and award ceremony on July 5 finally witness the peak对决!

Key Highlights of the NTU专场 Case Workshop
ATCC Camp Customs Clearance Guide: Master the Secrets of Important Stages to Ensure a Smooth Journey and Advance!
Partner Issues Breakdown: Solving Five Major Corporate Issues from the Host Unit's Perspective!
Historical Outstanding Works Analysis: No-Hiding-Malice Open High-Scoring Proposals, Learn the Strongest Competition Logic!
Team Matching & Career Networking: Meet Comrades, Expand Your Network, and Feel No Uncertainty in Competitions & the Future!
Free Q&A and Social Interaction: Face-to-face with organizers at the venue, and a LINE social group is available for continued interaction after the event!

Event time: 3/31 (Tue) 18:00 - 20:00
Location of the activity: Room 402, One Building, Management College, National University of Taiwan
Registration link: https://reurl.cc/yO189O
This workshop will help you crack the key to the competition and get you to the finals stage!
🚀 Cardo X ATCC, ready for you to challenge!


Activity Schedule
18:30 - 18:40 ATCC Case Method Sharing
18:40 - 19:00 Sharing and exchanging first-hand information and team preparation tips
19:00 - 19:20 How to Pass the Examination of Proposing a Camp Planning Outline
19:20 - 19:35 Host Perspective on Topic Direction
19:35 - 19:50 Recognizing and Sharing Analysis of Past Identification Chemistry Sister Works
19:50 - 20:00 Career Q&A

Speaker:
ATCC Li-Rong Chao, SVP/Administrator
Sure, please provide the text you would like translated.
If unable to attend at the last minute, cancel the registration through the event registration system or by filling out a form.
Form link: https://reurl.cc/RerZ3r
Three consecutive unexcused absences will be listed on a blacklist. Participants who register and attend can accumulate 10 CARDO points.
Do not arrive late; participants who do not enter within 10 minutes after the activity starts will be excluded.

activity_name_activity_session: 114th Academic Year School Marathon赛
activity_content:
One: Competition Date: March 28, 2017 (Saturday) at 8:30 AM (Opening Ceremony) to 11:00 AM (Closing).
Second leg: Meeting and departure point: Stadium Football Field (assembly from 8 am to 8:30 am).
Maximum number of participants: The total number of participants is capped at 4000. Please register as soon as possible online, and the registration data will be based on the online activity registration system.
Four: Participants: All faculty and staff members, students, spouses, alumni, and friends of National Taiwan University, as well as retired faculty and staff members, are all eligible to register for participation.
Five, Competition Categories: (One person is limited to one category)
Challenge Group
Male students' group
Female students' group
3. Faculty members, alumni, and friends of Taiwan University form teams: (Those older can register for the lower age group but must carefully evaluate their age when registering; they cannot change their age group after registration.)
Senior Group (Male/Female): Those aged 60 and above (born before March 28, 1955).
Male (Female) Group: Those aged under 60 who were born between March 29, 1955 and March 28, 1965.
Female Group: Those aged under 50 born between March 29, 1965 and March 28, 1975.
Male (Female) Group: Those under 40 years old (born after March 29, 1975).

Control group: Not divided into groups.
Friends of National Taiwan University include family members and retired faculty and staff
Persons registering with duplicate identities (current students of this school, faculty and staff members, dependents, alumni, and retired faculty/staff) are limited to registering for the student category and cannot register for other categories.
Six, Technology Conference: To be held on Monday, March 16, 2015 at noon and 30 at the Second Floor Classroom Room 247 of the Comprehensive Sports Center (Conference content: 1. Announcing matters related to the campus marathon; 2. Explanation of procedures for collecting bibs and chips).
activity_limit: 本活動限制一個人可報名 1 個場次
activity_name_event_page: 114th Academic Year School Marathon赛
session_name: Challenge Group - Student Group
session_content:
Distance of the competition: approximately 5 kilometers.
Persons of higher age can challenge a lower age group by downgrading, but please evaluate this carefully when registering and changes to the age group after registration are not allowed.
Reward办法：三、
Male and female students who rank in the top twenty each receive prize items and an electronic achievement certificate download. Students ranked twenty-first to one hundred in male and female student groups receive one prize item, and all other participants who complete the full course within sixty minutes are given an electronic achievement certificate download.
(Two) Entering the College/School Rankings, 1. Three students from each college will be selected for the overall ranking, and 2. Six students from each department or institute will be selected for the overall ranking.
(A) Setting up lottery prizes: Participants who complete the full course can receive one lottery ticket to participate in the draw.
Caution:
Contest participants must collect their bibs prior to the event; no on-site collection will be available on activity day.
Pick-up time for number slips: Starting from March 23, 20115 (Monday) at noon and ending on March 25, 20115 (Wednesday) at 8 PM; open daily from 9 AM to 8 PM. The location is the Cultural Corridor on the second floor of the Comprehensive Sports Center (located above the Service Counter on the first floor). Please bring your student ID, faculty/staff card, or identification document.
On the day of the race, all参赛选手 must wear运动 shoes. Please sew or pin the chip number onto your chest yourself. Those who do not comply with the above regulations will not be included in the results. A digital result proof will be provided for those who complete the full marathon within the specified time.
The competition is divided into two groups, with the first group being for male and female students, and the second group consisting of faculty members, alumni, and friends of National Taiwan University along with an adaptive group.
(G) (G) Receiving Shirts and Important Matters for Pickup:
The student group will receive it among the first 1500 registrants via the network.
The Professional Staff Alumni Group provides a free online registration spot for the first 350 applicants (limited to current and former employees of this school, alumni, and retired staff members).
The list of commemorative t-shirts will be announced on January 30th, 2015 (Friday), in the campus notice board and the sports room webpage (please check online for yourself, no further notice).
The contestants who won t-shirts should wear their t-shirts during the event on the day of the activity.
T恤 should be worn on the day of the competition and here is the reminder:
March 28th (Saturday), from 7:30 AM to 8:30 AM, pick up at the school central sports field.
When picking up, please bring the serial number tag to verify if you are entitled to a commemorative shirt (failure to present will result in non-receipt, and lost items will not be replaced).
Limited quantities of commemorative shirts are available in various sizes. Supplies will run out once they are all gone. Please queue up early to collect your shirt at the site.
All参赛人员 should collect their commemorative shirts within the specified time before the race; those who fail to collect them in time will be awarded as finishers.
Based on safety considerations, the conference will provide a recommended order of departure based on the reference scores provided during registration.

activity_name_activity_session: Online Course: Decoding the Behind-the-Scenes of Learning Planning | Exploring the System with NTU on March 26
activity_content:
In recent years, various colleges and universities have actively established a "learning planning system" centered on students as an important mechanism to support cross-disciplinary learning and autonomous learning. To promote exchanges among schools and the development of these systems, the Ministry of Education conducted a pilot project for flexible academic programs, which specially invited the Office of Learning Planning at National Taiwan University’s Academic Affairs Department to hold an online sharing lecture titled "Unveiling the Backstage Formula of Learning Planning." The lecture introduced practical experiences and system designs in implementing learning planning at National Taiwan University.

This lecture will take a holistic approach to institutional setup and share the operational models of learning planning systems, methods for recruiting and training learning planners, as well as case studies on guiding cross-disciplinary students through learning planning. The aim is to assist participants in understanding key practices and development trajectories of system promotion, and serve as a reference for future construction of student support systems at various schools.

Lecture Information
Topic | Revealing the Behind-the-Scenes Formula of Learning Planning
Content | Introduction of Learning Planning Systems and Practices, Recruitment and Training for Learning Planners, Case Studies of Cross-Disciplinary Student Guidance
Speaker | College of Academic Affairs Learning Planning Office, National Taiwan University

Activity Information
Time: March 26, Thursday (10:00–11:00) (Please log in to the video link ahead of time by 5-10 minutes on the day)
Registration period: From now until March 23rd (Monday) at 10:00 AM
Registration link: https://reurl.cc/epxG5m
Meeting Notice - Email Sent with Online Meeting Information on March 24 (Tue)

Participants
Learning Plan Development and Implementation Staff
Learning coaches or student advisors
Administrators and teachers interested in student learning guidance and cross-disciplinary learning support

We sincerely invite all teachers and colleagues to participate together in exchanging experiences of promoting learning planning systems and promoting the development of cross-domain support mechanisms.

activity_name_activity_session: 2026 Occupational Health Lecture (Three): Women's Health Assurance Guide: Prevention in Workplace and Life
activity_content:
Abstract
The lecture is divided into three main topics for introduction, covering common gynecological diseases, the impact of environmental and occupational exposure on women's health (such as chemicals, drugs), and measures and regulations related to female health protection. In the section on gynecological diseases, common issues such as urinary system diseases, uterine fibroids, abnormal menstruation (such as endometriosis), and common cancers in females (such as cervical cancer, breast cancer) will be introduced. Additionally, the lecture will explain the potential impact of environmental and occupational exposure on women's health and introduce relevant protective measures and regulations.
In addition to disease and regulatory explanations, the lecture will share practical preventive concepts including daily nutrition, exercise, and health management. This will help大家 better understand the connection between gynecological diseases and workplace life, and through concepts of daily care and health promotion, enhance self-care abilities to welcome a healthy lifestyle!

Second session: March 25, 2026 (Wednesday), 14:00-16:00

Three, activity method: Live broadcast of Microsoft Teams online video conference

This activity will be conducted through a Microsoft Teams online video livestream. Please prepare your own computer or mobile device to use, and participants who complete online check-in and participate in the entire lecture will receive 2 hours of digital learning credits for faculty and staff members of our school.

Office of Student Affairs Health Center BLS (Basic Life Support) training in March
One, this center was established to enhance the emergency handling capabilities of faculty and staff at three affiliated universities of National Taiwan University for dealing with unexpected accidents. Specialized in-house training on "Basic Life Support" (BLS) has been organized, featuring physicians from the Emergency Medicine Department of Taichung Area Hospital, senior rescue technicians, physical therapists from Taichung Area Hospital, as well as colleagues from this center. The training aims to organize practical exercises to cultivate students' ability to handle crises in real-life situations, thereby ensuring campus and club activity safety.

Test.

activity_name_activity_session: [Common Education Center - General Education Special Topic Lecture] Prof. Imre Szeman, Fellow of the Royal Society of Canada - "Why Do the Energy Humanities Matter Now?"
activity_content:
Activity Information
Time: March 24, 2026 (Tuesday) 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM
Location: Chaozhong Campus Bolya Teaching Building Room 102
Speaker: Prof. Imre Szeman (Fellow of the Royal Society of Canada and Professor of Human Geography at the University of Toronto)
Speaker: Prof. Guangji Hong (Department of Geography, National Taiwan University), Prof. Bette Tai (Department of English, National Taipei College of Education)
Language: English

Speaker Introduction:
Professor Imre Szeman is the inaugural director of the Institute for Environmental, Conservation and Sustainable Development at the University of Toronto Scarborough, where he also holds a professorship in Human Geography. He is an elected Fellow of the Royal Society of Canada. He has authored, co-authored, or edited 30 books, with recent representative works including:
"On Petrocultures: Globalization, Culture, and Energy" (2019): Examines the social-cultural dimensions of energy use and its impact on energy transition and climate change.
"Futures of the Sun: The Struggle Over Renewable Life" (2024): Exploring how businesses and nations manage the process of transitioning to renewable energy.
"Power Shift: Keywords for a New Politics of Energy" (Power Shift: Keywords for a New Politics of Energy, edited): A glossary of keywords related to contemporary energy politics.
His latest co-authored work, "Green Dreams: Why Technology Can't Save the World," will be published by Bloomsbury in 2026.

Activity schedule:
18:30-19:00: Registration and entry open
19:00-19:10: Opening remarks (Professor Hong Guangji from the Department of Geography, National Taiwan University)
19:10-20:20: Keynote Speech (Prof. Imre Szeman)
20:20-20:50: Q&A Session
20:50-21:00: Gifted thank-you certificates and group photo on site

Information:

Introduction:
Prof. Imre Szeman is the inaugural Director of the Institute for Environment, Conservation and Sustainability and Professor of Human Geography at the University of Toronto Scarborough. He is author, co-author, editor, or co-editor of thirty books, including (mostly recently) On Petrocultures: Globalization, Culture, and Energy (2019), which explores the socio-cultural dimensions of energy use and its implications for energy transition and climate change; Futures of the Sun: The Struggle Over Renewable Life (2024), a book examining corporate and state control of the transition to renewables; and Power Shift: Keywords for a New Politics of Energy (co-edited), a lexicon of the politics of energy today. His latest book, Green Dreams: Why Technology Can’t Save the World (co-written), will be published by Bloomsbury in 2026. He is a fellow of the Royal Society of Canada.

activity_name_activity_session: Taichung Medical University Series on Four Preventions - Anti-fraud, Counter-autocracy, and Anti-drug (Traffic Safety event to be held on a different date) Legal Education Seminar (Lunch Provided)
activity_content:
温馨提示：
The itinerary for this event is one full day with three sessions (※Check-in time is from 09:10 to 09:40). After registration, participants can choose the courses they want to attend flexibly.
And this instructor put in a lot of effort preparing all the course content, so I still sincerely encourage participants to fully take advantage of the opportunity and attend as many sessions as possible.
We have prepared practical items for our partners who participated in the anti-fraud, anti-harassment, and anti-drug sessions!

Training Agenda Time Content:

09:10–09:40 30 minutes Check-in

09:40–09:50 10 minutes Introduction

10:50~10:00 10 minutes Group photo

First Class: Drug Prevention for Students – 120 Minutes
Special Lecture 10:00–11:50
11:50–12:00 QA
12:00–12:10 Campus Practical Case Sharing and Experience Exchange

Dinner gathering 60 minutes

Second Class: Preventing Campus Fraud 100 Minutes
Special Lecture 13:10–14:40
14:40–14:50 QA
14:50–15:00 Campus Practical Case Sharing and Experience Exchange

15:00–15:10 Rest for 10 minutes

Third Class: Bullying Prevention and Restorative Justice 110 minutes
Special Lecture 15:10–16:50
16:50–17:00 QA
17:00–17:10 Sharing of campus practical case studies and experience exchange

Return home

The purpose of this study tour:

This seminar was specially organized to align with the Ministry of Education’s “Four Preventions in Campus Safety” policy. The content integrates four major issues: preventing campus fraud, preventing school bullying, preventing drug abuse among students, and traffic safety. It invites representatives from government agencies, academia, and practical experts for participation. Through specialized lectures, it aims to strengthen the understanding and preventive measures of faculty, staff, and students in universities and colleges regarding these topics.
To effectively enhance学员's professional skills in areas such as anti-fraud, anti-bullying, anti-drug use, and交通安全, to deepen campus safety awareness, and to jointly create a safe, friendly, and reassuring learning environment. Key themes are as follows:

Fighting Addiction: Strengthening Awareness of the Hazards and Recognition of Drugs, and Cultivating Prevention and Reporting Abilities.
Fraud Prevention: Enhancing Awareness of Scamming, Recognizing Common Tactics and Mitigation Measures to Avoid Campus Fraud Incidents.
Anti-Bullying: Establishing a Culturally Welcoming School Environment to Prevent Bullying Behaviors and Strengthen Both Staff and Students’ Legal Awareness and Self-Protection Abilities.
(Safety Education: Enhancing students' and teachers' awareness of交通安全, implementing pedestrian and cycling safety as well as traffic order maintenance, establishing a campus traffic culture of "pedestrian priority, obeying laws together," and creating a safe and orderly commuting environment.)

Welcome everyone to join us!

activity_name_activity_session: College of Literature [Joint Symposium on New Trends in Civilization] Green Dreams, or, Why Technology Cannot Save the World
activity_content:
Time: March 24 (Tue) 14:00-16:00
Location: National Taiwan University College of Literature Conference Room
Topic: Green Dreams, or, Why Technology Can't Save the World (English Speech)
Speaker: Imre Szeman (Professor of Human Geography at the University of Toronto, Canada; Fellow of the Royal Society of Canada)
Host: Li Jishaye (Professor of Foreign Languages at National University of Taiwan and Deputy Dean of the College of Arts)
Registration link: https://forms.gle/rauDBTWUravbej8BA
Organizers: National Taiwan University College of Literature, Trend Education Foundation, Joint Creation Civilization New Trends

Is technology really the savior of the climate crisis?
When Silicon Valley tech giants cry out for "net-zero emissions" and promote "green AI," are we falling into a "green dream" woven from data and algorithms? This special lecture on the "New Trends of Co-creative Civilization" will take an anthropological perspective, with Professor Imre Szeman, a Fellow of the Royal Society of Canada, specially invited. As a trailblazer in interdisciplinary field of “energy anthropology,” Professor Imre Szeman will lead us through the green packaging of tech giants and re-examine global sustainable development from a humanistic viewpoint. For anthropologists, when climate politics are dictated by technological narratives, critical analysis, interpretation, and alternative visions for the future will be key to reclaiming the leadership in climate action.

Parexel Campus Lecture Information

Subject: [Parexel百瑞精鼎] Decoding CRF Programming: A Career Guide for Senior Teams

Would you like to know what actual work in statistical programming looks like in the field of clinical research?
Have you heard of FSP (Functional Service Provider)?
Do you want to know how to build a successful career in the CRO industry?
This lecture invites senior managers and seasoned colleagues from Parexel to share, from both a managerial and practitioner’s perspective, the career path in statistical programming, essential skills required, and their real-world experiences in international clinical trials. Parexel provides a comprehensive training system and clear career development paths to help you grow steadily in the field of statistical programming.
Open Q&A time is available on-site, feel free to ask your career questions! Join Parexel's Statistical Programming team and change the world with data!

Parexel (Baori Jingding) is one of the top global contract research organizations (CROs) companies and Taiwan’s largest CRO company. Headquartered in the United States, it has over 60 offices worldwide with nearly 21,000 employees. Established in 1982, Parexel aims to become a leading supplier of innovative medical therapy development and commercialization services in the biopharmaceutical and medical devices industry globally. All top 50 international pharmaceutical companies have adopted Parexel’s services and solutions.
Taiwan is a core hub in the Asia-Pacific region, with over 600 employees distributed across more than 60 departments, offering diverse and expansive career opportunities.

Promotion pipeline is open: Performance excellence is the core culture, promoting outstanding employees based on performance, without age or tenure restrictions.

activity_name_activity_session: ⚖️2026 Advanced Graduate Studies Program⚖️
activity_content: The Teaching Development Center of the College of Education,承办 under the "Deepening Plan" project within the深耕计划, targets graduate students (masters and doctoral) studying at our school as its main service recipients. It holds training courses, workshops, and lecture forums. The topics cover knowledge, skills, and attitudes that graduate students need during their studies. It also invites domestic and international outstanding scholars to share their personal experiences, academic journeys, or current state of academia with the graduates, providing good guidance for graduate students' learning and career development.
activity_name_event_page: ⚖️2026 Advanced Graduate Studies Program⚖️
session_name: Graduate AI Empowerment Workshop
session_content:
Introduction to Activities
The rise of AI technology has both impacted and propelled the cultivation of graduate academic abilities and future skills. In response, the Teaching Development Center of the Office of Academic Affairs specially organized a "Graduate AI Enhancement Workshop," which covers theoretical foundations to practical exercises, guiding students in exploring the impact of AI on various fields, learning the application of related tools, enhancing their research and teaching capabilities, accumulating essential workplace skills ahead of time, and conducting necessary ethical considerations. This will enable graduates to become future talents with diverse competitive advantages in the era of AI.

★Content for each round★
Academic and teaching capabilities empowered by AI
Time: March 23, 2026 (Mon) 18:00-21:00
Speaker: Computer Playthings Station Manager Esor
Explanation: Discussing the impact and benefits of AI on higher education, learning how to appropriately apply AI tools for various daily tasks in graduate or teaching assistant roles, such as concept development for research topics, literature collection and synthesis, curriculum creation, and grading assignments, to enhance the efficiency of academic research/supplemental teaching.

2. [Play Session Two] AI-Transformed Workplace Expression and Visual Communication
Time: March 26, 2026 (Thu) 18:00-21:00
Speaker: Jianbao Presentation Hamber, Co-founder Kevin
Explanation: From the technical expertise of integrating AI into presentation creation and visualization techniques, to personalizing practices in restructuring presentation logic and real-world presentations, explore the self-thinking abilities, technological integration skills, communication and expression capabilities, and aesthetic sensibilities required for future workplaces.

Ethical Awareness and Philosophical Reflection in the Wake of the AI Wave
Time: March 30, 2026 (Mon) 18:00-21:00
Speaker: Professor Chen Jiangang from the Department of Political Science at National Chengchi University
Explanation: Crossing between technology and humanities, considering the impact of AI on ethical issues such as social justice, intellectual property values, and the meaning of life, to cultivate critical thinking, sense of responsibility, and decision-making ability.

Registration Notice
Activity format: Strip workshop, each session is 3 hours, totaling 9 hours.
Activity time: March 23, 26, and 30, 2026, 18:00–21:00 (registration opens at 17:30)

activity_name_activity_session: Overseas Compatriot and Mainland Chinese Student Counseling Practice Exchange Workshop
activity_content:
Thank you to everyone who has been putting in effort to serve Hsiao-lu students, so that there are more opportunities for mutual exchange and learning. To understand the current situation of Hsiao-lu students at school as well as the relevant resources provided by the school, the Student Affairs Office’s Committee for Overseas Students and Local Students, together with the Counseling Center, specially organized this event. We welcome everyone to actively register and participate.

This activity will be conducted through simulated scenarios and group discussions, guiding participants from different perspectives to understand the real-life situations of Hsiao-lu students' studies and adaptation at school, as well as further mastering the available counseling resources within the campus. It aims to promote cross-unit experience exchanges, strengthen cooperation and connections between departments and colleges/schools/universities.
Participants who complete the activity will receive 2 hours of lifetime learning credits and a light meal.
Time: March 23, 2025 (Monday) 2:00 PM - 4:00 PM
Location: Administrative Building First Conference Room
Content Arrangement: Introduction to Overseas Students Overview, Briefing on the Student Counseling Center of the Academic Affairs Office, Simulation Scenarios and Group Discussions, Q&A Session for Sharing Experiences.
Eligible participants include staff from institutes, departments (centers), and administrative units involved in promoting mentorship for overseas students. Faculty members from within the university are also welcome to register.

Join us for an interactive session designed to deepen our understanding of the academic and campus life experiences of Overseas Compatriots and Mainland Chinese students.
Through scenario-based activities and small-group discussions, participants will gain insight into student perspectives, become more familiar with campus support resources, and connect with colleagues across units to enhance collaboration.
Participants who fully attend the event will be awarded 2 lifelong learning hours. Refreshments will be provided.
Date: Monday, March 23, 2026, from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM
Location: Conference Room 1, Administrative Building
3. Agenda: Introduction to Overseas Compatriot and Mainland Chinese students, an overview of the Center for Student Well-Being, Office of Student Affairs; scenario-based simulations and small-group discussions; followed by a Q&A session for mutual exchange of experiences and insights.
Target Audience: Promote counseling efforts for overseas and mainland Chinese students by colleagues from various departments and administrative units at the university.

activity_content:
One, through awareness meetings and bicycle safety checks, to ensure that students at National Taiwan University maintain a normal and safe state of using bicycles, and to enhance their understanding and attitude towards "bicycle riding etiquette, nighttime bicycle riding safety, and lawful parking discipline."
Training and Lecture Instructor: Cheng Mingli

activity_name_activity_session: Is What They Say on NTUHC? - Sex Education for the Influencer Generation: Debunking Myths in College Students' Sexual Education in the Era of Short Videos
activity_content:
Time: March 17, 2011 (Tuesday) 12:20-14:00.
Speaker: Nursing师 Liang Shihui from Taipei City Public Hospital Ren'ai District Clinic.
Three: The Red-Gen Z and Sex Education "Is What They Say on the Internet True? Debunking Myths in College Students' Sexual Education in the Short Video Age"
Four, location: Classroom 103 of the First Activity Center. Participants in the online course will be provided with a link to the class via email before the session.
Five, target audience: School teachers, staff, and students. Participants who fully participate in the course, sign in/out, and complete satisfaction surveys can log 2 hours of lifelong learning credits.
Six, Meal Instructions: Due to restrictions on funding usage by the Ministry of Education, this activity will provide each student with a packed lunch; faculty members will be given a gift instead.
Seven, Activity Rewards:
Students who participate in this activity will receive one lottery ticket and a prize draw will be held at the end of the semester. The highest prize value is New Taiwan Dollars 4,000.
Faculty members who participate in this activity will receive one point card. Upon accumulating a certain number of points, they can exchange for a set of精美gifts.
Activity-related information can be found on this center's official Facebook/Instagram platforms:
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=61585288985745
IG: https://www.instagram.com/ntu.healthcenter/
activity_name_event_page: Is What They Say on NTUHC? - Sex Education for the Influencer Generation: Debunking Myths in College Students' Sexual Education in the Era of Short Videos
session_name: Is Reality What Red-Net Generation Sex Ed Says It Is? Decoding College Students' Misconceptions About Internet Sex Education (Physical)
session_content: In the era of short-video influencers where sexual knowledge is often packaged in 15-second content that’s hard to discern between true and false, do you think having many followers means expertise? And does getting millions of likes mean it’s correct? This lecture will start from the common misconceptions about internet-based sex education that college students frequently encounter and easily believe. We’ll dismantle the information traps behind short videos and clarify the correct concepts on HIV, sexually transmitted diseases, and gender-related issues. Let’s use reason to replace superstition, knowledge to defeat algorithms, so your sexual education isn’t just relying on hearsay from the internet.

target_audience: Student, Faculty, Staff, Project Personnel, Hospital Staff, Alumni, Academia Sinica

activity_name_activity_session: English Center Film Reading and Appreciation 🎬
activity_content:
[Cool Jungle Elite Program] Film Reading and Appreciation 🎬

Game 1: 2001: A Space Odyssey

You knew that as far back as 1968, there were films exploring interactions between AI and humans? What timeless启示can classic science fiction films from the last century offer us today?

This event featured the film 2001: A Space Odyssey, led by Professor Lee Kishi, director of our center and professor at National Taiwan University’s English Department, who would guide us through the philosophical thoughts, imagery, and ethical implications of this classic movie. After the introduction, we could watch the entire film in a small theater that resembles a cinema.

Come and join this event to explore space with us!

Time: 2:00 PM - 5:00 PM
Location: Foreign Language Center Small Theater

Limited spots! Please make sure you can attend before registering again.
Registering represents your agreement for us to film or record content of the activities and use them on our website, electronic newsletters, social media, and video platforms.
This activity can accumulate bilingual education center's "English Learning Passport" points. Please scan the QR code on that day; no make-up issuance will be provided after expiration.

activity_name_activity_session: National Taiwan University Wellness and Culture Association (Staff Division) Health and Intelligence Lecture
activity_content:
National Taiwan University Wellness and Culture Association (Staff Division) Health and Intelligence Lecture
Time: Friday, March 13th, 12:10–13:20 (UTC+8)
Location: Administrative Building First Conference Room
Topic: "Caring for Children vs. Caring for Elders – Long-Term Care and Life Stages"
Teacher: Xu Yiqian Clinical Psychologist
With the rapid changes and progress of modern society, how is "parenting" not as simple as it was in the past when our parents raised us? Modern parents seem to be facing a much more complex issue with "raising children," especially given the information explosion era where kids know more and learn faster than their parents do; and in a modern society that places great emphasis on child rights, what constitutes "discipline" seems quite different from how it was experienced by parents themselves back then...
While parents in the sandwich generation are still worrying about how to educate their children, they may also be facing another challenge: the aging of their own parents. Economic pressures, time constraints, physical demands, and other challenges intensify as one gradually enters middle age, requiring them to address corresponding developmental issues such as stagnation and advancement in career, menopause, midlife crises, and re-adjusting to physical and mental changes… leaving a group sandwiched between young children and aging parents feeling overwhelmed.
This lecture will discuss topics such as "From Rearing Children to Nurturing Parents" based on the speaker's experiences in practical work, exploring how these issues manifest in daily life and finding resonance and inspiration from others' stories.
Educational background:
Master of Clinical Psychology from the Department of Applied Psychology, School of Social Sciences, Xuanzang University
Chang Gung University College of Medicine Department of Psychiatric Rehabilitation (now Clinical Psychology) graduate
Graduation from the Clinical Course on Psychoanalytic Orientation Psychological Treatment of the Taiwan Society for Psychoanalysis
Taiwan Association for Psychoanalysis members
Licenses:
Chinese National Examination for Specialized Professionals and Technicians qualified Clinical Psychologist
ACP Certified Senior Horticultural Therapist
Long-term care service personnel
Level C Tree Climbing Coach
Registration method: From 9:00 AM on Monday, February 23rd to noon on Thursday, March 5th, register in the following manner. Registration will close upon reaching capacity.
Faculty members and colleagues: There are 100 spots available. Please go to MyNTU → Event Registration System.

activity_name_activity_session: Free Health Consultation Clinic - Service Hours in March 2015
activity_content:
To maintain colleagues' workplace health, a campus巡回 clinic was specially established, providing occupational medicine consultation services for faculty and staff at the university by physicians from the Occupational Medicine Department of Taipei Medical University and nursing professionals in-house.

Target audience: All faculty and staff members of the university (both Labor Insurance and Public Service Insurance colleagues can participate)

Second, workplace health counseling project:
Health issues: Explanation of health report, medical specialty advice, personal disease consultation.
Workplace Unlawful Interference Consultation: Inquiry and Counseling on Internal Complaints for Suspected Bullying (Verbal, Psychological, Physical) and Mental Health Adaptation Referral.
(III) Prevention of Human Factors Hazards: Evaluation of Office Chair Height (Visiting Service), Prevention of Poor Postures, Assessment of the Relationship Between Shoulder and Neck Pain/Sacral Back Pain Symptoms and Work Environment.
Maternal Health Protection: Providing workplace and occupational hazard assessments for female faculty colleagues who are expected to become pregnant, who are currently pregnant, or who have given birth recently or are breastfeeding.
FIVE、Fatigue-related consultations: Providing cardiovascular disease risk screening and assessment for colleagues who work shifts, night shifts, or long hours.

Three, fees: free, no health insurance card required.

Four, dates and locations: monthly outpatient services at different units, please see the content of the session registration for details.

Reservation items:
If there are overlapping appointment times, we will contact you by phone to arrange them in order and send a confirmation of successful reservation.
Bring your health check-up report for consultation.

activity_name_activity_session: Vision 2026 NTU Campus Recruitment - Cross-Institute Theme Forum
activity_content:
A career is not just about job hunting; it's a strategic布局 for maximizing value!

Are you ready to become the leader of the wave as Generative AI redefines workplace rules?
This year, the College of Career Development at NTU invited three distinguished alumni who have极致practical experience in "cross-domain integration" to return to their alma mater. From digital transformation in traditional construction, the path to leadership in technology startups, to insights into global talent, they help you see the endless possibilities for your future career.

This is not a dry lecture but rather a battlefield map shared by older sisters.
Your career, you create! Reserve your captain’s seat now.

Play One: Exploring and Practicing a Career in Digital Construction from a Civil Engineering Major at National Taiwan University to Feng Yu Group's Smart Construction
Guest Speaker: Hsieh Jhsing (President of Feng Yu Construction | Civil Engineering Alumnus)

Session Two: Fellow students, before maximizing your career, you must first do these 10 things.
Alumnus Speaking: Lim Zhichen Jamie (President and Partner of Taiwan Mobile / Chairman and Partner of AppWorks | Chemical Engineering Alum)

Session Three: When Your Career Has No Standard Answer: How to Build Your Market Value
Guest Speaker: Yufan Xie (General Manager of BaiShilian | Alumnus from History Department)
activity_name_event_page: Vision 2026 NTU Campus Recruitment - Cross-Institute Theme Forum
session_name: Play One: Exploring and Practicing a Career in Digital Construction from a Civil Engineering Major at National Taiwan University to Feng Yu Group's Smart Construction
session_content:
Guest Speaker: Hsieh Jhsing (President of Feng Yu Construction | Civil Engineering Alumnus)
Panelist: Professor Xi Shangxian from National Taiwan University Civil Engineering Department
Abstract:

Do you still have the impression of construction industry in construction sites?
When civil engineering meets AI and digital construction, a revolution in urban space is happening!

We invited President Shih Jhsing of Feng Yu Construction (a distinguished alumnus from the Civil Engineering program) to share his important choices and thoughts based on his career experience, in a deep conversation with BIM Center Director Shih Shang-hian teacher. This session will deconstruct how traditional industries can transform into pioneers of smart construction through technological empowerment, redefining what new construction industries are and future trends. If you aspire to turn academic research and development into tangible societal change, this dialogue will refresh your career imagination!

Notice:
To allow speakers to prepare for questions in advance, please have registered students fill out: https://reurl.cc/AbGQbE
The contact information for participants of this session will be provided to the speakers for reference, including their college/school, department, name and email.
Students who register online will be provided with commemorative items if they all participate.
location: 椰林大道底 主舞台區
organizer_unit: 學生事務處學生職業生涯發展中心

session_name: Session Three: When Your Career Has No Standard Answer: How to Build Your Market Value
session_content:
Guest Speaker: Yufan Xie (General Manager of BaiShilian | Alumnus from History Department)
Opening introduction of guests: Professor Chen Hihung, Chair of History Department
Highlights:

How should the competitiveness of self define in the AI age?
Mr. Xufan Xi, General Manager of BaiShilian, will share the true state of the talent market from a global industry perspective and return to his alma mater to share with his younger colleagues.
She wants to tell you: What matters in market buying is not your title, but your 'ability combination.'

This session will reframe four key dimensions of career:
Market value is not just a title but "a combination of exchangeable capabilities"
Cross-domain is not jumping haphazardly; it is intentional accumulation across domains.
Turn individuality into an asset rather than a weakness.
The key is not to find the right choice, but to understand the cost behind the choice.

activity_name_activity_session: National Taiwan University Coconut Grove Lectureship Leadership Series - President Liou Chun-chih of Academia Sinica
activity_content:
The Taichung University of Arts Coconut Lecture Series is a campus-wide seminar inviting scholars or industry leaders with outstanding achievements or significant international influence to give lectures and academic exchanges, in order to broaden the global vision of faculty members and students. This time, we are honored to have Dr. Ching-chi Liao, President of the Academia Sinica, delivering a lecture on campus. We welcome all faculty and students to participate enthusiastically.

Activity information:
Speaker: President Liao Junzhi of the Central研究院
Topic: Future Society: Viewing the Development of Science and Humanities Through Evolutionary Perspective
Time: March 6, 2026 (Friday) 15:30 – 17:00
Location: 2nd Floor Qian Siliang Memorial Lecture Hall (Integrated Grand Lecture Hall) of the Comprehensive Teaching Building in the Central Campus
Language: Chinese

Activity schedule:
14:50-15:30: Registration and入场
15:30-15:35: Opening Remarks
15:35-16:35: Keynote Speech
16:35-16:55: Q & A
16:55-17:00: Presenting awards and group photo on site
activity_name_event_page: National Taiwan University Coconut Grove Lectureship Leadership Series - President Liou Chun-chih of Academia Sinica
session_name: President Liou Chun-chih of the Central Research Institute
session_content:
Presentation Overview:
President Liao Junzhi served as the eleventh and twelfth president of the Academia Sinica, with a high reputation in academia. He was elected to membership in the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, the American Academy of Engineering, Academia Sinica, the International Association for the Development of Science (IADS), and an honorary foreign member of EMBO. He also received the Chevalier Order of the Legion of Honour from the French government, receiving international recognition for his academic achievements.
In terms of educational background, President Liao graduated from the Department of Chemical Engineering at National Taiwan University and obtained a Doctor of Engineering in Chemical Engineering from the University of Wisconsin-Madison in the United States. After that, he worked as an employee at the Life Sciences Laboratory of Eastman Kodak Company in New York; subsequently, he held teaching and research positions at Texas A&M University and the University of California, Los Angeles, accumulating extensive academic and research experience.
In his research field, President Liao has long been dedicated to foundational and forward-looking scientific research in metabolic system engineering, synthetic biology, systems biology, and microbial synthesis of biofuels. His achievements have had a profound impact on the development of related fields; his outstanding performance has also earned him multiple prestigious international research awards, including the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency’s Presidential Green Chemistry Challenge Award, the White House Renewable Energy Innovation Award, the President of Italy’s ENI Award for Renewable Energy, the National Academy of Sciences’ Industrial Applications Science Award in the United States, the Novozymes Outstanding Chemical and Bioprocess Engineering Award, the Samson Prize awarded by the Prime Minister of Israel, as well as multiple honors from the International Society for Metabolic Engineering such as the Greg Stephanopoulos Award.

activity_name_activity_session: 114-2 FLTC English Conversation
activity_content:
Want to improve your spoken English? Want to speak English fluently to express opinions?
Would you like to have opportunities to practice English outside of class and textbooks?
Do you want to make a partner who can help you practice speaking English?
Come join our 【English Chat】 at this center!

This activity involves trained small groups sharing English speaking tips and engaging in various oral practice activities, such as experience sharing, brainstorming, games, and role-playing, to help you speak English freely and comfortably in a relaxed and fun atmosphere.

This semester’s sessions have unique themes, where through ample practice during interactions with leaders and learning partners, speaking anxiety is reduced to naturally improve English expression and communication skills!

This activity has limited spots; please register only if you can attend.

Completion of registration indicates that you agree for the center to record or photograph activities, and those who complete the registration agree that the center may use the recorded videos or photos on the center’s website, electronic newsletters, social media platforms, video-sharing platforms, or any form of media. Those who do not agree should not register for this activity.
activity_name_event_page: 114-2 FLTC English Conversation
session_name: English version:\n\n[English Conversation] Fairy Tale Game - Pinocchio
session_content: In this session, we are going to warm up our speaking skills with a story you probably know well—Pinocchio. Instead of focusing on the plot, we will use this story to explore an interesting question and share our own opinions. Pinocchio is not just a fairy tale about a wooden boy; it raises ideas about honesty, choice, and what it really means to become "real." In this lesson, you will discuss, agree, disagree, and build on each other's ideas. There's no single right answer—what matters is how clearly and confidently you express your thoughts. Let's listen actively, speak bravely, and enjoy the conversation.

activity_name_event_page: 114-2 FLTC English Conversation
session_name: English Happy Hour: Unlocking Happiness
session_content: What does true happiness mean to you? In this class, we will explore different perspectives on happiness through the PERMA model from positive psychology. Instead of seeing happiness as only a moment of joy, we will learn that it can come from positive emotions, engagement, relationships, meaning, and accomplishment. Through interactive discussions and sharing activities, we will reflect on what brings happiness to our own lives and what would value the most. We will practice speaking while discovering new ways to think about happiness and well-being.

activity_name_event_page: 114-2 FLTC English Conversation
session_name: Once upon a time
session_content: Are you a fan of storytelling? In this activity, we will explore the joy of storytelling through a creative story relay activity. The lesson begins with a short introduction to the basic elements of a story, such as characters, setting, plot, and conflict, in order for us to understand how a complete story is built. We will then work in groups to develop characters and imagine different situations, gradually building our own story together. Through this process, we will use our imagination, practice spontaneous speaking, and learn how to connect ideas into a coherent narrative. The goal of the class is for us to enjoy the creative process of story writing! Here, not even the sky would be the limit!
