activity_name_activity_session: Cross-domain Combo! Semiconductor Cross-domain Learning Program Information Meeting
activity_content:
In response to the growing demand for semiconductor interdisciplinary talent internationally, our school has first launched the “Interdisciplinary Learning Pilot Program in Semiconductors,” which includes introductory learning (general education courses), advanced learning (professional courses, specialized skills and extended learning), and integrated learning (inter-university Bachelor’s degree in semiconductors). This program is suitable for students from various departments who are interested in the semiconductor field to design their own learning paths.

The seminar is specially invited by industry leaders and professors from relevant departments to support your exploration with confidence.

Want more information? Let the “Semiconductor Cross-Disciplinary Learning Program Information Meeting” be your starting point for realizing a cross-disciplinary dream!

In response to the growing global demand for cross-disciplinary semiconductor talents, NTU is launching its first Semiconductor Interdisciplinary Learning Initiative, featuring a structured learning framework that includes introductory studies (general education courses), advanced studies (discipline-specific courses, specialization programs, and extended learning), and integrative studies (a University Interdisciplinary Bachelor’s Program focusing on semiconductors). This initiative is designed for students from all departments who are interested in the semiconductor field and wish to design their own learning pathways.

Industry leaders and professors from related fields will join the briefing to share real-world experiences and help you explore with confidence.

For further information, join our info session and take the first step toward realizing your interdisciplinary aspirations.

activity_name_activity_session: The Nobel Prize Lecture Series - Dr. Song Congyuan's Top Research Lecture - Prof. May-Britt Moser (Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine laureate 2014)
activity_content:
Registration is full. Please watch the live stream on that day at: https://www.youtube.com/live/F89WJAr_Gp0
※Important: Please present your entry pass for check-in and registration between 13:30-13:50, thank you for your cooperation!

The "Song Kung-yuan Professorship of Outstanding Research" at our school was established with a generous donation from Mr. Song Kungyuan, the founder of Guangbao Group, starting in 2023 and will be jointly promoted by the Central研究院 with the International Peace Foundation (IPF) starting in 2025 to support the "Taiwan Bridge Project." This project involves collaboration between twelve domestic research institutions and the IPF. It aims to invite thirty-one Nobel Prize laureates to Taiwan for a series of lectures and exchanges from 2025 to 2026. We are inviting Professor May-Britt Moser, a recipient of the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, to deliver a lecture at this event. We warmly welcome all faculty and students to participate enthusiastically.

activity_name_event_page: The Nobel Prize Lecture Series - Dr. Song Congyuan's Top Research Lecture - Prof. May-Britt Moser (Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine laureate 2014)
session_name: Professor May-Britt Moser (2014 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine laureate)
session_content:
Speaker Introduction:
May-Britt Moser, a globally renowned neuroscientist and the 2014 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine recipient, discovered grid cells in the brain’s entorhinal cortex along with Edward Moss and John O’Keefe. This discovery revealed how the brain constructs spatial navigation and memory, providing key insights into memory deficits in conditions such as Alzheimer’s disease.
Professor Muséus is the director of the Center for Neural Computing at NTNU (Norges Teknisk-Naturvitenskapelige Universitet) and a co-director of the Kavli Institute for Systems Neuroscience, leading multidisciplinary teams that study the neural mechanisms of cognition from perspectives including neuroscience, behavioral science, computational models, and brain imaging. Her recent research explores how the brain integrates spatial, temporal, and memory information to form our overall perception of the world.
In addition to her outstanding research achievements, Professor Musser is highly praised for her passionate and inspiring speaking style. She excels at blending rigorous science with creative expression to inspire cross-disciplinary dialogue and inspiration, believing that the connection between science and humanities can promote true innovation. Her lecture will take listeners on a captivating journey of how a distinguished scientist integrates reason and imagination, exploring the mysteries of navigation and memory in the brain, and experiencing a prominent scientist's journey towards integrating rationality with creativity.

The "Song Kung-yuan Professorship of Outstanding Research" at our school was established with a generous donation from Mr. Song Kungyuan, the founder of Guangbao Group, starting in 2023 and jointly promoted by the Central研究院 with the International Peace Foundation (IPF) starting in 2025 to push forward the "Taiwan Bridge Project." session_name: Professor Morten P. Meldal (2022 Nobel Prize in Chemistry laureate)
session_content:
*Important: Please present your entry pass for check-in and registration between 13:30-13:50, thank you for your cooperation!*
*Important*: Participants are requested to check in from 13:30 to 13:50 using the Entry Pass. We appreciate your cooperation!

Speaker Introduction:
Professor Morten P. Meldal is currently the Head of the Center for Evolutionary Biology at the University of Copenhagen in Denmark. He is one of the key pioneers of the iconic chemical reaction—click chemistry—which he developed alongside K. Barry Sharpless and Carolyn R. Bertozzi, who shared the 2022 Nobel Prize in Chemistry with him.
Professor Meldal discovered that monovalent copper ions could catalyze the "three plus two" cycloaddition reaction between azides and alkynes to form stable 1,2,3-triazole rings (triazoles), known as a "click reaction." The click reaction can be carried out at room temperature in aqueous solutions with high speed and yield, exhibiting extremely high selectivity. It almost produces no by-products even when performed inside cells filled with various complex biological molecules. Due to its efficiency and selectivity, click chemistry has been widely applied for linking different molecular units, providing a new tool for drug development, bio-molecular labeling, and the synthesis of novel materials, significantly advancing contemporary fields such as biomedicine, chemistry, and material science.

session_content:
Performance Information

Time: January 23, 2026 (Fri) 12:30-13:10
Location | Yashengfang
Organizers | NTU Art Center
Registration link: https://reurl.cc/EbL33m

Caution事项

This activity is only available for free registration for NTU faculty and staff.
*Light meals will be provided for the activity. Resources are precious, please notify us promptly if you cannot attend after being selected to allow for a replacement from other colleagues or supervisors. Cancelling or missing out at short notice may affect your future participation in activities and please note this carefully.*

Performance Overview

The duo of Na Ja Music interprets Bach’s keyboard classics with delicate use of the dunce, where the hammers produce layered tones that transform intricate counterpoint structures into elegant dance rhythms. The two performers use the cymbal’s characteristic legato and ornamental techniques to make music flow like breath, reimagining works originally composed for keyboards as a new chapter filled with breathing sensations and dynamic tension.

This program will feature Bach's Keyboard Suite BWV827 and BWV830, as well as the French Suites BWV816, among others.

Performers

NanaFormosa Duo Percussion | Yuh-Ying Zhang and Ya-Hsin Cheng
Interpreter: Wu Keheng

Cast Members简介

Guide | Wu Jiaheng

Publisher, editor, translator, broadcaster, and performer. Hosts "Music Da Vinci" on Taitung Classical FM, Openbook's "Reading Ear," education radio's "Bookstore Looking to the Future," and "Classical Music Heart Vision." Also produces a podcast channel for Reading Republic at independent bookstores where he hosts salon music performances.

"Finding a Glint at the Corner," visiting over a hundred independent bookstores, "The World of Art Songs" was shortlisted for the 54th Golden Bell Awards (Education Program), and "Art Culture" for the 57th Golden Bell Awards.

Performer | Zhang Yuer Ying

Percussionist, graduated from the National Conservatory of Music in Lyon, France and the Karlsruhe Conservatory of Music in Germany. During his time in Europe, he won a bronze medal at the International Piobmire Competition by Claude Giot in France, a bronze medal at the International Duo Percussion Competition in Luxembourg, and a third prize at the 64th International Geneva Music Competition for Solo Percussion.

Since returning to Taiwan, That Na Duo has been active on the global music stage under the main project of That Na Duo, and has participated in cross-disciplinary productions across various types such as dance, theater, film music, and video. She has released albums titled "Mingguang" and "Huiyue Heart," which were nominated for the 31st Chuanti Music Award; she also recorded guest parts for World Routes Band's album "Multipolar Spread" and played in Yuyin Fourteen's album "Ji Guang Pianyu." She is currently兼任Guangdong Normal University Department of Music, National Jiayou University Western Instrument Department, and Nantou Applied Science University Department of Music as an assistant professor; she is also a Yamaha My Music collaborator and ISCM-Taiwan member.

Performer | Cheng Yaixin

Percussionist, graduated from the National Conservatory of Music in Lyon, France. Currently兼任professor at National Taiwan Normal University, Nantou Art University, and Tsinghua University’s music departments, a Yamaha My Music collaborator, and ISCM-Taiwan member. In 2005, he won third place in the International Iron Piping Competition held by Claude Giot in France; in 2009, he won third place in the International Duo Percussion Competition held in Luxembourg; and in 2010, his solo concert was nominated for the New Form Art Awards. As a member of NaNa Percussion Duo, they have performed across Europe, Asia, and America; their repertoire spans classical and contemporary music, experimental theater, visual art, soundscapes, and dance compositions. They have participated in cross-disciplinary albums such as "The Thousand Jade Lute," "Mountain and Night Swallow," "Multiplication Spread," and "Ji Guang Fragment." Their跨界works include composing the soundtrack for Hou Hsiao-hui’s film "Niao Yin Niu" with traditional Chinese dance, creating soundscapes for visual artist Su Yu-xian's documentary "Gypsum Drum," sound design for Tsinghua University’s permanent exhibition “Tataka’s Memories,” and percussion design for Nan Art Theater’s performance of Feng Jing’s choreography "Courier." Since 2011, he has been a regular member of the Tuning Gong Sinfonia Ensemble as its principal timpanist.

session_name: "Art and Culture Elective Course for Teachers: Ronglong Manufacturing: A List of Supplies for a Son"
session_content:
Content of the performance

My dad disconnected from this world at the end of 2021.
I am like a homing pigeon, starting out to seek out its story.
Find the map that leads to my dad.

After the burst of the economic miracle’s bubble, a father started running away due to debt, and his son embarked on a journey to find him to handle his father's debt issues. Following his father’s trail, what his son found were not his father but pieces of his father’s life.

This is a demonstration of an Object Theatre performance and also a journey of an actor putting together objects to resemble a father.

[About Creators/GROUP]

Flying People Collective Theater Group

The Flying Athlete Collective was established in May 2004, with Stone Pei-Yu as its director. "Flying Athlete" is a phonetic pun for "Non-human," and also refers to an ensemble of artistic creators who roam freely throughout the theater. The production specifications are set at "creating/producing performance art works with a spirit of multiplicity and fusion, while challenging traditional viewing distances." The Flying Athlete pushes the definition of puppets to its extreme, hoping to convey theatrical emotions through "operating" and "utilizing" lifeless objects (including puppets), thereby achieving the realm of "no object is too small to be a puppet."

Main Creator | Hong Jianzang

Graduated from Drama Department of National Taiwan University.
Continuing to collaborate with Taiwanese creative theater groups, trying various performance forms. Recent representative works include: "The Princess is Thankful" from the Poor Theater, "House Under Water" from Tainan People's Theater, "Style Haze" from Style Haze, "Say No Again" from Rejection Theater, "The Storehouse of Comfortable Goods" from Guotuo Theater, and "The Roundabout Story" from Performance Workshops.
Also dedicated to exploring the performative relationships between puppetry, objects, and people, having studied with multiple international puppet artists, and practicing them in his/her own创作. Some of the experiences include:

2023 New New Seven Stars: The Quest for Dragon Without Regret Play Production Directors and Performers
2025 Taipei Children's Art Festival "Wandering Fish"

Co-creation | Lin Yingpin

Theatre practitioner and artistic director of Teahupoo, a theatre series. Graduated from the Department of Drama and the Department of Sociology at National Taiwan University. With an emphasis on social issues as the center, different forms of theatrical practices as the radius, aiming to transform societal topics that are difficult for ordinary people to access through the lens of theatre.

activity_name_activity_session: 2026 Occupational Health Lecture (1): Key Moments of Aging Health: Preventive Guide for Herpes Zoster ("Chickenpox") "Lunch Boxes Provided for Participants Who Attend the Onsite Lecture on This Day"
activity_content:
This course runs in both live and online streaming formats.
Meals provided for participants of on-site lectures on the day.
Abstract
Have you heard the saying that if a herpes zoster (also known as the chickenpox – shingles virus) appears on one’s belly, they will die? Herpes zoster is commonly called “snake rash.” It is a disease caused by the varicella-zoster virus (VZV). According to data from the Bureau of Disease Control under the Ministry of Health and Welfare in Taiwan, about 1/3 of Taiwanese people will suffer at least one episode of herpes zoster in their lifetime. In recent years, there has been an increasing trend towards younger individuals developing herpes zoster. Stress and sleep problems also increase the risk of developing herpes zoster.
Herpes zoster can cause severe pain during an acute outbreak, and post-herpetic neuralgia may last for several months to years. If it affects the area around the eyes, it could lead to blindness! There is also a bidirectional relationship with cardiovascular disease risk factors, and the proportion of people in our country with three or more chronic conditions (the “triple-highs”) is quite high. Therefore, those with multiple chronic conditions should not underestimate its impact!
The lecture will explain who is at risk for shingles and how to prevent it, helping people reduce their future risk of infection.

Location of activities: Sustainability Hall, Huiyan Building (No. 71 Fanglan Road) & Microsoft Teams online video conference livestreaming

session_name: Professor Donna Struck (2018 Nobel Prize in Physics winner)
session_content: Speaker Introduction:\nProfessor Donna Strickland is currently a professor in the Physics and Astronomy Department at the University of Waterloo, Canada. She has been dedicated to experimental research in ultrafast lasers and nonlinear optics for many years, as well as physics education and science promotion. Strickland was one of the recipients of the 2018 Nobel Prize in Physics, along with Arthur Ashkin and Gerard Mourou. She is one of only three female Nobel laureates in physics over a century-long history. Her academic achievements and educational spirit continue to inspire young people worldwide to ask questions and explore unknown territories.\nDeveloping chirped pulse amplification (CPA) technology with Professor Mourou resulted in breakthroughs that surpassed the physical limitations of high-power ultrashort-pulse lasers during their amplification process, significantly boosting laser peak power. This foundational technology for ultrafast lasers and strong-field physics has wide-ranging applications in precision material processing, semiconductor manufacturing, nanoscience, LASIK vision correction, and medical treatments. Its impact on modern technology and human life is profound.

For further information, join us at the Information Session on the University Interdisciplinary Bachelor’s Program – Semiconductor Interdisciplinary Initiative—and take the first step toward realizing your interdisciplinary aspirations.

activity_content:
This session consists of two parts.
Date: December 19th, 2014 (Friday)
First session 10:00-11:00 / Professor Cao Shujuan from the Chinese Department: "Guanting and Book Academy – Social Practice of Tang and Song Literati"
Second session: 11:20-12:20 / Professor Lan Pe-jia from the Sociology Department: "Transnational Taiwan as a Method: Researching Migrant and Immigrant Families and Diverse Cultures"
activity_name_event_page: 114th Fu Ssu-yen Lecture: Prof. Lu Peijia from the Department of Sociology and Prof. Kao Shujuan from the Department of Chinese Literature
session_name: 114th Fu Ssu-yuen Lecture Series: (First Session) Professor Cao Shujuan from the Chinese Department: Guanting and书院—the social practice of Tang-Song literati
session_content:
Date: December 19th, 2014 (Friday) 10:00-11:00
Topic: Guanting and Book Institutes – Social Practice of Tang and Song Scholars
Speaker: Professor Cao Shujuan (Chinese Department)
Speech introduction:
In the lecture "Guanting and书院—Social Practice of Tang and Song Scholars," Professor Luo will begin by discussing "Building Homes" to explore how people respond to their living circumstances through space: from meeting basic needs, to pursuing stable lives and social connections, to finding beauty and value ideals, thereby forming deep yet fluid relationships with spaces. This relationship is partly rooted in past experiences of interacting with people, land, time, and objects, shaping individuals' perspectives on the future; at the same time, humans always retain the freedom to break through existing limitations, re-understand themselves, and plan their lives anew.
Through a detailed analysis of “Guanting” and “Shuyuan,” Professor Luo will lead the audience in understanding how Tang and Song literati manifested their social roles, cultural ideals, and spiritual practices within specific spaces.

session_name: 114th Fu Ssu-nien Lecture Series: (Second Session) Professor Lan Pe-chieh from the Department of Sociology: Transnational Taiwan as a Method: Researching Migrant and Multi-cultural Families
session_content:
Second round
Date: December 19, 2011 (Friday) 11:20-12:20
Topic: Cross-border Taiwan as a method: Researching migrant and immigrant families and multiculturalism
Speaker: Professor Lan Peigia (Sociology Department)
This lecture explores how "cross-border Taiwan" can be used as a methodological approach to studying Taiwanese society from a cross-border scale. In the age of frequent capital, labor, and cultural information flows, Taiwan cannot be studied as a closed geographical or political unit but is constantly reconstituted through transnational linkages. I will use my research on migrant workers, second-generation individuals, global childcare, and multiculturalism as examples to illustrate how strategies such as "transnational Taiwan from above" (top-down), "transnational Taiwan from below" (bottom-up), and "transnational relational analysis" can reveal the profound impact of cross-border Taiwan on national policies, cultural identities, and social inequalities.

activity_name_activity_session: The Nobel Prize Lecture Series - Dr. Song Congyuan's Top Research Lecture - Prof. Eric S. Maskin (Nobel Prize in Economics laureate 2007)
activity_content:
The "Song Kung-yuan Professorship of Outstanding Research" at our school was established with a generous donation from Mr. Song Kungyuan, the founder of Guangbao Group, starting in 2023. Starting in 2025, it will jointly promote the “Taiwan Bridge Project” with the Academia Sinica. It will collaborate with twelve domestic research institutions and the International Peace Foundation (IPF) to invite thirty-one Nobel Prize laureates to Taiwan over the period of 2025-2026 for series of lectures and exchanges. This session invites Professor Eric S. Maskin, a recipient of the Nobel Economics Prize, to deliver a lecture at our school. We warmly welcome all faculty and students to participate enthusiastically.

Activity information:
Speaker: Prof. Eric S. Maskin (2007 Nobel Prize in Economics laureate)
Topic: "Why globalization has failed to reduce inequality"
Time: December 15, 2025 (Monday) 14:00 - 15:40
Language: English

Speaker Introduction:
Eric Maskin is a professor of the Adams Chair at Harvard University, where he also holds an appointment as a Professor of Economics and Mathematics. He earned his bachelor’s and doctoral degrees from Harvard University and served as a postdoctoral researcher at Jesus College, Cambridge University in the UK. He is also an honorary member of the National Academy of Sciences, the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, and the Royal Society of London. In 2007, Professor Maskin was jointly awarded the Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences along with Leonid Hurwicz and Roger Myerson for their pioneering work in mechanism design theory (mechanism design theory). Professor Maskin’s research spans game theory, contract theory, social choice theory, political economy, and income distribution. His contributions have had a profound impact on modern economic theory and policy design.
In this lecture, Professor Maskin will explore the nexus between globalization and inequality based on his deep theoretical foundation and global perspective. The lecture will analyze how globalization affects economic opportunities for different countries and groups from an economic theory and practical policy standpoint, and consider the key roles that institutions and policies may play in promoting more inclusive and equitable development.

activity_name_event_page: The Nobel Prize Lecture Series - Dr. Song Congyuan's Top Research Lecture - Prof. Eric S. Maskin (Nobel Prize in Economics laureate 2007)
session_name: Eric S. Maskin (2007 Nobel Prize in Economics laureate)
session_content: Introduction of Speaker:\nEric Maskin is the Adams University Professor and Professor of Economics and Mathematics at Harvard University. He received his A.B. and Ph.D. in Mathematics from Harvard and was a postdoctoral fellow at Jesus College, Cambridge University. Before rejoining the Harvard faculty in 2012, he taught at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (1977–1984), Harvard (1985–2000), and the Institute for Advanced Study in Princeton (2000–2011). In 2007, Professor Maskin was awarded the Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences, together with Leonid Hurwicz and Roger Myerson, for their pioneering work in laying the foundations of mechanism design theory. He is a member of the U.S. National Academy of Sciences, the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, and a Foreign Member of the Royal Society. Professor Maskin’s research spans game theory, contract theory, social choice theory, political economy, and income distribution, and his work has profoundly shaped modern economic theory and policy design. In his upcoming lecture, “Why Globalization Has Failed to Reduce Inequality,” Professor Maskin will draw on his strong theoretical foundation and global perspective to explore the relationship between globalization and inequality. The talk will examine how globalization influences the distribution of economic opportunities across countries and individuals, and reflect on the potential role of institutions and policies in promoting more inclusive and equitable development.

activity_content:

activity_name_event_page: College of Music Concert "Sounds from Ganges to Irrawaddy"
session_name: College of Music Concert "Sounds from Ganges to Irrawaddy"
session_content:
Program Introduction

This concert explores the cultural context of the Ganges in India to the Irrawaddy in Myanmar, presenting rich and diverse musical traditions from South and Southeast Asia. It brings together students and faculty from "North Indian Tabla Music Culture and Practice" and "Myanmar Music Culture and Practice," showcasing a soundscape that crosses geographical and cultural boundaries.

The first half features a performance guided by renowned tabla player Abhiman Kaushal, showcasing intricate interpretations of teentaal and jhaptaal and other rhythmic cycles that highlight the laws, variations, and improvisations in North Indian percussion. The second half is led by Professor Lü Xinquan from his course "Burma Music Culture and Practice," featuring classic pieces such as ritual dances, lamp-lighting dance, and water splashing dance, combined with performances of music and dance to present a vivid and delicate aesthetic of Burmese traditional music and dance.

From the Ganges to the Irrawaddy, this concert links cultures through sound, inviting audiences on a musical journey spanning South and Southeast Asia.

Advisor
Lü Xinquán
Professor and Director of the Department of Musicology at National Taiwan University has conducted ethnographic research in Myanmar for many years and is the author of "Gilded and Unfading Splendor: The Re-enactment and Modernity of Myanmar’s Classical Musical Traditions" (2012). Since setting up a Burmese Dance course in 2017, he has organized the Kyagan Dance Ensemble to promote traditional Burmese dance.

Abhiman Kaushal
An outstanding exponent of tabla. Belonging to an illustrious lineage, he was initiated by his father, R B Kaushal, who was a disciple of the legendary Ustad Amir Hussain Khan. Later Abhiman became a direct disciple of highly revered Ustad Shaik Dawood and Pandit B Nandkumar from Hyderabad, India.

Abhiman has performed with many of India's classical music's prominent global artists. He has numerous recordings that have received high acclaim.

Topic: "Is Dijkstra’s Algorithm Optimal? Solving a 70-Year Mystery with a Turing Award Winner"
Time: December 12, 2025 (Friday) 10:00 – 11:30
Location: 2nd Floor Qian Siliang Memorial Lecture Hall (Integrated Grand Lecture Hall) of the Comprehensive Teaching Building in the Central Campus
Language: English

Activity schedule:
09:30-10:00: Check-in and entry open
Opening remarks: 10:00-10:05
10:05-11:05: Keynote Speech
11:05-11:25: Q&A交流
11:25-11:30: Presenting awards and group photo on site

Presentation Overview
Robert Tarjan is a James S. McDonnell Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at Princeton University, where he has held appointments at Cornell University, the University of California, Berkeley, Stanford University, and New York University. He is also an elected member of the National Academy of Sciences, the National Academy of Engineering, the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, and the American Philosophical Society. He has held research positions at Bell Labs, NEC, HP, Microsoft, and Intertrust Technologies. He invented or co-invented many of today’s highest-performing data structures and graph algorithms, having received the first International Mathematical Union Nevanlinna Prize (1982), along with John Hopcroft for receiving the Turing Award (1986), and with Daniel Sleator for inventing splay trees, he won the Paris Kanellakis Theory and Practice Award (1999).
In the field of computer science, Dijkstra's algorithm is a classic algorithm used for path planning. Given an initial position, it uses a greedy method to find the shortest paths from that point to all other reachable positions and then orders these shortest paths by increasing length. This naturally leads to the question: Is this algorithm already the best and most efficient? The answer depends on the nature of the problem. In this lecture, we will introduce our latest research findings, which give a "yes" answer; and briefly discuss other researchers' work, why it gives a "no," presenting different perspectives based on various problem settings.

activity_name_activity_session: Engineering Museum - "Builder of Mountains: A Century's Stake" Screening and Post-Show Discussion Meeting Activity
activity_content:
"Mountain Maker – A Century’s Stake” records the development of Taiwan's semiconductor industry with a macroscopic view and delicate brushstrokes, showcasing technological innovations, labor scenes, and national narratives from RCA to Hsinchu Science Park to TSMC. In the warm month of December, the Engineering Museum invites students and faculty members to watch the film together, participating in this compulsory course for Taiwanese people.

Post-movie discussion invites producer Jiang Xianbin, President Xu Jinru of Political and Economic Studies, Professor Huang Lingming of Science in History, and industry贵宾 to discuss behind-the-scenes humanistic social and geo-political issues related to technological development. To rethink the relationship between universities, industries, and society, and reflect on how Taiwan can continue to protect its mountains while creating new ones in the future.

activity_name_activity_session: English Speaking Reinforcement Workshop
activity_content:
Feeling that the time for the school's oral English speaking activities is always too short and you can't express yourself freely? Do you hope for a space with more interactive and friendly atmosphere to practice speaking?

[Intensive English Speaking Workshop Series] are conducted in small groups, led by Professor Zhuang Shiquan from the Foreign Languages Department of National Taiwan University. Each session lasts for two classes and guides deep discussions on various topics to help students improve their speaking skills in academic settings and enhance their critical thinking abilities. Whether you want to refine your English expression or strengthen your communication skills, this workshop is expected to bring plenty of benefits to you. We welcome interested students to register and participate!

Only a limited number of people are allowed per session; please seize the opportunity to register; if there is still space available before the event, other categories will be open for registration.
Those who have registered but cannot attend are requested to cancel their registration as soon as possible; failure to do so will result in the organizing unit retaining the right to exclude them from other sessions.
Anyone who completes the online registration for this activity agrees to allow the organizing unit to record or photograph the event and use the video or photos on the official website, electronic newsletters, social media platforms, video-sharing platforms, or any form of media. Those who do not agree should not participate in this activity.
This activity applies to the Double Language Center's "Stamp Activity for the English Learning Passport." Please scan the QR code at the end of each session to complete stamp collection. No make-up distribution will be provided after the activity ends.
activity_name_event_page: English Speaking Reinforcement Workshop
session_name: AI and Ethics: AI Chatbots versus Human Therapists
session_content:
Have you ever used AI to help you find information, only to end up talking about your frustrations?
When AI's "human touch" and emotional support capabilities become increasingly close to that of a human, are the boundaries between tools and relationships beginning to blur?

According to the survey, more and more people are starting to view AI as a therapist for mental health, seeking advice on how to relieve stress or vent some secrets that they cannot share with anyone else. True counseling is often expensive, so is it a great achievement of technological development that one can talk to an AI for mental health issues, or does it lead to even greater social isolation? Let's explore this topic together in English.

session_name: Cosmetic Surgery and Ethics: Teen’s Death Stirs Anger in Mexico
session_content:
In an era of numerous beauty clinics, do we truly understand the hidden dangers within?
Could the death of a desperate teenage girl who wants to be beautiful bring about any change?

A grieving father only discovered his 14-year-old daughter had undergone plastic surgery at her funeral. He angrily stated that he had never agreed to it and believed the cosmetic procedure was what caused his daughter's death. So, can minors under the age of 18 undergo plastic surgeries? Is parental consent enough for everything to be fine? Let's discuss this issue together in English.

activity_content:
The training session led by Ms. Lucy Lü, a lawyer from the National Anti-Bullying Training Talent Bank of China's Ministry of Education, introduces the perspective of restorative justice into the guidelines and cases for preventing school bullying, providing frontline teachers with thinking and handling methods to solve campus conflicts, bringing a warm approach to resolving disputes into schools.
Key points of the lecture include:
One common issue and conflict in schools: Recognizing不当管教and school bullying.
Second, what is restorative justice: not just admitting guilt and taking responsibility, but also repairing relationships.
activity_name_event_page: Academic Affairs Campus Safety Center X Bullying Prevention Committee for Mental Health Promotion Series of Activities (Central Campus)
session_name: How to effectively use mediation systems to handle school student bullying incidents
session_content:
Match Information:
Open for entry at 14:40
17:00-17:30 QA Time (Questions are welcome, but no questions? You may leave.)
Departure Dinner Preparation
Schools are the venues for providing an educational environment for teachers and students. Considering that bullying incidents primarily revolve around interpersonal relationships, a student-on-student bullying incident requires flexibility to provide students with space to make mistakes and learn. Given this, the Ministry of Education has established a new "conciliation procedure" in the "Guidelines on Preventing School Bullying," offering schools and parents another choice for handling bullying events beyond the investigation process. The conciliation procedure focuses on respecting the parties' wishes, conducting interviews between committee members and the parties involved only with their consent. It provides an unbiased, empathetic, and warm environment where both sides can have a dialogue. Through communication, students can understand reasons behind actions and the harm caused, discuss ways to compensate, ultimately aiming for conflict resolution and relationship repair.

activity_name_activity_session: 2025 Occupational Health Lecture (9): Going Against the Flow: Prevention and Screening for Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease
activity_content:
Abstract
Do you often feel a burning sensation in your chest, throat, or wake up at night feeling "acidic"? These are actually warning signs of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD)! This lecture will explain to you in simple terms why stomach acid might "flow" back and what habits can make the situation worse. The content includes common symptoms and causes, how to check if it's really refluxing, daily prevention tips (such as eating smaller meals, not lying down after meals, reducing coffee and fried foods), and commonly used medications and treatment methods. We will also review common bad habits among office workers and learn how to protect your digestion in both work and life so you can live each day comfortably and easily.

Activity time: November 27, 2025 (Thu) from 9:30 AM to 11:30 AM

Location: Live Microsoft Teams online video conference broadcast

This activity will be conducted through a Microsoft Teams online video livestream. Please prepare your own computer or mobile device to use, and participants who complete online check-in and participate in the entire lecture will receive 2 hours of digital learning credits for faculty and staff members of our school.

activity_name_activity_session: Basic Life Support (BLS) training for days 114/11/26, 27
activity_content:
Office of Student Affairs Health Center BLS training in November
One, this center was established to enhance the emergency handling capabilities of faculty and staff at three affiliated universities of National Taiwan University for dealing with unexpected accidents. Specialized in-house training on "Basic Life Support" (BLS) has been organized, featuring physicians from the Emergency Medicine Department of Taichung Area Hospital, senior rescue technicians, physical therapists from Taichung Area Hospital, as well as colleagues from this center. The training aims to organize practical exercises to cultivate students' ability to handle crises in real-life situations, thereby ensuring campus and club activity safety.
Two, Date: November 26 and 27th, (November 26th from 17:15 to 21:50, November 27th from 17:45 to 21:15).
Three: Target audience: Employees and students of three affiliated universities under National Taiwan University system, with 60 spots filled.
Four、 Location: 2F Wellness Classroom in Health Center.
Five: Content: Trauma emergency, casualty搬运techniques internship (carried out in groups), Hemostasis bandaging techniques internship.
CPR training, CPR technical practice (in groups), and practical exam, written
Test.
Six、 Registration Method: Please register through the school activity registration system.
To obtain a certificate for Basic First Aid, one must participate in the full course, pass the written test, technical assessment, submit pre-course work, and complete payment. Public servants are also eligible for an 8-hour lifetime learning credit.
Eight, Certificate Printing Fees Explanation:
The "certificate" for this course is optional and if you need one, you must bear the cost of NT$ per certificate.
100 yuan (production cost of Taiwan Sports Safety and First Aid Skills Promotion Association), and upon class commencement on that day.
Inform the staff and pay for fees when checking in.
Contact Information and Enrollment Eligibility:
Please make sure to provide your valid Taiwan mobile phone number when registering so that you can receive course notifications and contact information. If not provided,
Taiwan mobile phone numbers will be considered as abandoning the right to attend classes (for special circumstances, please contact承办02-)
panhx@ntu.edu.tw)
Tenth, welcome on-site registration: [Open on-site registration after actual situation of the students checking in at the site. The course will be open for registration beyond 15 minutes. Registration is capped at 60 people and closes upon reaching capacity.]

activity_name_activity_session: 114 Academic Year (76th Batch) School-wide Sports Meet - Track and Field Competition – Team Competitions (relay race, tug of war)
activity_content:
November 21-22, 114 years.
Location: School Central Area Athletics Field.
Three, registration method -- please have each team designate a person to log in online for registration. To avoid duplicate registrations, a limited registration method will be adopted. Detailed information must be filled out and submitted after which it will be reviewed by the organizing unit before completing the registration procedures.
Four, competition categories and participating units:
Male students group: By each department or institute.
Female students' group: By each department or institute.
Faculty and Staff Group: By department, office, group, school, or institute (department).
Alumni and Friends of National Taiwan University: Alumni are former students of the university, while Friends of National Taiwan University are part-time learners from the Outreach Promotion Department.
Five, each group competition projects:
Male Students Group: 2400 Meter Relay (12 people), Rope Pulling Event: 800 kg weight class (20 people).
Girls' team: 1200-meter relay (12 people), tug of war event: men's category up to 700 kg (20 people).
Staff Team: Mixed 1200-meter relay (12 people, mixed gender team with at least 4 of the same gender), Rope Pulling event: 900 kg weight class (at least 4 of the same gender).
Alumni and Taipei Medical University Friends Team: Mixed Relay Squad of 1200 meters (12 people, with at least 4 females).
activity_name_event_page: 114 Academic Year (76th Batch) School-wide Sports Meet - Track and Field Competition – Team Competitions (relay race, tug of war)
session_name: Male student team 2400-meter relay队
session_content:
One,参赛队伍以系或研究所为单位，参赛队员限本系（所）学生组队参加。
Rules of the competition:
Men's student relay team running 2400 meters: Each leg runs 200 meters, with a total of 12 runners. The number of alternates is at most 6 people. Time trials and finals are calculated based on the number of registered teams.
Female students 1200-meter relay team: Each leg runs 100 meters, with a total of 12 people. The number of alternates is at most 6. Time trials and finals are calculated based on the registered teams.
Mixed 1200-meter relay team for teachers, alumni, and Tsinghua friends: Each leg is 100 meters long, with a total of 12 people (at least 4 of the same gender), and the number of alternates can be up to 6. Individual times are calculated separately; each group's performance will be determined based on the number of registered teams using pre- and final round times.
No spiked shoes shall be worn during the competition and one must wear flat运动 shoes.
Each team must gather at the check-in area 30 minutes before each match to present their student ID, staff card, or trainee card for check-in (to collect their number shirt which they should wear during the match and return within 3 minutes after completing that match), failure to do so will be considered as forfeiting. Those not on the registration list are not allowed to participate in the competition.
No limit on the number of teams for each unit, and if fewer than 6 teams register, the project will be canceled (except for faculty/staff/alumni and Tsinghua friends groups).

activity_content:

Location: School Central Area Athletics Field.
Three, registration method -- please have each team designate a person to log in online for registration. To avoid duplicate registrations, a limited registration method will be adopted. Five, each group competition projects:
Male Students Group: 2400 Meter Relay (12 people), Rope Pulling Event: 800 kg weight class (20 people).

activity_name_event_page: 114 Academic Year (76th Batch) School-wide Sports Meet - Track and Field Competition – Team Competitions (relay race, tug of war)
session_name: Male student team – 800 kg weight class tug of war
session_content:
Male student group (800 kg category): The total weight of all participants shall not exceed 800 kilograms (inclusive of 800 kilograms).
700 kg weight class for female students: The total weight of all athletes shall not exceed 700 kg (inclusive of 700 kg).
Staff weight class (mixed team of male and female athletes, with at least 4 participants of the same gender): The total weight of all athletes shall not exceed 900 kilograms (inclusive).
All tug-of-war events in each group have no limit on the number of participants, with a maximum of 20 people registering.
The preliminary schedules for each team are determined by a draw conducted by the Competition Committee and published on the sports room website and the notice board of the school central sports hall.
Qualifiers and repechages were held on either side of the sports hall. After the repechages, 8 teams from each gender group participated in the finals during the games.
Each team gathers at the check-in area 30 minutes before the start of the competition to go through check-in procedures.
Check-in process: Check-in at check-in desk → Weighing → Register weight → Stamp and write down the number for confirmation → Start the competition.
To accommodate the large crowds at weigh-in on competition day, athletes can complete the weigh-in process in advance. Athletes who wish to pre-weigh-in should bring their student ID and conduct the weigh-in from 9 AM to 5:30 PM on November 5th and 6th at the Conference Room on the 2nd Floor of the Sports Room in the Comprehensive Sports Center.
Each team arrives at the designated venue for the scheduled time, reports to register, and is then split into two lines facing each other along the sideline of the tug-of-war rope. Participants with insufficient numbers are allowed to participate based on the number of attendees present.
On either side of the center line in the tug-of-war field, one meter away from each side, a决胜line is drawn. A red cloth banner is tied at the center of the tug-of-war rope to serve as a basis for determining the winner.
When the referee gives the "Ready" command, both teams of players grip the rope behind the决胜line. When the referee gives the "Go" command (either by blowing a whistle or horn), all pull together on the rope to try and pull the red flag marking the center of the rope over their own决胜line within one minute. The team that succeeds in doing so wins.
The competition uses a single-elimination format, with each match consisting of three sets played under a best-of-three format. Each set lasts one minute, with a two-minute break between sets.
In any game where a team has not touched its own winning line within one minute of the end of the match, the side marked by a red ribbon beyond the center line wins.
Before each match begins, a draw determines the场地, and in the second game, they should swap场地s. If a third game is needed to determine the winner, a new draw will be conducted to select the场地s before playing the third game.
16. In addition to the参赛选手, each team is set with a captain and a coach. Except for the参赛选手, the captain, and the coach who can hold flags to direct the entire team, no one else shall enter the competition area. After the start of the game, except in cases of special factors (injury to the player, emergency accident), personnel changes are only allowed upon the judgment of the referee; during halftime, personnel changes for any reason are not permitted. The weight change of the replaced personnel must not exceed the total weight limit of that group. Violators will be disqualified from the competition.
Athletes must compete in sports attire and flat sports shoes, and they are not allowed to wear gloves during the competition.
If it rains, the competition will be held inside at the 1F of the sports hall.
No limit on the number of teams that can register; if fewer than 6 teams sign up, cancel the competition for this event (except for faculty and staff category).
Registration teams exceeding 8 will proceed to the preliminary round (noon on November 13 and 14), with the top 4 teams in each group advancing to the final round (November 21-22).

activity_name_event_page: 114 Academic Year (76th) School-wide Sports Meet - Track and Field Competition -- Fun Competitions
session_name: Entertainment Competition Student Category - One Throw Wins
session_content:
Number of participants: 12 per team (mixed teams, with at least 4 members of the same gender).
Equipment: Slippers, traffic cones, frisbee.
Distance of the race: 10 meters.
Race time: 180 seconds
Racing Method and Rules:
Each team arranges its players in pairs and determines the batting order on its own. After a gun is fired, the比赛 begins.
20
Each team must advance to the 10-meter turnaround point, and after both members reach the turnaround point, each picks up a shoe placed at the turnaround定点 within the area. They stand up and throw the shoes upwards simultaneously; the throwing height should be above their head. If the result is in an alternating pattern (one person throws left, another right), they can place the shoes back into the designated area and turn around to return to the starting line. The last team member who crosses the starting line can then start the next team.
In the competition, if a group of contestants toss five times in a row without getting one head and one tail, they can choose to return their slippers to the starting point and rotate to the next group of contestants for the competition.
The race results are determined by stopping the clock as the last competitor crosses the finish line completely. The winner is based on the shortest time.
Ranking is calculated based on completion time; if there are ties among the top three in final times, they are ranked equally.
organizer_unit: 共同教育中心體育室

session_name: Fun Competition - Student Category - Battle Spinners
session_content:
Number: 12 per team (mixed teams, at least 4 members of the same gender)
Equipment: Traffic cones, soccer balls.
Distance of the race: 10 meters.
Race time: 180 seconds.
Racing Method and Rules:
Each group of three serves as a rotation unit and assigns their own order. They hold hands to form a circle, with the rugby ball placed inside the circle.
After the gun goes off, the competition begins where three people work together to kick the rugby ball towards a turnaround point while keeping the rugby ball within the circle.
After traveling in that manner from the starting point to the turnaround point, circling around the cone and back to the starting point, passing the橄榄球to the next group of runners in sequence until the last runner.
During the game, if a referee judges that an橄榄球 is outside of the three-person circle, it is considered a foul and must be returned to the original location.
Ranking is calculated based on completion time; if there are ties among the top three in final times, they are ranked equally.

activity_content:
November 21-22, 2014
Location: School Central Area Athletics Field
Individuals: Please register online and complete the registration procedures yourself. Schoolmates are务必use their school email address.
Fourth: Individual Competition Projects for Female Students' Team in Personal Competitions
Track events: high jump, long jump, shot put (8.8 pounds), discus throw (1 kg), javelin throw (0.6 kg).
Sprint: 100 meters, 200 meters, 400 meters, 800 meters, 1500 meters, 5000 meters.
activity_limit: 本活動限制一個人可報名 2 個場次
activity_name_event_page: 114 Academic Year (76th Batch) School-wide Sports Meeting - Female Students' Track and Field Competition
session_name: Female student team - 5000 meters
session_content:
Track and Field Competition Rules:
Adopt the latest athletics rules set by the Chinese Athletic Association of the Republic of China.
In track and field events other than male and female 100-meter races which use preliminary and final rounds, all other events use timed finals.
The race groups are allocated through on-site draw.
Track and field events shall be conducted according to racing rules.
No limit on the number of teams to participate, except for track and field events (including relay races), if the number of participants per team does not reach 6 people, the event will be canceled (except for the Faculty Staff Group, Alumni & NTU Friends Group).
Track and field events allow each unit unlimited number of participants per event (including relay and tug-of-war), with each athlete limited to registering for a maximum of two events (excluding relay and team events).
In cases where no more than 8 contestants arrive for check-in and registration during the preliminary round, they will be combined into a single competition.
When the number of registrants exceeds the stipulated amount, the conference will select the top six contestants based on the registration list.
organizer_unit: 共同教育中心體育室
note: 1.本學期若修習體育課，請填寫體育課授課老師姓名及上課班次。\n2.本人於報名時均清楚瞭解且願意遵守同意書內容，同意完成線上報名即視同親自簽署切結書。https://pe.ntu.edu.tw/#/news/3616/news (切結書內容請參閱本連結內之競賽規程第14頁)

November 21-22, 2014
Location: School Central Area Athletics Field
Individuals: Please register online and complete the registration procedures yourself. Schoolmates are务必use their school email address.
Fourth: Individual Competition Projects for Staff Group A in Teacher Training Personnel Competition
Men's events: 100m, 800m, 5000m, shot put, long jump.
Women's events: 60m, 800m, 5000m, shot put, long jump.
activity_limit: 本活動限制一個人可報名 2 個場次
activity_name_event_page: 114 Academic Year (76th Batch) School-wide Sports Meeting - Track and Field Competition for Teachers and Staff (Men's Group)
session_name: Staff Men's 100m
session_content:
Track and Field Competition Rules:
Adopt the latest athletics rules set by the Chinese Athletic Association of the Republic of China.
In track and field events other than male and female 100-meter races which use preliminary and final rounds, all other events use timed finals.
The race groups are allocated through on-site draw.
Track and field events shall be conducted according to racing rules.
No limit on the number of teams to participate, except for track and field events (including relay races), if the number of participants per team does not reach 6 people, the event will be canceled (except for the Faculty Staff Group, Alumni & NTU Friends Group).
Track and field events allow each unit unlimited number of participants per event (including relay and tug-of-war), with each athlete limited to registering for a maximum of two events (excluding relay and team events).
In cases where no more than 8 contestants arrive for check-in and registration during the preliminary round, they will be combined into a single competition.
When the number of registrants exceeds the stipulated amount, the conference will select the top six contestants based on the registration list.
organizer_unit: 共同教育中心體育室
target_audience: Faculty, Staff, Short-term Staff, Project Personnel, Hospital Staff, Retired Faculty, Retired Staff

activity_name_event_page: 114 Academic Year (76th Batch) School-wide Sports Meet - Track and Field Competition for Teachers and Staff (丙组)
session_name: Staff Women's C Group - 60m
session_content:
Track and Field Competition Rules:
Adopt the latest athletics rules set by the Chinese Athletic Association of the Republic of China.
In track and field events other than male and female 100-meter races which use preliminary and final rounds, all other events use timed finals.
The race groups are allocated through on-site draw.
Track and field events shall be conducted according to racing rules.
No limit on the number of teams to participate, except for track and field events (including relay races), if the number of participants per team does not reach 6 people, the event will be canceled (except for the Faculty Staff Group, Alumni & NTU Friends Group).
Track and field events allow each unit unlimited number of participants per event (including relay and tug-of-war), with each athlete limited to registering for a maximum of two events (excluding relay and team events).
In cases where no more than 8 contestants arrive for check-in and registration during the preliminary round, they will be combined into a single competition.
When the number of registrants exceeds the stipulated amount, the conference will select the top six contestants based on the registration list.

activity_name_activity_session: 114-1 FLTC Book Fair: Browse the book fair and get good books!
activity_content:
This week for Thanksgiving, are you planning on going home? Check out the book fair before you go!
🎁 And embark on a journey back home with the characters in the book, fill out the learning sheets, and have a chance to win:
Limited edition book exhibition!
Other精彩original books!

Activity information:
November 27 - December 31
Time: Monday to Friday, 9:00–12:00; 13:00–16:00 (closed for lunch from 12:00–13:00)
Location: 302 Room (eStudy Space) in the Foreign Teacher Center (ETC)
Sure, please provide the text you would like translated.
Book fair original books抢先看:

The Dutch House: The Novel by Ann Patchett

After World War II, Cyril Conroy capitalized on good fortune and achieved success in real estate, buying a Dutch Colonial mansion in Philadelphia for his wife secretly. However, he did not know that this supposedly happy house sowed the seeds of family ruin.

Danny and his sharp-tongued,辛辣, and confident sister Maeve are Cyril Conroy’s children. They grew up in this grand mansion from a young age, but they were both thrown out of the house along with all their possessions by their stepmother… their only reliance left was each other...

Crying in H Mart: A Memoir 《A Memoir of H Mart Tears》 Michelle Zauner

"After mom isn't here, I always cry when I go to H Mart."

Michelle Sann is the lead singer of an independent band. Born in Seoul and raised in the United States, she often went to the Korean supermarket H Mart with her mom as a child and would listen to her mom talk about点滴of her life in Korea while eating Korean food cooked by her mom.

Mom had assured her that this would be the most special year of her life at age 25. But she never expected that it would be the same year when Mom was diagnosed with cancer and her life came to a sudden end, causing her own life to fragment...

Great Expectations by Charles Dickens

In 1860, Dickens, already well-known at the time, began serializing this novel, "Great Expectations" (also known as "Little Dorrit"). The protagonist, orphan Philip Pirrip, and the fugitive Magwitch, who had a grudge against him for stealing a piece of pie and a knife, developed a long-lasting connection due to these trifling matters. Ordinary and abused Philip met fortune when he unexpectedly entered a grand mansion and became friends with little Estella, an attractive but heartless girl who mocked Philip's lowliness and vulgarity. Determined to climb the social ladder for his beloved Estella, Philip embarked on his great expectations, which also opened up his future.

More great books! Come and hunt for them at the book fair!

activity_name_activity_session: Vietnamese Series Lectures - Hearing New Stories from Southeast Asia and Feeling the Power of Cultural Exchange
activity_content:
The diverse cultural landscape of Taiwan is becoming richer, with new residents from Southeast Asia not only bringing linguistic and lifestyle diversity but also injecting new energy into media and art.
This series of lectures focuses on the "Vietnamese Community," inviting two active New Migrants in Taiwan to lead us see the connections between language, attire, performance, and identity.

Through their firsthand experiences and creative journeys, let us together ponder:
How does media in multicultural societies make more voices heard?
How does art interpret history and identity on the stage of cultural fusion?
activity_name_event_page: Vietnamese Series Lectures - Hearing New Stories from Southeast Asia and Feeling the Power of Cultural Exchange
session_name: Integration of Traditional Costumes in Vietnam: Culture, History, and Artistic Performances
session_content:
Speaker | Q Dưa Chuột
Luminary Technology College Department of Art Performance / Television Directing Department of Vietnam Drama and Film University
Host | Nguyen Thi Lien Huong
National Taiwan University lecturer / Vietnam language news broadcaster at Public Television Station

Date: November 19, 2025 (Wednesday)
Time: 3:30 PM - 5:30 PM
Location | National Taiwan University Joint Classroom 405 Classroom

Content Overview:
Traditional Vietnamese clothing exhibits a long and rich cultural heritage, manifesting the nation's aesthetic sensibilities and life philosophies in both everyday attire and formal wear for ceremonial occasions. This lecture will be delivered by Master Pei Wenkuí (Dưa chuột), currently an artist at the Department of Art Performance of Lingming Technical College, who will discuss classic Vietnamese garments such as ao dai and traditional headwear, drawing on his practical experience in choreography to connect clothing with bodily performance. Additionally, the speaker will share how he has integrated traditional culture into contemporary performances, reflecting this in both his teaching and creative process in Taiwan, thereby promoting understanding and dialogue between Taiwanese and Vietnamese cultures.

Experience:
Modern Dance Teacher at Shengmai Center for Modern Energy Training in Zhiling City (2012~2013)
Taipei City Outstanding Foreign Worker Third-Class Award (2015, 2016, 2018)
Taipei City Outstanding Foreign Worker Award (2017)
尊巴舞教练认证证书（2019 and 2020）
Guests of the Taoyuan New Immigrant Forum
Performance Piece:
Cloud Gate Dance Theatre's "Home Away From Home" (2021)
Frankfurt Heilä-Rauhoitus Theatre's "Home Away From Home" (2022)
Leeming Performing Arts (2023)
Film actor:
Longan Leaf Bug - Hong Kong Director (2023)

session_name: Hearing Diverse Voices: Media Resources for New Migrants in Southeast Asia – Taking the Vietnamese Community as an Example
session_content:
Speaker | Vũ Hải Yến (Vu Haiyen)
CCTV Vietnamese Program Host / Public Television Vietnamese News Anchor
Host | Angela Yuanchang
National Taiwan University lecturer / Vietnam language news broadcaster at Public Television Station

Date | December 8, 2025 (Monday)
Time: 3:30 PM - 5:30 PM
Location | National Taiwan University Joint Classroom 405 Classroom

Content Overview:
With the increasing number of Southeast Asian New Migrants in Taiwan, media plays a key role in promoting language integration, cultural exchange, and social participation. This lecture will delve into the current broadcast and television media resources provided by Taiwan for New Migrants, with an example being the Vietnamese community, and introduce practical experiences from units such as Central Radio and Television (RTI) and Public Television Station (PTS) regarding content production, language services, and cultural connections.
Through the speaker’s firsthand experiences and observations, this lecture will lead listeners to consider: How media helps new immigrants understand societal currents in their native language, establish cultural identity and belonging, and make Taiwan society hear more diverse and authentic voices.

activity_name_activity_session: Career Center | US Study Abroad Guide | Understand US Industry Talent Trends & Immigration Policies!
activity_content:
The global industrial landscape is constantly evolving, and the United States continues to exert significant influence in innovation and higher education.
How should one discern trends and plan for the next step when pursuing a留学 or international career in the US?

This roundtable focuses on three key areas: industry, talent, and overseas education.
Leading classmates to grasp the latest trends in the American workplace and education,
Think about how studying abroad and career planning can lead you towards a new chapter in your career.

activity_name_activity_session: FLTC English Conversation
activity_content:
Want to improve your spoken English? Want to speak English fluently to express opinions?
Would you like to have opportunities to practice English outside of class and textbooks?
Do you want to make a partner who can help you practice speaking English?
Come join our 【English Chat】 at this center!

This activity involves trained small groups sharing English speaking tips and engaging in various oral practice activities, such as experience sharing, brainstorming, games, and role-playing, to help you speak English freely and comfortably in a relaxed and fun atmosphere.

This semester’s sessions have unique themes, where through ample practice during interactions with leaders and learning partners, speaking anxiety is reduced to naturally improve English expression and communication skills!

Stand-up Comedy!
Stand-up comedy is one of today’s hottest performance styles, mixing jokes, stories, and real-life fun. In this session, we’ll watch hilarious clips, explore different comedy styles, and uncover how comedians turn everyday moments into laughter. We’ll also discuss where humor’s limits lie—what are the topics that can be joked about, and what are not? Join this session for laughter and inspiring discussions!

session_name: From迷茫 to possible: A Career Gathering Workshop
session_content:
From迷茫 to possible: A Career Gathering Workshop
This workshop aims to assist students in exploring themselves and career development appropriately. It is suitable for the following types of students:

Just entering school or still uncertain about their professional direction.

Considering switching majors or crossing over to a different field of study.

Confused About Graduation and Career Choices

In design, academic advisors and counselors aim to guide students through structured sessions, personal experiences, interdisciplinary clinical practice experience, and counseling psychologists’ life stories. They also incorporate specific interactive activities (such as red flower cards, career fantasies, life axes, and goals) that allow students to start from “self-awareness” and progress to “motivation and action planning.” Through supportive and integrative contexts, they aim to bolster confidence in exploration and courage for implementation.


Workshop times: November 18, 2011 (Tue), November 25, 2011 (Tue), December 2, 2011 (Tue)

Three times continuous, from 17:30 to 21:00.

Round 1: Self-Exploration X Career Ghost-Walk

Session 2: Introduction of Internal and External Resources, Life Axis, Goal Setting

Session 3: Interdisciplinary Senior and Junior (Ye Beichen, Counseling Psychologist) X Career Exploration Dialogue

This workshop series is a continuous one, and priority admission or management students and teaching fellows will be given when registering. Priority will be given to those who can attend all three sessions.



Location of Activities: 1st Floor Multi-Purpose Room at NTU Counseling Center (Building Hope Pavilion 1st Floor)

Leaders: Niu Shengru Tutor, Liao Manting Tutor Assistant, Zeng Yuhao Tutor Assistant, Pan Yiikan Tutor

External Lecturer: Ye Beichen, Counseling Psychologist (12/2)



Workshop Note: There are pre/post assessments in this workshop for students to refer to. They are used to gauge the effectiveness of students' participation before and after the workshop, solely as a measure of activity outcomes to assist mentors in evaluating future activities.



Admission Notice

Workshop considers the fit between design and members, selecting members through this form. Notification of acceptance is expected to be sent out before Friday, November 7th, 2023.

If you have any questions, please email: Ms. Shengrou (Nancy) Wu – Guidance Teacher shengrou@ntu.edu.tw or Ms. Manting Liao – Academic Counselor manting@ntu.edu.tw.

activity_name_activity_session: 114th Anniversary Campus Carnival活动
activity_content:
To celebrate the 97th anniversary of NTU’s founding, a campus celebration carnival was held in the B1 table tennis room. Creative and energetic performances and booths were showcased through activities to celebrate the school’s 97th birthday happily.
This event has 9 booths scheduled for three time slots: 11:30-12:30, 13:30-14:30, and 15:30-16:30. There are a total of 27 sessions offering free experiences. Space is limited, so hurry and register!

Note
The activity unit will send out a notice in advance to remind participants. Participants are required to arrive at the stall 10 minutes before the course starts; being late will result in forfeiture of participation, and the slot will not be reserved for that participant. The remaining slots will be available for those who register on-site to queue up and participate in the courses in order.
Fill out the feedback form and show it to the booth staff for confirmation, and you can obtain one activity stamp on the Taste Pavilion card.

activity_name_activity_session: Taiwan University Institute of Optical and Electronic Engineering - 2024/2025 Academic Year Faculty Research Areas Introduction Meeting
activity_content:
Welcome to participate in the faculty introduction seminar on 11/14 (Friday), where professors from our department will introduce their personal research areas. This session is open to all students with an interest in optics and photonics, welcome to join.

Time: November 14, 2025 (Friday) 13:30–17:00
Location: National Taiwan University Electrical and Computer Engineering College Electric Hall 2nd Floor Room 105

※1、There are meal boxes available on site, please register first at “Taiwan University Activity Registration System” to enable us to prepare meals. Thank you.
The schedule for each teacher's presentation will be announced at the活动现场, and any inconvenience caused is requested to be understood.
activity_name_event_page: Taiwan University Institute of Optical and Electronic Engineering - 2024/2025 Academic Year Faculty Research Areas Introduction Meeting
session_name: Taiwan University Institute of Optical and Electronic Engineering - 2024/2025 Academic Year Faculty Research Areas Introduction Meeting
session_content:
Photonics is fundamentally a multidisciplinary field that combines optics, physics, electrical engineering, materials science, mechanical engineering, and electronics. Its diversity also offers vast academic and application spaces. Research areas include: fiber communications, optical fibers, integrated optics, semiconductor lasers and photodetectors, semiconductor photonics phenomena, optoelectronics technology, laser technology and optoelectronic applications, nonlinear optics, optoelectronic measurement techniques, and biomedical applications of photonics. To help students gain a deeper understanding of the research areas of our faculty members in this program, we are organizing an "Photonics Department Introduction Seminar," inviting faculty to introduce their personal research fields and laboratory conditions. We welcome interested students to actively register for participation.

※1、There are meal boxes available on site, please register first at “Taiwan University Activity Registration System” to enable us to prepare meals. Thank you.
The schedule for each teacher's presentation will be announced at the活动现场, and any inconvenience caused is requested to be understood.

activity_name_activity_session: No.280 Musician's Association of the College of Arts "Autumn Six Strings: Bittersweet Return to Sweetness of Saudade"
activity_content:
Target: No.280 College Music Festival "Six Strings in Autumn: Bittersweet Resurgence of Saudade"

Performance time: November 14, 2025 (Friday) 12:30-13:20

Venue: National Taiwan University College of Literature Main Hall

Organizers
National Taiwan University College of Literature

Organizing Unit: National Taiwan University Musicology Institute

Content introduction

"Saudade" is a profound Portuguese word that goes far beyond the Chinese term "乡愁." It is not merely Nostalgia or Bittersweet Longing in English, but rather a complex emotion born from deep love and longing, with a subtle bitterness followed by sweetness. This longing not only revisits past people, events, and places, but also contains a fervent desire for the future, even a mysterious quest for the unknown.

In autumn, it is the season that best embodies this mindset. Therefore, in this concert, we will use the resonance of a classical guitar’s six strings to invite the audience to explore the rich hues of “Saudade” from four different cultural perspectives together, and savor the sweetness amidst the bitterness that belongs to life itself.
activity_name_event_page: No.280 Musician's Association of the College of Arts "Autumn Six Strings: Bittersweet Return to Sweetness of Saudade"
session_name: No.280 Musician's Association of the College of Arts "Autumn Six Strings: Bittersweet Return to Sweetness of Saudade"
session_content:
[Introductions of Performers]
Lu Yi (Luigi Miotto) | Classical guitar performer

Italian classical guitarist, born in Padua in 1999. Started playing the guitar at the age of eight and entered the Music Academy of Padua in 2015 to study under Professor Paola Muggia. Pursued studies abroad in Portugal at the Escola Superior de Música de Évora (2018–2019), where he studied with Artur Caldeira. Upon returning home, obtained his Bachelor's degree and Master's degree in classical guitar both with full marks in 2020 and 2023 respectively.

Lu Yicheng has participated in master classes with多位 international masters, including Florindo Baldissera, Stephen Goss, Alberto Mesirca, Daniel Paredes, Pavel Steidl, and Giacomo Susani. His performing足迹 have covered Italy and Portugal, encompassing solo performances, duets, quartets, and chamber music. In 2019 and 2024, he was invited twice to give solo recitals at the Foz CÃa Music Festival in Portugal, and in 2022, he premiered a duo concertino for guitar composed by Sergio Chiereghin in Italy.

In addition to performing, Luky also致力于promoting Brazilian classical music and has been working in collaboration with soprano Luiza Pena de Lima for many years, presenting the rich and diverse musical landscape of South America.

The training as a musician has deep resonance with Lu Yi for the beauty of Chinese tones, which led him to pursue his studies at the Institute of Asian and North African Studies at the University of Venice. He obtained bachelor’s degrees in Chinese and Arabic (2022), a master’s degree in translation and interpretation (2024), and a master’s degree in Arabic (2025) all with full marks. Currently, he is a joint doctoral student between the Center for Asia and Intercultural Studies at the University of Venice and Heidelberg University in Germany. His research focuses on the evolution of Chinese modern musical terminology. He has also visited the Institute of Musicology at National Taiwan University to conduct research under Professor Shen Dong’s guidance.

activity_name_activity_session: TELL Talks English Learning Lecture Series
activity_content:
TELL Talks is a series of English language learning lectures launched by the Foreign Language Teaching and Resource Center at National Taiwan University, aimed at providing students with enriching and practical knowledge while helping them improve their English skills. We invite professionals from various fields as speakers, whose lecture topics combine aspects such as language learning, cultural exchange, and new life insights, in order to offer inspiring and practical sharing. Whether you are just starting your English studies or wish to express yourself more confidently in academic, professional, or international exchanges, TELL Talks will provide you with diverse and practical learning resources!

Session 1: The New Wave of Smart Agriculture: A Dialogue Between Drones, AI and Crops
Smart Agriculture: The Dialogue Between Drones, AI, and Crops

Wow? AI can also be applied in agricultural development? And what does this have to do with the popular drones nowadays?

The development of AI technology is not something that can be described as happening in days or even years.
What sparks might occur when drones meet AI in the fields?

We invited Assistant Researcher Hong Ruobing from the Agricultural Engineering Research Center.
Sharing interesting new knowledge about technology and its applications和发展.
There's also a story about how he came to study and work in Taiwan when he initially came from Indonesia!

session_content:
Session Two: The Real Work of Language: Creating Understanding That Already Exists

Do you want to improve your cross-cultural communication skills at work?
The Swedish scholar and consultant Dr. Miriam Garvi, with rich practical experience in the workplace, will help you understand the actual challenges and techniques of cross-cultural communication to cultivate your international leadership skills. Don't miss out!

DateTime | Dec 4 (Thu) 10:30-12:00
Location: Foreign Language Teaching & Resource Center 302-1
Speaker | Dr. Miriam Garvi
Voiced Language | English

Limited spots available, please confirm your attendance before registering.
📸 This activity will use photos or videos taken as the main promotional records.
🎁 This activity can accumulate bilingual education center's "English Learning Passport" points. Please scan the QR code on-site to complete the registration.

We often assume that understanding is something we receive, like information handed to us. But in practice, real understanding is something we create together. In this talk, I invite students to look more closely at the words, assumptions, and mental models that shape the way we see the world. Many of these feel natural to us, yet they limit our ability to collaborate, to imagine better possibilities, and to work toward a shared purpose.
Sure, please provide the text you would like translated.
Dr. Miriam Garvi is a co-founder of Vision Pioneers and was formerly an Associate Professor of the Global MBA program at National Taiwan University, where she holds a PhD in Business Administration from Sweden.

She assists leaders and organizations with leadership and vision development, team building training, and ESG/CSR initiatives.

She recently launched a new training program for Adaptive Game-Changers in Asia, in partnership with the social enterprise DOMI Earth.

She leads a team of academics and professionals in research and consulting work on ESG leadership and purpose-driven organizations.

Irreplicability, Fraud, and Self-Correction in Science
13:20-15:10, November 12th (Wed.), 2025
Room 103, Boya Lecture Building, NTU Main Campus
