activity_name_event_page: 114-1 [Academic Ethics Lecture]
session_name: 114-1 [Academic Ethics Lecture]
session_content:
Activity information:
Topic: Irreplicability, Fraud, and Self-Correction in Science
Time: November 12, 2025 (Wed) 1:20 PM - 3:10 PM
Location: Chaozhong Campus Po-Ya Teaching Building Room 103
Speaker: Dr. David Cyranoski
Kyoto University Advanced Human Biology Institute (ASHBi) Senior Researcher
Language: English

Course description:
This lecture will explore the concept of “self-correction in academia” and why it often fails. People commonly argue that science has a self-correcting mechanism, as if these falsifications would inevitably be exposed. This lecture will illustrate why this belief is flawed—why there are no automatic mechanisms to detect fakes, why there are many obstacles in the process of exposing fakes, and why incentives to discourage faking are actually less than we imagine. It is a thought-provoking analysis that examines how academic communities operate and respond to research results that cannot be reproduced or are suspicious, with the aim of gaining deeper understanding into the mindset of those who engage in academic fraud.

This session covers academic ethics courses of category 1 for 1 hour and category 2 for 1 hour; starting from the 107 academic year, graduate students must cover at least three categories totaling 6 hours of academic ethics course study. Participants are responsible for supplementing their own academic ethics training hours.

Activity schedule:
12:50-13:20: Registration and入场
Speaker Introduction: 13:20-13:30
13:30-14:30: Keynote Speech
Question and Answer Session: 14:30-15:10
15:10-15:20: Fill out online sign-out form

Introduction
This lecture explores the concept of fraud self-correction and why it fails. It is often stated, after a fraud case, that science is self-correcting, as if the fraud was destined to be exposed.
By examining various case studies, the speaker demonstrates why this reasoning is fallacious, why there is no "mechanism" to catch fraud, why there are actually many obstacles to catching fraud, and why there are fewer incentives for committing fraud than one might imagine.
It aims to provoke thought on how communities function and handle replicable and suspicious results. I believe it aids in understanding the mindset of those who engage in fraud.

Agenda
12:50-13:20: Registration
13:20-13:30: Introduction of the speaker
13:30-14:30: Lecture Start
14:30-15:10: QA session
15:10-15:20: End of Lecture and sign-out sheet

activity_name_activity_session: Chang Gongyuan Professorial Chair Lecture Series on Nobel Prize Winners and Opening Ceremony of Taiwan Bridge Project
activity_content:
The "Song Kungyuan Professorship of Outstanding Research" at our school was established with a generous donation from Mr. Song Kungyuan, the founder of Guangbao Group, starting in 2023 and will be jointly promoted by the Central研究院 with the International Peace Foundation (IPF) starting in 2025 to support the "Taiwan Bridge Project." This initiative will invite thirty-one Nobel Prize laureates to Taiwan from 2025 to 2026, where they will give series of lectures and engage in exchanges. The session is a grand opening ceremony for this project, featuring a lecture by Prof. Sir Andre Geim, the Nobel Laureate in Physics. We welcome all faculty and students to participate enthusiastically.

session_name: Sir Andre Geim (Nobel Prize in Physics laureate in 2010) - Distinguished Research Lecture by Professor Chengrun Sun
session_content:
Introduction of Speaker:
Sir Andre Geim is a distinguished physicist known for his curiosity and creativity, which have led to several exciting discoveries in modern physics. He is best known as one of the discoverers of graphene, an extraordinary material composed of a single layer of carbon atoms with remarkable mechanical, thermal, and electronic properties. This breakthrough earned him the 2010 Nobel Prize in Physics, which he shared with his colleague Konstantin Novoselov. Sir Andre is also well known for his playful approach to science – such as levitating a frog using a strong magnetic field, an experiment that won him an Ig Nobel Prize! Today, through his work at the University of Manchester, he continues to push the boundaries of science and inspire new generations of researchers.

Activity Process:
08:45 - 09:15: Registration and Admission
09:20 - 09:45: Guides
09:45 - 10:15: Opening Ceremony
10:15 - 10:20: Introduction
10:20 - 11:05: Speech Start
11:05 - 11:50: Questions and Answers
11:50 - 12:00: Trophy Presentation and Group Photo Session

Speaker Introduction:
Sir Andre Geim is an outstanding physicist who has brought many exciting discoveries to modern physics. His most widely known achievement was the discovery of "graphene," a new material consisting of a single layer of carbon atoms, which is strong and exhibits unique thermal and electrical properties. This breakthrough led him to jointly receive the Nobel Prize in Physics in 2010 with his colleague Konstantin Novoselov. Geim is also renowned for his curious and creative research style, having once levitated frogs using a strong magnetic field and winning the Nobell Prize in Misery as a result. He continues to teach at the University of Manchester in the UK, striving to expand the boundaries of science and inspire new generations of researchers.

activity_content:
Topic:
How can we promote human-centric common education development in the AI age?
How to foster human-centered general education in the age of AI?

Time: November 7, 2025, 1:30 PM – 5:00 PM Classroom 602-604, Qian Siliang Lecture Hall

The development of AI is gradually impacting various activities in human society, promoting new educational methods and learning journeys, while also bringing new challenges. To respond to the rapid development and application of AI, it requires cultivating individuals with cross-disciplinary capabilities, blending digital skills and人文素养 (humanistic literacy) to meet future challenges.

The Joint Education Center invited six international speakers and six Taichung University of Technology (TUT) speakers to share their research and results in interdisciplinary fields.

activity_name_activity_session: November 5, 2011 "Eye Tour of National Taiwan University Century Campus: Eye Protection Campaign Health Education Campus Tour Activity"
activity_content:
One, to respond to the "20-20-20" eye care principle (taking a 20-second break every 20 minutes of screen time to look at something 20 feet away) and the "30-10-120" outdoor concept (engaging in at least two hours of outdoor activities daily to reduce the risk of myopia progression), the activity is specially arranged within the campus for cultural tours, allowing participants to naturally practice outdoor eye care habits while appreciating green spaces and attractions.
Tuesday, November 5, 2014, 12:10-13:30.
Three – Speaker: 4 volunteers from the Visitor Center.
Four, location: Campus Beauty (Library, Farm, Lu Gong Zhen Ecological Pool, Tongcheng San Song, Luhuming Square, Fu Zhong Bell, Drunken Moon Lake, Sociology and Anthropology College Library).
Fifth, target audience: teachers, staff, and students with high myopia (500 degrees or more) at our school. Those who need to participate in public service personnel hours must fully participate and complete check-in/check-out as well as satisfaction surveys will be eligible to log 2 hours of lifelong learning credits for public service personnel. The number of participants is divided into four groups.
Six: Participants have one walnut bread at hand. Please note if you are allergic.
On the day of the activity, please bring your own hats, towels, and water and other items you need.

activity_name_activity_session: School Anniversary Badminton Experience Fair
activity_content:
Activity Name: School Anniversary Badminton Experience Fair
Activity content
School Anniversary Badminton Experience Fair
November 4th (Tuesday), evening 6:00-8:00 PM
National Taiwan University New Sports Hall B1 Wall Playing Area
The Wen Kang Hui organized an "Annual Carnival of Experience for School Anniversary 114th" to encourage the faculty and staff at our school to foster a better sports atmosphere that promotes physical and mental health. It encourages colleagues to make good use of the unique resources provided by the school, cultivating a healthy habit of sustained exercise.
The activity is introduced in a relaxed, interactive, sharing, and stress-relieving atmosphere to introduce the sport of wallball to colleagues who have not tried it. Wallball is an indoor sport that can be played daily; its intensity ranges from light entertainment, moderate fitness, to high-speed urgency, allowing players to choose according to their preferences.
Seize this rare opportunity and participate enthusiastically!
The fun of wall ball goes beyond just surprising experiences and also opens up new opportunities for healthy exercise!
Please register through the school activity system; no on-site registration is accepted.
With a racket, learn the rules and basic movements from the coach, then hit some balls for fun.

activity_name_event_page: (Field Changes) [My Learning and Reflection Series] Lecture - Academian Zang Zhijia from Central研究院 "Wilderness in Search of History: My Archaeological Journey"
session_name: Change of Venue: Professor Zang Zhenhua from the Academia Sinica on "Wilderness in Search of History: My Archaeological Journey"
session_content:
Change: Due to high enrollment, the lecture hall was changed to Classroom 102 of the Wenbo Culture Teaching Building.

Activity Information
Time: October 31st, 2025 (Friday) 7:00 PM - 9:00 PM
Location: Chaozhong Campus Bolya Teaching Building Room 102
Host: Ding Shiting Deputy Vice Principal
Speaker: Dr. Zang Zhinhuai,院士
(National Taiwan University Professor Jin-Tao Hou of Tsinghua University, Academica Sinica Fellow)
Language: Chinese

Speaker Introduction:
Zang Zhunhua, who graduated from the Archaeology and Anthropology Department and Institute of History and Linguistics at National Taiwan University, received a PhD in anthropology from Harvard University in 1986. After returning to China, he held positions at the Academia Sinica’s Institute of Historical and Linguistic Sciences, progressing through assistant researcher, associate researcher, and research fellow roles, and他曾兼任考古学组主任。From 2002 to 2006, he was seconded as Director of the National Museum of Prehistory in Taiwan, overseeing several important archaeological and cultural preservation projects. In his later career, he transitioned into academia and education, currently serving as a Chou Mih-huai Distinguished Research Chair Professor at National Tsing Hua University, and is also an Academia Sinica Fellow.

Dr. Zang’s research spans Taiwan, South China, and the Philippines, with a core concern on prehistoric cultural interactions and the origins of the Austronesian people. His representative work, "Archaeology of the Penghu Islands," systematically reveals the interaction patterns between prehistoric populations in the Penghu Islands and their natural environment, making significant contributions to understanding migration and cultural dissemination in the Taiwan-Penghu region. Following this, he oversaw rescue excavations at the Thirteen Shipyards site and the Science Park in Tainan, demonstrating how to balance economic development with cultural preservation, and bringing Taiwanese archaeology into widespread attention on both societal and policy levels.

In research dissemination, it开创ed new frontiers in underwater archaeology in Taiwan by leading teams to conduct surveys and excavations in Penghu and coastal areas, where they gradually discovered sites such as the "General One Ship," providing valuable materials for studying maritime exchanges between East Asia and the South China Sea region. These achievements not only expanded the boundaries of Taiwanese archaeology but also deepened its international academic standing. Dr. Zang Zhenhua, with decades of research, preservation, and educational practice spanning from Penghu to Sanxia, from Nan Sha Lake to sunken ships underwater, left lasting marks. His contributions transcend disciplinary boundaries, becoming a significant milestone in Taiwan's humanities and social sciences.

Activity schedule:
18:30-19:00: Registration and entry open
Opening remarks: 19:00-19:05
19:05-20:35: Presentation Sharing
20:35-20:55: Live Q&A
20:55-21:00: Giftting thank-you certificates and group photo on site

activity_name_activity_session: October 30th (Thursday), "Challenge Smart Eating for a Healthy Body Shape" Health Education Lecture (In Person and Online)
activity_content:
To enhance the correct understanding of muscle building and fat reduction among faculty, staff, and students at our school, and to learn how to make smart food choices and balance their diets for healthy weight loss and long-term maintenance, we are hosting a health education lecture titled "Challenge Smart Eating: Healthy Body Posture" (both in-person and online).
Explanation:
One: Date: October 30th, 2011 (Thu) 12:10-14:00.
Speaker: Dr. Hsieh Hung-yu from Da Yun Clinic.
Third, physical location: Health Center二楼卫生室; Online participants will be provided with a link via email three days before the course.
Four, target audience: priority admission for graduate or doctoral students and undergraduate students at this school with a Body Mass Index (BMI) greater than 27.
If you need public service personnel hours, you must participate in the entire course and sign in/out as well as complete the satisfaction survey. This will log 2 hours of public service personnel hours.
Six, for physical participants, 35 boxed meals will be provided for those with a Body Mass Index (BMI) greater than or equal to 27. The distribution time of the meals will be arranged by the Health Center.
Admitted students will be notified via email three days prior and will receive a pre-test to complete before class.

activity_name_activity_session: October 29 (Wed), October 30 (Thu) Cancer Health Screening Activity (Please select either 29th or 30th, do not register for both)
activity_content:
To implement the cancer prevention strategy of “early detection, early diagnosis, and early treatment,” and to enhance health awareness among faculty and staff members at our school, we have collaborated with the Cancer Medical Division of Taichung Area Hospital to hold a series of “cancer health screening” activities offering free services.
Cancer Health Screening Activity:
Time: October 29 (Wednesday), October 30 (Thursday) 13:30-16:30.
Location: Health Center Ground Floor.
Bring your Health Insurance Card when inspected.
Screening items include:
Cervical smear: once a year for women aged 30 and above, every three years for those aged 25-29.
Human papillomavirus (HPV) testing in women aged 35, 45, and 65 years.
Mammography (by appointment at Taichung Cancer Medical Foundation): every two years for women aged 40-74.
Stool occult blood test: every two years for those aged 45-75 or with a family history at ages 40-44.
Pulmonary low-dose computed tomography (using预约reception desk at Taichung Cancer Medical Center):
Family history of lung cancer: male aged 45-75 or female aged 40-75 with a family member, parent, child, or sibling diagnosed with lung cancer. Required to prepare personal ID card, Health Insurance Card, and photocopies of the identity cards of family members who are patients. Photocopies of diagnosis certificates.
Second, severe smokers: those aged 50 to 74 with a smoking history of over 20 packs and who have not quit smoking for at least 15 years. Time: October 29 (Wednesday), October 30 (Thursday) 13:30-16:30.

Stool occult blood test: every two years for those aged 45-75 or with a family history at ages 40-44.

Severe smokers preparing to quit need to register after they have started the process of quitting smoking first. Participants in the cancer screening activity will receive a set of exquisite items from Taipei Medical University Cancer Hospital.

activity_name_activity_session: Basic Life Support (BLS) training for days 114/10/29 and 30
activity_content:
Office of Student Affairs Health Center BLS (Basic Life Support) training in October
One, this center was established to enhance the emergency handling capabilities of faculty and staff at three affiliated universities of National Taiwan University (NTU) in dealing with first-line accidents. Specialized NTU Hospital Emergency Medicine Department physicians, senior rescue technicians, NTU Hospital physical therapists, and members from this training center were invited to conduct a "Basic Life Support" (BLS) training. The plan included practical exercises to cultivate the crisis management skills of students for maintaining campus and club activity safety.
Two, Date: October 29 and 30th. (10/29 17:15-21:50, 10/30 17:45-21:15)
Three: Target audience: 30 spots are filled for faculty and staff members as well as students of National Taiwan University System.
Four, location: Global Hall and classrooms 201 and 215 of the School of Public Health Building at City Campus.
Five: Content: Trauma emergency, casualty搬运techniques internship (carried out in groups), Hemostasis bandaging techniques internship.
CPR training, CPR technical practice (in groups), and practical exam, written
Test.
Six、 Registration Method: Please register through the school activity registration system.
Seven, one must attend fully, pass the written test, technical assessment, submit pre-class assignments, and pay to obtain '基
Lifetime Learning Credit of 8 Hours for Public Servants; Certificate of Completion of 'Life-Saving Technique' course.
Eight, Certificate Printing Fees Explanation:
The "certificate" for this course is optional and if you need one, you must bear the cost of NT$ per certificate.
100 yuan (production cost of Taiwan Sports Safety and First Aid Skills Promotion Association), and upon class commencement on that day.
Inform the staff and pay for fees when checking in.
Contact Information and Enrollment Eligibility:
Please make sure to provide your valid Taiwan mobile phone number when registering so that you can receive course notifications and contact information. If not provided,
Taiwan mobile phone numbers will be considered as abandoning the right to attend classes (for special circumstances, please contact承办02-)
panhx@ntu.edu.tw)
Tenth, welcome on-site registration: [Open on-site registration after actual situation of the students checking in at the site. The course will be open for registration beyond 15 minutes. Registration will close once the quota of 30 people is reached.]

activity_name_activity_session: 2025 Financial Management Department Fundraising and Donation Operation Process Workshop (II)
activity_content:
Do you want to know how to raise funds and the process of donating?
To provide more opportunities for exchange and learning, and to promote fundraising effectiveness among units and institutes/departments, the Finance Management Office will hold a special workshop on October 23, 2014 (Thursday) at 9:00 AM entitled "Workshop on Fundraising and Donation Operation Processes of the Finance Management Office - Second Session of this Year."
Workshop content includes:
One, CFO Experience Sharing: Sharing how to enrich the school fund and assist in school development.
Second, Donation to NTU Explanation: Introduce the donation options and explain how to apply for a personalized donation form.
Three, Donation Process Explanation: Demonstrate and explain in detail the donation process on-site, including its finer details.
We sincerely invite relevant colleagues to enthusiastically register and participate together in order to enhance fundraising capabilities and promote school affairs as well as the development of various colleges and departments!

Time:
October 23rd (Thursday), 10:20–12:00 (check-in at 10:00)

Registration method:
Click on the activity link to register. Participants will be notified by email.
Participants who complete the course will receive 2 hours of lifelong learning credits.
Welcome to register and write down your questions, where we will be happy to clarify them for you!

activity_name_activity_session: [Taiwan University Disaster Training Course]
activity_content:
Course Theme: National Taiwan University Disaster Steward Training Course

Second, this series of courses is conducted entirely in Chinese. Participants who fully participate and pass the test will receive a certification as a Disaster Management Officer issued by the Ministry of Interior.

III. Course Description: With unavoidable earthquakes, typhoons, and extreme weather conditions, schools need more individuals with disaster knowledge. The Disaster Management Training course provides the most practical response techniques and methods for handling disasters, helping you make calm judgments and effective actions during emergencies. This is a cultivation of both professional and practical abilities; we sincerely invite you to join us in jointly safeguarding campus safety.

Participants who fully participate can receive 3000 Green Points upon completion of the activity.

Speaker Introduction:
Xuanxuan Limited Company Zheng Wanxuan Lecturer
(Taipei City Government Fire Bureau instructors: Liu Chongzhou, Zhang Shijie, Li Minlun)
National Taiwan University Civil Engineering Department Lecturer Huang Yin-nan
Fourth: National Taiwan University Center for Climate, Weather, and Disaster Research Professor Pan Zhongyi

Sixth, Target Audience: Students, teachers, staff, and project personnel of our school, up to 50 people per session, until capacity is reached.

October 20 and 21, 2023, from 09:00 to 17:30.
Second batch: October 31, 2023 and November 7, 2023, 09:00–17:30.

III. Course Description: With unavoidable earthquakes, typhoons, and extreme weather conditions, schools need more individuals with disaster knowledge. The Disaster Management Training course provides the most practical response techniques and methods for handling disasters, helping you make calm judgments and effective actions during emergencies. This is a cultivation of both professional and practical abilities; we sincerely invite you to join us in jointly safeguarding campus safety.

Participants who fully participate can receive 3000 Green Points upon completion of the activity.

Speaker Introduction:
Xuanxuan Limited Company Zheng Wanxuan Lecturer
(Taipei City Government Fire Bureau instructors: Liu Chongzhou, Zhang Shijie, Li Minlun)
National Taiwan University Civil Engineering Department Lecturer Huang Yin-nan
Fourth: National Taiwan University Center for Climate, Weather, and Disaster Research Professor Pan Zhongyi

Sixth, Target Audience: Students, teachers, staff, and project personnel of our school, up to 50 people per session, until capacity is reached.

Seven, time:
TERTIARY BATCH: October 31, 2023 and November 7, 2023 from 09:00 AM to 05:30 PM.

Eight, location: 406 Conference Room of Water Engineering Testing Institute, Da Tung University Campus.

Nine, Registration: Open until October 15, 2011 at 5:00 PM. After registration, please fill out the course form at the following link:
Second Batch: https://forms.gle/uTYMCzMEPMUtGb9j6

Tenth, this activity is free of registration fees and provides lunch.

Organizer: Chen Lihao from the Climate Weather Disaster Research Center (Tel: 02-3366-1711 #10; brendanchen@ntu.edu.tw)

Twelve, other notes:
This course requires training participants to confirm they will fully participate in the 2-day course and accept disaster management trainer training assessment (including theoretical and practical assessments).
Persons who fully participate in the two-day training course and pass the outcome test will be eligible to apply for a Ministry of Interior Disaster Steward Qualified Identification Card and certificate. Those who do not fully participate in the training or fail the test will not have the eligibility to apply for a Disaster Steward.

session_name: Swim relay比赛
session_content:
Free to form your own team and register for participation.
Number: 8 people per team, mixed gender, at least 3 people of single sex.
3. Competition methods and rules:
The race is 21 meters long and is timed.
In teams of four players each, based on batting order, the teams prepare at either end of the 21-meter turnaround area on shore. They complete the比赛 by running in a relay manner.
Swimmers must wear the floatation device around their waist throughout the race in the water until the current swimmer reaches the opposite shore and touches the wall, after which the floatation device is passed to the next swimmer, who can then start. The winner is determined by the length of time taken.

activity_name_activity_session: 2025 Interdisciplinary Career Sharing Event: Taiwan Pathfinder Program x Taichung University Career Handbook
activity_content:
National Taiwan University Office of Student Affairs Career Center Cross-Disciplinary Career Sharing Meeting: Taiwan Guide Program X NTU Graduates' Handbook

We believe that the path of a career is never a one-way street; it more resembles an inspiring journey filled with exploration, repeated attempts and practice, where you constantly adjust your direction and start anew from each experience.

This sharing session has the theme of 'Exploring → Practicing → Re-launching!', and we are honored to invite multiple distinguished alumni spanning different generations and fields, including: Chien Jinpei (Economics major), Security Chief of ASUS Computer Group; Mingzhao Li (Electrical Engineering and MBA major), former President of Wen Pei Institute/KKBOX co-founder; Yisheng Hsuehsung (Geography major), CEO of Huacang Electrical Machinery. They share their inspiring career stories.

Activity Process:
17:30-18:00 Check-in/Coffee Chat
18:00–18:20 Commander's Remarks/Ceremonial Photographs
18:20-19:00 Passion Worker - Alumnus Career Sharing
19:00-19:25 Taiwan Guide Plan - Student Sharing
19:25-19:55 Mentor/student discussion in enterprises
19:55-20:10 Interaction Exchange

activity_name_activity_session: International Affairs Training: International Cooperation and Guest Reception Workshop and Cross-cultural Communication
activity_content:
This international affairs training workshop aims to assist colleagues in various units with understanding the preparation and execution of international cooperation initiatives and international visitor reception cases. It also invites a team from the Fulbright professional group for an interactive discussion on how cross-cultural communication can be applied in daily international cooperation business.

Time: October 15 (Wed) 13:30-16:00
Location: School Central Campus First Conference Room
Process:
Check-in 13:20-13:30
13:30-14:10 International Cooperation and Reception of International Guests
14:10-14:20 Break
Cross-cultural Communication Workshop 14:20-16:00

This workshop is designed to assist colleagues in developing a comprehensive understanding of international collaboration, as well as the preparation and execution of hosting overseas delegations. Subsequently, a cross-cultural communication workshop will be conducted by the Fulbright EMI team, allowing participants to practice the application of cross-cultural communication skills in the context of international collaboration.

Date & Time: 13:30-16:00, Wednesday, October 15.
Conference Room I, Main Campus

Schedule:
13:20-13:30 Registration
13:30-14:10 International Collaboration and Hosting of Overseas Delegations
14:10-14:20 Break
14:20-16:00 Cross-Cultural Communications in International Collaboration (in collaboration with the Fulbright EMI team)

activity_name_activity_session: Celebrity Escape Game: Show Your Presentation Skills
activity_content:
You think it's another PowerPoint class?
No, we're doing a fair!
Navigate through game booths in a series of fun games.
Complete the challenge through stories, logic, and creativity.
Learn as you play, naturally packing presentation skills into your pocket.

13:30-14:30 | Chinese Language Briefing and Expression Training
14:30-15:30 | Carnival Escape Game
15:30-16:30 | English Presentation Communication Training

Information in English

Do you think this is just another presentation workshop?
It's a carnival!
Step right up to quirky game booths,
approach challenges through stories, logic, and creativity
and pocket presentation tricks while having fun.
Learn by playing, and let your presentation skills slip in like cotton candy at a fair—sweet, effortless, and unforgettable.
activity_name_event_page: Celebrity Escape Game: Show Your Presentation Skills
session_name: Celebrity Escape Game: Showcase Your Presentation Skills (Chinese Venue)
session_content:
How to transform a 10,000-word research paper into an eye-catching PowerPoint presentation and a captivating 3-minute research story? This speech will share how to use techniques such as content structuring, oral expression, and body language to make your expertise more accessible to others and highlight your research.

Matthew Ye Qingwei

activity_name_activity_session: "Joint Trend Lecture on New Cultural Trends" (Part 2) – The Current Experience and Narrative of Art Exhibitions: Taking the Practice of the Asian Art Museum in San Francisco as an Example
activity_content:
"Jointly Writing New Trends in Cultural Lecture: Decoding Bronzes and Cross-Disciplinary Narratives of Exhibitions"

Time: October 9, 2025 (Thu) 13:00–15:00
Topic: The Current Experience and Narrative of Art Exhibitions: An Example from the Asian Art Museum in San Francisco
Speaker: Hsieh Jie (Honorary Director of the Asia Art Museum in San Francisco, Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences)
Host: Ching Yu-ju (Professor and Dean of the College of Literature at National University of Taiwan)
Organizers: National Taiwan University System, Cultural Foundation of National Taiwan University System, National Taiwan University College of Literature

Exhibitions at museums are not just for displaying artworks but also important venues for dialogue with audiences and constructing cultural imagination. As one of the significant Asian art museums globally, the San Francisco Asian Art Museum has continuously innovated in exhibition design and narrative strategies. Director Xu Jie will share his curatorial philosophy and practical experience, through its successful cases to explain how cultural assets can be transformed into deep viewing experiences, and discuss how exhibitions connect history with the present day, building bridges for cross-cultural understanding.

activity_content:
The new semester begins with new plans and new lives.
Are you doing okay in your efforts to achieve goals and live well?
Let's program a pause button for ourselves during the semester!

The College of Literature and the School Counseling invite you
Let's make something special for ourselves.
Let's talk about goals and take care of ourselves.

Topic: Writing College X Management College "Finding Your Own Healing Spell" Candle-Making Workshop

Introduction to Content: At the start of a new academic year, to ensure that students can maintain their focus on themselves amidst busy schedules with studies, and continue to balance their learning and lives; in addition to self-care, we also hope to connect classmates regardless of their professional backgrounds, broaden their perspectives on academia and careers, offer each other blessings, and hope to increase their own sense of self-worth through feedback and appreciation. This is to foster a student's efficacy.

(Number of Participants: Approximately 10 per session, with three sessions planned for a total of 30 participants. (Preference given to students from the College of Literature and Management College))
Registration for this session does not guarantee admission; 10 participants will be selected from among 20 applicants, please wait for the notification of admission.

The workshop content is divided into three parts: preparation and introduction, candle-making and connection, and feedback and reflection. Apart from the candle-making and connection which requires about an hour of time, the other two parts each take approximately 30 minutes.

activity_name_activity_session: "One-of-a-Kind and Unmatched: Multidisciplinary Research and Exhibition of the Small Minister Niu Rhinoceros Bronze Vessel from the Shang Dynasty"
activity_content:
"Jointly Writing New Trends in Cultural Lecture" (Part 1)
Topic: A Multidisciplinary Study and Exhibition of the Unique Xia Jun Yuxi Niu Bronzer from the Shang Dynasty
Speaker: Hsieh Jie (Honorary Director of the Asia Art Museum in San Francisco, Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences)
Host: Lu Huiwen (Professor and Director of the Department of Art History at National Taiwan University)
Time: October 8, 2025 (Wed) 15:00–17:00
Location: National Taiwan University College of Literature Lecture Hall
Organizers: National Taiwan University System, Cultural Foundation of National Taiwan University System, National Taiwan University College of Literature

The core of the Chinese antiquities collection at the Asian Art Museum in San Francisco includes a very important set of ancient Chinese bronzes, with the most distinctive piece being an ox-shaped bronze vessel from the late Shang Dynasty. It is unique worldwide and inscribed with significant text that identifies its maker as "小臣艅." In the contemporary 21st century, research on surviving ancient objects can employ multidisciplinary and multi-level approaches to fully extract the information contained within them, deepening our understanding and making it accessible in museum public education. This lecture will explore various aspects of this vessel's characteristics and its significance using the Xiao Jun Yuhu-shaped bronze尊 as an example.
activity_name_event_page: "One-of-a-Kind and Unmatched: Multidisciplinary Research and Exhibition of the Small Minister Niu Rhinoceros Bronze Vessel from the Shang Dynasty"
session_name: "One-of-a-Kind and Unmatched: Multidisciplinary Research and Exhibition of the Small Minister Niu Rhinoceros Bronze Vessel from the Shang Dynasty"
session_content:
"Jointly Writing New Trends in Cultural Lecture: Decoding Bronzes and Cross-Disciplinary Narratives of Exhibitions"

Time: October 8, 2025 (Wed) 15:00–17:00
Topic: A Multidisciplinary Study and Exhibition of the Unique Xia Jun Yuxi Niu Bronzer from the Shang Dynasty
Speaker: Hsieh Jie (Honorary Director of the Asia Art Museum in San Francisco, Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences)
Host: Lu Huiwen (Professor and Director of the Department of Art History at National Taiwan University)
Organizers: National Taiwan University System, Cultural Foundation of National Taiwan University System, National Taiwan University College of Literature

"The尊犀牛形青銅尊 is a prized artifact of the Asian Art Museum in San Francisco, with its unusual shape and inscriptions that make it considered an apex achievement in ancient Chinese bronze art. Director Xu Jie, who has dedicated his career to the exhibition of Asian culture, will lead us in exploring the artistic, historical, and cultural implications of this object. This lecture combines perspectives from museology, archaeology, art history, and more to demonstrate how interdisciplinary approaches can interpret antiquities, deepening public education and cultural understanding. Finally, we will witness the modern splendor of millennia-old bronze together."

Foundation Introduction
Mr. Lin Yulun, the founder of Hongtai Enterprise Group, understands that “taking from society and giving back to society” is the most sincere way for entrepreneurs to回馈society. Since 2019, the foundation has supported various cultivation initiatives, nurturing students with a spirit of altruism, growth mindset, and goal orientation. From understanding social issues, solving problems, to amplifying their societal impact, they become changemakers who are对接ing international opportunities and the future, actively回馈ing to Taiwan, and creating more possibilities for Taiwan.

[Introduction to Yulun NTU Talent Cultivation Program]
The Yu Lin NTU Talent Cultivation Plan has now reached its ninth year, with the foundation collaborating with 11 Taiwanese NPO organizations to invite NTU students to form teams and propose solutions for real challenges faced by these organizations that are both "feasible," have a "social impact," and result in "fundraising effectiveness." Winners will receive a grant of 300,000 RMB plus performance bonuses, as well as an opportunity to compete for a fifteen-week internship valued at one million dollars in Boston.

activity_name_activity_session: Break the stereotypes and protect yourself – Exploring gender violence stereotypes and self-protection
activity_content:
Break the labels, protect yourself.
Exploring Gender Violence Stereotypes and Self-Protection

Have you ever heard those "seemingly normal" phrases that are actually full of bias in class, online, or in life?
Have you considered that gender violence is not just a news event but may actually exist in our daily lives?

This lecture will take you through uncovering the truth about gender violence, recognizing those overlooked stereotypes, and learning effective self-protection strategies through real-life cases and interactive discussions.
Let's not just talk about defense but also about change.

activity_name_event_page: Writing Together, Stronger Than Ever!
session_name: Writing Together, Stronger Than Ever! (For Teachers Only)
session_content:
Are you often unable to find enough uninterrupted time during your daily teaching, guidance, and administrative work to write? Even when ideas do come, they are frequently interrupted by various tasks, meetings, and emails. Maintaining a steady writing rhythm amidst a busy schedule is indeed challenging.

To help teachers regain their focus and rhythm, we specially held the Writing Together activity this semester with strong support from the Teaching Affairs Office.

This activity has been greatly enjoyed by students, with many who have participated mentioning that writing in a quiet and supportive environment allows for more focused thoughts and flowing inspiration. In fact, such "co-writing" activities are quite common in universities abroad, often viewed as an important community form for promoting teachers and graduate students to consistently produce and concentrate on their writing.

In this teacher session, in addition to providing quiet and comfortable writing spaces and English writing consultation services, we also offer an opportunity for AI-assisted writing tools to be discussed, allowing teachers to share and discuss how to leverage these tools to improve writing efficiency and quality.

We sincerely invite you to bring your writing project and join this small writing community for teachers where we can write in a quiet environment, inspiring each other with the aroma of coffee.

Another option is to sign up for a discussion and exchange about AI-assisted writing tools by filling out the following questionnaire. https://forms.gle/G3FyvxwT7rCYbfpp6

activity_name_activity_session: CARDO 114-1 Internship Program for TIP Companies Explanation Meeting
activity_content: Internships serve as a crucial bridge between school education and workplace professionalism, and they are also key mechanisms for integrating theory with practice. An effective internship system requires collaboration between schools and enterprises to be successful. Given this, the Management College of Taiwan University invited companies committed to establishing an internship system to become partners in the TAIDA Internship Program (TIP), a program jointly operating the supply chain for intern activities, including demand confirmation, resume compilation, communication selection, mentor coaching, and performance evaluation. The goal is to build an effective platform for internship matching and utilization, together cultivating elite leadership talents for both enterprises and society.\n\nNational Taiwan University’s Business Administration College upholds the philosophy of cultivating outstanding management talents, striving to nurture students with professional knowledge in both academics and research. It also hopes that its graduates will have an advantage in the workplace when facing global competition. TIP offers a way for students to understand the workplace within just a few months, observing work skills and trends, training workplace etiquette and attitudes, confirming their interests and goal directions, strengthening their weaknesses early before graduation, and adding internship experience to their resumes to secure the key first step towards future career development.
activity_name_event_page: CARDO 114-1 Internship Program for TIP Companies Explanation Meeting
session_name: 1001_Living Green Ivy
session_content:
Company Introduction
Founded in 2010, Green Ivy believes that the power of business can bring positive change to the world. As Taiwan’s third B Corp-certified company and also the only one in Asia to have won the “Best for the World” award consecutively for five years, Green Ivy believes there is always a better way to treat oneself and the environment, from what we eat to use, responding to its corporate mission of making more sustainable choices a part of daily life.

Internship Program
Schedule: From January 5, 2026 (Monday) until the end of the subsequent semester. Any adjustments will be notified prior to admission.
Winter holiday period: Five days a week, Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM – 6:30 PM
Title: 1. Sales Intern Offline 2. Education Development Intern 3. Retail Management Intern 4. Strategy Development Intern 5. Product Research and Development Intern
Compensation and benefits: negotiable

Activity Schedule
Opening and group photo from 12:30~12:40
Internship Plan Content Explanation
13:10–13:50 Share Internship Experience (please prioritize alumni from the College of Management, National Taiwan University)
13:50-14:00 Q&D

Upon being placed on the blacklist, all registered activities will be immediately canceled and one will lose the right to register for other career-related activities.

session_name: Taishang Shihua Bank
session_content:
Company Introduction
A premier stage for professional careers in quality customer experience financial institutions in the APAC region!
Taichung Shihhua Bank was established in 1975 and has 165 branches in Taiwan as well as 67 overseas offices.
Through innovation, digitalization, and application of technology to upgrade products and innovate services; with data, digital, and technology as the three main axes, building a culture driven by data to create seamless, warm financial services both online and offline, steadily advancing towards “the best financial institution in Asia-Pacific.”
Joining the Global RE100 Initiative to commit to carbon-neutral operations in daily business practices, aiming for 100% renewable energy use across all domestic locations by 2030 while continuing the "zero-carbon transformation" journey. Demonstrating our responsibility as a "pioneer of sustainable development." We have received multiple accolades including the Future Enterprise Award from IDC, Best Digital Bank from Euromoney, and Taiwan FinTech Advocacy Award from Asian Banking & Finance.

Internship Program
Objective: Taichung-Shihwa Bank Reserve Talent Intern Program
Schedule: December 2025 – March 2026
Title: 1. Personal Finance Planning Talent 2. International Legal Finance (Product) Talent 3. IT Information Application Talent 4. AI Business Analysis Talent
Staff quota: 1. Personal Finance Planning Talent: 1-4 people 2. International Legal Finance (Product) Talent: 5-10 people 3. IT Information Application Talent: unlimited 4. AI Business Analysis Talent: unlimited
During the储备人才 internship program, you will participate in department projects to gain an understanding of financial industry operations and have opportunities to interact with CMA senior colleagues for consulting. Through the entire internship program, it will help you better understand your career path and provide opportunities for seamless transition into a full-time position or fast-track to become a CMA.
Personal Loan Planning Talent:
During the training program, laying the foundation for product planning, channel understanding, and customer operations capabilities by participating in diverse core projects systematically accumulates experience to become a driver of strategy transformation.
2. Capital International (Product) Talent:
Responsible for participating in cross-border or complex product architecture and marketing strategy development, such as trade finance, syndicated loans, and structured financing, becoming a corporate financial advisor assisting the bank in promoting legal financial product innovation services.
IT Information Application Talent:
Developing and implementing strategic plans while serving as a bridge between business units and IT to ensure that technical solutions meet business needs, becoming a comprehensive professional with expertise in financial knowledge and information technology.
AI Business Analysis Talent
Designing risk solutions driven by data to assist in planning major AI technology initiatives within the bank and working together to drive AI applications, becoming trailblazers for transforming the financial industry.
Qualifications and Requirements: Graduates from any major with sophomore/junior standing (upcoming seniors) are welcome.

session_name: Starbucks
session_content:
Company Introduction
Established formally on January 1, 1998, by a joint venture between Starbucks Coffee International company from the United States and three companies under Tung Un Group in Taiwan—Tung Un Enterprise, Tung Un Supermarket—the cooperative opened Tung Un Starbucks in Taiwan. It sourced and roasted high-quality highland coffee beans globally, selected local top talents for training, and provided Taiwanese consumers with high-quality coffee and services. In 2017, Starbucks Coffee International authorized Tung Un Enterprise and Tung Un Supermarket to operate it, renaming the company as "Tou Lin Lifestyle Co., Ltd."
Operating the coffee business focuses on human characteristics and quality, with a strong emphasis on respecting customers and employees. It prioritizes sourcing the best coffee beans globally for roasting and producing the highest-quality coffee products for consumers, along with providing the most comfortable consumption environment. Becoming the global leader in specialty coffee is Starbucks Coffee's mission.

Internship Program
Goal: Through internships, students can experience the workplace early, establish correct work attitudes, apply theoretical knowledge to practical situations, strengthen their innovation and execution abilities, communication and leadership skills, enhance their competitiveness in the job market, expand their professional networks, and explore their career paths. Upon graduation, they will be ready to don the armor and face challenges and trials in the workplace.
Period: February 1, 20115 ~ January 31, 20116; July 1, 20115 ~ June 30, 20116
Title: Intern
Quota: 5 people
Job Content: 1. Customer Service and Store Environment Cleaning 2. Beverage Preparation and Promotion of Coffee Culture 3. Product Sales and Execution of Marketing Activities 4. Inventory Concepts and Monitoring of Operational Performance
Qualifications: 1. Likes interacting with people and possesses a team spirit 2. Shows interest in coffee culture and has an active, proactive learning attitude 3. Shares the work ethic of the food and beverage service industry
Salary and benefits: Monthly salary of 29,500~31,800

session_name: CITI Bank
session_content:
This activity has been changed due to certain reasons, and the venue will now be the second conference room of the Management College Multi-functional Living Hall.

Company Introduction
Citi is a leading brand among global banks.
Chase Bank established an office in Taiwan in 1964, followed by the establishment of a Taipei branch the next year. Chase’s leading products and services in the market have received high praise from the outside world. In corporate and institutional business, Chase tailored comprehensive services and solutions for different customer needs, including large enterprises, multinational corporations, small and medium-sized enterprises, financial peers, and foreign-funded entities. Beyond its achievements in financial operations, Chase has also been committed to practicing corporate social responsibility by deepening environmental, social, and governance (ESG) sustainability efforts. This commitment is aimed at responding to the United Nations’ Sustainable Development Goals with concrete actions, pursuing sustainable growth for the company.

Internship Program
Objective: During their summer internship at one of the most globalized banks, Citibank’s interns will have the opportunity to actually understand the operations of the financial industry and expand their skills. Citibank offers extensive professional training and opportunities for internships to interact with senior executives, fully supporting the growth and development of its summer interns. Interns who perform exceptionally at the end of the program will have the opportunity to be directly recruited as reserve managers, gaining access to top global financial institutions before graduation.
Schedule: June 2026-August 2026
Title: Summer Analyst
Number of spots: 5-10
Job Content: Intensive Project Execution Training for 10 Weeks during Summer Internship (June-August 2026)
Interacting with senior executives globally or in APAC and other country interns to accumulate professional experience
Through an internship program to understand cross-border banking operations and set career goals early on.
Before the internship ends, there is an opportunity to be directly promoted to next year’s reserve manager.
Qualifications and requirements: Expected to graduate between 2025-2026, and current university or graduate students who can join the full-time储备manager program starting in July 2026.
Deadline for submission: November 28, 2025

session_name: Deloitte Consulting Services for Enrolled Agents
session_content:
Company Introduction
Currently, Deloitte has over 450,000 employees globally and provides services in more than 150 countries and regions worldwide. In the Fortune Global 500 list, over 90% of companies receive outstanding quality services from Deloitte’s members. Currently, it has approximately 4,500 employees domestically, located in five branches in Taipei, Hsinchu researches, tax, legal, strategy risk and transaction, as well as technology and transformation. The "strategy, risk, and transaction services" and "technology and transformation services" focus on professional advisory and consulting services, collectively known as Deloitte Consulting Businesses (helping enterprises face changing market environments and challenges to create sustainable value).

Internship Program
Deloitte Seeds Intern Program 2026
Challenging business projects, building your influence.
Turn ideas into action and become the key player in solving problems.
Open a new chapter in your career and find your专属stage.

The 2026 Deloitte Seeds Intern Program is a six-month internship program for the Advisory Services department, where you will have a professional consultant as your mentor to accumulate invaluable practical experience and receive professional skill training. It also offers diverse networking activities to broaden horizons. This allows interns to personally experience daily life in an advisory role and transform creativity into tangible impact through participating in business issues and project work. We welcome students from related fields such as accounting and finance, enterprise management, information engineering, information management, environmental science, and social sciences to join.
This year, we offer seven professional fields for students to explore and apply for. You can choose the most suitable direction based on your profession and interests, together with Deloitte, to embark on your career journey:
ESG Stream
Finance Stream
Digital Transformation Stream
Technical Stream
Cyber Stream
Infrastructure & Real Estate Stream
Actuarial & Insurance Services Stream

Period: January 115th ~ June 115th
Title: Deloitte Seeds Intern Program
Quota: 75
Job Content: Department Internship, Project实战, Regular Feedback and成果Presentation
Qualifications and Requirements: Graduating students (junior year of undergraduate or second year of graduate school) and third-year students
Salary and benefits: $200 per hour

activity_name_activity_session: Fulbright Series Workshop
activity_content: The Center for Bilingual Education invited the former Fulbright Academic Exchange Program to lead an English workshop, bringing a rich academic feast to our students. This series of lectures will allow Taiwan University students to experience and understand American culture in English. Through the sharing and interaction of Fulbright scholars, we hope to broaden students' international perspectives, inspiring their interest and understanding of intercultural communication. It will be a rare opportunity for us to experience the connection across language and culture together, deepening our academic exchanges and cooperation.

session_name: Autumn Adventure
session_content: One Pumpkin Spice Latte, please! Join NTU EMI-ETF Tom and special guest NTUST EMI-ETF Henry for an unbe-leaf-ably fun Culture Unplugged on fall! We’ll explore how fall looks, feels, and tastes around the world, and get crafty as we reflect on what fall means to us.

session_name: Past and Present Scrapbooking/Past and Present Scrapbooking: A Time Travel with Photocards
session_content: Every day we collect “scraps,” small pieces of paper like photos, tickets, and receipts. These scraps aren't trash— they're records of the places we've been and the things we've done. Join NTU EMI-ETF Maya to learn how you can save these scraps and the memories they represent through scrapbooking. We will talk about the significance of scrapbooking in American history and create our own scrapbook pages!\n\nNo prior experience is required, and art supplies will be provided. Just bring yourself and some scraps!

session_name: What's so scary? Halloween and cultures of fear.
session_content: Are there really razor blades in Halloween candy? Why do movie villains all look the same? Join EMI-ETFs Maya and Tom to learn about Halloween and how it shapes cultural perceptions of fear. We’ll explore what fear means around the world, and why what we think is scary is anything but accidental. Come for the candy and face paint, stay for the spook-tacular fun!

Join EMI-ETF Tom to explore the #LandBack movement in Indigenous history, in the United States and around the world, to celebrate Native American Heritage Month.

session_name: Beyond Borders: Exploring Culture and Identity Through Fusion Cuisine
session_content: From Macau-style Portuguese egg tarts to Filipino spaghetti, "fusion cuisine"--a popular term for foods that blend ingredients and cooking techniques from different cultures--exists all around the world. Join Maya and former EMI-ETF Maggie to explore fusion foods and the ways they reflect people’s diverse histories and identities. We’ll also unpack what makes food “authentic” or “inauthentic,” and explore why fusion food can sometimes be divisive.

session_name: ComeTrue: Reality TV and Culture / What? You Can Also Be on a Reality Show! / Viewing Culture Through Reality Television
session_content: Hollywood has told us plenty of stories—what would you do if you became one of them? Reality television is certainly fun, but it also transports us to a unique cultural and historical moment. Join EMI-ETF Tom to learn about what we can learn from reality TV, from Love Island to Chopped to The Amazing Race. Can you handle the spotlight?

activity_name_activity_session: Fulbright Series Workshop
activity_content: The Center for Bilingual Education invited the former Fulbright Academic Exchange Program to lead an English workshop, bringing a rich academic feast to our students. This series of lectures will allow Taiwan University students to experience and understand American culture in English. Through the sharing and interaction of Fulbright scholars, we hope to broaden students' international perspectives, inspiring their interest and understanding of intercultural communication. It will be a rare opportunity for us to experience the connection across language and culture together, deepening our academic exchanges and cooperation.
activity_name_event_page: Fulbright Series Workshop
session_name: Welcoming Winter / Greetings to Winter's Approach
session_content: As the weather gets colder, many of us in Taipei are breaking out our winter coats, hats, and boots, but for many, true winter doesn’t start until the Winter Solstice. This year, the Northern Hemisphere’s Winter Solstice is on December 21st, the shortest day and longest night of the year. Escape the cold and join EMI-ETF Maya to learn more about the significance of the Winter Solstice around the world and to explore the many ways people celebrate it.

session_name: Tabletop Tales: What Do the Games We Play Say About Us?/ Table Games Tell Stories: From Mahjong to Monopoly, They Have Revealed Us!
session_content: Whether it was Mahjong or Monopoly, chances are you grew up playing tabletop games. They’re a fun way to pass time with friends and family, but they also tell unique stories about the histories and cultures they come from. Join EMI-ETF Maya for a lunchtime “game night” to explore the cultural significance of tabletop games. You’ll also try your hand at winning a game or two as you practice your English with some fun, language-based games from the US.

session_name: Funny Business: Laughs Galore: Comedic Party
session_content:
What did the ocean say to the beach? Nothing, it just waved. Join EMI-ETF Tom to celebrate April Fools' Day by learning about the history of the holiday and learning about comedy around the world! We’ll explore why we love to laugh, what it even means to be “funny,” and practice cracking jokes and wordplay across languages. We promise you’ll LOL!
The ocean said nothing; it just "waved." 🌊👋

Let's explore the world of humor together with Tom from EMI-ETF on this lighthearted April Fools' Day! We'll talk about the origins of April Fools' Day, see how people make fun of each other in different cultures, and also ponder: Why do humans love to laugh? What makes something funny?

There will be a chance to try out jokes and wordplay together, challenging yourselves to create laughs using different languages. Get ready your sense of humor and let's laugh, learn, and play with language! Guaranteed to make you LOL 😂✨

activity_name_activity_session: Basic Life Support (BLS) training on days 114/9/24, 25
activity_content:
Office of Academic Affairs Health Center BLS (Basic Life Support) training in September
One, this center was established to enhance the emergency handling capabilities of faculty and staff at three affiliated universities of National Taiwan University for dealing with unexpected accidents. Specialized in-house training on "Basic Life Support" (BLS) has been organized, featuring physicians from the Emergency Medicine Department of Taichung Area Hospital, senior rescue technicians, physical therapists from Taichung Area Hospital, as well as colleagues from this center. The training aims to organize practical exercises to cultivate students' ability to handle crises in real-life situations, thereby ensuring campus and club activity safety.
Two, Date: September 24 and 25th. (9/24 17:15-21:50, 9/25 17:45-21:15)
Three: Target audience: Employees and students of three affiliated universities under National Taiwan University system, with 60 spots filled.
Four、 Location: 2F Wellness Classroom in Health Center.
Five: Content: Trauma emergency, casualty搬运techniques internship (carried out in groups), Hemostasis bandaging techniques internship.
CPR training, CPR technical practice (in groups), and practical exam, written
Test.
Six、 Registration Method: Please register through the school activity registration system.
To obtain a certificate for Basic First Aid, one must participate in the full course, pass the written test, technical assessment, submit pre-course work, and complete payment. Public servants are also eligible for an 8-hour lifetime learning credit.

The "certificate" for this course is optional and if you need one, you must bear the cost of NT$ per certificate.

activity_name_activity_session: Psychology Season Lecture "Autumn • Cultivating Mind in Daily Life" 114-1 Heart Counseling Center Mental Health Promotion Lectures
activity_content:
Taiwan University Student Affairs Office Counseling Center for Students organizes the lecture "Psychological Season - Autumn • Practical Calendar of Mind"

As autumn breezes gently blow, we gradually enter a season of reflection and harvest, during which we often feel anxiousness and pressure amidst our busy lives, or struggle with time constraints and procrastination. This semester’s psychological lecture series focuses on two major themes: “Anxiety Emotions” and “Time Management,” inviting professional psychologists to lead us through their practical experiences and professional perspectives, so that we can re-examine the pace of life and spirit. From understanding the essence of emotions to exploring opportunities for action, the lectures not only provide methods for awareness and adjustment but also accompany us in finding stability and forward momentum during autumn’s footsteps.

Furthermore, to make the lecture content more aligned with the audience’s needs, invite the audience to submit questions or expectations about the lecture topic before the activity by filling out a Google Form regarding the lecture session you have registered for. We will then compile the feedback from the form and provide this information to the speaker as reference. Link to the form: https://forms.gle/FNb7wk9FagqfMWHZ8

Session 1: "Z焦" Point Interview - For Those Who Don't Want Anxiety Control You
Time: September 19, 2025, Friday, 6:30 PM - 8:30 PM
Location: Yuying Teaching Building Room 202
Introduction: Through the lecture, we will learn how to coexist with anxiety, re-understand its impact on life, and transform it.
Speaker: Lee Hsin-teh, Clinical Psychologist
Currently a part-time psychologist at the Chihseh Psychological Counseling Center and the Psychiatry Counseling Center of National Taiwan University, and co-founder of "Psychologist's Midnight Study Hall." She has also written jointly with others the book titled "Your Own Master: A Book for Young Students Answering Their Questions About Adolescence and Uncertainty."

Session 2: Real "Live" Control – For Those Who Don’t Want to Be Delayed
Time: November 10, 2025 (Mon) 6:30 PM - 8:30 PM
Location: Yuying Teaching Building Room 202
Introduction: The lecture will combine neuroscience, psychology, and practical strategies to explore the emotional and cognitive underpinnings of everyday procrastination in order to develop effective time management techniques.
Speaker: Chien Rwamun, Consulting Psychologist
Current director of the Liberte Counseling Center, providing long-term legal aftercare services along with unit colleagues, including domestic violence prevention and addiction to drugs and alcohol. My personal expertise lies in issues related to refusal syndrome, gaming and internet addiction, as well as procrastination. I also offer online courses through CircleWe Counseling to accompany community members in exploring strategies to overcome procrastination.

Note: This series of lectures is conducted entirely in Chinese.
activity_name_event_page: Psychology Season Lecture "Autumn • Cultivating Mind in Daily Life" 114-1 Heart Counseling Center Mental Health Promotion Lectures
session_name: "Focus" Interview - For Those Who Don't Want Anxiety to Control Them
session_content: Overthinking, worrying about bad things happening, caring too much about negative reviews, and criticizing oneself can all be signs of excessive anxiety affecting your life. While anxiety won’t completely disappear, we can learn to coexist with it and re-understand its impact on our lives.
instructor: 李炯德 臨床心理師
organizer_unit: 學生事務處學生心理輔導中心

activity_name_event_page: Library Database Training at the Academy of Social Sciences Library (Spring Semester of Academic Year 20114)
session_name: SAGE Research Methods - Research Methods Database
session_content:
The SAGE Research Methods database is a cutting-edge, one-stop online resource developed for social science research methods that aids teachers in teaching research methods, designing research projects, understanding or implementing new research methods, conducting project research, and writing research findings.

It includes over 1,000 books and reference works, as well as journal articles; over 2,200 case studies of actual research projects; over 500 sets of teaching data to assist students in mastering data analysis through hands-on experience; and four packages containing over 1,500 teaching videos that bring theories and concepts of research methods, statistics, and evaluation to life.

The database features the following:
Advanced search tools allow you to browse and search for data, guiding you to the information you need.
The research methods map can present a graphical representation of research method terminology, concepts, authors, and literature.
The method list allows you to save books, chapters, and journal articles for later reading or sharing on the website with others.
Researchers can explore their chosen research methods in the reference materials, which are both deep and broad and can be adjusted as needed.
Online reading, printing, and sending of full contents are available, covering 700 research method books included in SAGE.
SAGE Research Methods has a rich database and special features that can suggest relevant research methods for you and link to related authors.
Accessible on SAGE is a selection of research method videos featuring some top authors who have been specially authorized.
organizer_unit: 圖書館社會科學資源服務組

session_name: Statista - Comprehensive Data Database
session_content:
Statista is a global platform for data and business information that provides extensive statistical data, reports, and in-depth analysis. Data comes from 170 countries, over 22,500 sources, offering more than 80,000 industries/topics including: advertising & marketing, agriculture, petrochemicals, construction, consumer goods, e-commerce, politics & economics, energy, finance & insurance, health & pharmaceuticals, networking & telecommunications, lifestyle, media publishing, metals/aerospace/electronics/industrial machinery/auto ship manufacturing, real estate, retail trade, business services, society, sports & leisure, technology & communications, transportation & logistics, and tourism.

The database features the following:
Combining third-party data with our own analysis and synthesis to ensure a wide range of data sources, after thorough verification and providing analysis.
Industry spans multiple sectors (B2B/B2C), including traditional industries, technology, manufacturing, and services.
Platform is intuitive and highly user-friendly.
Cover topics related to consumer terminals, e-commerce, digital economy, innovation, and emerging trends.
Organized data chart assets can be directly applied to business presentations.
Visual output can be in the form of charts and provides automatic generation for various download formats (PPT, PDF, PNG, XLS) as well as reference paper formats.
Support for different systems Citation (Automatic citation output, APA, Chicago, IEEE, Harvard, MLA, Bluebook).
New Research AI.

Speaker: Dr. Chang Haoxiang from the Family Medicine Department of Taipei Medical University Hospital.
Third, physical location: Health Center二楼卫生室; Online participants will be provided with a link via email three days before the course.

Participants in the physical courses are not provided with lunch; please arrange your own.

"Brotherhood in Play: A Sharing Session on Foreign Invested Enterprises vs. Entrepreneur Career Dual Track"

This will help inspire their own career directions and values.

session_name: HSBC
session_content:
Company Introduction
HSBC is one of the largest banks and financial services institutions globally, headquartered in the UK and operating in over 62 countries and regions. HSBC has been established with a branch in Taipei for 40 years, showcasing excellence in personal finance, wealth management, and corporate banking.

Speech Theme
2026 Young Banker Development Program

Plan Overview
HSBC Young Banker Development Program aims to cultivate future leaders of HSBC, tailored specifically for the professional knowledge and personal skills required for a career in the financial sector. This program includes both the Graduate Programme and Internship Programme, offering full-time employment and summer internship opportunities. We warmly welcome you/you to join us if you are passionate about developing your career in a truly international bank!

Eligibility: All require qualifications for working in Taiwan; GPA of 3.0/4.0 or equivalent; fluent in both Chinese and English, proactive, enthusiastic about challenges, and committed to excellence.
Graduate Programme: Through实战 rotations within the business units, cross-border collaborative efforts, challenging perspectives, and igniting personal potential, become leaders of HSBC’s new generation.
Provides: Opportunities for rotation across units in the International Wealth & Premier Banking (IWB) business, including client channels, digital products, risk management, and strategy projects, to help you/you become a well-rounded talent.
Educational requirement: Graduates or fresh graduates expected to graduate in 2026 (undergraduate degree, major不限)
Planning timeline: 18 months

Internship Programme: Participants who excel during summer internships will be eligible for a 2027 Young Banker ticket!
International Wealth & Premier Banking (International Wealth Management and Excellence in Financial Services)
Educational requirement: undergraduate or graduate students currently enrolled in any university (majoring is not a requirement). Expected graduation in 2027.
Schedule: 8 weeks

Apply here: https://reurl.cc/LnZKN7
Application date: From now until November 5, 2025; first come, first served.
Reporting date: Expected arrival in July 2026
Note: Each applicant can only choose one region and one program for application; please consider carefully before applying.

12:20-12:30 Opening & Group Photo
HSBC Taiwan Introduction and Development Program Overview
12:50-13:05 Introduction by Senior Sisters
Hosted discussion with seniors and peers at 13:05-13:25

session_name: Yushan Bank
session_content:
Speech Theme
Financial Career Sharing (Private Banking Business, M&A, Corporate Finance)

Activity Introduction
This session will feature a career share from Chen Yingjen sister, a current Private Banking Center Fund PM at present, and Ku Yuwei brother, a Current PRM from Xin Yi Finance & Gold Regional Center.
Participation in this lecture can provide you with an understanding of:
🚀Career Development for Reserve Officers at MA
Private banking wealth management services内容
Corporate Financial Business Content

Welcome for students from all majors and grades who want to learn about the financial industry or become a Master Analyst to register and participate!
Upperclassmen or second-year masters preferred!

Company Introduction
Yushan Bank
"Different Jade Mountain, Different Future"
Honesty, integrity, professionalism, responsibility
Naming ourselves after Yushan, our resolve is written in our name. In 1992, a team of professionals with a shared mission to "operate the best bank" established an expert-led system and worked together to create a bank for bankers.
Human capital is a leading indicator and also a key indicator.
Yushan upholds core values of integrity and professionalism, consistently dedicated to nurturing outstanding talent. By combining financial expertise, technological innovation, risk management, and corporate social responsibility, it continues to move towards its shared vision.
Innovation is both thinking and action.
Yushan leverages technology to lead in launching numerous innovative digital financial services, integrating physical and digital channels to create seamless service networks that enhance customer experiences and共创美好生活的愿景.

[Introduction from Speaker Chen Yingzhen]
Current Position: Private Banking Division, Fund PM at Qishe Bank (Qishe is a stylized form of "玉山", meaning "Jade Mountain")
Experience: Aisino Bank (玉山銀行) 2020 MA
2021-2023玉山銀行個金總處 process planning
Yushan Bank Private Banking Headquarters 2024
Education: National Taiwan University Business Research Institute
Motto: Seize every opportunity to achieve oneself uniquely
Introduction: After graduating in 2020, I joined Qishe Bank as an MA and underwent a six-month rotation before deciding to work in the back office unit as a process planner to assist in refining depositing, remitting, loaning, and wealth management processes. This role allowed me to enhance customer experience. In 2024, I had the opportunity to rotate to a product manager position for funds, coinciding with the launch of policies by the Asian Asset Management Center, which enabled me to engage with and plan both public offering and private placement fund products. This allowed me to better understand customer needs from a more front-line perspective, meeting the demands of a broader range of customers, while also growing the wealth management scale through collaboration with my team.

Presenter Qiu Junwei introduces
Current Position: PRM of Trust & Gold Center, Qishe Region, Taixing Bank
Experience: Audit Intern at KPMG Accounting Firm
Education: National Taiwan University School of Accountancy
Motto: Pessimists are often correct, optimists usually succeed.
Introduction: I am邱軍瑋, graduated from the Department of Information Management at National Taiwan University in R08. After graduation, I joined the Yuanshuang MA program and have been with it until now. After undergoing a two-month training period and rotation, I was assigned to an operations unit where I currently hold the position of ARM for three and a half years. Through interacting with diverse financial products, I gradually entered the vast legal finance field, building my own knowledge map, transforming from a naive newcomer into a customer's shadow CFO. Over the past few years, I have grown alongside customers while creating double-digit profit growth with the team.

session_name: Yutong Automobile
session_content:
Speech Theme
Career Parking Lot Race - Drive Your Career Path

Company Introduction
Yamaha Motors is the exclusive distributor for global automotive brands Toyota, Lexus, and HINO in Taiwan, holding a market share of No. 1 in the auto industry for many consecutive years, and also leading profit in Taiwan's automobile sector. In recent years, with the trend towards MaaS (Mobility as a Service), it has entered through shared services by launching new ventures such as iRent, yoxi, and AndyPay to promote its new mobility transportation service.

Activity Introduction
E Chen will take you on a journey to explore Taichung University days and the growth journey and turning points after joining Cathay Auto – from the initial intention of joining Cathay, to practical experiences in the company and cross-disciplinary challenges, so that classmates can better understand the culture and workplace landscape of Cathay Automotive.

At the same time, we will impart the first-hand job hunting techniques, including skills for writing resumes, key points for interview preparation, and characteristics that companies value. This will help students secure ideal positions and smoothly start their careers in the future job hunt race!
Each attendee will receive a prize ticket at the event, with the opportunity to win Marshall portable Bluetooth speakers and Disney联名 merchandise and other gifts!

Resume and Interview Skills Sharing 13:20-13:50

Current position: General Manager of Management Department at Hanting Auto
Experience: Human Resources Department Head at Hanting Auto
Taiho Auto Investor Relations Department Head
Vice Section Leader of Huiqi Auto Affiliate Enterprise Department
Education: National Taiwan University Business Research Institute
National Taiwan University Mathematics Department
Mr. Chen Zhanyu, Minister of Transportation and Communications, holds a Master’s degree in Business Administration from Taiwan University and a Bachelor’s degree in Mathematics. With nearly two decades at Auto Home Services, he has held key positions such as Deputy Department Head in the Related Enterprises Room, Investor Relations Room, Human Resources Room, among others. Currently, he serves as the General Manager of the Management Department. Spanning mergers and acquisitions, human resources, and overall management across various sectors, he has accumulated extensive practical experience and a multifaceted perspective.

session_name: DBS Bank
session_content:
Company Introduction
DBS Group entered the Taiwanese market in 1983 with a commitment to providing comprehensive financial services, including consumer finance, wealth management, SME and corporate banking, from a customer-centric perspective. DBS has consistently upheld its promise to深耕Taiwan by not only increasing capital several times but also acquiring Baohua Bank in 2008 and completing the transfer of ANZ’s personal and wealth management business in Taiwan in 2017. In August 2023, it consolidated DBS (Taiwan)'s consumer finance operations, further expanding its footprint in Taiwan to become the largest foreign bank in the country.

Position Recruitment
DBS Reserve Officer

Job Content
DBS Bank (Taiwan)'s 2026 Management Associate Programme (MAP) officially kicks off, training areas cover consumer finance, corporate finance, operations management, and technology.
Highlights of the plan:
12 months of training and guidance, thoroughly strengthening your career growth and development
Participating in challenging project initiatives accelerates your career growth.
Expert leadership guidance to help you advance step by step.
Based on individual performance, one can advance to the position of manager in as little as 18 to 24 months.

Qualification Requirements
Welcome to domestic and foreign university and research institute graduates or those with less than two years of work experience (open to all academic backgrounds and work experiences), who possess innovative thinking, entrepreneurial spirit, and an international perspective. Applicants are also required to graduate in July 2026 and formally join DBS Bank (Taiwan) after completing national military service.

Compensation and Benefits
A salary and benefits package that is competitively priced, with an annual salary of one million yuan enjoyed from the very first year.
