session_content:
Company Introduction
September 26, 2024, Taipei – The Japanese fashion brand GU, which embodies the brand spirit "YOUR FREEDOM Let Fashion Be Free," has reached a ten-year milestone since opening its first physical store, "ATT 4 FUN Store," in Taiwan in 2014. Over the past decade, GU has continuously expanded its presence in Taiwan, currently operating 23 physical stores across northern, central, and southern regions, as well as one online store.
In addition to continuously listening to consumer voices for product development and improvement, providing trendy fashion items for consumers who love to dress up; it also offers enriched styling inspiration through constantly evolving shopping services so that more people can enjoy the fun of free dressing! Looking ahead to the next decade, GU aims to lead global fashion trends with its strategic goal of globalization. At the same time, it continues to deeply penetrate the Taiwan market and hopes to become a beloved national clothing brand among Taiwanese consumers.

Theme/Topic of Activity
2026 Global Reserve Elite Program

Plan Overview and Four Key Features
The GU "Global Reserve Elite Program" is a talent development program aimed at international individuals who aspire to become "global leaders." It selects outstanding candidates with a desire for self-challenge and growth potential, nurturing them into future business leaders alongside the group. They are rapidly promoted to mid-to-high level managers.

Realism and Rapid Promotion
Age and background are all problems! Become a professional manager based on your personal capabilities quickly!
Compensation system is competitive in the market.
Starting salary upon joining NT$67,000/month (with opportunities for two annual bonuses). Challenges a million-dollar salary.
Growth incubator capabilities and diverse development opportunities
Developing a holistic mindset and capability for business leaders, providing comprehensive education and development systems. Pursue diverse careers in sales departments and headquarters according to your aspirations!
Stepping Towards International Stage
GU is a rapidly growing enterprise, we hold high aspirations and a mindset of challenges. Currently operating in the Japanese, Chinese, Hong Kong, and American markets, we look forward to each GMC joining us to create more opportunities for international stages together!


Qualification Requirements
Applicants of all educational backgrounds, ages, and work experiences are welcome.

Wages and Benefits
Starting salary upon hiring NT$67,000/month (with opportunities for two annual bonuses). Challenge a million-dollar salary.

activity_name_activity_session: Outsourced Safety Management Training Education
activity_content: This course aims to assist government agency personnel in mastering core knowledge and practical skills for information service procurement and outsourcing management. The content of the course combines legal requirements, contract template application principles, and cybersecurity inspection points, and explains common omissions and risk mitigation strategies through real-case analysis to help participants enhance their compliance and cybersecurity oversight capabilities at various stages such as bidding, evaluation, performance execution, and acceptance. Additionally, the course will delve into the security reinforcement measures for information system procurement at each stage and outsourcing vendor audit practices, ensuring the school's information systems are secure and resilient throughout their entire lifecycle.

activity_name_activity_session: Writing with Two Core: Foundation Knowledge Augmented by AI
activity_content:
Strong AI capabilities built on solid writing expression soft power

(1)
Topic: New Assistant for Abstract Writing: Leveraging AI Tools to Enhance Chinese and English Research Abstracts (Taught in Chinese)
Time: September 16 (Tue) 14:20 - 16:00
Introduction: "Abstracts" are an essential component of academic writing that require conveying core information within limited word counts, presenting a challenge for researchers. This lecture will guide participants in mastering the structure and knowledge related to writing abstracts in both Chinese and English, then use AI tools to optimize the content, thereby cultivating writers' self-awareness and critical thinking skills while improving their efficiency and quality of writing. (No on-site registration provided)
Speaker: Chen Qiaoling, Su Hongling (Writing Teaching Center teachers)

(2)
Topic: Read Your Literature Well: Journal Reading Techniques and Essential Tools (Taught in Chinese)
Time: 10/01 (Wed) 15:30 - 17:10
Introduction: This workshop will introduce reading techniques for English journal papers and combine practical sessions to help学员 acquire AI tools that are helpful for reading, thereby improving reading efficiency and comprehension. (Registration is available on-site.)
Speaker: Yishu Jun (Writing Teaching Center teacher)

(3)
Topic: AI Mastery Research Method: Quickly and Accurately Nailing Down Chapters of Literature in One Go (Taught in Chinese)
Time: 10/29 (Wed) 13:20 - 15:00
Introduction: With piles of literature? Time is like leftover coffee grounds? In the first half, we will lead students to unlock the secrets of reading and writing literature efficiently; in the second half, a simulated workshop on writing will apply various AI tools so that you can fully understand how each AI tool assists at every stage of literature writing – from brainstorming to completion – with AI by your side!
Speaker: Li Weidian (Director of Writing Instruction Center)

(4)
Exploring Data and Telling Its Story with AI - AI-Enabled Data Commentary (Delivered in English)
Time and Date: November 25 (Tuesday) 1:20 PM - 3:00 PM
Synopsis: In this hands-on session, you’ll learn how to use Julius.ai to explore and visualize large datasets and how ChatGPT can help you turn your findings into clear, effective commentary. Perfect for students working on research or data-driven writing projects.
Speaker: Kevin Kau, Project Instructor, AWEC

activity_name_event_page: CARDO 114-1 TCP Student Recruitment Meeting and Foundation Course
session_name: KPMG Asia Pacific Sustainability
session_content:
Company Introduction
KPMG’s Sustainability Advisory (Taiwan) is the first sustainability advisory team established among Taiwan's four major accounting firms, witnessing Taiwan's development trajectory from CSR to ESG. The team members come from diverse fields including accounting, public policy, public relations and marketing, economics, society, management, supply chain management, energy, environmental engineering, and chemical engineering. They possess extensive professional experience in industry services, capable of providing comprehensive advisory, consulting, and assurance services.
Facing rapidly evolving domestic and international sustainability standards and issues, KPMG Ernst & Young assists clients in developing sustainable solutions that have a unique corporate DNA. It transforms ESG risks and challenges into new opportunities and creates a win-win model for the company, its customers, and suppliers. This helps Taiwanese companies to enter the global stage and become more competitive!

[TCP Plan Content]
Area of Training: ESG Practice Management Consultant
Goal: Cultivate future leaders with a sustainable perspective, create comprehensive sustainability consultants and green managers人才。
Course Plan: Introduce trends and concepts of corporate sustainability, arrange industry-forward sustainability special topic projects and discussions, including sustainable finance, enterprise net-zero and decarbonization transformation, climate change and enterprise financial risk assessment, circular economy, non-financial value evaluation, social entrepreneurship topics, etc.
All courses include lectures, workshops, and special topic reports, and combine sustainable career planning sharing and visits to sustainable enterprises to deepen学员's practical and theoretical knowledge, establish a comprehensive foundation for understanding sustainable development.
Career Development: After the course ends, KPMG will select outstanding graduates as interns or formal employees on a preference basis.
Schedule: See CARDO website

[Registration Method]
The seminar and the first class on November 11 (Thursday) must be attended.
Fill out the TCP Application Form
3. Provide a Chinese-English resume: Personal bilingual CV/resume in any format, as long as it is simple and readable with key experiences highlighted. File size limit 2MB (Note: the Chinese resume should answer questions and the English resume should be integrated into one file). Send to sherylchang@kpmg.com.tw.
Subject of the Letter: Application for TCP KPMG ESG Practice Management Consultant _ Student ID Name
Resume Name: Resume_StudentID_Name
At the end of the Chinese resume page, answer the following two questions (in Chinese). Each question is limited to 500 words or less.
What do you think that corporate sustainability consultants bring value to a company’s operations, and how can they drive change in sustainable development (including environmental and social issues)? What are the three most important qualities of a corporate sustainability consultant in your view? Please explain your reasons.
What sustainability issue are you most concerned about in which industry? Why is this issue the one you're most concerned about? Please provide examples of companies that perform well and poorly in this area (one example each), explaining why you think each case is strong/weak, and if you were a sustainability consultant, how you would assist these companies in improving their performance on this sustainability issue.
Application deadline: September 18 (Thu) before 23:59
Admission Notice: CARDO will send out an email on October 7 (Tue) to notify the admitted students, who are required to confirm their attendance within two days.

session_name: Opening Doors to Research in American and British Literature and Linguistics – MLA International Bibliography
session_content: MLA International Bibliography is a权威 literature index produced by the Modern Language Association, covering fields such as literature, linguistics, drama, film, folklore, rhetoric and composition research, among others. It contains over three million entries and provides international publications in multiple languages, free online courses, and teaching videos. Supplementary resources like MLA Thesaurus and MLA Directory of Periodicals help researchers to master subject terms and journal submission information.\nLinguistics Abstracts Online covers literature related to linguistics and language studies, encompassing fields such as applied linguistics, phonology, language and psychology, semantics, and language disorders. It provides high-quality journal indexes and abstracts. The database uses the EBSCO Thesaurus system for precise searching and is suitable for use in courses and research on linguistics, psycholinguistics, and language education.

session_name: Web of Science and Journal Citation Reports – Exploring Core Literature and Research Trends
session_content: The Core Collection of Web of Science covers various fields including Science (SCIE), Social Sciences (SSCI), and Arts & Humanities (AHCI), featuring reviewed journals, conference proceedings, and books. It reveals thematic connections between articles through citation networks. This session will teach you how to use the practical search functions and diverse filtering mechanisms of Web of Science in conjunction with search techniques and strategies to quickly identify high-quality academic articles. Additionally, it will help researchers understand trends and distributions in research by using citation networks and analysis tools, aiding more effective thematic exploration.

session_name: The Writing Research God Assistant – Full Open Techniques for Zotero
session_content: Facing a vast amount of academic resources and complicated citation formats, you need a reliable helper, and Zotero is such a tool. This course teaches you how to install Zotero, quickly collect online bibliographies and PDF files, and automatically organize them. You will learn how to insert citations in Word with one click, generate complete reference lists, and flexibly adjust the format of references. It will also introduce advanced features of Zotero, such as cloud backup, shared literature libraries for multiple users, and mobile applications, helping you create an efficient and painless writing process.

session_name: A panoramic view of historical events – ProQuest and Taiwan News Smart Network
session_content: The ProQuest Chinese Newspapers Collection includes 12 English-language historical newspapers such as The North China Herald, China Press, and China Weekly Review that were published in China, along with full-page images of the original papers.\nTaiwan News Smart Network covers over nine major newspapers in Taiwan (including the United Daily News, China Times, Free Journal, Business Times, etc.), accumulating more than 13 million "news headline extracts" since 1951, and supports features such as thematic browsing, cross-newspaper search, and full-text image linking.\nThis session will introduce how to use these two databases to search for news, magazines, and historical newspapers. We welcome teachers and students from related fields such as history, journalism, politics, sociology, and literature to register and participate.

session_name: Stay informed about international news – The New York Times
session_content: The New York Times is compiled by a large group of professional editors, focusing on international news events, advocating for press freedom, and providing readers with timely and accurate information. Its fairness and authority are well-regarded. The columns and editorials also have stable and substantial loyal reader bases. The New York Times News Database not only includes newspaper content but also extends out many distinctive resources through these valuable news contents, suitable as references for teachers in designing lesson plans, and practical resources for students to learn independently and cultivate an international perspective.

activity_name_activity_session: Academic Affairs Campus Safety Center X Bullying Prevention Committee for Mental Health Promotion Series of Activities (Chengzhong Campus)
activity_content: Harmonizing school bullying incidents, analyzing case studies.
activity_name_event_page: Academic Affairs Campus Safety Center X Bullying Prevention Committee for Mental Health Promotion Series of Activities (Chengzhong Campus)
session_name: Harmonizing school bullying incidents, analyzing case studies
session_content: The speaker discusses campus bullying incidents and types based on their experience as a member of an investigation committee for such cases, then delves into the key points of the "Guidelines for Preventing and Addressing School Bullying," focusing on the mechanisms to prevent and address school bullying. They also list ways events are handled, including: implementing preventive counseling mechanisms, establishing professional, fair, and effective handling systems, strengthening supervisory authorities' responsibilities, ensuring the rights and interests of those who have been bullied, building a talent pool for mediation and investigation professionals, and establishing sound supporting mechanisms. After the open course ends at 16:30, colleagues can bring up real-life cases from their surroundings to raise doubts about whether they fall under the scope of bullying. (Open for questions from 16:30 to 17:30)
organizer_unit: 學生事務處校園安全中心
note: 敬備餐盒

activity_name_activity_session: Library 2025 Comprehensive Service for All-Tier Care: Reading Light Travel – Online Book Sharing Session
activity_content: The library, in coordination with the School’s Center for Research and Development of Comprehensive Education, which is统筹planning the “Taishan University All-around Care Service Plan,” implements reading promotion activities based on the concept of “wellness” (全人健康), encompassing eight dimensions: social, environmental, occupational, emotional, intellectual, spiritual, physical, and psychological. As we approach the end of the year, four online book-sharing sessions will be held. These sessions aim to soothe colleagues’ moods through good books and provide them with different forms of inspiration and insights. We welcome those interested to register and participate.
activity_name_event_page: Library 2025 Comprehensive Service for All-Tier Care: Reading Light Travel – Online Book Sharing Session
session_name: From "Set Out with an Empathetic World in Mind" on What Life Sees When Traveling
session_content:
His footprint spans eight countries in the Americas and Africa, from which he tries to find the purpose of his own existence by looking into the lives of others – Philippines, Ethiopia, Iran, Bangladesh, Colombia and Ecuador, Sri Lanka, and Oman.

Online Book Sharing Event Invites Author of "Starting Out, Feeling the Vulnerable World," Zhong Weilun, to Narrate His Experiences Over the Past Few Years: He Has Walked Along Wet Lakeshores, The Edge of Volcanic Fields, Crowded City Streets, and True Ancient Cities. Witnessing Magnificent Yet Easily Vanishing Scenery, Attempting Empathy for Those He Met on His Journeys, Reflecting on His Own Inner Desires, And Discovering Resilience in the Face of Vulnerability Among People.

Speaker/Zhong Weilun
Travel enthusiast and graphic designer. Studied visual communication and fashion design, lived in New York for four years. After a period of Southeast Asian travel, returned home where all experiences are written in the book "Travelling Deeper into Deep Asia."
Like life cannot go back, a journey can only move forward. Setting out once again to traverse eight countries in the Americas and Africa is no longer just travel but moving towards the periphery to see things and people forgotten, and experiencing and reflecting on them in "Departure Together with Fragile World."
Preferring to be socially awkward and sharp-tongued rather than attention-seeking, while still having soft-hearted tolerance.
organizer_unit: 圖書館推廣服務組
target_audience: Student, Faculty, Staff, Project Personnel, Hospital Staff, Alumni, Academia Sinica, Retired Faculty, Retired Staff

activity_name_activity_session: Workplace Sexual Harassment Prevention and Training for Hosts in Public Places - Employee Education on Responsibility
activity_content:
One, pursuant to the 2023 academic year administrative staff training plan of our school and the measures and procedures for handling sexual harassment prevention and complaints under Point 6, Item 1 of our school's guidelines on preventing and addressing sexual harassment.
Second, the "Gender Equality in Employment Act" amended on August 16, 2011 and implemented on March 8, 2013, strengthened the employer's awareness and responsibility in preventing sexual harassment. It stipulates that employers must take immediate effective corrective and remedial measures regardless of whether they have received a victim’s complaint or heard about any incidents of sexual harassment. They should also provide or refer to assistance such as counseling, medical care, and psychological counseling. This act aims to improve the protection and support system for victims. Additionally, the Ministry of Labor revised the "Guidelines on Measures for Preventing Sexual Harassment in Workplaces" on January 17, 2013 to clarify the measures employers should take. These guidelines specify the employer's prevention principles, education and training, complaint channels, investigation procedures, criteria for setting up a complaint handling unit, its composition, disciplinary actions, and other related measures. Article 9 of these guidelines states that employers must have their employees undergo workplace sexual harassment prevention education and training. For personnel in managerial positions or involved in the handling, investigation, and decision-making of sexual harassment cases, regular educational training sessions are held annually.
III. Based on the above, to enable all levels of administrators at our school to understand the key points of the amendments to the "Gender Equality Act" and the "Guidelines for Preventing Sexual Harassment in Workplaces," and to strengthen the sensitivity, awareness, and identification of power imbalances among management layers, we are jointly creating a gender-sensitive workplace. We therefore organize the following educational training sessions for administrators at all levels. Please encourage all levels of administrators to enthusiastically register and participate in these sessions. The agenda for these sessions is as follows:
Time: August 20, 2014 (Wednesday) from 10 am to 12 pm.
Location: Administrative Building First Conference Room, Central Campus.
Topic: Preventing Workplace Sexual Harassment and Host Employer Liability Responsibilities.
Lecturer: Huang Yuling Attorney.
Eligibility and Number of Participants: 100 people (with staff members with a comparable managerial position given priority for training).
Four, registration method: Please complete the registration by 5 PM on August 15th (Friday) in the school activity registration system. Participants who are admitted will be notified via email, and training participants can register for leave of absence.
Fifth, participants should arrive at the venue 10 to 15 minutes early on the day of the seminar and sign in. No separate notice will be sent. Participants who attend the entire event will be granted 2 hours of public service lifetime learning credits and a meal box will be provided. Additionally, please bring your own environmentally friendly water cup to comply with environmental policies; no disposable non-disposable cups will be provided.

Topic of Study: Stress Relief Workshop in the Workplace - Alcohol Ink Art Experience Class.
Workshop: Huang Yuchian, Clinical Psychologist
Seminar time: 2:30 PM - 4:30 PM on Wednesday, August 13, 2019.
Learning location: School Main Campus Hallian Building 3rd floor Conference Room 307.
Training Participants and Quota: 30 staff members of this school.
Registration method:
Participants in this training should first obtain approval from their unit's supervisor, and then register as either official leave or personal leave. They must complete registration through the school’s activity registration system by 9:00 AM on Wednesday, July 16 (inclusive) to noon on Friday, August 1 (inclusive). The registration system will close once the quota is full, and there will be no opportunity for on-site registration thereafter.
Four, please arrive at the seminar venue 10 to 15 minutes early on the day of the training for signing in. No separate notice will be sent. Participants who attend the entire session will be granted a lifetime learning hour credit of 2 hours. Additionally, please bring your own environmentally friendly water cup as part of our commitment to environmental policies and do not provide single-use disposable cups.
Five, please find attached a course outline for your reference. For any related questions, please contact the Retirement Insurance Section (course content, 02-33665951) or the Training Section (registration system matters, 02-33665941) of the Personnel Department.
Six, the course outline is as follows:
Would you like to temporarily let go of busy work and allow your soul to settle into the flow of colors?
We sincerely invite you to participate in this alcohol ink art experience course, where you can enjoy the fun of creation through free-flowing colors and techniques, relieve stress, cultivate beauty, and add a touch of art's warmth to your life.
Knowing basic techniques and operating methods of alcohol ink through courses, experiencing the creative process of color flow and diffusion, completing a unique artistic coaster exclusively for you, and relaxing while enhancing your artistic素养and living aesthetics.

activity_name_activity_session: 2025 Occupational Health Lecture (7): What is Workplace Bullying? How Should One Handle Workplace Bullying?
activity_content:
Abstract
In the workplace, are sarcastic remarks, high-pressure coaching, or public humiliation reasonable management and conflict resolution, or do they constitute workplace bullying? This lecture will analyze common inappropriate behaviors in the workplace through actual cases by a lawyer and assist everyone in clarifying what constitutes workplace bullying from a legal perspective, as well as how to handle it.

Activity time: July 28, 2025 (Monday) from 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM
Location: Cisco Webex Online Video Conference Live Stream

This activity will be conducted through Cisco Webex online video conferencing. Please prepare your own computer or mobile device to participate, complete the online check-in and attend the entire lecture. Participants who have done so will receive 2 hours of digital learning credits for faculty and administrative staff members at our school.

Explore daily habits and their impact on work and life

activity_name_activity_session: 114th Network and Security Technology Conference
activity_content:
Would you like to enrich your self with computer information knowledge? Would you like to improve your information implementation skills? Would you like to cultivate digital technology capabilities?

It's time again to elevate our information literacy for the year!

The Knowledge Center invites experts from various fields to share rich theoretical knowledge and practical experience annually. This year, it has planned diverse network technology courses focusing on the topics of AI and cybersecurity, which cover subjects such as information security, artificial intelligence, big data, cloud applications, and campus copyright issues. The content includes foundational principles, actual case studies, and hands-on practice sessions.

All courses are free of charge, regardless of information background knowledge, welcome to enroll enthusiastically.

Note:
A satisfaction survey QR code is provided for each course. Please scan the QR code and fill out relevant data before submitting if you need public service hours.
If you register for any of our courses in this activity and attend less than two out of every three registered sessions, it will affect your right to participate in future sessions of that course.

Note: These terms are typically used in network configurations and refer to specific NAT (Network Address Translation) behaviors. "Loopback" refers to a scenario where packets destined for an internal IP address are sent back to the same internal host, effectively creating a loop within the network. "Hairpin" refers to a situation where packets that would normally be routed out of the network are instead forwarded internally, as if they were being reflected or mirrored.

session_name: Kubernetes Private Cloud Implementation Guide: The First Step in Enterprise IT Modernization - On-Premises Event
session_content:
Course content:
With the thriving development of cloud technologies, more and more enterprises are choosing to adopt private cloud architectures to enhance security, improve performance, and maintain flexibility. This course will focus on Kubernetes as its core, guiding学员to deeply understand how to manage and practice private cloud platform services using Kubernetes.
The course integrates theoretical foundations with practical exercises, covering Kubernetes core concepts, container deployment, automated operations, and enterprise-level integrations (such as CI/CD, monitoring, identity authentication integration, etc.). It is suitable for IT managers, DevOps engineers, system architects, or technical personnel looking to adopt modern cloud architectures.

Course Outline:
Why Choose Kubernetes to Build Private Clouds
Explanation of Kubernetes Architecture and Core Components
Monitoring and Logging Management
Deployment and Resource Management
Security and Access Control
Integrate CI/CD Workflow

session_name: Exploring the Introduction of Zero Trust Architecture in Government Agencies – Physical Session
session_content:
Course Overview:
In the digital age, traditional perimeter-based security measures are insufficient to address hybrid threats both inside and outside. Zero Trust Architecture (Zero Trust Architecture) is now at the core of the key transformation in government agencies and corporate cybersecurity efforts.
This course will lead学员 to quickly master the key content of government zero trust architecture, combining reference guidelines for government agencies' adoption, introducing in a clear and concise manner the concept, dimensions, and adoption strategies for implementing a zero trust architecture at government agencies. This will help学员 who want to adopt a zero trust architecture at government agencies establish correct concepts.

Outline:
Overview of Zero Trust Architecture
Explanation of Government Zero Trust Architecture
Introduction and Challenges
Steps and Process for Adopting Zero Trust Architecture
Common Challenges and Solutions

session_name: Google Cloud Storage - The Heart-Thumping Portable Treasure – Online Session
session_content:
Course introduction
This course is designed for educators who want to improve the efficiency and security of managing digital data. In a two-hour session, we will start with the basics of Google Cloud Storage and delve into advanced file organization techniques, including workspace organization, systematic folder structures, naming conventions, and effective use of tags.
Say goodbye to disorganized files and instantly have an irresistible portable treasure bag!
The course will focus on two major pillars: "Security" and "Collaboration," helping you understand how to set up permissions and practical scenarios, ensuring that your important data is adequately protected during sharing and collaboration. At the same time, learn how to effectively utilize different workspace areas, mastering project management for both personal and team projects, course material development, and student assignment submission in various work and teaching situations, thereby enhancing your digital productivity!

Course Outline:
Google Cloud Storage Overview
Google Cloud Drive Main Sections Explanation and Applications
Google Cloud Drive File Management Tips
Google Cloud Disk Instant Editing and Collaboration
Google Cloud Disk: Differences Between Streaming and Synchronization

Course details:
Understand that in addition to "Personal Cloud Drive" in the cloud storage, there are also different sections such as "Work Area," "Shared Cloud Drive," "Computer," and "Starred Items," each serving a specific purpose.
Learn how to establish and manage "workspaces" to centrally manage files related to ongoing projects.
Creating a "shared cloud drive" and collaborating on ongoing projects within an organization
Mastering file management techniques such as using colors, emojis, stars, and advanced search features helps us efficiently and quickly find and manage files.
How to learn how to use cloud storage for real-time file management and collaboration on devices such as smartphones, tablets, and computers.
Learn how to install and set up "Cloud Disk PC Edition", managing cloud files on your local computer in streaming or sync mode.

Apps and Devices: Google Drive

Course Objectives:
Only free-tier educators (teachers, staff, students)
Educators (teachers, staff, students) at schools have already purchased GWS ePlus.

Faculty Explanation: Guo He Lin Cheng Hsun Lecturer - Google Certified Trainer
Presently
Officially Certified Professional Teacher by Google
Officially Certified Training Instructor by Google
Google Official Certified Administrator
International School Network Administrator
曾担任

session_name: Google Workspace Ecosystem Boosts Your Productivity – Online Sessions
session_content:
Course introduction
In today's efficient team collaborations, do you yearn for smoother communication and file collaboration methods? The core feature of Google productivity tools, "shared editing," is designed precisely for this.
This course will take you deep into exploring the applications of tools like Google Docs, Sheets, and Slides. We'll cover everything from collaborative real-time editing, setting permissions, assigning tasks, tracking revision histories, to how to use annotation and suggestion modes for precise communication. You’ll learn how to integrate the Google Workspace ecosystem so that team members can break down communication barriers regardless of when or where they are, significantly improving project execution efficiency and achieving seamless teamwork.

Course Outline:
Core concepts and foundations of team collaboration
Using Google Workspace real-time collaboration and precise communication
Google Workspace ecosystem integration applications
Google Workspace Collaboration实务情境分享

Course details:
Understand the advantages of Google Workspace cloud collaboration.
2. Learn how to effectively set up and manage Google productivity tools: documents, spreadsheets, presentations
Learn to use collaborative real-time communication techniques for Google productivity tools, establishing smooth real-time communication.
Learn the annotation and suggestion mode of Google productivity tools to establish efficient asynchronous communication.
Learn to track Google productivity tools revision logs to avoid disappearing files or editing mistakes.
Understand Google ecosystem collaboration scenarios and integrated applications

session_name: Google Classroom is all-encompassing teaching platform! Online sessions
session_content:
Course introduction
Want to create a dedicated digital learning platform but don’t know where to start? This course will guide you from scratch, mastering the core tool of Google for Education – Google Classroom.
In this two-hour practical workshop, we will cover the full range of operations from foundational class setup and inviting teachers and students to advanced course settings, assignment distribution and grading. The courses will focus on practical applications, including how to use Classroom for grade calculation, effective management and archiving of student assignments, as well as sharing diverse teaching scenarios so that you can apply them in your classroom immediately and easily improve teaching efficiency and interaction quality. Whether you are a beginner or looking to refine your skills, you will leave with much to take away.

Google Classroom course settings, collaborative lesson planning, assignment distribution and grading
Advanced Features and Practical Applications of Google Classroom (Including ePlus Version Functionality)

Creating a Google Classroom course from scratch and including how to invite teachers and students to the class.
Learn how to collaborate with teachers in lesson planning, using drafts to build course resources, classroom exercises, etc.
Learn how to distribute, grade, and manage student assignments in the course.
How to learn how to use Google Classroom for grading
Enroll in learning management courses that include archiving student assignments and storing completed courses.
Other advanced settings such as rating scales, originality reports, and paid advanced features allow for customizing your digital classroom.

session_name: Automate Your Work with Google Apps Script: Mastering Workflow Automation (Basics Course) - Online Session
session_content:
Course introduction
Want to automate repetitive document tasks but don’t know where to start? This two-hour beginner’s workshop will guide you smoothly into the world of Google Apps Script.
The course will start from fundamental concepts, explaining how Apps Script works and teaching you how to smartly leverage existing templates and AI-generated code, even if you don't have a deep programming background. You'll learn how to integrate various services of Google Workspace (such as spreadsheets, documents, Gmail), creating automated tools tailored to your needs, such as sending personalized emails automatically.
Anyone from an administrative position, a project manager, or a teacher can lay a solid foundation for efficient automated workflows through this course, leaving time for more creative pursuits.

Understand the basic concepts and operation mode of Google Apps Script.
Master how to use AI tools (such as Gemini) to assist in generating and understanding code.
Through example implementation, help understand automated workflows and how to set up file integrations, triggers, etc., to lay the foundation for more complex automation processes.

Learn to set up Google Apps Script, understand its interface, and start our plan!
Understand the composition and operation flow of Google Apps Script, including code, connected files, connected APIs, and setting trigger conditions.
Create a practical case: Export from Google Forms to Excel and establish calendar events
Explore personal automation needs and learn to communicate with Gemini to generate corresponding code and debug it to practice personal automation workflows.

session_name: NotebookLM - Google Creates Your Personal AI Assistant – Online Session
session_content:
Course introduction
In the age of information explosion in AI, how do we efficiently integrate and apply various data sources while ensuring accuracy? This course will take you on a deep dive into Google's innovative tool, NotebookLM.
We will guide you on how to easily import personal files, web pages, notes, and various types of resources into NotebookLM to create your own personalized notebook. Through this course, you will learn how to use AI to quickly generate precise text summaries, voice summaries, and mind maps, significantly improving your learning and productivity. More importantly, this course will reveal how to leverage NotebookLM with your own data to effectively reduce the likelihood of AI hallucinations, generating more reliable and trustworthy information comparisons and content transformations. This will allow you to master the key skills for creating personalized AI applications.

Course Outline:
Introduction to Core Features and Applications of NotebookLM
Establishing a personal专属AI database using NotebookLM
Communicate with NotebookLM to generate extended content and applications.

Course details:
Recognize Google NotebookLM and understand its features for reducing AI hallucinations.
Learn how to create a personalized notebook to import various data sources such as personal files, web pages, and notes to build an individualized AI database.
Learning to use NotebookLM to quickly generate key points, voice summaries, and mind maps etc.
Learning to communicate with NotebookLM to generate reliable data comparisons and perform content format conversions for data extension applications.

Applications and Devices: NotebookLM

Only free-tier educators (teachers, staff, students)

activity_name_activity_session: Analysis and Response to Malicious Programs in Security Events
activity_content:
Course Introduction
The cybersecurity threats facing Academic Network are becoming increasingly severe, especially from malicious programs such as ransomware, remote backdoors, and spyware. These techniques have become more sophisticated with a variety of methods often combining social engineering, phishing emails, and exploit attacks. The course aims to introduce the common attack trends and behavioral characteristics of malicious programs through an overview of information security, analyzing actual event cases to lead learners in deep understanding of attackers' thinking and tactics, thereby enhancing their handling and response capabilities against cybersecurity threats.

Two, agency/office responsible for processing
Agency: Ministry of Education
Organizers: ASOC (Information Security Operations Center), Northern Branch, Information Management and Computing Center, National Taiwan University

Participants or Participants Target Group
Taiwan Academic Network Regional Center, County Education Network Centers, and School Security Personnel at all levels.

Course Information
Course Date: July 14, 2011 (Mon) 14:00-17:00
Course location: National Taiwan University Computer and Information Network Center Room 106

Registration Information
Open for registration from 12:00 PM on June 30, 20114 (Monday), with the deadline for registration being 12:00 PM on July 10, 20114 (Thursday). The limit for the physical course is 80 participants. Registration will be closed early if this limit is reached.

Course Content
Topic: Malware Analysis of Security Events and Response Handling
Speaker: Hsu Jinming

Outline:
Introduction to Information Security and Event Sharing
Malware Attacks and Response Handling
Overview of Security Incident Handling and Response

Seven, other注意事项
Participants in the training course must complete it fully and complete the course test and questionnaire after its conclusion to receive certification for 3 hours of public servant lifelong learning credits.
Organizers reserve the right to make final modifications, changes, interpret activities, and cancel the event at any time. They will notify participants of any changes via a separate message.

activity_name_event_page: 2025 [Art and Culture General Education Course for Faculty Members]
session_name: "Enriched by Arts and Humanities Elective Course for Teachers: Aesthetic Differences - Discovering Another Side of Yourself 7/9 (Wed) 2:00 pm"
session_content:
Face to Face in Mutual Interest - Discovering Another Aspect of Yourself

Let's discover our own faces. This class will begin with body games to observe and explore the concept of "face." We'll create self-portraits using mirrors, sensory interactions, and painting materials. Each face is a story and a window to connecting with the world. Let’s find that “you” we’ve never seen through creation.

Activity Information

Time: July 9, 2025 (Wed) 14:00-16:30, July 23, 2025 (Wed) 14:00-16:30
Location | National Taiwan University Art and Culture Center Yuhongfang
Speaker | Jiang Chunnian (Spring Roll Teacher), Taipei City Integrated Art Education Research Association

Instructor Information

Teacher Jiang Chunnian (Spring Roll Teacher)

Current president of the Taipei City Art Integration Education Research Association, with a long-standing commitment to special education and art integration teaching practices. Starting from illustration and picture books, he has developed a style of cross-media creation and teaching that integrates visual, auditory, and bodily elements. He is skilled in using art to initiate sensory dialogue, connecting people with each other and spaces with the world, believing that art is not just for expression but also a language of integration and understanding. His practice focuses on accompanying every learner to be seen and understood at their own pace, while continuously exploring how art can serve as an action force for cultural connections.

"Allowing each unique life to display its wings and gaze at a wide world, yet also having solid ground supporting them."

Taipei Association for Integrated Art Education Research

Ms. Lin Ssin-ning, the dance educator, founded in 2011 Taiwan’s first nonprofit social organization centered around arts-integrated education and therapy, focusing on serving individuals with autism spectrum and other disabilities. The Arts Integration Society integrates dance,美术, music, theater, and natural environments to develop creative integrated courses that assist children, teenagers, and adults in activating their bodily functions and reconnecting with nature and happiness. Currently based at Xiangshan Farm, it collaborates with the environment to create a diverse inclusive learning space.

Points to Note

Free for registration for National Taiwan University faculty and staff only
A snack pack (not a light meal) will be provided, resources are precious. Please notify us promptly if you cannot attend after being admitted to ensure a proper replacement for other colleagues and supervisors. Cancelling or missing out unexpectedly may affect your future participation in activities.
Please wear comfortable clothing that allows for movement and go barefoot as needed during the activity.
instructor: 姜春年（春捲老師）,臺北市藝術統合教育研究會
target_audience: Faculty, Staff, Civil Servant, Project Personnel, Hospital Staff

session_name: "Arts and Humanities Elective Course for Teachers: 'Boundaries of Music: A Tour through Four European Countries during the Baroque Period'"
session_content:
Activity Information

Date | November 6, 2025 (Thu) 12:30-13:10
Location | Yaxiufang
Organizers | NTU Arts and Humanities Center

This activity is only available for free registration for NTU faculty and staff.
*Light meals will be provided for the activity. Resources are precious, please notify us promptly if you cannot attend after being selected to allow for a replacement from other colleagues or supervisors. Cancelling or missing out at short notice may affect your future participation in activities and please note this carefully.*

This concert features the sheng (a Chinese wooden flute) and a grand piano as its core elements, taking audiences on a journey through the 17th and 18th centuries to explore unique aspects of Baroque music styles across Europe. From the exuberant melodies and intense emotions found in Italian music, the dramatic and refined elegance displayed by French compositions, the intricate structures and profound philosophical depth inherent in German works, to the dignified grace and purity embodied in British music – each piece serves as a window into history, reflecting the social, artistic, and intellectual landscape of its time.

This performance not only focuses on the meticulous interpretation of music itself, but also includes guided commentary that connects to the composers' lives and historical context. This allows viewers to immerse themselves in the interplay between oboe and harpsichord sounds while gaining a deeper understanding of how musical styles from different countries interacted, influenced, and blended during the Baroque period, ultimately shaping Europe’s diverse and dazzling musical landscape.

We sincerely invite you to embark on this musical journey that blends performance with knowledge, where together we can feel the artistic soul and cultural charm of 17th and 18th century Europe through动人旋律.

Performing team
Artist Star
Yan Yijiang | Wooden Flute
Shinji Ishida | Great Violin

Team Introduction
Artist Star has been in existence for ten years and has long dedicated itself to promoting European classical music and the art of the oboe. Operating with a spirit of one step at a time and deep-rooted in the local community, the team is managed by团长梁益彰，一位屡获国际奖项的木笛演奏家。艺术总监由大键琴家须藤真地子（Machiko Suto）担任，策划了多场音乐会、讲座和研习课程，推动古乐教育与欣赏风气。

Established in 2024, "Yixing Bamboo Flute Ensemble," actively promoting the education and roots of bamboo flute music. Yixing presents a diverse array of musical styles from Baroque to modern through flexible performance configurations and an expansive, multicultural curatorial perspective, continuously infusing contemporary vitality into classical music.

activity_name_event_page: 2025 [Art and Culture General Education Course for Faculty Members]
session_name: Design Path for Art and Humanities Elective Course for Teachers: From Self-Awareness to Declaration Writing – Self-Exploration Workshop
session_content:
Course Information

Date | November 21, 2025 (Friday), November 28, 2025 (Friday) 14:00-16:30
Location | Yaxiufang
Organizers | NTU Arts and Humanities Center
Registration link: https://reurl.cc/Ln97ve

This activity is only available for free registration for NTU faculty and staff.
This activity consists of two consecutive weeks' courses and cannot be separated for registration.
*Snacks will be provided for the activity. Resources are limited, please notify us promptly if you cannot attend after being accepted to allow a substitute from other colleagues or supervisors. Cancelling or missing the event at short notice may affect your future participation in activities. Please note this carefully.*

Course Overview

Have you ever reflected on your position and meaning in life during daily work?

During this two-week workshop, participants will explore their positioning and value in educational and cultural administration through the history and practice of "manifestos" within the fields of art, design, and philosophy. Through exercises that combine self-narratives using both images and writing, participants will develop their own "positioning manifestos," sorting out the beliefs, methods, and feelings behind their work.

We will use design thinking as a method to reflect on our life journey and work experiences, further clarifying the relationship between roles, aspirations, and actions. During the practice of design, we find our place, heal from fatigue.

Course Planning

Week 1: Design as Self-Reflection

We will introduce the history of the design manifesto and discuss how it is practiced in work. Participants will engage in "Who Am I?" writing guided by images to explore their professional positioning and sense of value.

Week 2: My Declaration and Zine Creation

Students continue to delve deeply into the most profound feelings about their social roles from Week 1 and learn techniques for making zines, presenting personal reflections in both image and text formats, and sharing them.

Teacher Introduction

Bushwa’s Room is a Taiwanese artist and designer who is also an independent curator. She frequently observes, records, and transforms between different cultural communities through interviews, text, and images to further integrate information and propose transformations – including forms such as visual design, physical exhibitions, short-term and long-term courses, online forums, etc. She graduated from Pratt Institute’s Visual Communication Design program and has exhibited solo shows in art spaces like Field Project in New York and Living Skin. Her project services and collaborating units include the Taiwan Institute of Design, National Taipei University of Technology, Pratt Institute, Ugly Duckling Presse, Processing Foundation, Taipei Art Book Fair, and other organizations. She is currently the founder of SpOnAcT, a design culture organization, and co-founder of Co-Assembly, a design studio.

session_name: "Afternoon Myths - Dreaming Through Six Countries"
session_content:
Course Information

Activity Date: 2025/11/20
Activity time | 12:30-13:10
Venue | National Taiwan University Art and Culture Center Yashengfang
Registration link: https://reurl.cc/EQOqLK

This activity is only available for free registration for NTU faculty and staff.
*Light meals will be provided for the activity. Resources are precious, please notify us promptly if you cannot attend after being selected to allow for a replacement from other colleagues or supervisors. Cancelling or missing out at short notice may affect your future participation in activities and please note this carefully.*

Course Overview

"Afternoon Myths - Dreaming Through Six Countries" is a cross-cultural musical performance that blends the pipa, piano, and a trio of Swedish fiddles. This time, traditional Chinese zither (三味線) and erhu player Zhong Yu-rui will use the very rare "Swedish fiddle" in Taiwan for their performance! The program selects myths and legends from six countries: Japan, Egypt, Sweden, India, South Korea, and the Buncun族 of Taiwan. Traditional melodies are combined with Japanese, Egyptian, Indian, Swedish, Korean, and Taiwanese indigenous Buncun myths and legends as creative inspiration. Through improvisation reconstruction and contemporary vocabulary, it reinterprets these diverse cultures to showcase how they can collide in music and reveal a new appearance.

The creative material for this live concert includes: the Japanese Edo-period dance piece "Genroku Hanabi Odori," Egyptian-inspired scales and traditional rhythms Malfuf, Swedish folk song "Pom Pom Pom," mythological episodes from the Indian epic Mahabharata, Korean pieces "Gyeongbokgung Dalmaji" and "Kakye'kum Sanjeol," as well as the eight-part harmony and ancient melodies of the Bunun tribe in Taiwan. The creator starts with multiple cultural backgrounds and combines modern compositional techniques with free improvisation, while preserving the original cultural characteristics and also expanding new soundscapes.

"Afternoon Myths - Dreaming Through Six Kingdoms" is not only an exploration and dialogue of the linguistic vocabulary of different cultures, but also an artistic experiment in transforming mythological stories into sound narratives. The performance aims to lead viewers between memories and imaginations of different civilizations, experiencing a musical journey at the boundary between history, sound, and poetry during afternoon hours.

Performing team

Liang Jianning | Lute
Swedish Violin | Zhong Yu-Rui
Piano | Tsui Ruiting

Biwa Liang Jianning
Pianist, composer, arranger, and music producer active in classical, modern, pop, and world music fields.
Graduated from the National Taiwan University of Arts, School of Traditional Chinese Music with a Master’s degree. Has dedicated over two decades to performing and composing for the pipa, having performed internationally for over twenty years. Performances have taken him to countries such as the United States, France, Germany, Australia, Japan, the Netherlands, India, China, and the UK. He has also served as an adjudicator for Singapore’s international Chinese music grade exams, showcasing his broad international perspective and artistic cultivation.

Swedish violin Rui Wen
World music performer.
Taiwan erhu, Japanese津輕三味線, Swedish violinist. Graduated from Culture University Drama Department and Music Department Master's Program in Chinese music. After graduation, he actively engaged in learning various musical styles including traditional opera, pop跨界, theater, and world music. Recently, he has been invited to perform in Japan, the United States, Lithuania, and China. He has also participated in recording of artist albums and film and game scores.

Tse Kwai-ting (Piano)
Composer, arranger,跨界 and improvisational performer, professional piano accompanist.
Graduated from the Theory Composition Department of the Chinese Music Division at National Taiwan University of Arts, with a master's degree. His musical style is diverse, encompassing rock music, theater music, and national music compositions. He often draws inspiration from daily life to make music more relatable to everyday experiences and as a source for his creations.

activity_name_event_page: 113-2 [Academic Ethics Lecture]
session_name: Academic Ethics Lecture June 24, 2014 - Legal Consultant Lin Cheng-Hsia from Jun Chi Intellectual Property Affairs Office
session_content:
Activity information:
Topic: Applications of Generative AI in Educational Research and Ethical Labeling
Time: June 24, 2025 (Tuesday) 2:10 PM - 4:10 PM
Location: Shuangyi Teaching Building, South China Lecture Hall (Room 101), School Central Area
Speaker: Lin Chengxia Legal Consultant
Chair of Intellectual Property Affairs Office / CC Taiwan Plan
Language: Chinese

Course description:
With the growing prevalence of generative AI tools, creation and research are increasingly relying on AI assistance. However, is the use of AI merely a "tool-based application" or has it evolved into a "assistive collaboration"? This distinction will impact key issues such as copyright attribution and academic honesty disclosure responsibilities. To claim legal protection, creators must clearly identify the specific contributions of human creativity to meet the requirement of "human-generated work" under copyright law. This lecture will take an end-user perspective to explain how to determine the level of AI involvement, disclose AI contributions, and define the role of AI in various application scenarios, as well as assess legal risks. Through case studies, it aims to help attendees grasp specific response strategies to prevent potential infringement disputes arising from AI usage, while ensuring the legitimacy of their contributions and maintaining academic exchanges on a fair and trustworthy foundation.
This session covers the academic ethics course category 1 for 2 hours; starting from the 107 academic year, graduate and doctoral students must cover at least three types totaling 6 hours of academic ethics courses. Participants are responsible for supplementing their own academic ethics training hours.

activity_name_activity_session: Occupational Health Lecture 2025 (5): Exploring Back Pain Prevention from a Human Factors Perspective: Practical Approaches to Workplace Health Promotion
activity_content:
Abstract
The content of this lecture includes the following parts-
Background: Why prevent lower back pain instead of waiting for it to occur and then treat it? Epidemiology (musculoskeletal diseases, related diagnoses, etc.) of lower back pain.
Introduction to Human Engineering: Role of Ergonomics in Preventing Occupational Hazards: Effects on the Back from Poor Posture, Lifting Heavy Objects, and Repetitive Motion. Common Workplace Posture Errors and Correction Suggestions.
Core Muscles and Lower Back Pain (20 minutes): Definition and Importance of Core Muscles, Mechanism of Core Stability in Reducing Spinal Load
Practical preventive strategies and stretching exercises: Teaching 3-5 simple and feasible core training methods suitable for workplace execution.

Congratulations on reaching level max in your 20-year student mode, completing all classroom tasks and skill points. Now, prepare to enter a new副本: the Workplace New Village. Are You Ready? The missions here include understanding workplace language, adapting to corporate culture, changing time perspectives, and practicing responding to supervisor instructions, workplace communication, with many many challenges to solve! activity_name_activity_session: 2025 Occupational Health Lecture (Four): "Why Is It So Hard to Speak Well? The Art of Effective Supervisor and Colleague Communication"
activity_content:
Abstract
This lecture is divided into two main parts.
Part 1: Worries About Going to Work – Self-Understanding and Exploration of Inner Iceberg
In the workplace, we often suppress emotions and hide our true selves. Through self-awareness, we can see behind the emotions the needs and beliefs they represent. The iceberg theory helps us understand that external behavior is just a small part of our inner experiences. Learning to recognize one’s own feelings and desires is the first step towards achieving mental and physical balance.
This is an important practice of self-dialogue and clarifying the sources of stress.
Part Two: How to Have Both Depth and Clarity – Understanding Communication Postures and Practical Strategies
Communication is not just about speaking out but also making the other person truly understand you. We often communicate using defensive ways, such as pleasing, blaming or being overly rational. Recognizing five communication postures helps clarify one’s habitual reactions in a relationship. Through consistent communication, we can express our stance while respecting others. Learning communication strategies allows us to feel more secure at work and reduce misunderstandings.

activity_name_activity_session: 精准医学研究中心 20114 Summer Institute - Orientation Program for 2019 Begins Registration!
activity_content:
Precision medicine research involves high-throughput technologies and big data processing, through the establishment of integrated centers that facilitate cross-disciplinary and cross-unit collaborations between molecular biology, biochemistry, multidisciplinary studies, structural biomedical science, biomedical informatics, and smart healthcare.
This explanation will invite the following distinguished research teams to join you in exploring and unveiling the mysteries of the "Summer Research Camp"! We sincerely invite all students across campus to enthusiastically register for participation.

Explanation session time: June 10, 2011 (Tue) 9:30 AM - 11:30 AM
Registration Deadline: June 9, 2014 (Monday) at 12:00 PM
Explanation details: http://www.cgm.ntu.edu.tw/web/news/news_in.jsp?npno=NP1747377911900
Introduction of the research team:

Molecular Bioimaging Center
Molecular Imaging research aims to understand molecular reaction processes within the human body and biological systems, providing non-invasive cellular and molecular-level images in living organisms. The Solid Foundation Center focuses on integrating resources across disciplines from a fundamental perspective of gene expression and molecular reactions to enhance humanity's understanding of biomedical science, offering earlier, more accurate, and personalized medical imaging diagnostics that reduce mortality rates for major diseases, improving national healthcare and quality of life, and reducing unnecessary medical tests and procedures. Research in this field will promote the development and upgrading of biotechnology and emerging medical care industries, including the biomedicine imaging industry, and cultivate high-level professionals in imaging medical hardware equipment.

Infectious Diseases Research Group
The Infection Research Team at the College of Medicine, National Taiwan University has been conducting in-depth research on pathogenic and symbiotic microorganisms. Through innovative scientific and medical research, they aim to develop new diagnostic methods, treatment drugs, or vaccines, focusing on precision diagnosis, precision treatment, and precision prevention of infections. The expectation is that these results will not only help control or eradicate one or two infectious diseases but also assist in formulating and promoting new strategies for the prevention or control of infectious diseases, fulfilling the university’s responsibility to society.

Proteome and Protein Function Core
The core proteomics laboratory research aims to understand the dynamic structures, modifications, distribution, and interactions of whole or specific protein groups within species; combining the analysis of clinical manifestations of diseases to identify molecular disease mechanisms, thereby developing early diagnostic protein biomarkers and new therapeutic detection methods. The goal is to improve patient outcomes and achieve the main purpose of translational medical research.

Genome Core
The core laboratory is led by Professor Yu Songliang of the Medical Technology Department. The laboratory focuses on providing services in genomics and has established multiple genomics analysis platforms to support related basic and translational research. It also sets up a 'Precision Medicine Molecular Diagnostic Laboratory' certified by the Bureau of Food and Drug Administration, which develops various in-house assays to provide cancer genomic testing services to domestic medical institutions.

Bioinformatics and Biostatistics Core
In recent years, with the thriving development of molecular genetics, genetic medicine, and bioinformatics-related fields, the relationship between genetic codes and biomedical information has become increasingly不容忽视. In the vast sea of genomic data, extracting meaningful biomedical messages requires traditional statistical analysis and data processing methods to be insufficient; this necessitates a collaborative effort among molecular biologists, statisticians in biological sciences, medical researchers, and IT managers. Drawing from extensive experience in handling and consulting on biomedical data as well as clinical trial data, the Bioinformatics and Biostatistics Core Laboratory was thus born with the aim of laying a solid foundation for the development of genomics medicine and clinical information.

Metabolic Core
The establishment of the MetaboLights core laboratory at the National Taiwan University’s Genomics Research Center is primarily to provide metabolic composition information through leading global metabolomics analysis technologies, promoting translational research and human health. As a bridge between basic research and clinical diagnosis based on interdisciplinary technology, the MetaboLights core laboratory aims to realize advanced technologies that can influence and improve future personalized medicine development related to metabolism.

Animal Resource Core
Specializes in the manufacturing, propagation, and utilization of genetically modified mice (including gene knockout and gene insertion) for research. Resources provided include the College of Medicine Animal Center, the Institute of Genomics and Precision Medicine, and the Department of Medical Laboratory and Biotechnology Sciences. Research topics include cardiovascular, cancer immunology, and genome editing technologies.

Infectious Diseases Research Group
The Infection Research Team at the College of Medicine, National Taiwan University has been conducting in-depth research on pathogenic and symbiotic microorganisms. Through innovative scientific and medical research, they aim to develop new diagnostic methods, treatment drugs, or vaccines, focusing on precision diagnosis, precision treatment, and precision prevention of infections. The expectation is that these results will not only help control or eradicate one or two infectious diseases but also assist in formulating and promoting new strategies for the prevention or control of infectious diseases, fulfilling the university’s responsibility to society.

Proteome and Protein Function Core
The core proteomics laboratory research aims to understand the dynamic structures, modifications, distribution, and interactions of whole or specific protein groups within species; combining the analysis of clinical manifestations of diseases to identify molecular disease mechanisms, thereby developing early diagnostic protein biomarkers and new therapeutic detection methods. The goal is to improve patient outcomes and achieve the main purpose of translational medical research.

Genome Core
The core laboratory is led by Professor Yu Song Song-Liang from the Medical Technology Department, focusing mainly on providing services in genomics and setting up various genomics analysis platforms to support related basic and translational research. In addition, a "Precision Medicine Molecular Diagnostic Laboratory" compliant with the Quality Certification of the Ministry of Health and Welfare's Food and Drug Administration has been established, developing multiple laboratory-developed tests to provide cancer genomic testing services for domestic medical institutions.

Bioinformatics and Biostatistics Core
In recent years, with the thriving development of molecular genetics, genetic medicine, and bioinformatics-related fields, the relationship between genetic codes and biomedical information has become increasingly不容忽视. In the vast sea of genomic data, extracting meaningful biomedical messages requires traditional statistical analysis and data processing methods to be insufficient; this necessitates a collaborative effort among molecular biologists, statisticians in biological sciences, medical researchers, and IT managers. Drawing from extensive experience in handling and consulting on biomedical data as well as clinical trial data, the Bioinformatics and Biostatistics Core Laboratory was thus born with the aim of laying a solid foundation for the development of genomics medicine and clinical information.

Metabolic Core
The establishment of the MetaboLights core laboratory at the National Taiwan University’s Genomics Research Center is primarily to provide metabolic composition information through leading global metabolomics analysis technologies, promoting translational research and human health. As a bridge between basic research and clinical diagnosis based on interdisciplinary technology, the MetaboLights core laboratory aims to realize advanced technologies that can influence and improve future personalized medicine development related to metabolism.

Animal Resource Core
Specializes in the manufacturing, propagation, and utilization of genetically modified mice (including gene knockout and gene insertion) for research. Resources provided include the College of Medicine Animal Center, the Institute of Genomics and Precision Medicine, and the Department of Medical Laboratory and Biotechnology Sciences. Research topics include cardiovascular, cancer immunology, and genome editing technologies.

session_name: Resolution of School Bullying Cases and Analysis of Case Studies (On-site Event)
session_content: The speaker discusses campus bullying incidents and types based on their experience as a member of an investigation committee for such cases, then delves into the key points of the "Guidelines for Preventing and Addressing School Bullying," focusing on the mechanisms to prevent and address school bullying. They also list ways to handle incidents, including: implementing preventive counseling systems, establishing professional, fair, and effective handling mechanisms, strengthening supervisory authorities' responsibilities, ensuring the rights and interests of those who are bullied, building a talent pool for mediation and investigation professionals, and setting up sound supporting mechanisms. After the open course ends, colleagues can bring up actual cases from their surroundings to raise doubts about whether they fall under the scope of bullying.

Pioneer of neurosurgery and laser medicine in Taiwan. He studied at the University of Tokyo and Massachusetts General Hospital, Harvard University, mastering cutting-edge techniques and introducing electrocautery treatment for trigeminal neuralgia and surgical hand technique, earning him the title of "Father of Laser Medicine in Taiwan." Additionally, he served as a legislator advocating for several important medical bills and organizing nationwide commendation activities for outstanding nurses, contributing significantly to improving nursing quality.

activity_name_activity_session: Film Screening and Post-Show Discussion for "The Enduring Life - Lucie's Exploration" Documentary (Light snacks 🧀 will be served to the first 60 registrants who arrive.)
activity_content:
"Everyone has power; it's just a matter of learning how to apply it! Do what you like to do, and do it." — Lucie Cheng (Cao Luxi)

The core philosophy of the National Taiwan University’s Explore Learning Program encourages experimentation and exploration, as long as you are willing to do so, without imposing too much pressure. The Innovation Design College at National Taiwan University has collaborated with Independent Review @天下 to host a screening and post-movie discussion for the documentary "Enduring Life – Lucie's Exploration," which won Best Documentary in the 34th Golden Horse Awards.

Everyone has the power, it's just a matter of learning how to apply it. Chen Luqi’s curiosity and exploration about life have never stopped, constantly using惊叹号to challenge, inspire, and motivate young people. Her revolutionary flames burned through American campuses in the 1970s when she pioneered feminist, minority studies, and Asian-American research at UCLA; during Taiwan's political liberalization and social movements of the 1990s, she opened alternative media to inspire热血y youth, give a voice to the marginalized, and advocate for immigrants and migrant workers, pushing for societal change. She likes to say: "Nobody has ever done this before!" Moving from global issues to local actions, from knowledge to action, from history to the future, Chen Luqi weaves a brilliant life with an unclassifiable temporal axis.

Learning is about conforming to rules, and creating possibilities instead. Exploration is about waiting for opportunities, and going out to change the world instead.
And this is precisely what motivated the National Taiwan University’s Exploration Learning Program to encourage “you” to embark on your journey with courage and a mindset of exploration; exploration is both a心态and an勇气.

We invite you to walk into Lucie’s life story and also see how NTU responds to the times with action, bringing the spirit of exploration and its influence from higher education to social education and family education. We hope this will expand our impact on society, allowing more people to practice the spirit of exploratory learning. Through a series of exploration journeys, we aim to sustain the power of that phrase: "Everyone has the power; it's about how one learns to apply! Do what you like to do, and that’s right!"

— Activity Information —
Date/Time: May 29, 2025 (Thu) 18:30-20:30 (Entry from 18:10-18:30)
Location: 3rd floor Wos Space of National Taiwan University Second Year Students Activity Center (85 Roosevelt Road, Section 4, Da-An District, Taipei)
Panelists: Associate Professor and Director of the Master's Program in Chinese Language Teaching at National Taiwan University, Wang Junyi; Managing Director of Independent Commentator @天下, Liao Yunzh ang; and Founder of Mínyě Imaging Studio, Lin Xinye.
Organizers: National Taiwan University College of Design, Independent Review @天下
Cooperation in Imaging: Mysterious Wild Film Studio
There is light refreshments available at the event, water fountains are onsite, and we encourage bringing your own reusable cup to help protect the environment.

activity_name_activity_session: Fulbright Student and Scholar Award Information Session
activity_content:
The Fulbright Taiwan (Fulbright Foundation of Taiwan) held a Fulbright Student and Scholar Award Information Session on May 28th (Wednesday), from 17:30 to 18:30, in the First Conference Room. Representatives from the Academic Exchange Fund will introduce the Fulbright Program during the session.

Over 60 Nobel laureates and 88 Pulitzer Prize winners have participated in the Fulbright Program, with more than 1,600 Taiwanese students and scholars having received Fulbright grants. The program annually provides up to 10 grants, including a full tuition scholarship for a master’s or doctoral degree that can reach up to NT$1.6 million per year. There are also various grant programs for scholars and teachers, such as senior scholar research grants. Participants include both internal university faculty and students as well as those interested in applying. Applicants are welcome to apply.

Fulbright Program Grants
Undergraduate category: Fulbright Research Award for Doctoral Students, Fulbright Award for Master’s Study, and Fulbright Award for Bachelor’s Study. Application deadline is August 1st.

II. Scholars and Professionals Category: Fulbright Scholar Award, Fuelsbrett-Tai石化 Enterprise Scholarship (senior scholars), Postdoctoral Fulbright Research Awards, Fulbright–Professional and Creative Artists Scholarship, Fulbright-Taiwan Ministry of Culture Arts Professional Talent Support Program, Fuelsbrett-Tai石化 Enterprise Scholarship (professional personnel). Application deadline is October 31.

Third category: Teacher and Teaching Assistant Category: The Scholarship for Teachers and Teaching Assistants to Study in the US and Assist in Chinese Language Teaching Applications are due by October 1st.

Fulbright Program Information Website:
Fulbright Taiwan: https://www.fulbright.org.tw/

Registrants please arrive 10 minutes early and thank you.

This will be an experience that combines knowledge and fun~

session_name: "Dialogue Through the Ages Series Four: New Perspectives on Dream of the Red Chamber (First Part) 'Bao Yu's Multimedia Classroom: The Sounds of Reading, Performance, and Transformation – From the Novel Dream of the Red Chamber to the Film Version'"
session_content:
Speaker: LAM Ling Hon
Host/Guest Speakers: Sebastian Hsien-hao Liao

First Round
"Multimedia Classroom of Baoyu: Voices of Transformation – From Reading 'Dream of the Red Chamber' to Film Version"
Time: May 20 (Tue) 3:00 PM – 5:00 PM
Location: One Floor Conference Room of National Taiwan University History Museum, Foreign Languages and Literature Department

Speech Summary:
First Round
"Multimedia Classroom of Baoyu: Voices of Transformation – From Reading 'Dream of the Red Chamber' to Film Version"
The silent reading of The Western Chamber by Baoyu and Jia Baoxiang in Dream of the Red Chamber became a taboo, but romantic drama plays were repeatedly staged openly at the Jia family’s residence. This contradiction reveals rich information about the intersections between various media forms and experiences—between performance and reading, oral and visual—that are distinct from each other. When the novel itself is visually realized through modern mass media, that interactive relationship becomes more pronounced, revealing how subjects and communities are created through various ways of mediation by sound. The same scene of “reading The Western Chamber” in four different film versions from 1924, 1944, 1962, and 1977 is presented differently in their respective geo-linguistic contexts, showing the historical transformations of social meanings carried by silent reading, speaking, and performance across different times and spaces. Rather than comparing which version is most faithful or superior, what matters here is to show that every time we handle Dream of the Red Chamber through cross-media means, we are once again highlighting the media issues already deeply reflected in the novel itself.

session_content:
New Perspectives in Research on Dream of the Red Chamber

Speaker: LAM Ling Hon
Host/Guest Speakers: Sebastian Hsien-hao Liao

Second Round
"How Cinema Adapted the Original to Create an Environment: How ‘Dream of the Red Chamber’ Became a Novel through Film?"

Time: May 22 (Thu) 3:00 PM – 5:00 PM

Location: One Floor Conference Room of National Taiwan University History Museum, Foreign Languages and Literature Department

Registration link: https://forms.gle/TDHuF1wzJZSPdvsy8

Speech Summary:
Second Round
"How films adapted 'The Dream of the Red Chamber' became novels: How 'Dream of the Red Chamber' transformed from an original text to a created environment"

For most redologists, adapting films is probably a non-issue, at best used to liven up the dry atmosphere. The term "adaptation" implies an subordinate position in terms of time and causality chains, offering little help in understanding the original novel. No matter how good the adaptation, it only serves as marginal examples for recent reception history, which can be easily forgotten or deviate from the authentic source. Historically, however, turning The Story of the Stone into a "novel" was precisely through film adaptations. Director Cheng Shuren claimed that his film version of The Story of the Stone had "novelized" it. To understand this seemingly absurd claim, I suggest exploring another ambiguity in the English usage of adapt – not to modify or transform an original work, but to "adapt" a setting, which means creating the environment rather than passively responding to it. In other words, we cannot merely observe how The Story of the Stone has been transmitted and transformed across different media environments; instead, we must reverse this perspective and consider The Story of the Stone itself as an unfinished environment that needs new media to "first" open up and explore. This subtlety involves temporal inversion and transformation, such as the competition between Cheng Shuren’s ancient costume film version of The Story of the Stone in 1927 and the earlier fashion film version released a year prior, which served as a microcosm of that era.
