activity_name_activity_session: 2025 Occupational Health Lecture (III): Maternal Protection - A Brief Discussion on the Impact of Environmental Hormones on Human Health (Using Dioxin and Perfluorinated Compounds as Examples) Online Lecture
activity_content:
Abstract
Endocrine Disrupting Chemicals (EDCs) refer to a class of chemicals that interfere with the human endocrine system and mimic natural hormone functions, which are widely present in our living and working environments. In recent years, increasing research has shown that these substances may have long-term and profound effects on women’s health, particularly for those during pregnancy, lactation, and the reproductive years, potentially affecting their offspring’s health as well.

This lecture will focus on "maternal protection" and provide a brief introduction to the definition and sources of environmental hormones. It will concentrate on two representative pollutants—dioxins (Dioxins) and perfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS). We will explore the pathways of exposure, accumulation properties in the human body, and potential impacts on female reproductive, fetal development, and lactation functions. It will also reference both domestic and international research and cases to help listeners better understand their health risks.

The lecture will also cover common exposure scenarios and feasible protective measures for work environments commonly found in universities, such as laboratory operations, cleaning tasks, and administrative offices. It will provide specific recommendations for maternal protection, including personal protective measures, workplace adjustments, and strategies to reduce risks in daily life, helping female employees maintain a healthy and safe working environment during their special physiological stages (such as pregnancy and lactation).


Activity time: May 19, 2025 (Monday) from 2:00 PM to 4:00 PM
Location: Cisco Webex Online Video Conference Live Stream

This activity will be conducted through Cisco Webex online video conferencing. Please prepare your own computer or mobile device to participate, complete the online check-in and attend the entire lecture. Participants who have done so will receive 2 hours of digital learning credits for faculty and administrative staff members at our school.

activity_name_activity_session: May 14, 2011 (Wednesday), May 15, 2011 (Thursday) from 13:30 to 16:00, held a "Cancer Health Screening Activity" jointly with Taipei Medical University Hospital.
activity_content:
To implement early detection, diagnosis, and treatment, we jointly held a “Cancer Health Screening Activity” with Taipei Medical University Hospital:
One: Time: May 14 (Wednesday), May 15 (Thursday) 13:30 - 16:00.
Location: First Floor of the Wellness Center.
Bring your Health Insurance Card when inspected.
Screening items include:
Cervical smear: once a year for women aged 30 and above, every three years for those aged 25-29.
Human papillomavirus (HPV) testing for women aged 35, 45, and 65 years.
Mammography (by appointment at Taipei Medical University Hospital): once every two years for women aged 45-75.
Stool occult blood test: for individuals aged 45-75 years or those aged 40-45 with a family history
Low-dose computed tomography of the lung (scheduled at the outpatient clinic of Taichung Hospital, eligibility determined by appointment; evaluated and confirmed by a Taichung Hospital case manager on the same day)
Family history of lung cancer: male or female between 45 and 75 years old with a parent, child, or sibling diagnosed with lung cancer.
Heavy smokers: those aged 50 to 74 with a smoking history of over 20 packs-per-year who have not quit smoking for 15 years.
Five, participants in the cancer screening activity will receive a set of exquisite items from Taichung Veterans' General Hospital.
Six, participants who complete check-in and satisfaction surveys will be credited with 1 hour of official duties.

activity_name_activity_session: 🎮 Career Sharing and Talent Trend Lecture 🕹️ "Beyond Games, Telling Stories" - Opening Your Multifaceted Career in Multiple Universes Through Diverse Arts: Blizzard Animation Vice President STEVEN CHEN
activity_content:
You have been deeply attracted by games or animations and wonder how characters, plots, and scenes are created?
Behind those detailed scenes lies what creative process and cross-disciplinary collaboration?

The Career Center of the Academic Affairs Office specially invited Steven Chen, a vice president of Blizzard Entertainment and Blizzard Animation, to share how he has connected imagination and influence by telling stories to create popular and heartwarming animations and games from Taiwan all the way into the production sites of top global games like World of Warcraft and Diablo!

He will bring insights and experience gained from observing and mastering the operations of major multimedia content houses in Europe and America, as well as firsthand industry experience: the production process and behind-the-scenes stories of Blizzard Entertainment's animation, along with an understanding of the profile and key skills of creative industry talent.

This lecture not only doubles your inspiration but could also be a key upgrade point in the story of your career adventure, whether you are a gamer, an anime enthusiast, a detail-oriented person, or simply thinking about your starting point for a multidisciplinary career.

Career Sharing and Talent Trends Lecture
"Off-Game Storytelling" - Exploring Your Multifaceted Career with Diverse Arts
Activity date: May 14, 2025 (Wed) 14:30 – 16:00
Location of Activity: Administrative Building First Conference Room
activity_name_event_page: 🎮 Career Sharing and Talent Trend Lecture 🕹️ "Beyond Games, Telling Stories" - Opening Your Multifaceted Career in Multiple Universes Through Diverse Arts: Blizzard Animation Vice President STEVEN CHEN

session_content:
Career Sharing and Talent Trends Lecture
"Off-Game Storytelling" - Exploring Your Multifaceted Career with Diverse Arts
Activity date: May 14, 2025 (Wed) 14:30 – 16:00
Location of Activity: Administrative Building First Conference Room

Speaker Introduction
Blizzard Entertainment Vice President STEVEN CHEN陈璿

Steven Chen currently serves as the Vice President of Digital Animation at Blizzard Entertainment, leading a team composed of digital effects (DFX) artists and department heads in charge of overseeing the execution of "Blizzard Animation" content – a CG studio under Blizzard’s “Story and Franchise Development” team. As a senior总监, he focuses on directing the artistic style and execution of the team's animation and works, while also handling their output and quality. Steven joined Blizzard Entertainment in 2006 with professional background in concept art and traditional drawing, serving as a character model artist. Over the past few years, he has progressively advanced within the studio, holding positions such as Senior Lead for Visual Development, Surface Treatment Supervisor, and Senior Lead, contributing to multiple Blizzard games including the StarCraft II trilogy, each expansion pack of World of Warcraft, Diablo III, and The Lord of the Rings: Rise of the Stairs, as well as Hearthstone and Battlegrounds. In his current role as a Senior Animation Director, he collaborates with production directors and technical directors in assisting the operations of Blizzard Animation studio. Chen Hsin was born and raised in Kaohsiung’s Kaohtou District until moving abroad at age 13; despite living in multiple countries around the world for over two decades, he still considers Taiwan his hometown.

About Blizzard Entertainment
Blizzard Entertainment, a subsidiary of Activision Blizzard (NASDAQ: MSFT), which owns popular games such as "World of Warcraft," "Overwatch," "Diablo," and "StarCraft," is one of the leading entertainment experience developers and publishers globally. Over the past 30 years, Blizzard has developed several acclaimed titles in the gaming industry and received multiple annual game award honors. Its games have attracted over hundreds of millions of players through platforms like PC's Battle.net, Xbox, PlayStation, Nintendo Switch, iOS, and Android worldwide.

activity_content:
Work Win Look: Tips and Tricks for Mixing and Matching Custom Tailored Suits

Speaker Bio:
Professor Peace
Current Design Director of Granby suits
Current常务监事 of中华民国洋服同业总会
Former Technical Chairman of Taipei City Tailors Business Association
2024 Youth Sewing Competition Gold Medal Award of the 2024 Asia Federation Fashion Congress
2024 Asia Industry Alliance Tailoring Conference Best Sewing Award
2024 Asian Industry Alliance Fashion Grand Prix Business Attire Design Category Silver Award

Speech content:
Translating the text from Chinese to English:

Going from a student to a new member of society, besides one’s core skills being important, "career dressing" is also a must-have course! How to dress in a way that reflects your own style while still maintaining professionalism and personal charm is definitely one of those daily skills everyone needs to learn.

The Taipei Federation of Tailors' Association, representing Taiwan, has consecutively won the world championship for 10 years. The craftsmanship in handmade tailored suits is at the top level worldwide. During this lecture, we also specially invited Chen Pei-hsuan, Peace Teacher and current Design Director of Gran Suit, who previously won a gold medal, to share with各位 how to blend西装 with everyday clothes for smart and stylish outfits in the workplace. Not only does it showcase one's personal style but also makes them stand out among others! ★

Speech content:
Translating the text from Chinese to English:

Going from a student to a new member of society, besides one’s core skills being important, "career dressing" is also a must-have course! How to dress in a way that reflects your own style while still maintaining professionalism and personal charm is definitely one of those daily skills everyone needs to learn.

The Taipei Federation of Tailors' Association, representing Taiwan, has consecutively won the world championship for 10 years. The craftsmanship in handmade tailored suits is at the top level worldwide. During this lecture, we also specially invited Chen Pei-hsuan, Peace Teacher, who previously won a gold medal and currently serves as the chief designer of Granzi Tailors, to share with各位 how to mix and match tailored suits with regular clothes worn in daily life for workplace settings. Not only does it showcase one's personal style but also makes them stand out among others!

activity_name_activity_session: "Eat right and your brain won't tire!" The Art of Brain Health from Diet | Environment Safety Center Providing Lunch on Lecture Day
activity_content:
Abstract:
This course will introduce the key role of the "Mediterranean Diet" in maintaining brain health, delving into its core dietary principles, which include consuming more fruits and vegetables, whole grains, nuts, and healthy fats; eating moderate amounts of fish and poultry; and reducing intake of red meat and sweets. We will also explore the relationship between gut microbiota and brain health, providing practical lifestyle diet suggestions to help you incorporate these practices into your daily life to prevent dementia and stimulate thinking.

Time: May 12, 2025 (Monday) noon 12:10 - 1:00 PM
Location: Sustainability Hall, Environmental Research Building (No. 71 Fanglan Road)

This lecture will provide healthy meal boxes (chicken or pork options), aligning with the course content for an actual experience of the deliciousness and charm of balanced nutrition! By pairing lectures with demonstrations of healthy eating, I hope you not only take away knowledge but also incorporate a health philosophy into your meals, experiencing "eating well, having a better brain" in daily life.
Limited quantities available, please register enthusiastically.

Teacher Information
Speaker: Wu Jiatong, Nutritionist
Specializations: Balanced Eating for Health, Weight Management Nutrition, Chronic Disease Nutrition (Cardiovascular Diseases, Diabetes, Kidney Disease), Dementia Nutrition, Cancer Nutrition
Educational background:
Master's from the Institute of Food Safety and Health at the College of Public Health, University of Taiwan
Taipei City Community Nutrition Promotion Center Dietitian
National Taiwan University Hospital Cancer Center Branch Dietitian
Taipei荣民總醫院 Nutrition Department Clinical Dietitian

activity_name_activity_session: National Taiwan University Coconut Grove Lectureship Navigation Series -院士贺端华 from the Academia Sinica
activity_content:
The Taichung University of Arts Coconut Grove Lecture Series is a school-wide seminar inviting scholars or industry leaders with outstanding achievements or significant international influence to give lectures and academic exchanges, in order to broaden faculty and students' macroscopic global perspectives. This time, Professor Dengduanhua from the Academia Sinica will be delivering the lecture, welcome all faculty and students to actively participate.

Activity information:
Speaker: Academian Hsia T’ang-hua from Central Research Institute
Topic: "Plants Are Earth's Guardians"
Time: May 9, 2025 (Friday) 15:30 - 17:00
Location: B1 Grand International Conference Hall, Building 1, Management College
Language: Chinese

Speaker Introduction:
Dr. Dianhua Cai is an Academician of the Academia Sinica, an Associate Member of the Chinese Academy of Science Fellows (TWAS), and a Fellow of the American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS) and the American Society for Plant Biology (ASPB). He served as ASPB President from 2010 to 2012, becoming the first president of the organization from Taiwan. In 2024, Dr. Cai was awarded the highest honor of the ASPB – the Charles Reid Barnes Honorary Life Membership Award for his outstanding contributions to plant research field.

Dr. Hsieh graduated from the Department of Plant Science at National Taiwan University, obtained a bachelor’s degree, and then pursued a PhD in biochemistry at Michigan State University in Michigan, USA, followed by postdoctoral research in molecular genetics at MIT. Dr. Hsieh is considered a pioneer in applying molecular genetic tools to plant research, with his main areas of study including: (1) regulation and signal transmission of plant hormones, (2) function of stress proteins in plants, and (3) production of biofuels and bioproducts from agricultural waste among other applications.

Currently, Professor Xie serves as a Guest Chair Professor at the Institute of Plant and Microbial Sciences of the Academia Sinica, where he served as the institute’s director from 2003 to 2008. In the international academic community, Professor Xie has taught at Washington University in St. Louis for over three decades, currently holding the title of Honorary Emeritus Professor and Consultant there, and is also appointed as a Distinguished Visiting Research Chair at National Taiwan University, Honorary Chair Professor at National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University, National Cheng Kung University, National Tsing Hua University, and China Medical University. He has dedicated himself to nurturing the next generation. In addition, he serves on the editorial boards of multiple international journals and is a consultant for several biotech companies, making significant contributions to global plant science research and applications.

Activity schedule:
14:50-15:30: Registration and entry open (registration closes at 15:25)
Opening remarks: 15:30-15:35
15:35-16:35: Presentation Sharing
16:35-16:55: Live Q&A
16:55-17:00: Presenting awards and group photo on site

activity_name_activity_session: Mindfulness for Stress Relief - A Living Awareness Course by Neuroscience Experts
activity_content:
In the fast-paced high-speed modern era, modern people lack little in terms of stress, which can even affect sleep and mental and physical health.
In this lecture, Professor Wu will combine brain imaging research to help us understand what mindfulness is and through short mindfulness exercises, reduce anxiety in our hearts, improve sleep quality, allowing ourselves to be good friends with our inner selves and find a balance in life.

Speaker: Professor Wu Changwei
Affiliated with the Institute of Mind-Brain Sciences at Taipei Medical University
Specializes in using functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) technology on neuroscientific topics such as sleep, music, and mindfulness, which originate from everyday observations.

Time: May 8 (Thu) 12:00–13:30
Location: Administrative Building First Conference Room
Note: Login for 1 hour of learning time and prepared vegetarian meal available.

National Taiwan University Fu Chi Faculty Staff Association cordially invites

activity_name_activity_session: [Taiwan University College of Literature Joint Lecture on New Trends in Civilization] — Comparative Phonological Systems
activity_content:
Time: May 8 (Thu) 2:20 PM - 4:20 PM
Location: National Taiwan University College of Literature Lecture Hall
Topic: Comparative Sound Systems
Host: Ching Yu-ju (Professor and Dean of the College of Liberal Arts at National University of Taiwan)
Speaker: Andrew F. Jones (Professor, Department of East Asian Languages and Cultures, University of California, Berkeley)
Organizers: National Taiwan University College of Literature, Trend Education Foundation, Co-Working Civilization New Trends, Symphony of Humanities and Technology

This lecture will trace the history of sound conversion technology, from public broadcasting systems to portable speakers, and explore how these systems’ design is intimately connected with their diverse political and social functions and impacts. From China’s wired broadcast networks, to the sound clashes across the Taiwan Strait during the Cold War era, and how audio amplification systems became the foundation for new communities and possibilities of popular music worldwide. This lecture delves into how sound technology evolved from a scientific invention to become a socio-technical entity with significant political dynamism and cultural shaping power, leading to a more comprehensive understanding and reflection on contemporary media, political operations, and musical culture.

Without the loudspeaker, Adolf Hitler is supposed to have said, “We would never have conquered Germany.” While Thomas Edison’s invention of the phonograph in the 1870s allowed humans to realize the age-old dream of fixing that most ephemeral of wave-phenomena, sound, onto material media, it was the loudspeaker that enabled an industry based on mass production of music and a politics capable of mobilizing national audiences. In this lecture, it will trace the history of sound transduction from public address systems to portable audio devices and demonstrate how the design of these systems is intimately connected to their varied political and social functions and implications. The lecture will cover China’s wired broadcasting network, Cold War-era clashes in sound across the Taiwan Strait, as well as how amplified sound systems became the foundation for new communities and popular musical possibilities worldwide.

Speaker: Andrew F. Jones (Professor, Department of East Asian Languages and Cultures, University of California, Berkeley)
Organizers: National Taiwan University College of Literature, Trend Education Foundation, Co-Working Civilization New Trends, Symphony of Humanities and Technology

This lecture will trace the history of sound conversion technology, from public broadcasting systems to portable speakers, and explore how these systems’ design is intimately connected with their diverse political and social functions and impacts. From China’s wired broadcast networks, to the sound clashes across the Taiwan Strait during the Cold War era, and how audio amplification systems became the foundation for new communities and possibilities of popular music worldwide. This lecture delves into how sound technology evolved from a scientific invention to become a socio-technical entity with significant political dynamism and cultural shaping power, leading to a more comprehensive understanding and reflection on contemporary media, political operations, and musical culture.

Without the loudspeaker, Adolf Hitler is supposed to have said, “We would never have conquered Germany.” While Thomas Edison’s invention of the phonograph in the 1870s allowed humans to realize the age-old dream of fixing that most ephemeral of wave-phenomena, sound, onto material media, it was the loudspeaker that enabled an industry based on mass production of music and a politics capable of mobilizing national audiences. In this lecture, it will trace the history of sound transduction from public address systems to portable audio devices and demonstrate how the design of these systems is intimately connected to their varied political and social functions and implications. The lecture will cover China’s wired broadcasting network, Cold War-era clashes in sound across the Taiwan Strait, as well as how amplified sound systems became the foundation for new communities and popular musical possibilities worldwide.

activity_content: The forum aims to promote the diverse applications and innovations of agricultural technology. It will focus on frontier technologies in three major areas: "environment, biology, and humanities," and delve into discussions around cutting-edge topics such as multi-dimensional health, precision agriculture, and food innovation. The event will invite representatives from academia, industry, and government to share their latest research findings and application cases, with the expectation of fostering cross-disciplinary collaboration to explore how technology can enhance agricultural productivity, promote food safety, meet diverse health needs, and provide more innovative solutions for future agricultural development.

activity_name_activity_session: Open Innovation Design Academy D-School offers Stanford courses that you can learn in D-School, where you can master the strongest cross-disciplinary resources and take you "to win" towards the future!
activity_content:
Do you know? The Stanford University’s “Design Thinking” and popular “Designing Your Life” courses, which have blown up globally, can also be learned in the D-School! **🎓💡

The D-School combines these original ideas with local contexts, helping you solve complex problems in life, learning, and career through design methods. It allows you to break traditional blind spots using creative thinking to pave the way for your future.

📣 If you once envied Stanford’s schedule, now you can experience it without leaving!
Come to D-School and create your own unique path. 🛠✨

D-School Joint Unboxing Explanation Meeting – Miss It Next Year!
Do you know? The D-School holds this in-person meeting annually, only during the second semester.
Wanna have one-on-one chats with teachers and seniors in person for clarification? Opportunity like this is only available once a year!
Last year's classmates who didn't make it, don't miss out this year either. The queue is as long as at a concert, from the ordinary teaching hall to Xiumen Platform, aren't you coming? 🎤🔥

D-School gives you your most important opportunity before graduation!
Are you still wondering: "What exactly can cross-disciplinary learning help me with?"
Still worried: "Why didn't I know about these resources after graduation?"

Don't wait until you regret it! This workshop will help you understand the cross-disciplinary advantages that D-School can give you!

This workshop will unlock all possibilities for you in D-School.
Bachelor's Degree Program in Innovative Fields – Cultivating Talent Adapted for Future Changes Across Disciplines.
Leadership Program — Harness Your Students' Influence and Advance on the Path of Dreams
The ChuangChuang Program – Entrepreneurial Spirit of Multidisciplinary Creativity and Fearlessness in Failure Fuels Your Startup Soul
Explore learning – decide for yourself what you want to do, and National Taiwan University supports you; the whole world is your classroom.

What else can you gain from this event?
Teacher Master and Senior Sister Q&A, Answer All Your Questions!
🎁 Professor D and D Goodie Bag make learning more fun!

session_name: Kadan Kai Dan Internship Program Briefing Meeting
session_content:
2025 Kaida Digital Sustainability Seedling Cultivation Program Internship Plan Briefing Meeting

Plan Overview
The internship program that KDAN releases every year provides opportunities for students just entering the workforce to cultivate workplace skills, put their knowledge into practice, and increase their competitiveness. The origin of the Digital Sustainability Seedling Cultivation Program is to combine KADN’s products built with a "sustainability" spirit, along with the “seeds” represented by students, in order to hope that you can grow sufficiently nourished – with enough “sunlight,” “water,” and “air”—at KDAN’s fertile soil to flourish.

KDAN welcomes even more partners with ideas for digital sustainability, unbound by disciplines and frameworks. As long as you are passionate, we welcome you to join this fertile soil of talent development where you can absorb nutrients and create your own future in the realm of digital.

Want to intern in software industry? Here's a 3-minute look at KDAN (凯迪) Digital Sustainability Seedling Cultivation Program: https://www.kdan.com/zh-tw/blog/about/kdan-digital-internship/

Meeting Content
KDAN is what kind of company #SoftwareIndustry #SiliconValleyCulture #StartupStyle
Explore Future Digital Career Paths: Find Relevant Digital Internship Content! #SmartChoices #CareerDevelopment
KDAN Digital Sustainability Seedling Cultivation Program 3.0 #5 Key Training Points #Eligibility for Application #Training Content

Participate in full day, draw Starbucks gift vouchers (20 winners) on-site and participate in on-site prize drawing.
Free精致 meal boxes for the first 30 attendees on the day

Recruitment Timeline
Registration deadline: Now until August 25, 2025.
Internship period: From now until June 2026 (start date can be discussed) (can be aligned with internship credit application)

Internship registration link: https://kdanmobile.teamdoor.io/
Also please have your classmates apply on the official website of the "Interdisciplinary Internship - Taiwan Guide Program".

About KDAN (Stock Code: 7737)
KDAN (KaiTun) is dedicated to providing diverse AI-intelligent workflow and data solutions, including digital document management, electronic signatures, automated data processing, and AI-driven decision-making services. These assist enterprises in enhancing operational efficiency and organizational agility, optimizing decision-making processes, and creating business value in a safer and more efficient manner towards achieving the vision of sustainable development.

KDAN headquarters is in Taiwan, with operations spanning China, the United States, Japan, South Korea, and Singapore. It has accumulated over 12 million global members, with more than 50,000 corporate members. It also ranks among the top 500 high-growth companies in Asia-Pacific by Financial Times, leading in the IT and software category in Taiwan.

Introducing the Internship at Zhongding Group: A Journey Along Its Smart and Innovative Path
Group Introduction
Intern Planning of Innovation and Wisdom Department
3. QA

Internship vacancy information: https://ntucace.ntu.edu.tw/institution/profile/detail/sn/68
Sure, please provide the text you would like translated.
Zhongding is an international turnkey engineering group that is both "No. 1 in Taiwan and one of the top 100 globally." It handles everything from project planning, design, procurement, manufacturing, construction supervision, testing operations to global major projects. As it has grown, apart from its existing service areas such as oil refining, petrochemicals, and chemical engineering, it has also expanded into power generation, steel, warehousing and transportation, traffic, incineration plants, public works, environmental engineering, and high-tech fields; and successfully transferred its engineering technology from abroad to Taiwan, becoming a partner of choice for internationally renowned engineering companies. This showcases impressive achievements.
Zhongding embodies the concept of being "Guardians of Earth's Sustainability" by integrating sustainable development into its core business. This has earned it a top honor from S and P Global’s 2023 Sustainability Yearbook, which recognized Zhongding as one of the best-performing companies globally in construction engineering with an impressive score of 100 out of 100 and a total score of 86, placing it at the highest level among global construction engineering firms.

Speaker: Shih Sui-chen,院士 (Academy of Science for Academica Sinica fellow, former Director of the National Palace Museum)
Language: Chinese

Speaker Introduction:
Shih Shou-chen, a distinguished art historian, has spent his career researching Chinese painting history and East Asian art history. He graduated from the Institute of History at this university and earned a PhD in Art and Archaeology from Princeton University; he joined the Academia Sinica’s Institute of Historical Language and Culture in 1984 and served as a professor and director of the Department of Art History at National Taiwan University, as well as vice president and president of the National Palace Museum. He was elected an Academician of the Academia Sinica for the 29th session in 2012.
Its research approaches Chinese painting history from a cultural history perspective, focusing on the relationship between style transformations and cultural contexts, regional artistic development, Sino-Japanese art exchanges, and the interaction and transformation of "high culture" and "low culture" concepts. It offers insightful perspectives to Chinese art history theory and methods. In recent years, it has led cross-national projects such as “The Mobile Peach Blossom Source” at the Academia Sinica and “Shaping of East Asian Cultural Imagery,” promoting an East Asian perspective in Chinese art history research and delving into historical interactions between artworks and audiences.
Dr. Shi has made remarkable academic achievements, having twice received the Outstanding Research Award from the NSFC and the Special Merit Award for Talents by the NSFC. His book "From Style to Painting Intent: Reflections on Chinese Painting History" won the 35th Golden Bridge Award from the Executive Yuan's News Agency, he was awarded the First Annual Academia Sinica Humanities and Social Sciences Academic Book Prize for "Moving桃花源—Chinese Landscape Painting in the East Asian World," and in 2020, he was selected as one of the most influential research books in the humanities and social sciences field by the Ministry of Science and Technology. He enjoys high reputation and influence within the art history community.

session_name: Cai Minsheng, President of Min Sheng Healthcare System, "Think Big, Act Big, Be Big – Cultivate Virtue and Act with Kindness toward People"
session_content:
Activity Information
Time: May 22, 2025 (Thursday) 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM
Location: Chaozhong Campus Bolya Teaching Building Room 102
Host: Ding Shiting Deputy Vice Principal
Speaker: Yang Minsheng, CEO (Minsheng Healthcare System)
Language: Chinese

Speaker Introduction:
President Yang Min-Sheng of Minsheng Healthcare System graduated from Jingu High School and was admitted to the Medical College of National Taiwan University. During his early university days, he once considered switching majors due to academic setbacks, but in his third year when he encountered clinical courses, he personally experienced the profound meaning of the doctor-patient relationship, which solidified his resolve to become a physician. After graduation, he served as an外科 surgeon at Taipei Medical University Hospital for eight years, accumulating practical clinical experience and gradually became aware of the limitations imposed by the existing healthcare system on both patients and doctors.

In 1975, President Yang established "Yang Minsheng General Hospital," proposing the founding concepts of "shared medical resources" and "patient-centered care." This hospital promoted open medicine and created a treatment space where doctors could freely enter and exit, and patients could actively participate in decision-making. At the time, this concept was considered forward-thinking and laid the core values of Minsheng Healthcare System. As the scale expanded, the hospital transformed into "Minsheng Comprehensive Hospital," gradually expanding its branches to develop today's multi-institutional Minsheng healthcare system that covers acute and chronic care, making it a significant private medical benchmark in Northern Taiwan.

In addition to his work in the healthcare sector, President Yang has long been involved in public service. He served as the president of the Taoyuan County Physicians' Association and was concerned with physicians' rights and local medical environments. After joining the Taoyuan Rotary Club in 1975, he continued to participate in international Rotary affairs for a long time, holding positions such as club president, regional supervisor, chairman of the Taiwan headquarters, and chairperson of the Japan-Taiwan International Rotary Friendship Society, actively promoting social service and international exchanges.

Starting from his medical初心, President Yang Min-sheng embarked on a healthcare entrepreneurship path that balances professionalism, systems, and human concerns in a reformer’s stance. With the compassion of a physician and the foresight of an entrepreneur, he built a people-centered healthcare network, writing a page with significant inspiration for Taiwan's healthcare development.

activity_name_activity_session: "Advanced Lecture Series" Fourth Fiftieth "Literary Studies between Universalism and Apocalypse"
activity_content:
Date: Tuesday, April 29, 2025
Time: 09:00 – 11:00 AM (GMT +8)
Format: Online Meeting via Cisco Webex

Introduction:
The lecture will consider how literary study as a discipline has evolved over the past century through a struggle against universalism, and how in our time new perspectives have arisen to reflect the emergence of methods (such as digital humanities) and public issues (such as climate change). For a discipline that has overcome universalist assumptions almost in living memory, could a new universalism be on the horizon?

Speaker:
Roland Greene (Mark Pigott KBE Professor, Anthony P. Meher Family Professor of the Humanities; Director, Humanities Center; Professor of English and Comparative Literature, Stanford University, USA)

Moderator:
Sebastian Hsien-hao Liao (Distinguished Professor, Department of Foreign Languages and Literatures; Dean, Institute for the Humanities and Social Sciences, NTU)

activity_name_activity_session: [Taiwan University College of Literature Cross-Disciplinary Forum: AI Two] – What Can We Learn from Previous Artificial Intelligence Before Deep Learning?
activity_content:
Time: April 29 (Tue) 14:00-16:00
Location: National Taiwan University College of Literature Conference Room
Topic: What Can We Learn from Previous Artificial Intelligence Before Deep Learning?
What Can We Learn from Artificial Intelligence Before Deep Learning?
Speaker: Yang Zhewang (Professor of History of Science and Philosophy of Science at the University of Toronto, Toronto, Canada)
Speaker: Hsieh Shuhua (Professor at the Institute of Linguistics, National University of Taiwan and Deputy Dean of the College of Literature)
Speaker: Yang Zhenshang (Research Fellow at Statistical Science Research Institute, Academia Sinica)
Speaker: Zhang Guomin (Associate Research Fellow of Institute of History and Philology, Academia Sinica)
Organizers: National Taiwan University College of Literature, New Millennium Taiwan Humanities

Discussing the past and future of AI!
The AI hype was driven by deep learning, a framework based on neural network architectures that gained momentum in the 2010s. Current developments in artificial intelligence are characterized by deep learning technologies dominating almost all fields. However, before this "deep learning revolution," artificial intelligence research had explored other avenues as well. In the second half of the 20th century, the mainstream direction for AI research was not to develop computer architectures and algorithms that simulate brain neural networks but to build logical reasoning systems that mimic cognitive processes of the mind. Can we reconsider the potential of artificial intelligence from a historical perspective by leaving behind the "black box" of deep learning?

This interdisciplinary forum will explore the trajectory of artificial intelligence through a dialogue between history and technology, while also reflecting on possibilities beyond deep learning.

activity_name_activity_session: "With Leadership in Mind Series Lecture | Guest Lectures by Mr. Chih-Hao Chu (Partnership Manager of Silan Garden Venture Capital in Silicon Valley) and Mr. Shengmin Lee (Founder of CTPS Lab)!"
activity_content:
Hello,
We are a leadership program at National Taiwan University, dedicated to cultivating students' thinking for leadership, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Enrollment for the May session is now open! We will be hosting two sessions, inviting leadership program instructors Qiu Zhixiao and Li Shengmin to share insights on their personal career trajectories, stories as leaders, and experiences joining the program. Students interested in joining are welcome to register and attend!

Session 1: From Entrepreneurship to Teaching, Leading Your Career
Speaker: Chih-Hao Kuo (Partners at Acorn Campus Silicon Valley, an investor in Taiwan, and a board member of Playrix)
Time: April 25 (Fri) 19:00-21:00
Location: Electrical Engineering Hall 106
Introduction: As the Chief Technology Officer of GigaMedia, the only internet company listed on Nasdaq in Taiwan, Professor Chu Zhihao deeply understands the importance of running a team and has applied his experience to teaching leadership courses. Curious about how Professor Chu embarked on his entrepreneurial journey, moving from the electronics industry into biotech and biomedical industries? How did he learn from failure and then mentor startup teams and foster产学 partnerships? Register now!

Session 2: Handling Unknown Problems? A Capability That Generation Z Must Have
Speaker: Li Shengmin (founder of CTPS Lab, problem-solving coach, corporate consultant)
Time: May 7 (Wed) 19:00-21:00
Location: Ordinary Teaching Building Room 102
Introduction: After graduating from National Taiwan University’s Department of Electrical Engineering, Teacher Sherman has spanned the finance, telecommunications, IT and technology industries. Currently, he leads a course on solving complex problems (CTPS) that enrolls over 1000 students. Why is it important to solve unfamiliar problems? How did Teacher Sherman transition between different fields and arrive at his current path? If you are curious, hurry and sign up to find out!

For any questions, please contact us via email at ntuleadership@ntu.edu.tw.
activity_name_event_page: "With Leadership in Mind Series Lecture | Guest Lectures by Mr. Chih-Hao Chu (Partnership Manager of Silan Garden Venture Capital in Silicon Valley) and Mr. Shengmin Lee (Founder of CTPS Lab)!"
session_name: Qu Zhihao, a partner at Sequoia China in Taiwan, teaches you ✨ from entrepreneurship to teaching, leading your career ✨
session_content:
There are lots of things I want to do, but time just isn’t enough? ❓ How should students start their own businesses and where does the funding come from? ❓ And why should I develop leadership skills when I don’t want to be a leader myself?
This time, we invited Program Leadership Instructor #Kew-hawk to clarify doubts for you!

While studying at the Electrical Engineering Department of National Taiwan University, Professor Chih-Hao Yu co-founded GigaMedia, the only internet company listed on the Nasdaq in Taiwan. This experience as CTO helped him realize the importance of running a team.
He is now a senior angel investor and Taiwan partner of the Silicon Valley Seed Fund, having moved from the electronics industry into biotech and life science industries to promote investments in forward-looking technologies, assist in talent development and team building for startups, and accelerate connections and collaborations between academia and industry.
This speech will provide you with an opportunity to broaden your horizons and accumulate practical wisdom. Don't forget to jot down the time, let's listen to Teacher Qiu's独家 experience and insights together!

From entrepreneurship to teaching, leading your career.
Speaker: Kwai Chih-hao (Partner at Acorn Campus Silicon Valley, and Board Member of Neko Entertainment)
Time: April 25 (Fri) 19:00-21:00
Location: Electrical Engineering Hall 106
Registration Form: https://forms.gle/KwMdTcMC9vurQeGJ6

activity_name_event_page: "With Leadership in Mind Series Lecture | Guest Lectures by Mr. Chih-Hao Chu (Partnership Manager of Silan Garden Venture Capital in Silicon Valley) and Mr. Shengmin Lee (Founder of CTPS Lab)!"
session_name: Taiwan University's CTPS course, which attracted thousands of students and has a market value over a million, comes to improve your problem-solving ability 🦋
session_content:
"What is the point of solving problems? How do you join McKinsey anyway? I heard that CTPS was initially not taken seriously."
Listen as Teacher Li Shengmin eloquently shares how he developed CTPS into a course that attracted thousands of students at National Taiwan University!

After graduating from National Taiwan University’s Electrical Engineering Department, Professor Sherman did not choose to become an engineer but instead honed his skills at IBM, McKinsey & Company, Capital One, and Hon Hai (Foxconn), spanning the financial, telecommunications, IT, and technology industries. He later joined a leadership program where he taught the popular course “Theory and Practice of Problem Solving” (CTPS), which attracts over 1,000 students per semester.
Handling unfamiliar problems is a capability that everyone must master, and only by continuously solving unfamiliar problems can one sustain value creation. Teacher Seungmin hopes to become the best at teaching "problem-solving" in all of Taiwan within five years, and he is now gradually practicing and achieving this goal.
It ranges from project issues to career issues, tackling problems across different fields and teaching the secrets. Why is solving problems important? How do we switch between different fields and arrive at today's choices? If you are curious, hurry and sign up to find out!

✨Handling unfamiliar problems? A capability new generations must possess✨

activity_content:

Venue: College Hall Lobby

Entry is free.

National Taiwan University College of Literature

Orchestra of Harmony: Huazhi and Sound
activity_name_event_page: College of Arts Music Festival "Sound of Harmony and the Melody of Words"
session_name: College of Arts Music Festival "Sound of Harmony and the Melody of Words"
session_content:
[Intro to the Ensemble] "Huazhi Huandong" is a Japanese traditional music ensemble composed by尺八 player Liu Rengrong and Japanese箏 player Xu Shuping. The name of the ensemble was given by master Katsuko Hanae, who plays the Japanese箏. In Japanese, "hua" can mean "flower," while also representing "zhonghua," which means "Chinese." "He" in Japanese culture symbolizes harmony and music performance. "Huazhi Huandong" showcases a grand ensemble like flowers, not only playing traditional instruments of the Yamato people but also capable of performing Chinese music. It is an artistic group with dual musical abilities (bi-musicality). In the future, it hopes to create new musical life by composing and blending elements from China and Japan.

Performer
Jing Rong Liu | Shizhubei

Taiwan native, Bachelor of the Chinese Music Department at National Taiwan University of Arts and Master of Ethnomusicology at National Tainan College of Art. He studied尺八 with Grandmaster Kōtoku ōdaka. In 2011, he entered the NHK Folk Instrument Selection Show with a performance of an ancient尺八 piece "鹿の遠音", which was broadcast nationwide in Japan. This marked Taiwan's first performer to receive this honor. Besides his passion for traditional music performances, he also participates in various interdisciplinary performances exploring the possibilities of combining tradition and modernity. In 2021, he published a flute textbook titled "Emperor Flute Heart Method," and in 2022, he recorded and released an album called "Beautiful Island." He is currently the团长of "Huazhi Huanduyin" ensemble, Artistic Director of "Taipei Shakuqun Ensemble," member of "Shangxian Music Group," Vice President of Taiwan Flute-Bamboo Association, and兼任teacher at China Culture University.



Xu Sui Ping | Japanese Koto

Taishan, Taiwan native. Graduated from the Graduate Institute of Ethnomusicology, National Taichung Arts University, in the Performance Group. Initially taught by Professor Zheng Deqian on the Japanese筝, later studied with Doctor Tōba Masako, Doctor Miyata Keigo-roku, and Kōbaka Kokusho. In 2022, he studied with Professor Chiou Tsun-mu from Tokyo University of the Arts. From 2007 to 2009, he went to Japan where he won second place in the Hachibuchi Institute Japanese筝 Competition and was selected for NHK Selection Show, which aired nationwide and broadcast his recording. He is the first Taiwanese performer to receive this honor. He held a solo concert at Tokyo Kioi Hall after returning to Taiwan. After coming back, he formed "Harmony of East and West" with shakuhachi player Liu Linyung. He assisted in the recording of "Spring Sea" for the Taishan Arts University Japanese筝 and shakuhachi ensemble which was nominated for the TransArt Golden Melody Awards. In 2021, he published his first Chinese textbook on the Japanese筝 titled "Harmony of South Sound." Recently invited to perform worldwide, collaborating with multiple masters of shakuhachi and suona. Actively promoting Japanese folk music education and performances. Currently a part-time lecturer at National Taichung Arts University, vice-leader of "Harmony of East and West."

Performance Pieces

"Three Preludes Lettered by Wind" Chang泽 Shunjun, Composer

"Teng Wuxing" Yuan Yong曲 (Note: Assuming this is a title with artist and composer)

"Kabuki Genji Chapter One 'Toukui'" composed by Sakuragi Shojirou and lyrics by Sannomura Shuya

"Spring Night Rain" He Dailing Words and Music

"Koyomagata under the Title of for Mōdane by Kōkue Ikeuchi and Takuya Yatsu"

"Dance of Farewell" Kōgai Gōkūzaru Composition

session_content:
Through the Career Aptitude Diagnostic Test (CPAS), understanding one’s 12 personality traits and five categories of aptitude for work and leadership potential to explore personal career fit, encouraging students to discover their career development potentials and directions. This workshop collaborates with professional assessment consultants and is conducted through a simultaneous online viewing of videos and offline analysis and consultation methods. During the in-person workshop, consultants provide analysis and consultation services. The expectation is that students will understand their career fit orientations, thereby assisting students in preparing for career planning.

activity_name_activity_session: "Entrepreneur Growth Group 113-2": Practicing what you've learned through internships? Business School classmates Coffee Chat!
activity_content:
All classmates: We are the tutors in charge of the Tutoring Center for the College Program,昱豪, Man-Ting.

Seeking internship opportunities during college and finding a dream job after graduation are goals that many MBA classmates strive for, though they often also bring pressure. This can lead to anxiety, disappointment, and confusion in moments when we cannot achieve what we desire. What do internships offer? Is it really necessary? What preparations should one make before an internship? Should one balance GPA while participating in business competitions and academic clubs? If someday I suddenly feel like I might have "taken the wrong path," and that there are other areas I'd rather explore, how can I handle this? Feeling overshadowed by every classmate who seems to be so much more capable and experienced than myself, what can I do to break through?

Here are some of the many questions that many students may have, and thus, the tutoring center will be hosting three small "coffee chat" activities in the near future (with a maximum of 8 people per session, with priority given to students from the College of Management). This allows students who find themselves in similar situations or who once experienced similar feelings to exchange ideas through games and conversations. In each session, we will also specially invite two outstanding seniors to share their insights and experiences during their career exploration process with the students.

activity_name_activity_session: Institute of Literature, Tsinghua University Joint Lecture on New Trends in Civilization – Imagining as a Method of Knowledge Acquisition
activity_content:
Time: April 24 (Thu) 14:20-16:20
Location: National Taiwan University College of Literature Lecture Hall
Topic: Imagination as a Technique for Acquiring Knowledge
Host: Ching Yu-ju (Professor and Dean of the College of Liberal Arts at National University of Taiwan)
Speaker: Yang Zhewang (Professor of History of Science and Philosophy of Science at the University of Toronto, Toronto, Canada)
Organizers: National Taiwan University College of Literature, Trend Education Foundation, Co-Working Civilization New Trends, Symphony of Humanities and Technology

How imagination shapes knowledge? Explorations from the Middle Ages to the Enlightenment
During the medieval to Enlightenment Europe, "imagination" played a key role in knowledge construction. The intellectual elite developed mental images into a system of mnemonic techniques to form an “Imaginary Palace” for organizing vast amounts of information. Artists and craftsmen used imagery-based thinking for design and experimentation. Simultaneously, texts about nature and biology often blended reality with fiction until the 17th and 18th centuries when scientific inquiry was gradually distinguished from fantastical writing, laying the foundation for modern natural knowledge. This lecture will analyze this trajectory and explore its impact on modern cognition and creative methods.

activity_name_event_page: Library Master Lecture: "MASTER Exploration, Opening the Window of Wisdom"
session_name: Cross-disciplinary exploration of self-consciousness and bodily experience [Professor Liang Yiyu, Department of Philosophy]
session_content:
Speech Overview
What is self-awareness? Is there any relationship between self-awareness and our bodily experiences? Are there some basic, lowest-bar forms of self-awareness embedded in everyday bodily experiences? In new research, we find that the boundary between philosophy and science is becoming increasingly blurred. Through virtual reality (VR) studies, philosophers are starting to approach understanding bodily experiences and self-awareness in a multidisciplinary way by combining mind philosophy with empirical sciences.

Speaker Introduction
Professor Liang Yuyu is a Ph.D. in Philosophy from Indiana University, USA. He currently serves as an associate professor at the Department of Philosophy in the College of Liberal Arts and also holds a joint appointment with the Institute for Brain and Mind Sciences in the Medical School. His research interests include philosophical studies on mind, perception, self-awareness, among others, and he established the "Neuroscience and Philosophy Laboratory." He focuses on interdisciplinary research combining analytical philosophy, cognitive neuroscience, and virtual reality technology, dedicated to studying bodily experiences and self-awareness. This has brought new perspectives and contributions to academia.

session_name: Utilize Patents and Literature Maps: Master Innovation Research Trends [Professor Liu Ruxi, Special Appointment Professor of Chemistry]
session_content:
Speech Introduction
This lecture will introduce methods for exploring research and development innovation directions by combining patents with papers, helping researchers quickly grasp the trends in their respective fields. The speaker will use paper and patent maps as entry points to deeply analyze how to find technological breakthroughs, seize technological高地, support technology innovation, improve product quality, and lead market development.

Speaker Introduction
Professor Liu Rugi is a distinguished visiting professor at our school’s Department of Chemistry. He specializes in core technologies in materials chemistry and has been dedicated to developing new materials that convert light into electricity, heat, and other forms of energy. His research finds wide applications in the fields of optoelectronics, energy, and biomedicine. Professor Liu obtained his PhD in Chemistry from Tsinghua University in 1990 and his PhD from the Department of Chemistry and High-Temperature Superconductivity Center at the University of Cambridge in 1992. He served as a Senior Researcher and Director at the Industrial Materials Research Institute of the National Chiao Tung University before joining our school in 1995, where he was appointed as an endowed professor in 2016. Professor Liu has received numerous academic awards throughout his career, including the MOST Outstanding Research Award (2013, 2018), the Tsinghua Academic Paper Award (2017), the IUPAC and NMS Research Award (2015), the Xuangxiang Science Lecture Chair (2019), the Ministry of Education Academic Award (2020), and the Synchrotron Radiation Ring Award (2024). He has also been selected multiple times as a Highly Cited Researcher by Clarivate. To date, he has published over 640 international journal articles, with over 35,342 citations (h-index 95), holds more than 200 patents, and his research is highly esteemed internationally.

activity_name_event_page: Library Master Lecture: "MASTER Exploration, Opening the Window of Wisdom"
session_name: Human uniqueness in the Age of AI: From the mind, consciousness, to happiness [Professor Ye Sulin, Special Appointment Professor of Psychology]
session_content:
Speech Overview
The AI wave is profoundly changing human life while prompting a reflection on the essence of humanity. The development of AI has evolved from behaviorism to machine learning, reflecting both mimicry and breakthroughs in modeling human cognition. Although AI excels in certain domains, it cannot match humans in terms of the uniqueness of human consciousness, which involves first-person subjective experiences such as phenomenological awareness and experiential selfhood. These phenomena are directly felt in the present moment and manifest in bodily experiences and interactions with environments. In the age of AI, understanding and valuing humanity’s uniqueness is particularly important; over-reliance on technology may lead to issues like diminished attention spans and social isolation. Therefore, cultivating a sense of experience and care, emphasizing holistic development, is key to enhancing human well-being and a profound reflection on the future.

Speaker Introduction
Professor Ye Suling is a人文 lecture professor at our school, as well as an Fu Sinian Memorial Lecture Professor. She is also a Lifetime Chair Professor of the Psychology Department and a Joint Appointment Professor in the Institute for Brain and Mind Sciences. She served as the Head of the Psychology Department, Deputy Director of the Center for Neural Biology and Cognitive Science, and Deputy Director of the Center for Artificial Intelligence and Robotics. Currently, she serves as Associate Dean of the College of Science. From 2019 to 2020, she was a Fellow at the Stanford University Center for Behavioral Sciences, and in 2023-2024, she received an Honorary Member from the American Academy of Humanities. She has received multiple honors including Ministry of Education Academic Awards, Outstanding Research Awards from the National Science Council, and Teaching Excellence Awards from Taiwan University. Her research covers sensory information processing, attention and consciousness on perception of the world, and extends foundational research to practical applications. Her interests include perception, attention, consciousness, multisensory integration, positive aging, mindfulness, and happiness, with a focus on display technology, eye-tracking devices, affective computing, and intelligent technology applications. Professor Ye hopes that psychological research can benefit others and make life more complete.

activity_name_event_page: Library Master Lecture: "MASTER Exploration, Opening the Window of Wisdom"
session_name: The best route for others may not be yours. [Mechanical Engineering Professor Jian Kuixuan]
session_content:
Speech Overview
Route planning sounds like a simple mathematical problem, but it actually contains profound individual differences. Each vehicle has its own characteristics, from size and dynamic behavior to the diversity of operating modes, all of which affect how it performs on the road. When we apply general algorithms to all vehicles, we often overlook these subtle differences, leading to unexpected results. This talk will help you rethink what "the best route" means and explore how to design tailored solutions based on each vehicle’s unique needs.

Speaker Introduction
Dr. Kuei-yuan Chien is a professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at our school, and he also holds joint appointments as a Professor in the Institute of Industrial Engineering and the Bachelor’s Program for Innovation Studies. His research focuses on mechanical engineering design, design analysis, and stochastic factor simulation. Dr. Chien actively participates in campus affairs and has served as an Associate Provost at NTU and Deputy Executive Dean of the College of Innovative Design. He is dedicated to promoting integrated engineering courses and practical education. Additionally, he oversees the operations and teaching at NTU’s D-School, guiding faculty and students in addressing interdisciplinary issues, fostering creativity, and showcasing their creative potential.

Want to create the next unicorn company in the AI era?

When AI technology has been integrated into various life scenarios, AI will not replace humans, and those who do not use AI talent will be replaced!

activity_name_activity_session: Fourth Forty Lecture Series: "Is Sphinx Genuine? Risks of Cognitive Centralization and Digital Accelerationism in Generative Artificial Intelligence"
activity_content:
Date: April 23, 2025 (Wednesday)

Time: 14:00 - 16:00 (GMT 08)

Location: Cisco Webex Online Meeting Room

Registration link: https://forms.gle/Gzv1geFZWCL5bxf17

Speaker: Wu Jing (Professor and Deputy Director of the Philosophy Department at China Nanjing Normal University and Director of the "Digital and Humanities" Research Center)
Host: Li Xiao Hao (President of the Institute of Humanities and Social Sciences at National Taiwan University, and a distinguished professor in the Department of Foreign Languages and Literatures)

Speech Summary:
The technical foundation of general artificial intelligence is built on a mechanism that aims to generate universal knowledge. Current mainstream large language models attempt to simplify the human knowledge system accumulated through language into learning and mimicking vast amounts of data, ultimately forming a new type of knowledge architecture centered around digital algorithms.

But this type of model suffers from significant technical contradictions: while capable of handling multiple tasks (such as writing and translation), it often encounters issues like "fast learning, fast forgetting" (over-reliance on training data), "taking the easy way out" (data bias), and "parroting" (lack of genuine understanding). More crucially, over time, the practical performance of these models declines steadily – particularly in complex scenarios requiring synthesis judgments (such as medical diagnosis, legal consultation) – leading to many commercial applications becoming unsustainable and contributing to a tech monopoly.

The deep impacts of this technology route warrant caution: when humans entrust the complexity of language and experience to algorithms, they not only become overly reliant on technical systems but also lead to a closed and monolithic production of knowledge. Behind what appears as an objective and neutral algorithm lies, in reality, a replication of the logic of capital expansion – replacing diverse local knowledge with standardized technological solutions.

While the rapid advancement of large language models is seen as a hallmark of a new phase in the Information Age, their development faces unresolved challenges and limitations. Currently, the development of large language models has exposed real bottlenecks such as high energy consumption and high costs. Instead of blindly pursuing technological breakthroughs, it would be better to establish a governance system involving multiple parties, ensuring algorithmic transparency while allowing algorithms to serve the diverse development of human civilization.

activity_name_activity_session: TELL Talks English Learning Lecture Series
activity_content:
TELL Talks is a series of English language learning lectures launched by the Foreign Language Teaching and Resource Center at National Taiwan University, aimed at helping students find joy in their English studies, overcome obstacles, and acquire practical communication skills through engaging lecture content. By inviting speakers with rich experience, combining topics such as language learning, cultural exchange, and workplace实战, it offers inspiring and practical insights. Whether you are just starting to learn English or want to express yourself more confidently in academic, professional, or international exchanges, TELL Talks will provide you with diverse and practical learning resources!

Session 3: To Go or Not to Go? Sharing of Learning Journey on American Graduate Studies and JD Program Law Degree Studies

Is Studying in America Really Worth It? Is Reading a JD Different from an LLM?

When international局势 change rapidly, studying abroad is no longer just a dream but a real challenge.
Are you ready to leave? Or should you wait a bit?

We invited Larry, who previously served as an assistant research associate at the Foreign Teacher Center,
A current student of the JD program at Pennsylvania State University Law School, USA
From the perspective of a legal professional, sincerely sharing his experiences and observations in America!

What does JD learn? What sets it apart from LLMs?
Is it worth studying in America? What should one prepare?
What personality traits are suitable for people who go abroad to study?
USA Life Observation and Legal Rights Reminder!

Session 4: How to Speak of Taiwan’s History in English?
Sharing practical experience on discussing the February 28 Incident, white terror, and Taiwan's memory with foreign guests.

Have you ever wondered: If one day, when facing foreign friends or international visitors and they ask about the "February 28 Incident" or "White Terror," how would you introduce it in English? To what extent should you discuss it? How could you choose your words to balance truth with understanding?

This lecture invites Fu, who has rich experience in guiding tours and public communication. Starting from his firsthand tour experiences, he will share how he transforms history into an international language and leads tourists to understand stories about Taiwan using English. In addition to discussing linguistic strategies and interpretive angles, Fu will also talk about the amusing incidents and challenges encountered during actual interactions, as well as how he prepares for each cross-cultural dialogue on "memory and history."

You will reap:
How to introduce Taiwanese historical events (such as February 28 Incident and White Terror) in English?
Handling Interpretations of Sensitive Issues and Language Choices
Sharing Real-Interaction Experience in International Tours: Questions, Misunderstandings, and Surprises from Foreign Guests
How Tour Guides Prepare Content and Translate It into Communicable English

Graduate Exchange Experience Sharing Lecture | News Department Alumna's Personal Experience

Study exchange for graduate students is an opportunity or a challenge?
When you have research paper proposals in your hand, a supervising professor, and unfinished final assignments, is it even possible to go for an exchange?

This lecture invites Alicia, a senior from National Taiwan University's School of Journalism, who has just completed an entire year-long graduate exchange program at the University of Oklahoma. She will sincerely share what she didn't expect before leaving, the highlights and challenges of her exchange life, as well as how her research progress and daily life balance out or clash with each other.

Application Process and Considerations for Graduate Exchange Programs
Balancing Writing Progress with Overseas Course Living
Analysis of Pros and Cons of Graduate-Level Exchange
If I could go back in time, what would I most want to have known beforehand?

activity_name_activity_session: There are still spots available for on-site registration [Session 113-2 Tutoring Lecture] They're so "competing"! How am I supposed to survive in "overburdening"?
activity_content:
Always feeling that my classmates are very "stressed" or "amped"?
Feeling very tired at NTU but can’t stop?
Feeling like you're falling behind others if you don't make progress?

Have you had these thoughts before?

The term "internal combustion" originates from China and is commonly used to refer to a state of overcompetition within a certain circle. To be described as "very competitive" means someone possesses considerable advantages in competition, such as excellent academic or professional experience and strong programming skills.
To get more resources, squeeze into the narrow path of graduate schools or big companies, it seems one must constantly remain diligent to maintain outstanding performance and be able to produce impressive成绩单 at the right time. When everyone is pushing towards the same goal, one feels compelled to work harder than others, we seem to also gradually fall into a "overburdening" situation.
But sometimes I also feel like I’ve been on high alert, very tired, or even start to doubt the meaning of all this effort…

This lecture invites Luo Huiqun, a counseling psychologist, to explore the impact of "overqualification" on us and also think together about how to stabilize ourselves within "overqualification" and live as our true selves.

Course Information
Time: April 22, 2025 (Tue) 19:00 - 21:00
Location: 1st floor Classroom 113 of Bo利馆 (Note: "Bo利馆" appears to be a misspelling or non-standard term and should be replaced with the correct name if available.)
Speaker: Luo Huiqun Consulting Psychologist

Faculty Member Now
President of Southern Psychological Counseling Institute
President of New Taipei City Counseling Psychologists' Association
Taiwan Counseling Psychology Association Board Members

Organizers: Academic Affairs Office Counseling Center
Partner organizations: College of Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Management School
Organizers: Lin Ziming, Zeng Yuhao, Liao Manting Tutor Coordinator

3. 若為外籍生請提供居留證正反面掃描檔寄至career@ntu.edu.tw (信件請註明參加企業參訪用)
4. 本活動限臺大在校學生報名。歡迎有興趣參與今年暑假「跨領域實習－臺灣引路人計畫」英業達實習的同學們報名參加！

activity_name_activity_session: College of Literature Music Festival "Midday Stroll through Brazilian Music"
activity_content:
Target: No. 276 College of Literature Music Festival "Midday Stroll in Brazilian Music"
Performance time: April 18, 2025 (Fri.), 12:30-13:20
Venue: College Hall Lobby
Entry method: Free entry; Host unit: National Taiwan University College of Literature
Organizing Unit: National Taiwan University Department of Musicology
Program Introduction
This concert will take you on a journey through the diverse flavors of Brazilian music. From upbeat Choro to passionate Samba, each piece exudes the unique charm of Brazilian music. Additionally, there is an award-winning European Music Competition piece composed for her brother by Luísa Sobral titled "The Love's Love," which dances with passion in its rhythm and gets you lost in its emotional melody. This concert will offer you a midday musical feast filled with the essence of Brazilian music. Welcome aboard to join us on this unique journey into music!
activity_name_event_page: College of Literature Music Festival "Midday Stroll through Brazilian Music"
session_name: College of Literature Music Festival "Midday Stroll through Brazilian Music"
session_content:
Performer

Peng Shuyu | Seven-string guitar
Guitarist and producer. Specializes in Latin style. Has frequently visited Brazil and Argentina, studying under renowned guitarists (including Alessandro Penezzi, Maurício Carrilho, and César Angeleri). In 2009, along with a music partner, they founded the band "Xu Huo Feng" in Taiwan, marking the beginning of Brazilian Choro music performances there. They released their first album, "Xu Huo Feng - Departure," in 2010. In the pop music field, he has arranged and recorded for singer Lily Wang's albums, and has performed at several important Asian music festivals such as the Shanghai Simple Life Festival, Fuji Rock Festival in Japan, Shimonoseki Art Festival, and Sky Music Festival in Malaysia.
In 2019, they performed a new fusion of Taiwanese and Brazilian music at the “Taiwan World Music Festival,” which received praise from curators. In 2021, their album "Bossa Zoo" was nominated for Best Cross-genre Album and Best Producer at the 32nd Trad Arts Golden Melody Awards. Currently teaching the course “Exploring Styles and Collaborative Interpretation” at National Taipei College of Arts, they frequently invite outstanding musicians both domestically and internationally to hold Brazilian music workshops.

Chai Weijing | Accordion
Graduated from the Department of Music, National Taiwan Normal University. Served as the President of the Taiwan Harmonica Association for the 5th and 6th terms.
Promoting and reviving the culture and arts of tuina (Chinese small hand piano) music, interpreting styles ranging from classical to jazz, chanson, tango, original compositions, and world music. Performances have taken place in various cultural activities, music festivals, and lectures across Asia and Europe. I also serve as a composer for accompanying music and arranging, recording, and producing multiple albums.
Established Jingjing Talent and Cultural Marketing Company in Kaohsiung in 2022. Invited to perform as a bandoneon player for the "Ice Carnival" CCTV Spring Festival Gala in Beijing in 2018. In 2014, served as the handpan performer at the world premiere of Koji Ishigure's Suite No. 2 "Wind Rising" conducted by Japanese music master Kōji Ishigure at National Music Hall in Taipei. Established Wenxings International Arts and Culture Company in Taipei in 2014. Established Vincent’s Accordion Studio, a handpan studio under the name of Chai Weijing, in Taipei in 2013.

Chen Chongqing | Lute and plucked string performer
Winner of the First Annual "New Talent in Traditional Chinese Instruments" by Two Platforms Theater. Currently an adjunct professor at National Taiwan University of Arts, a member of Silk Void Jazz Band and Xijing Ensemble, and pursuing a PhD program in Musicology at National Taiwan University. Often participates in international performances, traveling to Europe, America, Asia, and the Caribbean region. In 2020, he won a "Best Album" and "Best Fusion Music" gold award from the Global Music Awards (Global Music Awards). In May 2023, he served as a resident musician for the "Spotlight Taiwan Project," a collaboration between the Ministry of Culture and the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, in Saint Lucia, where he conducted workshops and performed.

Strike | Shih Menghang
Graduated from the World Percussion Program at CalArts, USA, specializing in world percussion. Studied at National Taipei College of Arts, Taiwan, specializing in Western classical percussion. With a foundation in classical music, I have recently delved deeply into the field of world music and specialize in South and North Indian music, West African Ghanaian music, Persian music, Balkan music, Arabic music, Brazilian music, Latin music, and Gamelan music from Bali.

I got you!

Pi Xingjiya: For My Loved Ones

Jacob Baronim: Samba Formula

Clara Nunes: Flea Market

Love between Two People

activity_content:
Career Capability Series Lecture | Graduate Students
Remember why you chose to go to graduate school?
Interest? Avoiding就业?
Is it because "getting into college lets you go to university"?

We invited three speakers from different fields,
They were all graduate students and也曾 felt迷茫 about the future.
They are walking on the path they love.
This time, I will share my personal experiences and turning points with you!
Post-graduate career choices

How to Convert Academic Abilities into Workplace Competitiveness!

activity_name_activity_session: 2025 Occupational Health Lecture (II): Work Will Harm "The Heart": Abnormal Workload and Prevention of Brain and Cardiovascular Diseases Online Lecture
activity_content:
Abstract
Underlying modern workplace efficiency and flexible working hours is the deep threat to health posed by abnormal workloads. Long hours, intensive shifts, and night work have all been scientifically proven to be closely related to an increased risk of brain and cardiovascular diseases.
This lecture will teach participants how to use a risk matrix to assess the risks of developing cardiovascular diseases and workload risks, establish the concept of health risk分级management, and introduce strategies for workplace health promotion and prevention of cerebrovascular disease.

Activity time: April 17, 2025 (Thu) from 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM
Location: Cisco Webex Online Video Conference Live Stream

This activity will be conducted through Cisco Webex online video conferencing. Please prepare your own computer or mobile device to participate, complete the online check-in and attend the entire lecture. Participants who have done so will receive 2 hours of digital learning credits for faculty and administrative staff members at our school.

activity_name_activity_session: 2025 NTU Talks Student-Centered Learning Experiments: A Cross-Cultural Perspective
activity_content:
Have you ever wondered how one can fully leverage their unique intelligence and creativity in learning?
Are you curious about the nine intelligence types and how you can leverage and improve your learning effectiveness?

Welcome all adventurers who are passionate about learning to participate in this special lecture!
Explore the interesting relationship between intelligence and creativity and discover your own potential!


Key points of the lecture: This lecture will explore the following topics.
🎯 1. Develop each form of intelligence: How to strengthen potential according to the principles of multiple intelligences to make learning more personalized.
₂. The Synergy of Smarts: Understanding How Nine Types of Intelligence Amplify Each Other to Produce Powerful Learning Responses.
3. Cross-cultural Creativity: Exploring how cultural background influences individual and community intelligence, broadening your perspective.
4. Interactive Learning Experience: The lecture will include experiments and activities that allow students to participate actively and generate inspiration, as well as engage in full exchanges with attendees!

Speaker: Dr. Randall Nadeau, Executive Director of the Fulbright Academic Exchange Foundation
Topic | Student-Centered Learning Experiments from a Cross-Cultural Perspective
Time | April 16, 2025 (Wed) 14:30-16:00
Location | Liang Guoshu International Conference Hall (3rd floor of Sociology and Social Science College, National Taiwan University)
Other | Attendees will receive 7 points for the English learning passport during this lecture for those who participate in full. 🌟
Limited spots, up to 150 people.
The entire lecture is conducted in English.

activity_name_activity_session: Parexel 2025 Career Development Hiring说明会
activity_content:
PAREXEL International is a top-tier clinical research organization (CRO) in the pharmaceutical clinical trials industry globally. Its headquarters are located in Boston, the biopharmaceutical hub of the United States. It has offices worldwide totaling approximately 83 locations and employs around 20,000 people.

Since its establishment in 1982, BRI has aimed to become a leading provider of innovative medical therapy development and commercialization services in the global biopharmaceutical and medical devices industry. All of the top 50 international renowned pharmaceutical companies have adopted BRI’s services and solutions.


| WHY PAREXEL – PAREXEL is your best career development choice among foreign CROs in Taiwan |

Internationalized work environment
Parexel International, one of the top three global CRO companies, is a major player in Taiwan. As a core hub in the Asia-Pacific region, it has over 700 employees spread across more than 60 departments, offering diverse career opportunities.

Long-standing clinical industry experience and resources
Working with multiple world-class pharmaceutical and biotech companies, PAREXEL participates in 99% of clinical trials for the top 200 drugs sold globally.

Taiwan is rapidly growing, with an important presence in the Asia-Pacific region.
Parexel International Taiwan is an important hub in the Asia-Pacific region, with more than 1/3 of the company’s employees located in the region.

Stable organizational operations and high job security
The excellent company policies provide a stable career development environment, with nearly six out of ten managers having been at PAREXEL for more than eight years.

Recruiting fresh talent and providing comprehensive skill training
Many opportunities and excellent on-the-job training in statistics and databases related fields are provided for students with a background in biotechnology.

Internal Career Development
In 2023 recruitment positions, nearly four out of every five are internal transfers and promotions. As Taiwan serves as the Asia-Pacific hub with over 60 departments, it offers you multiple career possibilities.

promotion pipeline is smooth
High performance is at the core of our company culture, and we promote outstanding employees based on their performance, without age or tenure restrictions.

Highly flexible work style
Commuters: No fines, no tracking, no need to rush buses or drive fast anymore!
Family: Flexible scheduling of work hours to align with family needs

Diverse Ethnic Groups, Broadening视野
Parexel Taiwan employees come from all over the world, including the US, Germany, France, Japan, South Korea, China, Canada, Kenya, and Malaysia.
Cross-cultural collaboration among multinational employees within the company offers many opportunities for communication in English and a second foreign language.

Recent accolades and honors
2023 Top 100 Remote Work Companies by FlexJobs
Selected as "Top CRO to Work With" by WCG CenterWatch Global Site in 2022
