session_name: Parexel 2025 Career Development Hiring说明会
session_content:
PAREXEL International is a top-tier clinical research organization (CRO) in the pharmaceutical clinical trials industry globally. Its headquarters are located in Boston, the biopharmaceutical hub of the United States. It has offices worldwide totaling approximately 83 locations and employs around 20,000 people.

Since its establishment in 1982, BRI has aimed to become a leading provider of innovative medical therapy development and commercialization services in the global biopharmaceutical and medical devices industry. All of the top 50 international renowned pharmaceutical companies have adopted BRI’s services and solutions.


| WHY PAREXEL – PAREXEL is your best career development choice among foreign CROs in Taiwan |

Internationalized work environment
Parexel International, one of the top three global CRO companies, is a major player in Taiwan. As a core hub in the Asia-Pacific region, it has over 700 employees spread across more than 60 departments, offering diverse career opportunities.

Long-standing clinical industry experience and resources
Working with multiple world-class pharmaceutical and biotech companies, PAREXEL participates in 99% of clinical trials for the top 200 drugs sold globally.

Taiwan is rapidly growing, with an important presence in the Asia-Pacific region.
Parexel International Taiwan is an important hub in the Asia-Pacific region, with more than 1/3 of the company’s employees located in the region.

Stable organizational operations and high job security
The excellent company policies provide a stable career development environment, with nearly six out of ten managers having been at PAREXEL for more than eight years.

Recruiting fresh talent and providing comprehensive skill training
Many opportunities and excellent on-the-job training in statistics and databases related fields are provided for students with a background in biotechnology.

Internal Career Development
In 2023 recruitment positions, nearly four out of every five are internal transfers and promotions. As Taiwan serves as the Asia-Pacific hub with over 60 departments, it offers you multiple career possibilities.

promotion pipeline is smooth
High performance is at the core of our company culture, and we promote outstanding employees based on their performance, without age or tenure restrictions.

Highly flexible work style
Commuters: No fines, no tracking, no need to rush buses or drive fast anymore!
Family: Flexible scheduling of work hours to align with family needs

Diverse Ethnic Groups, Broadening视野
Parexel Taiwan employees come from all over the world, including the US, Germany, France, Japan, South Korea, China, Canada, Kenya, and Malaysia.
Cross-cultural collaboration among multinational employees within the company offers many opportunities for communication in English and a second foreign language.

activity_name_activity_session: "Awakening of 'Painting' - A Guided Tour of Original Painting Leaflets During the Japanese Occupation Period"
activity_content:
"The Scattered Steps in Taiwan: An Exhibition of Postcards from the Japanese Era features official and private postcards (viewbooks) from the colonial period, showcasing the beauty of Taiwan's landscape, scenic spots, abundant natural resources, social customs, indigenous images and objects through diverse themes based on what travelers saw and experienced."

Rare and precious illustrated books require storage in controlled humidity and light-free environments, which is why the postcards on display are copies, while the originals remain in the mysterious library’s special collection vault!

The library invites you to participate in the original "Painting" Awakening - Japanese Colonial Period Painting Book exhibition for readers. See the质感, colors, and binding charm of the originals!

"Cool Jungle Elite Program" Film Lecture 🎬

Match 1: "What You Thought Was the Future Is Happening"

In this rapidly changing era, artificial intelligence (AI) is transforming our lives at an unprecedented pace. Have you ever wondered if the future might be closer and more real than we imagine?

This lecture is accompanied by the documentary "A.I. Artificial Intelligence: The Code for Consciousness" (2020), which was shared by Professor Lien Yu-ren, President of ViewSonic Optus College and Associate Professor at中原University's Applied Electronics Department. He led us in exploring how AI restructures education, technology, employment, and human society.

Come join this lecture and explore endless possibilities for the future with us!

Date: 4/15 (Tue)
Time: 2:00 PM - 3:30 PM
Location: New Moon Platform
Sign up now to get an early look at future trends!

None provided; please provide text to translate.

Session 2: The Tough Carbon Problem

April 22 is World Earth Day, reminding us to look back at the challenges this planet faces. Keeping an eye on environmental issues, let’s ponder: “How difficult is the carbon problem in a world of climate change?”
Carbon pricing, carbon trading, net-zero transformation... Are these just solutions, or another test of social choices?

This lecture invites Professor Liu Zhongen from the Department of Sociology at National Taiwan University, paired with the 2024 documentary "Climate Change: A Tough Issue," to lead us through a multifaceted perspective in understanding the institutional dilemmas, policy challenges, and transition realities behind carbon emissions.

This is not just a climate lecture; it’s an open dialogue about “How should we choose for our future?”

Time: 2:00 PM - 3:30 PM
Location: National Taiwan University Foreign Language Teaching and Resource Center Theater

session_name: "Writing is修行" - Hsieh Ting-yu: Television Scriptwriting
session_content:
Topic: Writing is Practice

Speaker Bio:
Chih-Yu Yu: Television director. Graduated from the Department of Library Science, National Taiwan University. Holds a Master’s degree in Film Creation from the California Institute of Arts. During his university days, he won an honorable mention at the Golden Bursar Award for a short film titled “One Afternoon.” He has experience writing scripts across various genres, including commercial television dramas and animation. His directorial work on the TV movie “Life Is Sweet” (based on Li Weijing’s novel) was nominated in the “Best Television Movie and Mini-Series” category at the Asia Television Awards. He hopes to heal himself through scriptwriting, as well as others.

Activity content:
Working in screenwriting for nearly three decades, having witnessed various changes in Taiwan’s entertainment industry, I feel like a “living fossil.” I will lead everyone to recognize the film and television industry with an archaeological attitude. Deeply feeling that to survive means one must approach it as if in spiritual cultivation. If I hadn’t worked as a screenwriter, would I be this kind of person now? As my work progressed, I also gradually grew into an unexpected version of myself. Thus, I want to introduce the work of screenwriting from such a perspective and hope to break through the conventional perceptions of it.
What the scriptwriter really needs is not talent, and what are those skills necessary for their survival in the journey of becoming a seasoned编剧? This is something I will discuss.
A scene of heaven in an instant, followed by hell in an instant is actually the daily routine for a scriptwriter. If you want to experience the vulnerable and dark sides of humanity, and really understand this world and yourself, then become a scriptwriter! Welcome to the cruel world!

Activity Flow:
Introduction and an Overview of the Evolution of the Film and Television Industry
Theme Content: Scriptwriting (approximately 1 hour)
Q&A Session: Audience Free to Ask Questions

session_name: "Social Work and Law: Sharing Experience from Advocacy for Judicial Reform"
session_content:
Topic: Social Work and Law: Sharing Experience from Advocacy for Judicial Reform

Speaker Bio:
Vice President Li Mingruo graduated from the Law School of National Taiwan University and the Institute of Social Work at National Taiwan University.

Content of the round:
My thesis at the Social Work program was on a study of Family Violence Investigators and Guardians Ad Litem in terms of their role in promoting the best interests of children. After graduation, I worked briefly at a law firm mainly handling family matters. However, my choice of this topic is more about being interested in how legal concepts can form dialogue and judgments when social work professionals assist, rather than having an interest in family matters themselves. This question focuses on how different disciplines can collaborate fundamentally.

After entering the Commission for Judicial Reform in 2019, I have gained experience with practice of冤案、監所案件个案 but also more organizational work advocating externally and interacting with media. Unlike a full-time lawyer, I am more like a judicial reform caseworker who seeks resources to assist specific cases and leverage them to address systemic issues. Many times, this role is akin to that of a social worker. My current second career is in this advocacy organization for the judiciary, where within this scope, I can share my thoughts on social work and law over the past six years.

session_content:
Topic: Meeting a Better Version of Yourself - Career Development and Interview Techniques

Speaker Bio:
Hui Ruo Shen (formerly Vice President of Market Risk Management Department at China Investment Commercial Bank)

Content of the round:
Ms. Shen Huiru has over 30 years of experience in the financial industry, starting as an accountant in construction companies and serving as the vice general manager for market risk management at China Trust Commercial Bank. She possesses extensive leadership experience in departments, expertise in recruiting and managing professionals, and holds multiple professional certifications such as a Certified Financial Risk Manager (CFRM) and Certified Internal Auditor (CIA). She has also led her department to receive an award for best risk defense within the bank. After retirement, she offered courses at the Public Affairs Institute titled "Peeling Back the Veil of Financial Statements – An Introduction to Accounting & Financial Statement Analysis," where she not only imparted financial knowledge applicable to social sciences but also shared her experiences and advised students on how to grow professionally and continuously improve in their careers.

session_content:
Topic: Chinese mainland and East Asia Studies – Enrollment Information Meeting

To encourage students to systematically study interdisciplinary courses, our institute offers a China Research Program and an East Asia Research Program.

The China Research Program in mainland China has been established for over ten years, with Professor Ye Guojun from the Institute of Developing Countries offering "Introduction to China Research Program" as a required course to help students establish their research foundation.

The East Asia Studies Program emphasizes English instruction and plans for at least 15 credits of courses, with at least two being fully in English, suitable for students interested in or looking to improve their English proficiency in East Asia.

Welcome and register enthusiastically to hear from top speakers and broaden your academic视野!

session_name: "Every path leads to society."
session_content:
Topic: "Every Walk of Life Enters Society"

Speaker Bio:
Speaker: Inviting 8 alumni, fields: Media work, Public office, Finance industry, Marketing department, NGO or NPO
Activity date: April 20, 2025; Time: 1 PM to 1:30 PM
Location: Social Science Building R319
Host: Guan Ling Wu

Content of the round:
Have you ever heard someone ask, "What does a sociology major study? What can one do after graduation?" Each time I hear this question, some doubts always come to mind: What are the "multiple paths" of a sociology major? Which direction will the future take? Don't worry, the mysterious face of these multiple paths in the future is about to be unveiled...

Friendship Sharing Event "Every Profession Goes to Society" is happening 🌟
What the Department of Sociology provides us with, beyond academic knowledge, is a variety of career options. However, when faced with such diversity, we often feel lost and need to invest more effort in exploring it. Therefore, through this sharing session, we invited alumni from the department who have already entered society to share their career stories and experiences with you. This will expand the possibilities for your future imagination. Listen to the paths they took; capture frames of yourself within each story, then look back at where you are now and where you're headed.

session_name: Storytelling Power x Interviewing Power x Career Switching Power: A Social Science Major’s Career Odyssey -(Business Week)
session_content:
Topic: Storytelling Power x Interviewing Power x Career Switching Power: A Social Science Major's Career Odyssey (Business Weekly)

Speaker: Deputy Editor-in-Chief of Business Weekly, Wu Zhongjie

Speaker Bio:
Executive Editor of Commercial Times Wu Zhongjie graduated from the Sociology Department and Journalism Master's Program at National Taiwan University. He won an award for a feature titled "New Battlefield in the Sino-US Struggle – Hong Kong" in the Asia Excellence News Award, and has received awards such as Modern Finance News Awards and Taiwan Healthcare Reporting Awards.
How did a graduate with a background in social work, who had previously interned at the psychiatry department of a hospital and was anti-business, infiltrate Taiwan’s largest financial magazine, even covering semiconductors and technology news, and accidentally become the last print media journalist to interview Nvidia co-founder Huang仁勳?
Visited international business leaders such as the founder of TSMC, Zhang忠谋, the chairman of TSMC,魏哲家, the founder of Nvidia, Huang仁勳, the CEO of AMD, Su Zhi Feng, and the Chairman of Alphabet, John Hennessy, before the pandemic. Before the pandemic, he flew between ten to twelve times a year as an international traveler for reporting visits to 24 countries including Estonia, Burkina Faso, and Myanmar. Currently, he is also the producer of the "Understand Business, See Shangzou" program on Shangzou YouTube Channel, helping it grow from seven thousand subscribers to nearly forty-six thousand subscribers in just over a year.


Content of the round:
The social work studies were originally a supplementary course for many middle-to-high-level managers to take in their 30s and 40s? - Social Work is actually more 'useful' than you think.
Why is being a journalist still considered "the happiest job in the world" even in an era when KOLs dominate?
Being a financial/depth reporter is worth more than one thinks.
In the age of AI, why is telling a good story your most indispensable ability?
From never having taken an imaging course to becoming a YouTube show producer

12:20-12:30 Opening and Case Sharing Time
Career Sharing 12:30-13:40
13:40-14:10 Q&A Time

session_content:
Topic: Sharing of Career Experiences and Preparation Tips for International Economic Business Personnel on Assignment

Speaker Bio:
Ministry of Economy and Trade / Multilateral Trade Office - Chief Examiner Hsieh Yi-Chieh

Economic Affairs Bureau / Multilateral Trade Division - Xu Zijuan Staff Member

Taipei Chamber of Commerce and Industry / International Trade Youth Empowerment Program - Ye Shuwei Manager
Employed by the Taipei Chamber of Commerce and Industry for Export Trade, responsible for training in trade talent and industry-university cooperation business.
Alibaba Bureau of International Trade Promotion "Trade Small Tigers Internship Program in Emerging Markets" for 7 years
Alibaba Bureau of International Trade and Economic Cooperation's "Youth Empowerment Program" for 3 years


Activity content:
The International Trade Office of the Ministry of Economic Affairs serves as an engine for Taiwan’s economic and trade development. Overseas “International Economic Business Personnel” shoulder the responsibility of establishing and maintaining multilateral or bilateral economic and trade relations with various countries, whether it is in areas such as market expansion, overcoming trade barriers and dispute resolution, drafting and guiding carbon reduction policies, etc., which are part of the daily work of business personnel. This invitation to two business personnel for experience sharing allows more students to recognize the rich career experiences of economic and trade personnel while also understanding related special examination information; additionally, the project team will explain the annual activities of the training program to assist students in exploring careers in economics and trade.

2025 H2U Youth Healthy Oasis Internship Program, Rallying Passionate Minds to Dare Dreams and Strive
Plan Overview
H2U is the largest digital health company in Taiwan and a leader in the digital health industry. The annual internship program leads by professionals from various digital health workplaces, training interns to accumulate practical experience, familiarize themselves with the company's operational processes, and actively cultivate potential talent for both their career and the company’s benefit.
This year, internships are specially recruiting positions for Business Analysts, Business Development, AI and Data Analysis, Social Media Marketing, Video Editing, Course Marketing, Article Editing, and Sponsorship Analysis for Running Events. If you are interested in digital health and data management, welcome to join and help shape your future with us at Be Team H2U.

Internship registration link: https://forms.gle/zq5kZVDxVjg2Ew3z8
Also please have your classmates apply on the official website of the "Interdisciplinary Internship - Taiwan Guide Program".


Four distinctive features
One-on-one Mentorship program
Participate in departmental actual operations
Project-based Internship
Manager training

Internship Time
Summer Internship - June 23, 2025 ~ August 29, 2025, Monday to Friday
Long-term internship - June 2025 to January 2026, Monday to Friday in August and September; at least two days per week from October to January (strongly recommended for better learning outcomes from long-term internships)
Job Brief
Business Development Intern
Operations Room Business Analyst
Innovation Research Room Director AI and Data Analysis Intern
Morning Health Community Marketing Internship
Morning Health Video Editor Intern
Morning Health Course Department Internship Plan
Journaling and Article Editing for Movement Articles
8. Workout Journal Video Editing
9. Running Journal Marathon Project Assistant
Intern for Sponsorship Analysis (Taichung)

You will receive/You'll receive
Mentorship program for professional workplace training
For those interested in developing a career as a management consulting firm, providing training on management consultant programs, resume audits, and simulated interviews are special trainings.
Limited registration for sports events and Weekly Running Day (open to all)
Snacks in the vending machine are for you to take.
More treasures waiting to be discovered.

Recruitment Timeline
Registration deadline: From now until May 4, 2025 (Sunday) at 23:59
Interview notification: Before 12:00 PM on May 13 (Tuesday)
Interview time: May 15 (Thu) - May 28 (Wed)
Admission List Announcement: June 5 (Thu)
Partners who perform well during their internship have the opportunity to become full-time partners of Yongyue Health!

About Us - Practitioners of Holistic Health Living Experience
Taiwan's leading brand in health, Everjoy Health, is a pioneer in digital health. It has the highest market share of health check-up systems in Taiwan, "Ickheim Health Platform." Its B2B service, "H2U eXpert," serves over 250 enterprises. Its C2C services include the largest health media platform in Taiwan, "Early Bird Health," a fitness community, "Fitness Notes," and a health and weight loss community, "iFit Slim Love."
H2U ranked in the Financial Times Asia-Pacific 500 Strong List in 2025, won the AP Business Excellence Award and Harvard Business Review Disruptor Award in 2024, was selected as one of the top 50 global digital health care companies by IFAH in 2019, and is currently actively recruiting. We welcome your joining!

activity_name_activity_session: No. 275 College of Literature Music Concert: Folk Literature and Music – Lanterns in the Wind Mourning Song Group (Lecture Music Concert)
activity_content:
No. 275 College of Literature Music Concert: Folk Literature and Music – Lanterns in the Wind Mourning Song Group (Lecture Music Concert)
Location: NTU Yashengfang

Time | April 11, 2025 (Friday) 14:30 (14:00 entry)
Lecture (14:30-16:20)
Half-time break for 10 minutes
Concert (16:30-17:30)

Location | National Taiwan University Art and Culture Center Yuhongfang
Ticket | Free
⭐️ [Organizing Unit] National Taiwan University College of Literature
Organizing Unit: National Taiwan University Musicology Institute
Organizing Units: National Taiwan University Arts and Culture Center, New Millennium Taiwanese Humanities Research Project
Host | Professor Yamano Shin'endo

Wind Lanterns in the Rain Mourning Song Ensemble
Chairman | Lin Chung-kan
Born in 1990 in Pohua Township, Bungho Tribe in Tainan, awarded the Soul of a Craftsman flag by President Tsai Ing-wen in 2019, attended cultural Olympics performances in Paris in 2024, and invited to perform at the College of Music in National Taiwan University in 2025.
Members | Tsai Pei-chu, Chuow Li-hua, Lin Su-chen

Band Introduction
Feng Zhong D灯 was established in 2017 and has been used since its establishment at Yun College of Technology. The origin of the牽亡歌 dates back to a martial troupe from the mid-Qing Dynasty's Ma Shan region, which incorporated local folk music based on a humorous incident involving siblings, aunties, and cousins. A hundred years ago, this troupe evolved into the牵亡歌. The牵亡歌 leads the spirits through the underworld to reach the Western World while also helping living people express their emotions. It includes traditional colors of Taiwan, ballads, and folk opera, which are forms of local art.

Program Introduction
In traditional Taiwanese funerals, the song of escorting the deceased carries the function of guiding the spirits to the afterlife through a combination of music and rituals. The song of escorting the deceased originates from martial culture "Eighteen Nags Pianzhen" during the middle Qing dynasty, blending Falun Pai sanma ceremony, folk literature, and music. Initially used as religious ceremonies, it later evolved into a part of funeral rites, becoming the final chapter of life music.

Performances: "Yung Ming Bodhisattva", "Dongying", "Lu Guan", "Towing a Ship Across the River", "Flower Garden Tour"

activity_name_activity_session: 114 Academic Year Anti-Harassment Prevention Promotion Training and Education for School Staff
activity_content:
One, pursuant to this school's 20114 academic personnel training plan.
Second, pursuant to the Measures for Preventing Sexual Harassment in Workplace (Article 9), employers should ensure that their employees receive education and training on preventing workplace sexual harassment; specifically, for personnel holding managerial positions, those involved in handling, investigating, and deciding on sexual harassment complaints, such training should be held annually. Additionally, Article 7 of the Administrative Council's "Gender Equality Training Plan for Various Agencies" states that each agency should design different training methods based on various targets, with at least two hours of course training provided to each person annually. Therefore, this seminar is organized to encourage colleagues to actively participate. Moreover, it is encouraged for colleagues to utilize digital learning websites to complete courses related to preventing sexual harassment and enhance their gender sensitivity, jointly creating a gender-friendly workplace.
Here is the translated text:

Three, the planned lecture as follows:
April 10, 2014 (Thursday), 9:30 AM to 11:30 AM.
Location: Administrative Building First Conference Room, Central Campus.
Topic: Rejecting Sexual Harassment - Starting from Recognizing Diverse Gender Identities.
Speaker: Chen Yueshan Attorney.
Eligibility and Number of Participants: 100 members of this school staff.
Four, Registration Method: Participants in this seminar must first obtain approval from their unit supervisor and either register as a public leave or use personal leave. They are to complete registration through the school's activity registration system within the period from March 14, 20114 (Friday) at 8:00 AM to April 7, 2013 (Monday) at noon.
Fifth, please arrive at the venue 10 to 15 minutes early on the day of the training and sign in. No separate notice will be sent. Participants who attend the entire session will be granted 2 hours of public service lifetime learning credits. Please bring your own environmentally friendly cup for environmental policy compliance.

Are you ready to make your application documents captivating and stand out?
Starting from zero: Learning the rules of resume writing, the art of personal statements, interview secrets, and techniques for choosing projects that fit you best 🔥
Help your classmates highlight their academic achievements and personal traits to seize opportunities for overseas study programs, scholarships, and top research grants! 🔥

Week 1: Objective vs. Subjective: Is Science Always Correct? Viewing the Boundaries of Science Through Bias
Week 2: The Societal Code of Science Issues: How Do Social, Environmental, Behavioral, and Biological Factors Intersect to Impact Health?

session_content:

Week 1: Decoding Culture and Community – Through real-life examples, we unravel the history and evolution of communities, seeing how culture shapes the world!

session_name: 2025 Graduate Student New Training Lecture Hall
session_content:
The "Graduate School Orientation Lecture Hall" is held annually during the start of graduate students' enrollment, aimed at providing new graduate students with an introduction to the scientific research spirit and internal research resources of the school. The hope is that before they undergo a role transition and begin their research work, students will be given preliminary knowledge to understand the differences between the research life and university classes. Additionally, since many graduate students are not graduates from the university's undergraduate program, it is hoped that this provides an initial tour of campus resources so that when needed, they can find corresponding administrative or teaching units as well as self-study channels.

Morning Special Lecture
"Ethics and Research Integrity – Towards Responsible Graduate Studies"
Keynote Speech: NCKU - Associate President/Cheif Ethics Officer: Zhou Qian
To guide graduate students in recognizing the essence and responsibility of academic research. It covers basic concepts of academic ethics, distinguishing self-plagiarism from plagiarism, principles of authorship, good relationships between teachers and students, and norms for academic publication and dissemination. It also explores the role and potential risks of artificial intelligence in research. By means of systematic introduction and case discussions, it assists students in establishing a trustworthy attitude towards research to lay a solid foundation for their future academic work.
Speech Outline
Academic research and academic ethics
Plagiarism and self-plagiarism
Author defines
Teacher-student relationship
Academic publications and publishing
Generative AI in Academic Research
Conclusion

session_content:
This activity will be held online using Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company's registration system. Please register by synchronizing with the following link.
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company registration link: https://reurl.cc/XZO9EE
Company Introduction
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) was established in 1987 and pioneered the business model of providing specialized semiconductor manufacturing services, thereby becoming a leading company in this field worldwide. The company supports its customers and partner ecosystems' thriving growth with its industry-leading process technologies and design solution portfolios, unlocking innovation within the global semiconductor industry. As a global corporate citizen, it operates across Asia, Europe, and North America, committed to acting as an advocate for corporate social responsibility.
In 2023, providing services to 528 customers, producing 11,895 different products that are widely applied in various terminal markets such as high-performance computing, smart phones, the Internet of Things, automotive electronics, and consumer electronics; and offering advanced process technologies for the professional semiconductor manufacturing service company producing chips for its clients.

Position Recruitment
Information Technology Engineer (IT)
Corporate Planning Organization (CPO)
Material Management and Risk Management Engineer (MM&RM)
Human Resources Specialist (HR)
Finance and Accounting Specialist (FIN)

Qualification Requirements and Conditions
Graduates with majors in related fields such as finance, economics, accounting, statistics, psychology, among others, who are eligible to start work by the end of 2025 and have completed their master’s or doctoral studies by 2025.

Wages and Benefits
Terms: Negotiable (regular salary of over 40,000 yuan)
Benefits: Comprehensive insurance and retirement plans:
In addition to insuring each employee for labor insurance, universal health insurance, and monthly retirement contributions as required by law, we also provide group comprehensive insurance that includes life insurance, accident insurance, medical insurance, and cancer insurance to increase the overall protection of our employees.
Flexible leave policy:
A special leave system that provides better than labor laws is available to employees after they have been hired for three months. Combined with a flexible leave system, this allows employees to schedule their holidays throughout the year more conveniently. We also legally provide various types of leaves, so when staff members need to take leave, they can do so without any worries.
Inclined work environment:
Being considerate of colleagues' work and life needs, providing comprehensive services and facilities in areas such as food, housing, transportation, education, and entertainment so that colleagues can easily balance their work and personal lives.
Employee Assistance Program:
Emphasizing the physical and mental health of colleagues, annual employee health check-ups and wellness programs help colleagues stay informed about their own health conditions. Employee life and work counseling services, along with outpatient medical services, can provide colleagues with timely professional counseling advice and treatment suggestions.
scholarship system
Encouraging colleagues to pursue further studies and providing subsidies for employee education training fees so that they can participate in related courses outside of TSMC.

activity_name_activity_session: Psychology Lecture: "Spring Sleep Well for Good Mood" 113-2 Mental Health Promotion Lecture at Heartwell Center
activity_content:
Taiwan University Student Affairs Office Counseling Center for Students主办"The Psychology Issue - Spring Sleep Well for Good Mood" lecture

As spring’s footsteps approach quietly, the earth awakens and life grows; our hearts deserve a gentle and soothing care. This semester’s psychology lecture series focuses on two major health issues: sleep and emotions. We invite two professional clinical psychologists to guide us through their rich practical experience in understanding how to release stress, improve sleep quality, and master practical strategies for regulating the body and mind. These insights will help us find a stable rhythm in our daily lives. Let’s infuse warmth into our bodies and minds during these sunny days!

Additionally, to make the lecture content more relevant to the audience’s needs, an anonymous question form was piloted this semester. The audience is invited to submit questions about the lecture topic or share their expectations for the content before the event, based on the lectures they have already registered for. We will compile the feedback from the form and provide this information to the lecturers as a reference. The link to the form: https://forms.gle/bZuZuJiBwzHz9xgY7

Session 1: Good Sleep Plan Initiative
Time: March 24, 2025 (Mon) 6:30 PM - 8:30 PM
Location: Yuying Teaching Building Room 202
Introduction: Answer your curiosity and doubts about sleep through a lecture by a sleep specialist, and create your own plan for better sleep together.
Lecturer: Huang Yalin Clinical Psychologist
Currently a clinical psychologist at Chang Gung Memorial Hospital’s Sleep Center and Psychiatry Department, graduated from the Counseling and Clinical Program of National Chengchi University’s Psychology Institute. Has served in various high schools and universities, co-translated “The Handbook for Clinical Practice of Assessment and Treatment of Insomnia,” and has published multiple papers on sleep-related topics both domestically and internationally. Specializes in clinical treatment of sleep-related issues.

Session 2: Emotion Regulation - Becoming the Master of Your Emotions
Time: April 29, 2025 (Tue) 18:30-20:30
Location: Yuying Teaching Building Room 202
Introduction: This lecture will introduce the relationship between emotions and ourselves, how to collaborate with our emotions to move towards what we expect in life.
Teacher: Chen Shuh Chin Clinical Psychologist
Current clinical psychologist at Mackay Hospital Suicide Prevention Center; a PhD candidate in the Department of Psychology, Taiwan University.曾至美國華盛頓大學行為研究與治療研究室（辯證行為治療創辦單位）擔任短期訪問學者.多年的助人工作中，專精於運用辯證行為治療（Dialectical Behavior Therapy, DBT）陪伴大家一同學習如何整理情緒並與情緒共存。

Note: This series of lectures is conducted entirely in Chinese.

Welcome to those interested in sleep, bring your curious questions, come to get answers from lectures by sleep-specialized psychologists, and together create your own good sleep plan!

activity_name_activity_session: Daffodil Festival series guided tours - Ecological Tour
activity_content:
Taichung University of Technology campus covers approximately four times the area of Da’an Forest Park, which is adjacent to Fragrant Orchid Mountain, Toad Mountain, and Xindian Creek. It is an important green space in Da’an District because early researchers took advantage of its proximity to conduct studies and create a unique human environment. There are also two water body environments, making it have special ecology compared to other large parks within the city.
After decades, the tropical garden plants have grown into large trees, and phenomena of strangulation by native fig trees are not less spectacular than animal behaviors like predator-prey relationships... Occasionally, blue magpies can be seen in flocks, along with crested serpent-birds, crested goshawks, and crested drongos, among other special guests.
There are also adorable residents you shouldn't miss, such as squirrels, black-crowned night herons, red-crowned cranes, and spoonbills.
And turtles with red ears, thrushes, magpies, starlings, gourami, and several other外来 species that help us understand their impact on local ecosystems...

Topic:
One. Fuyuan Tropical Plant Ecosystem Observation
The Story of Lu Gong Zhen Spring Water Pond
Song of Moon Lake - Recognizing the Delicate Guests of Moon Lake

activity_name_event_page: AI assistance, riding the wind soaring nine thousand miles.
session_name: Rights保障 for Generative AI creation and reasonable disclosure
session_content: The application of generative AI tools and modules has become a significant trend in contemporary educational environments. However, the core topic of this lecture is how to adhere to legal compliance and ethical responsibilities when using these tools. From a user’s perspective, understanding the basic scope of copyright protection – namely, the form of expression – is crucial. If there is inheritance of expression, one must be cautious about selecting materials that are legally authorized, whether from CC-licensed or public domain sources; as creators of AI-generated works, they need to assert human creativity according to current intellectual property laws in order to legally claim rights protection for AI-assisted creations.\n\nThis lecture will analyze real-world cases to deeply explore the application and solutions for the aforementioned scenario, assisting participants in preventing infringement disputes or risks arising from the use of AI tools while clarifying the boundaries between personal creation and AI collaboration. The speaker will emphasize how to disclose the role of AI in a reasonable manner to ensure academic contributions align with principles of fairness and equity.

activity_name_event_page: AI assistance, riding the wind soaring nine thousand miles.
session_name: Double-Edge Sword: LLMs, Writing, and You: How to Navigate This Dual-Function Blade?
session_content:
Outline - LLMs are everywhere. Almost every person reading this has used ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Perplexity, or DeepSeek to edit their work, generate code, act as a therapist, give feedback on an idea, or create entire homework assignments. LLMs offer significant benefits in terms of speed, efficiency, and adhering to general writing conventions. However, you are also aware that LLMs come with substantial drawbacks. There are ethical considerations, information hallucinations, poor writing quality, and more. Additionally, recent studies have highlighted long-term effects on the minds of people who use them. There is much to be concerned about!

In this lecture, we’ll discuss the pitfalls of using LLMs and how to adapt to minimize their damage. This includes thinking about which situations LLMs work best and which they don’t, how to let LLMs be a good editor for you but also how to be a good editor for them, and what to watch out for each time you type in a prompt. In the end, LLMs are one of many tools you can use to help your writing. They’re especially great for working in your non-native language. But like any other tool, it doesn’t fit every situation, and you have to learn to use it before you can use it well. After this lecture, you’ll be a big step closer to that goal.

LLMs (large language models) are ubiquitous, such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Perplexity, and DeepSeek, deeply embedded in daily writing, editing, code generation, and learning. They enhance efficiency and speed while adhering to basic writing rules, but they also come with ethical issues, misinformation (hallucination phenomena), unstable quality of writing, and even potentially affecting long-term thinking patterns.
This lecture will explore the pitfalls and adaptation strategies for the use of LLMs, including:
Scenarios for which LLMs Are Applicable and Unapplicable
How to Make LLMs Your Ally Rather Than a Barrier
How to Enhance Review and Prompt Design Capability
LLMs are powerful but not omnipotent tools; learning to use them correctly can truly unlock their value. Attend the lecture to help you驾驭 LLMs more effectively and enhance your writing and critical thinking skills!

activity_name_activity_session: CARDO 113-2 Internship Program for TIP Enterprises Presentation Meeting
activity_content:
Internships serve as a crucial bridge between school education and workplace professionalism, and they are also key mechanisms for integrating theory with practice. An effective internship system requires collaboration between schools and enterprises to be successful. Given this, the Management College of Taiwan University invited companies committed to establishing an internship system to become partners in the TAIDA Internship Program (TIP), a program jointly operating the supply chain for intern activities, including demand confirmation, resume compilation, communication selection, mentor coaching, and performance evaluation. The goal is to build an effective platform for internship matching and utilization, together cultivating elite leadership talents for both enterprises and society.

National Taiwan University’s Business Administration College upholds the philosophy of cultivating outstanding management talents, striving to nurture students with professional knowledge in both academics and research. It also hopes that its graduates will have an advantage in the workplace when facing global competition. TIP offers a way for students to understand the workplace within just a few months, observing work skills and trends, training workplace etiquette and attitudes, confirming their interests and goal directions, strengthening their weaknesses early before graduation, and adding internship experience to their resumes to secure the key first step towards future career development.

Company Introduction
Ernst & Young - ESG Strategy and Sustainable Development Advisory Department
The EY team is dedicated to developing new global sustainable development solutions for clients, with the expectation that clients will find business opportunities and create value from sustainability and decarbonization while helping them integrate ESG into broader strategies.

Internship Program
Goal: Provide excellent internship environments for students at National Taiwan University to cultivate talent with a passion for sustainability and expertise.
Duration: June 10, 2011 ~ December 10, 2011
Title: Sustainable Intern; Number of名额: 1-3
Job Content:
Assist in creating proposal for sustainable development planning and prepare training presentation on sustainability.
Assist enterprises in developing sustainable strategies and optimizing existing sustainable management organizations and mechanisms, establish a sustainable culture.
Assist enterprises in developing ESG brand strategies and connecting with relevant matters
Assist customers in adopting various international standards, including: GRI, TCFD, SASB, RBA, SBTi, Equator Principles, Principles for Responsible Investment, Principles for Responsible Insurance, Principles for Responsible Banking, and Stewardship Code for Institutional Investors.
Assist enterprises in establishing sustainable supply chains and related ESG management programs优化
Assist enterprises in compiling sustainability reports (ESG Reports)
Other work items: - Participating in new service development
Utilizing new tools to develop methodologies, continuous learning and innovation, sharing knowledge with team members, while optimizing service efficiency and processes – departmental administration and marketing affairs
The service offerings include:
TCFD, SASB, Carbon Strategy Analysis, CDP, Sustainable Supply Chain, TNFD, SROI, EcoVadis, Human Rights尽职调查, PRI, PSI, PRB, DJSI, MSCI
Environmental, energy and climate-related ISO or management systems, SBTi, internal carbon pricing
Sustainability Services: ESG Reports, Sustainability Training Programs, Establishment of a Sustainability Committee, Evaluation of External Board Performance for Sustainability and other Sustainability Development Strategies
Assurance on the verification or review of non-historical financial information
Qualifications 1. Team spirit and good communication and expression skills; enthusiasm for sustainable development/ESG issues; proactive, efficient at work, and able to operate independently 2. Familiarity or willingness to develop content related to corporate sustainability improvement 3. Familiarity or willingness to develop research on corporate-related regulations and trends in the renewable energy sector 4. Good project management skills 5. Skilled in information collection and analysis with the ability to write high-quality reports
Candidates with the following experience尤佳: are particularly welcome.
Experience, enthusiasm, and sensitivity in climate-related financial disclosures, SBTi, carbon and resource management, corporate ESG risk management, environmental impact assessments of corporate finances, international supply chain procurement or auditing practices, etc.
Experience in executing enterprise operation and management analysis, market analysis, brand strategy, and risk analysis projects.
* Relevant certifications such as TCFD, environmental, energy resources and climate-related ISO or management systems, etc.
Salary and Benefits: Intern $205/hr, Consultant over $40k, Senior Consultant negotiable
Application method: https://reurl.cc/OYkeNA
Deadline for submission: April 30, 20114

session_name: Elite PR
session_content:
Company Introduction
Elite Public Relations Group is Taiwan’s largest public relations firm, founded in 1987. “Achieve by Communication: Dreams Through Communication” is our vision, and becoming “Where the Elite Meet: The Gathering Place for the Elite” is our goal for talent cultivation.
The Elite Public Relations Group has 13 subsidiaries, including Elite, Model, Classic, Spectacular, Profound, Precise Digital Strategy Marketing, Essence, Cohesive, Sustainable Enterprise Excellence, Refine PR, Essence Asia-Pacific PR, Merge Integration Marketing, and Refine Integrated Marketing. As Taiwan’s largest and fastest-growing independent public relations group, it is a designated partner for many international public relations companies. The Elite Group is dedicated to developing international cooperation business, and in recent years has established alliances of independent public relations company partners with over 50 overseas partner companies, extending its reach from Taiwan into the Asia-Pacific region, as well as Europe and North America.
Elite Public Relations Group has approximately 200 employees, including a team of public relations consultants and digital multimedia designers, providing clients with comprehensive communication services that are diverse, professional, and effective. It has received multiple awards for international and Taiwan-based PR and digital categories, including the Silver Award for Agency of the Year in 2020 in Taiwan’s PR agency category, as well as 28 wins in PR Awards Asia, which includes six Gold Awards and ten major Chinese-language PR and communication-related awards.
Elite Public Relations Group has been established for nearly 40 years, with founder Kung Cheng-jiu originally serving in high-level positions at media companies. At the time, he recognized the importance of public relations services to corporate operations and development, leading him to establish Taiwan’s first PR company – Elite Public Relations – and publish a comprehensive book on both theoretical and practical aspects of local PR. Over the years, Elite has served more than 200 domestic and international enterprises. The group hopes that in the future, Elite Public Relations Group will continue to lead the vibrant development of the Taiwanese PR industry!

Internship Program
Goal: PR Internship Program Launch 60 Days #EliteIntern Summer Internship Plan
Schedule: July 1, 2025 to August 29, 2025 Monday to Friday 09:00-17:30
Title: Intern Student for Summer Program Slots: 20
Job Content: Interned at 13 subsidiaries under the Group. Details can be found in the Internship Plan Document: https://reurl.cc/p9O9a4
Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree or above, especially master’s level. Open to all majors. Please do not register if you have plans to go abroad or personal reasons that prevent you from fully participating during the course and group competition period.
Compensation and Benefits: Internships are not part-time jobs! We plan various courses and competitions to enrich our offerings. Interns can actually experience PR life, integrate with their respective subsidiaries, participate in training sessions, meetings, on-site visits, birthday parties, outings, as well as cross-company lunch meet-ups for different stages of PR journey sharing events.
After completing one month of internship, we will evaluate daily performance and fitability as the basis for second-month internship eligibility. After completing two months of internship, a graduation certificate and a scholarship of one thousand yuan will be issued.
Application method: For more details, refer to the Internship Program document: https://reurl.cc/p9O9a4
Deadline for submission: Before 17:00 on May 1, 2025 (Thursday)

session_name: Goodwill Securities
session_content:
Company Introduction
Daqing Securities was established in 1988 and became a listed company on the Taiwan Stock Exchange in 2003, with net assets of NT$4.642 billion. Over the years, it has adhered to the经营理念 of "practical and steady, honest management," providing various financial services to its clients, including securities trading, consulting, underwriting, proprietary trading, futures, and credit transactions. Currently, there are approximately 400 employees in total across Taiwan, with 13 branches spread throughout North, Central, and South regions, located in nine places in the North, two in the Central region, and two in the South. After being integrated into Asia Value Capital Group, Daqing Securities has consolidated the group's diverse financial service capabilities, enhancing asset management services through competitive asset management products and professional reliable investment advisors to create more comprehensive value for clients and shareholders. In 2022, it was renamed Good Securities with the English name "GOOD FINANCE SECURITIES CO., LTD." This reminds us that "it is not just about wealth; it's about good," emphasizing that wealth is merely a tool. The new brand name aligns more closely with the company’s core values and reinforces its development vision.

Internship Program
Period: July 1, 20114 ~ December 31, 20114
Title: Financial Advisor; Number of Positions: 20
Responsibilities: As a financial advisor, you possess sufficient professional knowledge to provide meaningful solutions for clients who have unmet needs. You are the warm welcome companion and guide that leads them into a beautiful financial journey, listening to their aspirations, financial situations, investment experiences, risk-bearing capabilities, offering advice, selling products, and following up on subsequent investment outcomes and ongoing adjustments.
Qualifications: Holding securities broker and senior securities broker certifications are particularly advantageous.
Salary benefits: 1. Monthly salary of NT$ 30,000 2. CPF (Central Provident Fund) 3. In-service education and training
Application method: Please send your application to the HR mailbox: james.wang@goodfinance.com
Deadline for submission: 11/6/30

Company Introduction
Qwen Company is a related entity of诚享 International Company and a subsidiary of Questwood Corporation. It is a trade/trade agency company that collaborates directly with retailers in the Oceania region, particularly focusing on product packaging and transportation design. Its main partners are Australian retail companies such as Bunnings (home improvement/DIY), Coles (FMCG), The Reject Shop (discount stores), Priceline (Pharmacy), etc. Operating in the Australian market for five years now, it also represents Metromax America Corporation, a retail company that includes Wal-Mart, Sam’s Club, Costco, Big Lots, Target, Home Depot, Aldi, Lowe’s, among others. It is actively developing markets in Central and South America as well.

Soring Products
Product报价
cost analysis
financial statements preparation
Internship Conditions:
One、
A. Annual transcripts from freshman year of university are required (including class rank or percentage).
Sophomore or higher in any college major
Two、
Recipients receive scholarships from the company for tuition and fees of undergraduate second, third, and fourth years totaling NT$30,000.
Three,
Hire students must intern at our company during winter/summer breaks (excluding labor and health insurance). Salary is 30,000.
During regular working hours, schedule three days on your own from Monday to Friday and work a full day of internships on Saturdays. Salary is 25,000 yuan.

Priority is given to those from impoverished backgrounds.

activity_content:
Target: No. 274 College of Literature Music Festival "Hearing Literature • Seeing Aká"
Performance time: March 14, 2025 (Friday) 12:30-13:20
Venue: College Hall Lobby
Entry is free.
Organizers
National Taiwan University College of Literature
Organizing Unit: National Taiwan University Department of Musicology
Vocal Ensemble Voco Novo
activity_name_event_page: Literature College Music Festival "Hearing Literature • Seeing Aká"
session_name: Literature College Music Festival "Hearing Literature • Seeing Aká"
session_content:
Performance Pieces
Strive to Soar
Early early
A moment ago
I went to Paris again.
Xi Zi Girl
Tender
7. Flower
Mountain

Band Introduction
Voco Novo, a cappella group was established in 2009 and is one of the few groups in Taiwan’s a cappella scene that has experience with multiple跨界 collaborations. They are skilled at their craft, have diverse musical styles, and not only perform popular and contemporary vocal jazz music but also excel in showcasing the natural sound and lines of songs without any instrumentation, as well as original compositions rooted in personal experiences. They are adept at reinterpreting Taiwanese folk songs to create aural pictures. “Visible Music” is Voco Novo’s ideal for music, aiming to help listeners imagine beauty in Taiwan through their music and feel the scenery of another land.

Voco Novo, established in less than a year, won numerous awards in both domestic and international competitions. This includes the "Best Performance Award" and "Best Arrangement Award" for "The Best Song" at the 2010 Taiwan International Duo Art Festival; the "Best Lead Singer Award" at the 2010 Seoul International A Cappella Festival, South Korea; the "Gold Award Second Place" at the 2011 Taiwan International Duo Art Festival, along with another "Best Performance Award" and "Best Arrangement Award"; the "Gold Award Second Place" in the jazz category and "Gold Award Third Place" in the pop category at the 2012 vokal.total. International A Cappella Festival in Graz, Austria, for their piece "Flower Under the Tree," which also won the "Best Arrangement Award"; the 2013 CARA "American Modern A cappella Album Award" for "Best Asian Album" and "Best World Music Song"; second place at the 2015 Tampere World Voice Music Festival in Finland; and their latest album received the "Best Cross-genre Album" award at the 35th Chuanyi Golden Melody Awards in 2024.

Program Introduction
Voco Novo, a cappella singers showcase a unique arrangement and interpretation style that differs from the mainstream performance form of a cappella groups today, presenting songs with the most natural sound color and lines of human voices, as well as original compositions rooted in personal experiences. They also adapt Taiwanese folk songs into a cappella music with vivid imagery. "Visible Music" is Voco Novo's ideal for music, aiming to help listeners imagine beauty in Taiwan through music and feel the scenery of another land.

session_name: Shengde Pharmaceutical Co.
session_content:
Company Introduction
Sinomach Pharmaceutical was established in 1959 as a multi-dose pharmaceutical company. In 1998, Sinomach underwent its first business model transformation focusing on the development and manufacturing of liquid drug products to provide diversified liquid medication solutions that meet clinical needs. After ten years of effort, Sinomach’s liquid product market share in Taiwan reached over 70%. To break through growth limitations, Sinomach initiated a second transformation at various levels, implementing reforms and innovations to adopt a dual-track strategy for organic and strategic growth. Besides expanding its range of liquid products, Sinomach also ventured into the investment sector for the first time, positioning itself as “Biotech Industrial Bank” by founding Qingsheng Venture Capital and Qingsheng Biotech, investing in and nurturing the biotech industry. In 2018, Sinomach entered a mature phase where both its pharmaceutical and investment businesses achieved excellent results. As its investment business expanded year after year, Sinomach launched a third transformation to officially become “Professional Biotech Venture Capital,” with the goal of assisting invested companies in repositioning, acquiring, and selling them to create a biotech unicorn valued at 30 billion yuan, while exploring opportunities in emerging industries and seeking a second growth engine.

Production, Sales, People, Logistics, Finance
Quota: 25名
Goal: Sinopharm hopes to help participants like you understand the current state and future development trends of the industry, business operation models, and the work attitude required before stepping out of school through the Sinopharm Talent Training Camp CLB. It also aims to assist you in further understanding your future career direction.
Course Plan: We will provide an internship program that meets the expectations of gamers and helps you upgrade quickly, including:
World-class comprehensive selection courses:
Provides foundational courses across production, sales, research and development, finance, and other aspects of enterprise operation, business presentations for "Must-Know in the Workplace," project management skills, and diverse liberal arts courses such as understanding enterprises and industries – helping students quickly understand businesses and industries.
Career insights from industry heavyweights:
Senior industry players like Lien Rongjin, chairman of Shengde Group, and senior executives from over ten companies under the Shengde Group personally teach and share secrets to career planning and maintaining enthusiasm.
Internship opportunities at multi-unit company:
Provide hot internship opportunities from multiple enterprises under Shengde Group for students to accumulate cross-departmental practical experience and workplace战斗力.
Career对接: After courses end, top performers will be invited by Shentong to visit China Investment Company.
Selection criteria: 1. Fill out the CARDO application form 2. Pass the company interview

activity_name_activity_session: "Modern Manchu Identity and the Evolution of the Chinese Nation" - A Lecture on Modern Manchu Identity and the Transformation of the Chinese Nation
activity_content: National Chengchi University's Professor Lo Kailun International Sinology Lecture Chair and National Taiwan University's Yu Ying-shih International Sinological Research Center will jointly host a series lecture by Professor Mark C. Elliott of Harvard University on March 13 (Thursday) in the first conference hall of the Administration Building.\nWelcome for all students and faculty of this school to enthusiastically participate.
activity_name_event_page: "Modern Manchu Identity and the Evolution of the Chinese Nation" - A Lecture on Modern Manchu Identity and the Transformation of the Chinese Nation
session_name: "Modern Manchu Identity and the Evolution of the Chinese Nation" - A Lecture on Modern Manchu Identity and the Transformation of the Chinese Nation
session_content:
Speaker: Professor Mark C. Elliott / Chair Professor of East Asian Languages and Civilizations at Harvard University
Host: Professor Chen Weihua / Chair Professor of History at National Taiwan University and Director of the Yü Yingchi International Sinology Research Center
Topic: Modern Manchu Identity and the Evolution of the Chinese Nation
Time: March 13th, Thursday (14:00-16:00)
Location: Administrative Building First Conference Hall
Speech Summary:
To further explore the connections between Qing history and modern China, the third lecture continues to focus on themes of identity and unity, examining the fate of the Manchus after the fall of the Qing dynasty when they went from being a pariah people in the Republic to a minority nationality in the PRC. Having played a crucial role in the political awakening of the Han nation, the Manchus were essential to sustaining the narrative that modern China’s “unified polyethnic state” transcends the hua-yi discourse. In leading to a re-emphasis of the ethnocultural unity of the Chinese nation, renewed reliance on historical narrative as a political resource in the 21st century creates new challenges for historians.


To further explore the connections between Qing history and modern China, the third lecture continued to focus on the themes of identity and unification, with a particular emphasis on the historical trajectories of Manchu people after their demise in the aftermath of the Republic era as弃民 (exiles) and minorities during the Republic era. The Manchus played a key role in the awakening of Han Chinese politics, and for maintaining the narrative of a “unified multi-ethnic state” beyond the limitations of the “barbarian-barbarian dichotomy,” the Manchu were still seen as an indispensable component. By the 21st century, historical narratives once again became political resources, seemingly prompting renewed attention to the unity of Chinese culture, which also posed new challenges for historians.

activity_name_activity_session: "Joint Lecture on New Trends of Civilization" — Future Society (Part II): Viewing the Development of Society (Higher Education) and Technology (Artificial Intelligence) from an Evolutionary Perspective (Director Li Junzhi, Academia Sinica)
activity_content:
"Jointly Writing New Trends in Civilization Academic Lecture"
Topic: Future Society (Part 2): Viewing the Development of Science and Technology (AI) and Society from an Evolutionary Perspective (University Level)
Speaker: Liou Jen-Chyi (President of the Academia Sinica)
Host: Ching Yu-ju (President of National Taiwan University College of Liberal Arts and a Member of the Academia Sinica)
Time: March 13 (Thu) 14:20-15:40
Location: National Taiwan University College of Literature Lecture Hall
Registration link: https://forms.gle/gBm6v72CaLa5iVmo6
Organizers: National Taiwan University College of Literature, Trend Education Foundation, Co-Working Civilization New Trends, Symphony of Humanities and Technology

How does human civilization evolve? Will artificial intelligence surpass humans?
The fundamental problems addressed by humanities and science are to seek regularities in the variability of history, society, or natural phenomena in order to predict future trends. From Darwin’s theory of survival of the fittest to the rise of artificial intelligence today, we are at the crest of a technological transformation wave. Universities are a microcosm of human society, while AI is the pinnacle of current technology. This lecture will introduce the evolution theory from a macro perspective, discuss the differences between humans and machines, and illustrate how an evolutionary approach—interaction between variation and selection—can be used to understand the rapid changes in both society and technology using examples such as universities and artificial intelligence.

activity_content:

The courses combine diverse nutritional knowledge with exercise training classes, helping to establish correct dietary concepts and cultivate a habit of consistent exercise, integrating a healthy lifestyle into daily life.

activity_name_activity_session: Winning Off to Success! English Workplace Skills Workshop
activity_content:
Content of Activities
Under the guidance of experienced teacher Olivia, a senior at school, students improve their English work skills and create career value!
Perfect for all new graduates or anyone looking to learn from expatriates or multinational company professionals.

Content of Matches:

March 12 (Wednesday) New Multi Gain Gold Training Center
3/20 (Thursday) Efficiency English Briefing Technique
English interview and resume winning strategies on 4/23 (Wednesday)
English speaking ability essential for the workplace

Class teacher
Hsing Hong Olivia Teacher
Education: Master of TESOL from Pennsylvania University, USA
Proficient in: TOEFL/TOEIC/Business English
Experience: Senior English Lecturer / Invited Speaker for Enterprises and Academic Institutions / Interview on Education Radio

Target Audience: This course is limited to faculty and staff members as well as students of our school. Whether you are at any level of English proficiency, this course welcomes participants who wish to enhance their workplace-related abilities in English.

Class Information:
Classroom: National Taiwan University School of Social Science Library 2F Presentation Room
Class time: March 12 (Wed), March 20 (Thu), April 23 (Wed), April 28 (Mon); 1:20 PM – 3:20 PM.
Don't miss this great learning opportunity!
Register now: https://my.ntu.edu.tw/actregister/sessionList.aspx?actID=20241911_04

2025 "Interdisciplinary Internship - Taiwan Pathfinder Program" summer internship applications are about to begin.
The Student Career Center of the Academic Affairs Office invited over 70 companies from various fields to participate, offering internship positions regardless of major. This year, we are continuously hiring summer and long-term interns across various fields. We have 1:1 career mentors guiding you through real-world project work. There are also a variety of training courses and solid case competitions to help build your workplace competitiveness. 💪

The orientation meeting for internships not only shares the content and application procedures of the internship program but also invites graduating alumni and previous interns to share their experiences working at SAIC Motor.

Daily entry provides meals for the first 70 attendees, and a Marshall portable Bluetooth speaker and a Kobo 7-inch color e-reader will be drawn from those present. Welcome everyone to sign up!

session_name: Talent Recruitment Plan Meeting for Internship Program at [Youda Optoelectronics]
session_content:
The uncertainty and anxiety about the future you lack is just an opportunity to explore and practice yourself, with friends accompanying you on your career step.
The Friend A Seed Summer Internship program has been held over more than 20 years, offering project-based internships to give you a deep understanding of real-world work scenarios, apply what you've learned, and prepare for your future career.

Three distinctive features
Internship project: Responsible for independent projects that apply what has been learned.
Mentor Coaching: Dedicated Mentor Assistance for Project Execution and Work Environment Adaptation.
Expert Review: Strict internal and external expert reviews, with opportunities for a pre-offer and generous scholarships.

Three Big Benefits
Intern stipend: Competitive intern stipend (monthly salary), 50 yuan meal allowance provided for lunch and dinner.
Free dormitory:贴心 provides free double room and shuttle bus within the factory area.
Multifaceted Experience: Offering comprehensive fitness facilities, product showroom tours, cultural experiences, hosting events, and featuring celebrity speeches.

Explanation will provide answer/comment prizes.

session_content:
Nestle Taiwan X NTU Sustainability Field Meeting
Do you love challenges, possess unlimited creativity, and hope to have a positive social impact in your work?

Join Nestle and bring your creativity and inspiration!
At Nestlé, you will have the opportunity to participate in various fields such as #innovative product development, #marketing strategy formulation, and #digital transformation. We encourage every partner to challenge the status quo and come up with new ideas, turning inspiration into key forces that transform industries.

Join Nestle and lead the sustainable future!
We are actively promoting sustainable development, from reducing our carbon footprint, developing recyclable packaging materials, supporting local agriculture and social responsibility initiatives, creating a more positive impact on the environment, society, and consumers.

Still want to know more? Welcome to participate in this NTU Sustainability专场说明会! We will invite representatives from Nestlé’s sustainability department, Nespresso marketing team, and current interns at NTU to share compelling stories about the Be a Force for Good initiative, as well as how Nestlé Taiwan’s reserve cadre and internship program can help you achieve your career goals!

session_name: March 10 (I) Brain Rot Generation: Are We Losing the Ability to Think?
session_content: In an age of endless scrolling, short-form videos, and instant gratification, are we losing our ability to focus and think critically? The term "brain rot" refers to the phenomenon of excessive screen time and shallow content consumption reducing our attention span and deep thinking skills. This discussion explores whether digital culture is making us less intelligent or simply changing how we process information. Are we truly experiencing "brain rot," or is this just a new way of engaging with the world?\n\nIn an endless age of scrolling through smartphones, short videos, and instant gratification, are we losing our ability to focus and critical thinking? "Brain rot" refers to the phenomenon where attention declines and cognitive abilities weaken due to excessive use of digital media and consumption of shallow content. This gathering will explore whether digital culture has truly made us less intelligent or simply altered how we receive and process information. Are we really "brain-rotted," or is this just a part of cultural evolution? What can be done to save our brains?

session_name: March 17 (I) Debunking Impostor Syndrome: The Fear of Not Being Good Enough
session_content: Many people, even those who are highly accomplished, feel like they don’t deserve their success. Imposter syndrome is the persistent fear of being exposed as a “fraud” despite external achievements. It can affect confidence, motivation, and mental well-being. This discussion examines why so many people struggle with self-doubt, whether imposter syndrome is a personal issue or a societal one, and how we can overcome the fear of not being “good enough.”\n\nMany people, even after achieving success, still feel unworthy and fear being exposed as a “fraud.” This phenomenon is known as the Imposter Syndrome, which can affect confidence, motivation, and mental health. This gathering will explore why this self-doubt is so prevalent, whether it’s a personal issue or a result of societal structures, and how to overcome fears of not being good enough.

session_name: March 24 (Monday) Chasing Stars: How Idols Inspire and Empower
session_content: For many fans, idols are more than just entertainers—they are sources of inspiration, motivation, and personal growth. Whether through their hard work, resilience, or messages of self-love, idols have a profound impact on their followers. This discussion explores how fandom culture helps individuals build confidence, develop skills, and create meaningful connections. Can pursuing stars actually help us become better versions of ourselves?\n\nFor many fans, idols are not just entertainment figures but also sources of motivation and growth. Their efforts, perseverance, and self-love messages often have a profound impact on their fans. This gathering will explore how the culture of idol worship can help individuals grow, boost confidence, develop skills, and even build meaningful community relationships. Does following stars really make us better?

activity_name_activity_session: Overseas Chinese and Mainland Chinese Student Service Intelligence Workshop
activity_content:
Thank you to everyone who has been putting in effort to serve Hsinchu students. To give more opportunities for mutual exchange and learning, as well as to understand the situation of Hsinchu students at school and related resources from the schools, the Student Affairs Office's Overseas Chinese and Local Students Guidance Group specially held this event. We welcome everyone to participate enthusiastically.
Participants who complete the activity will receive 3 hours of lifetime learning credits, and there will be meals available for lunch.
Time: March 10, 2025 (Monday) 9:30 AM - 12:00 PM
Location: Administrative Building First Conference Room
3. Content Arrangement: Sharing of practical work experiences from the Introduction to Overseas Students Overview, Counseling Center for Academic Affairs, Counseling Center for Student Psychology, Campus Safety Center, Career Development Center for Students, Office of Learning Planning in the Academic Affairs Department, and Student Case Counseling, Q&A session to exchange insights.
Eligible participants include staff from institutes, departments (centers), and administrative units involved in promoting mentorship for overseas students. Faculty members from within the university are also welcome to register.

Thank you for being so dedicated to serving Overseas Chinese and Mainland Chinese students. To provide more opportunities for mutual exchange and learning and better understand the current status and relevant resources available to these students, the Overseas Chinese and Mainland Chinese Students Advising Division, Office of Student Affairs, will hold the following sessions. Welcome to join us!
Participants who fully attend the event will be awarded 3 lifelong learning hours. Lunch will be provided.
Date: March 10, 2025 (Monday) from 9:30 AM to 12:00 PM
Location: Conference Room 1, Administrative Building
3. Agenda: Introduction to overseas Chinese and mainland Chinese students, presentations from the Center for Student Well-Being, Student Counseling Center, Student Safety Center, and Career Center of Office of Student Affairs, Academic Advising Office of Office of Academic Affairs, and a sharing on practical work experience in student case counseling, followed by a Q&A session for exchanging insights.
Target Audience: Colleagues from departments and administrative units promoting counseling services for overseas Chinese and mainland Chinese students and interested university colleagues.

session_name: Career Lecture - Guo Fu Hao Hua
session_content:
"Sustainability in the Workplace: Roles and Development of Accounting Firms and ESG Advisors"
Company Introduction
Crowe Taiwan, a Crowe Global independent member firm in Taiwan, was established on January 1, 2013 by combining First Accounting Firm, Jingyao Accounting Firm, and Jianxing Accounting Firm based on the shared business philosophy. Another accounting firm, Dingxin Accounting Firm, joined Crowe Taiwan on January 1, 2015 to form a strong professional service team. The firm has over 41 practicing accountants and more than 450 professionals who provide auditing, tax, and corporate consulting services for clients. With thirty-plus years of practical experience accumulated by the service team, they have served various industries including general companies, publicly traded and listed firms, multinational corporations, schools, and non-profit organizations such as foundations. Crowe Taiwan not only has its headquarters in Taipei but also has branches in Taichung, Taichung, Changhua, and Kaohsiung to form a global service network with Crowe Global (Crowe Taiwan). Crowe Global operates over 200 independent member firms in more than 130 countries worldwide, providing auditing, tax, and corporate consulting services through its global network.

activity_name_event_page: Career Lecture 113-2
session_name: Career Lecture - Sonatus US Auto Software Company
session_content:
Speech Theme
The starting point doesn't determine the endpoint.
Introduction to my journey from being a mold worker to becoming an overseas business manager, as well as experiences and insights from my time at National Taiwan University's EMBA program and National Chengchi University’s PhD program.

Company Introduction
Sonatus Inc. is a technology company dedicated to accelerating automotive software innovation and driving the development of Software-Defined Vehicles (SDV). Founded in 2018, it has headquarters in Sunnyvale, California, USA, and currently operates branches or offices in ten countries globally, including Tokyo, Seoul, Shanghai, Taipei, Bengaluru, Paris, Frankfurt, Dublin, Warsaw, New Jersey, Detroit, among others.
Sonatus’s vehicle and cloud software solutions provide the key technology for automotive manufacturers to realize the full potential of a Software-Defined Car. Its products and platforms serve as important software building blocks, enabling automotive companies and their ecosystems to innovate faster throughout the vehicle lifecycle and continuously improve cost, functionality, reliability, and user experience.
The company’s software platform has been applied to modern, Kia, and Genesis-branded production vehicles. By the end of 2024, more than four million vehicles had Sonatus’ software and services installed. Sonatus’ solutions cover the entire lifecycle from vehicle design, engineering, production, through after-sales service, supporting various types of vehicles including passenger cars and commercial vehicles.
Sonatus’s core advantage lies in its unique team composition, which blends engineering and operations talent from the IT/data center sector with top-tier talent from automotive OEMs and suppliers. This enables the company to have unparalleled expertise in software-defined vehicle technology.
The company has secured over $110 million in funding, with investors including Foxconn, the Kia Corporation of Hyundai Motor Group, LG Electronics, Marvell, and Capital of SAIC among others – known automotive, tech, and venture capital companies. Sonatus’s innovative achievements have also been recognized by the industry, having received multiple honors such as the 2022 PACE Award and CES Innovation Awards.
Looking ahead, Sonatus aims to become a leading supplier in software-defined automotive technology through its world-class research and innovation in car software and cloud software, providing strong support for the digital transformation of the auto industry.
