activity_name_activity_session: Fire Safety Management Initial and Refresher Training
activity_content:
One, pursuant to this school's 20114 academic personnel training plan.
II. According to Article 13 of the Fire Prevention Law, buildings with a certain scale or above should have their management entities appoint fire safety managers and require them to formulate fire protection plans. These fire protection plans must be reported by the management entity to the competent authority for record-keeping at the location of the building before implementation, and they should carry out necessary business related to fire safety management according to these plans. Fire safety managers should be personnel at a management or supervisory level within the premises, and after being trained by the competent authority or registered professional institutions designated by the central competent authority for a certain number of hours, holding a qualified certificate, they can take up their posts; during their tenure, they must undergo regular refresher training. Re-examining the provisions in Articles 3, 4, and 5 of the management method for fire safety managers, the competent authority provides training to fire safety managers, which is divided into initial training and refresher training. Those who obtain a qualified certificate after initial training are deemed qualified as fire safety managers. Persons obtaining this qualification should undergo at least one refresher training session every three years starting from the end of their initial training. The duration of initial training must not be less than 12 hours, while that of refresher training must not be less than 6 hours.
The relevant matters of the above-mentioned training:
Lecture: Instructor from Taipei City Government Fire Bureau.
Location: 1 Conference Room, Administrative Building, Total Campus of Our School.
Training Participants:
Basic Training: Personnel responsible for fire management in each unit of this school (quota 100 people, application deadline reached).
Re-training: Applicants with Fire Management Certificate (either initial or retraining) certificates (quota of 100 people, registration closes upon capacity is reached. A copy of the initial/retraining certificate must be submitted during registration).
Time:
Introductory training: March 5-6, 2011 (Wednesday and Thursday).
Retraining: March 7, 2014 (Friday).
Exams are conducted immediately after the course ends; those who pass receive a certification for initial training, and those who pass retraining receive a certification for retraining (valid for 3 years).
Four, Registration Procedure (please make sure to complete both paper and online registration procedures simultaneously):
Paper registration:
Please fill out the registration form, have it signed by your unit supervisor, and submit it to Ms. Huang in the Training Section of Human Resources before the stated deadline (all fields on the form should be filled out accurately; incomplete forms will be returned for correction).
Trainees who attend refresher courses must attach a copy of their initial (re)training qualification certificate on the back of the registration form.
Online registration:
Other relevant matters:
This training is conducted on a public holiday with the consent of unit management and participants are responsible for completing their leave procedures. Please make sure to attend all sessions, which will be verified for lifetime learning credits. Participants should sign in 10 minutes prior to the start of the session on the day of the training and no separate notice will be sent. Relevant course materials for this training will be sent to participants separately.
Lunch boxes are provided at the training; please bring your own eco-friendly utensils and water cup in accordance with the environmental policy.
Sixth, the electronic version of the correction letter for this case (with registration form and initial/review training course schedule), document number: 1130125693, can be downloaded from the announcements section under Campus Announcements on our school’s homepage.

activity_name_event_page: Fire Safety Management Initial and Refresher Training
session_name: Re-training for Fire Management Personnel
session_content:
One, pursuant to this school's 20114 academic personnel training plan.
II. According to Article 13 of the Fire Prevention Law, buildings with a certain scale or above should have their management entities appoint fire safety managers and require them to formulate fire protection plans. These fire protection plans must be reported by the management entity to the competent authority for record-keeping at the location of the building before implementation, and they should carry out necessary business related to fire safety management according to these plans. Fire safety managers should be personnel at a management or supervisory level within the premises, and after being trained by the competent authority or registered professional institutions designated by the central competent authority for a certain number of hours, holding a qualified certificate, they can take up their posts; during their tenure, they must undergo regular refresher training. Re-examining the provisions in Articles 3, 4, and 5 of the management method for fire safety managers, the competent authority provides training to fire safety managers, which is divided into initial training and refresher training. Those who obtain a qualified certificate after initial training are deemed qualified as fire safety managers. Persons obtaining this qualification should undergo at least one refresher training session every three years starting from the end of their initial training. The duration of initial training must not be less than 12 hours, while that of refresher training must not be less than 6 hours.
The relevant matters of the above-mentioned training:
Lecture: Instructor from Taipei City Government Fire Bureau.
Location: 1 Conference Room, Administrative Building, Total Campus of Our School.
Training Participants:
Basic Training: Personnel responsible for fire management in each unit of this school (quota 100 people, application deadline reached).
Re-training: Applicants with Fire Management Certificate (either initial or retraining) certificates (quota of 100 people, registration closes upon capacity is reached. A copy of the initial/retraining certificate must be submitted during registration).

Retraining: March 7, 2014 (Friday).

activity_name_activity_session: Taishun Chen, Chairman of China Great Ming Group, at Taichung University Coconut Grove Lectures Innovation Series
activity_content:
The Taichung Campus Lecture Series is a school-wide seminar inviting scholars or industry leaders with outstanding achievements or significant international influence to give lectures and academic exchanges, in order to broaden faculty and students' macroscopic global perspectives. This time, we are inviting Chairman Chen Tiejun of CUMG (China United Materials Group) for the lecture. We welcome all faculty and students to actively participate.

Activity information:
Speaker: Chen Tien-ming, Chairman of Grand Moldovan Group
Theme: Journey from Taiwan to Global
"Taiwan Company's Global Layout"
Time: March 5, 2025 (Wed) 15:30 - 17:00
Location: 2nd Floor Qian Siliang Memorial Lecture Hall (Integrated Grand Lecture Hall) of the Comprehensive Teaching Building in the Central Campus
Language: Chinese (Simultaneous interpreting available)

Activity schedule:
14:50-15:30: Registration and entry open (registration closes at 15:25)
Opening remarks: 15:30-15:35
15:35-16:35: Presentation Sharing
Discussion and Exchange: 16:35-16:55

Information:

From Taiwan to the World - "YAGEO's Journey to Globalization"
March 5th (Wednesday), 15:30-17:00, 2025
Venue: NTU Shih-Liang Chien Lecture Hall, 2nd floor, Zhonghe Lecture Building
Mandarin (simultaneous interpretation with headsets)

activity_name_event_page: Taishun Chen, Chairman of China Great Ming Group, at Taichung University Coconut Grove Lectures Innovation Series
session_name: Taishun Chen, Chairman of China Great Ming Group, at Taichung University Coconut Grove Lectures Innovation Series
session_content:
Details of the lecture;
https://cge.ntu.edu.tw/News_Content_n_68407_s_249212.html

Detailed Introduction to the Lecture:
https://cge.ntu.edu.tw/en/News_Content_n_68474_s_249213.html

Topic introduction for the speech:
This lecture will discuss how a Taiwanese company, which has been jointly owned for 38 years since its inception, transformed into a globalized company and is currently one of the top 30 companies in Taiwan's securities market. Chairman Chen Tien-ming experienced choices such as education, family life, love, employment, and entrepreneurship. What are the considerations when seeking employment? Why is choosing a company over a specific industry more important? What are the conditions for entrepreneurial success? How did Taiwanese companies break out of traditional thinking to enter the global market? Welcome to listen!

Speaker Introduction:
Mr. Chen Taiming is the founder and chairman of Kung Ji Group, as well as the chairman of companies such as Tongxin Electronics and Fu Ding Advanced Electronics. Since founding Kung Ji in 1986, Chairman Chen has led the company into becoming a global leader in electronic components services. Besides continuously developing advanced material technologies, he also actively pursues cross-border acquisitions, successfully acquiring Philips passive devices business group and American technology companies with over a century of history such as Base Electronic and Pulse Electronics among others, currently employing 45,000 people globally and having manufacturing bases spread across 61 locations in 35 countries. He is ranked second on the Taiwan CEO 100 list by Harvard Business Review. Chairman Chen has also actively participated in social responsibility and education development, receiving an honorary doctorate from Sun Yat-sen University in 2021 and the Outstanding Alumnus Award from his alma mater National Chengchi University in 2022 among other honors. He has made significant contributions to Taiwan's industry and the global electronics sector.

Crowe Taiwan, a Crowe Global independent member firm in Taiwan, was established on January 1, 2013 by combining First Accounting Firm, Jingyao Accounting Firm, and Jianxing Accounting Firm based on the shared business philosophy. Another accounting firm, Dingxin Accounting Firm, joined Crowe Taiwan on January 1, 2015 to form a strong professional service team. The firm has over 41 practicing accountants and more than 450 professionals who provide auditing, tax, and corporate consulting services for clients. With thirty-plus years of practical experience accumulated by the service team, they have served various industries including general companies, publicly traded and listed firms, multinational corporations, schools, and non-profit organizations such as foundations. Crowe Taiwan not only has its headquarters in Taipei but also has branches in Taichung, Taichung, Changhua, and Kaohsiung to form a global service network with Crowe Global (Crowe Taiwan). AnyMind Group Business Visit

Company Introduction
AnyMind is a comprehensive commerce enablement company that provides various digital systems and services to enterprises, influencers/creators, and website content publishers. It aims to assist anyone who wants to start a business using new-generation digital commerce tools, making it easier and faster for them to conduct their activities while fulfilling the corporate mission of "Making Every Business Borderless." In just nine years, AnyMind Group has expanded into 17 Asian markets including Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam, Japan, Hong Kong, Indonesia, Malaysia, and India. It currently operates 23 offices with over 1,800 employees from more than 27 different nationalities, and it has acquired seven companies in Asia and developed nine digital systems.
The on-the-ground teams at AnyMind Group, which serve clients across various industries such as beauty and cosmetics, FMCG (Fast-Moving Consumer Goods), kids, and healthcare in countries throughout Asia, can assist brands in cross-border marketing or brand events, allowing good brands and ideas to be less constrained by market limitations. In addition, AnyMind Group is not only Google’s official Google Certified Publisher Partners but also an officially certified creator service supplier for YouTube. We welcome anyone with passion for marketing and the digital space to grow alongside top-tier international tech companies and create more value for our clients.

activity_name_activity_session: Forum Reflection on Neoliberalism (Session 13): "Peace and Neoliberalism"
activity_content:
Date: March 3, 2025 (Monday)

Time: 09:30 - 16:20 (GMT 08)

Location: National Taiwan University Linze Hall Third Floor Multimedia Classroom 1301

Forum Abstract:

Peace has been difficult to achieve in every era, and when examining the reasons behind this difficulty, various theories abound: human nature, gender, power, religion, economic interests, or their combinations. However, since capitalism took hold of modernity, it seems that war has become one of its primary motivations. Its latest evolution—“neoliberalism”—has been repeatedly squeezing the space for peace since the 1980s. Neoliberalism is characterized by a quasi-religious belief in market freedom and through “de-regulation,” Western digital media platforms have gained control over the flow of global information, thereby fostering an absolute faith among the digital generation that values Western values, especially extreme individual freedom espoused by neoliberalism. The pervasive domination of data capitalism and digital colonialism has led to a habituation where the digital generation views the world through the lens of neoliberalism’s black-and-white filter, with any cracks in this digital net (such as Douyin) being swiftly sealed off. Neoliberalism appeared on the brink of collapse due to trade wars but had already revived into version 2.0 thanks to the backing of the seven major digital companies in Silicon Valley (the Mag 7). This form of governance through lifestyle and value control, using digital brainwashing tactics, has become increasingly effective without even engaging in combat. In this context where neoliberalism nearly unifies global ideologies, the identities of God and devil are no longer debatable; does this mean that the hope for peace is also becoming more distant?

Introduction: Liao Xiaohao (President of the Institute of Humanities and Social Sciences at National Taiwan University, and Professor with Special Appointment in the Department of Foreign Languages and Literatures)

Speaker:
Wu Yushan (Member of the Academia Sinica and Adjunct Research Fellow of the Institute of Political Science at the Academia Sinica)
Chang Deng-Chih (Associate Director of the Institute of Humanities and Social Sciences at National Taiwan University and Professor of Political Science)
Chang Ming Wang (Research Fellow and Deputy Director of the European and American Studies Research Division at the Academia Sinica)
Da Wei (Honorary Professor at the Institute of Science and Society, Yangming University of Transportation, National Taiwan Normal University)

Organizers: Institute of Advanced Studies, National Taiwan University Humanities and Social Sciences
Organizers: National Science and Technology Program Humanities and Social Sciences Research Center, National Taiwan University, Foundation Day & Moonlight for Cultural Education Donation

session_name: Use PsycINFO to find psychological literature.
session_content: APA PsycInfo is a professional literature database developed by the American Psychological Association, an essential tool for psychological research. It assists in discovering knowledge in areas such as psychology, behavioral science, education, medicine, psychiatry, and social work through its precise indexing and rich resources. This course teaches you how to use the EBSCOhost platform to search PsycInfo and ASC (Academic Search Complete) databases, learn various searching techniques and filtering functions, and find journal articles, research papers, books, etc., assisting in deep exploration of research resources within the field of psychology.

session_name: First Episode: Career Starting Point: Breaking Down the Myth of Direction
session_content:
Date: February 19, 2025
Time: 18:30-20:00
Location: M5 Literature College Lecture Hall
Speaker Background: 1111 Human Resources Consultant Ruowaiting Chao
Activity content:
This lecture is specifically designed for individuals who are career-mired and aims to help attendees clarify their career development direction. The content focuses on three key areas,
Analyzing the Current Situation and Exposing Misconceptions: Revealing the Causes of Common Career Indecision and Helping Participants Identify Incorrect Beliefs to Re-focus on Their Professional Goals.
Analyzing Workplace Trends and Skill Requirements: Introducing trending career directions, analyzing core competencies needed for future markets, and inspiring inspiration for career choices.
Developing an Implementation Plan: Providing actionable career planning tools and cases to help participants develop personalized growth paths.
Participants will learn how to effectively assess their current state and develop clear career strategies, laying a foundation for career development.

session_name: Second Round: Putting Theory into Practice: Finding Professional Opportunities
session_content:
Second Round: Putting Theory into Practice: Finding Professional Opportunities

Date: February 26, 2025

Time: 18:30-20:00

Location: M5 Literature College Lecture Hall

Note: Students attending this session must bring their personal computers for on-site practical work.

Speaker Background:
1111 Career Advisor Ruowaitingteng
Kung-Feng Jiang, a former clerk at Taiwan Changshe Co., Ltd. and a graduate of Chinese Department at National Taiwan University.

This lecture will help participants convert what they have learned into workplace advantages by addressing the frustration of a mismatch between their educational background and job requirements. It is divided into three parts,

Skills for Translating Academic Background into Workplace Relevance: Introducing how to align an academic background with workplace needs and analyzing the translation skills and success stories.
Resume and CV Optimization Suggestions: Provide professional resume writing tips and presentation-making suggestions to highlight the advantage of personal connections in learning and using.
Sharing Success Stories: Explaining Actual Experience of Cross-Disciplinary Job Hunting and Inspiring Participants to Explore New Possibilities in Professional Applications.

Through examples and practical guidance, participants will be able to clearly demonstrate their personal professional value and quickly find the right workplace stage.

Location: 2nd Floor Seminar Room of Sociology and Anthropology Library
Session time: February 19, 2025 (Wednesday) 19:00-20:30
Speaker Introduction
Professor Shih Yu is a Distinguished National Lecturer of the Ministry of Education and an Honorary Professor in the Department of Political Science at our university. Professor Shih has authored numerous books over his 35-year career (1990-2024), totaling 114 published volumes. The types of publications are diverse, including: 31 monographs on academic subjects written in Chinese and 19 in English; 17 edited volumes in foreign languages such as English and Japanese, and 14 in Chinese; a collection of his own political commentary essays in Chinese with 14 books and 4 co-authored ones; and three collections of poetry in Chinese. The topics covered by these works are extensive, ranging from foreign policy and political psychology to cross-strait relations, ethnic minorities, knowledge communities, non-Western international relations theory, but with a core concern that remains consistent: deconstructing and reconstructing the changing world through the lens of China.

activity_name_activity_session: Academic Lecture on the 117th Birthday Anniversary of Professor Chen Peizhe (a Distinguished Member of the Academia Sinica), Dean Dr. Si Liang Gao's Memorial Academic Lecture in Clinical Medicine Research Institute
activity_content:
February 6, 2014 (Thursday) at 10:00 AM
Location: International Conference Hall of Tsinghua Memorial Hall, Da Tzu University Campus
Speaker: Professor Chen Peizhe from the Department of Clinical Medicine
Topic: Washed Away by the Waves of Popularity - Eradicating Hepatitis B into History

Viruses are the smallest units of life and can only reproduce by utilizing host cells, thus causing various diseases in hosts. This is also true for humans. For example, smallpox, measles, hepatitis viruses, and HIV. With advances in science and medicine, humans have gradually understood the characteristics of viruses and developed vaccines to prevent viral infections as well as drugs to treat viral infections, leading to significant progress. Thanks to effective vaccination campaigns, smallpox has been eradicated from human beings, becoming a part of history. Today, I want to talk about another very important chronic infection, hepatitis B virus (HBV). From the discovery of HBV and its immense burden on major diseases (especially in Chinese communities), as well as the development of vaccines, promoting widespread vaccination campaigns and antiviral drug treatments, this common disease has gradually become a rare one. The process of controlling HBV has involved three generations of scholars from Taiwan continuously contributing over five decades, showcasing Taiwan’s value to the world.

activity_name_event_page: Academic Ethics Lecture (Change of Venue) 113-1
session_name: Academic Ethics Lecture: January 9, 2014 - Associate Vice President and Chair of the Ethics Committee, Professor Zhou Qian, National Yangming University
session_content:
Important venue change: Due to high registration, the venue has been switched to the Comprehensive Lecture Hall on the second floor of the General Administration Building of the school. Please note this for attendees who have registered.

Activity information:
Topic: Writing Research Papers and Academic Ethics: Plagiarism and Self-Plagiarism
Time: January 9, 2025 (Thursday) 2:10 PM - 4:10 PM
Location: (Venue Change) School Central Campus Comprehensive Teaching Building Second Floor Qian Siliang Memorial Lecture Hall (Comprehensive Large Lecture Hall)
Speaker: Zhou Qian Life-long Lecturer
Deputy President and Ethics Commissioner of National Yang Ming Transportation University
Education Research Institute/Faculty Development Center Professor
Language: Chinese

Course description:
This lecture will introduce basic concepts of academic research, explore the内涵of academic ethics and its observance, explain the narrow and broad definitions of plagiarism, common scenarios distinguishing it from improper citation, and refer to relevant regulations by the Ministry of Education and NCKU. It will also define self-plagiarism, categorize its types, provide reasons for avoiding it, and explain how to adhere to these regulations in academic writing to enhance academic integrity and exhibit good scholarly conduct.

This session covers the academic ethics course category 2 for 2 hours; starting from the 107 academic year, graduate and doctoral students must cover at least three types totaling 6 hours of academic ethics courses. Participants are responsible for supplementing their own academic ethics training hours.

activity_name_activity_session: International Affairs Staff Training: International Student and Scholar Services Workshop
activity_content:
This training will include the following content:
Deputy heads of departments and international affairs staff share and discuss practical methods and strategies for addressing common issues in the execution of various business operations:
International scholar reception
Services and counseling-related operational processes for international students
International non-degree students: Non-degree students are reviewed by admission committees, their application data is filled out, and they receive counseling.
Introduction to the International Pathfinder Program
Taiwan Entry Permit Business for Academics and Non-degree Students in Mainland China

All international affairs personnel from all colleges and institutes are welcome to participate in this activity to enhance skills through mutual exchange and learning, and to promote cooperation and communication.
Lunch is provided on activity day.
Participants in the course can log 3 hours of public service lifelong learning credits.

This training session will cover the following topics by sharing and discussing best practices and solutions for challenges faced by departmental coordinators and staff from the International Office in handling the following tasks:

Welcoming and assisting international scholars.
Streamlining service and counseling workflows for international degree students.
Managing processes for international non-degree students, such as departmental reviews, data entry, and support services.
Learning about the International Mentoring Program.
Understanding entry permit procedures for scholars and non-degree students from mainland China.

All international affairs staff from colleges and departments are encouraged to participate. This training provides an opportunity to learn, share, and collaborate to improve support for international scholars and students.
By attending this Workshop, you will gain practical insights and tools for improving daily operations related to international affairs.
You will also learn from other departments' staff and colleagues through interdepartmental collaboration during the workshop, thereby enhancing the efficiency and quality of services provided to international students and scholars.
Lunch will be provided.
Participants who attend fully will receive 3 lifelong learning hours.
